metadata.title: Political Statements Timeline: Iran-Israel Conflict
metadata.description: A chronological collection of key political statements from world leaders regarding the Iran-Israel conflict, spanning from 2023 through early March 2026.
sources: U.S. Department of Defense Press Releases
Office of the Supreme Leader of Iran (khamenei.ir)
metadata.methodology: Statements were collected from official government communications, press conferences, verified social media accounts, and credible international news sources. Each statement has been reviewed for accuracy with a fact-check rating. All quotes are sourced from verified public records with clickable source links for independent verification.
metadata.disclaimer: All statements are sourced from official government communications, press conferences, and verified news reports. Quotes may be lightly paraphrased for clarity while preserving the speaker's documented position. Click source links to verify each statement independently. Fact-check ratings reflect editorial assessment based on available evidence.

statements.text: The Iran deal was the worst agreement ever negotiated. We got out and the world is safer because of it.
statements.context: Speaking at a campaign rally in Iowa, Trump reiterated his justification for withdrawing the U.S. from the JCPOA in 2018, claiming the decision improved global security.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Trump did withdraw from the JCPOA, multiple intelligence assessments indicated Iran resumed enrichment activities after the withdrawal, arguably reducing safety. The claim that the world is 'safer' is contested by most nonproliferation experts.

statements.text: Nobody wants peace more than me. I would have a deal with Iran done in one week if I were president.
statements.context: During a town hall event, Trump claimed he could rapidly negotiate a new deal with Iran while simultaneously maintaining his stance that maximum pressure was the correct approach.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim of reaching a deal within one week is unsubstantiated and contradicts the complexity of nuclear negotiations, which historically take months or years. No framework for such rapid negotiations was ever presented.

statements.text: Iran understands only strength. When I was president, they didn't dare act up. Now look at the chaos.
statements.context: During a Super Tuesday victory speech, Trump contrasted his administration's approach to Iran with that of the Biden administration, claiming deterrence had collapsed.
statements.factCheckDetail: While some Iranian proxy activity decreased during specific periods, Iran continued enrichment and proxy operations throughout the Trump presidency, including attacks on Saudi oil facilities in 2019. The framing omits significant escalation events like the Soleimani strike aftermath.

statements.text: If Iran does anything to harm Israel, there will be consequences like nobody has ever seen before.
statements.context: In the final weeks of the 2024 presidential campaign, Trump issued a strong warning to Iran following increased tensions in the region around the anniversary of the October 7 attacks.
statements.factCheckDetail: As a candidate at the time, Trump had no authority to enforce this threat. The statement was interpreted as campaign rhetoric rather than a concrete policy commitment.

statements.text: Maximum pressure is back and it's stronger than ever. Iran's economy will collapse if they don't come to the table.
statements.context: In his first major foreign policy address after returning to office, Trump announced the reimposition of broad sanctions on Iran and signaled willingness to negotiate a new deal.
statements.factCheckDetail: While new sanctions were imposed, Iran had already adapted to sanctions through alternative trade networks with China and Russia. Economic analysts noted the impact would be less severe than the first round of maximum pressure due to these workarounds.

statements.text: I told Bibi, you do what you have to do. America stands with Israel, one hundred percent.
statements.context: After reports surfaced of Israeli intelligence indicating accelerated Iranian enrichment, Trump publicly affirmed U.S. support for Israel's security decisions, which critics said amounted to a green light for preemptive strikes.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflected the administration's public posture of unconditional support for Israeli security decisions, as confirmed by multiple White House officials.

statements.text: We don't want war with Iran. Nobody wants war less than me. But if they keep pushing, all options are on the table.
statements.context: Responding to questions about military buildup in the Persian Gulf, Trump attempted to balance his stated desire for peace with warnings about military readiness, a contradiction critics highlighted.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement contradicts concurrent actions including carrier group deployments to the Persian Gulf and expanded military aid to Israel. The phrase 'all options on the table' is traditionally understood as an implicit threat of military force.

statements.text: Iran has been warned. They chose this path. What happens next is on them, not on us.
statements.context: Following the initiation of Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities, Trump framed the escalation as entirely Iran's responsibility, despite criticism that U.S. diplomatic disengagement contributed to the crisis.
statements.factCheckDetail: Attribution of sole responsibility to Iran is disputed. Multiple analysts point to the collapse of diplomatic channels, withdrawal from the JCPOA, and explicit U.S. support for Israeli military action as contributing factors to the escalation.

statements.text: We are working to bring this to a close very quickly. It will be a tremendous outcome, believe me.
statements.context: As the conflict entered its third week with expanding scope, Trump claimed to be personally managing de-escalation efforts, though no concrete diplomatic framework was announced.
statements.factCheckDetail: No evidence of active negotiations or a de-escalation framework was presented. The claim of an imminent resolution contradicted reports from diplomatic sources indicating no back-channel communications were underway.

statements.text: Good reason needed for ground troops, but everything is on the table. We will finish the job.
statements.context: Bloomberg interview suggesting ground invasion possible.

statements.text: Iran has two choices: unconditional surrender or we finish the job. There is no third option.
statements.context: Press conference at White House.

statements.text: We've already won in many ways, but we haven't won enough. The new so-called supreme leader is a lightweight. He's going to have to get approval from us — if he doesn't, he's not going to last long.
statements.context: Phone interview with Times of Israel. Called war a 'short-term excursion' and said ending it would be a 'mutual' decision with Netanyahu. Said he has someone in mind to lead Iran.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim of having 'already won' is premature given Iran continues retaliatory strikes and Hormuz remains closed. The assertion of authority over Iran's leadership selection contradicts international norms of sovereignty.

statements.text: This is a little excursion. It'll be finished pretty quickly. We're doing tremendously well.
statements.context: March 9 press conference. Contradicts Hegseth's signaling of a 4-8 week campaign. Markets remain uncertain about conflict timeline.
statements.factCheckDetail: Characterizing a multi-front war involving 3 carrier groups, 45,000+ deployed troops, 20% of global oil supply disrupted, and 1,300+ confirmed deaths as a 'little excursion' significantly understates the scale and complexity of operations.

statements.text: I think the war is very complete, pretty much. We'll decide together with Israel when it ends. Washington will have the final say.
statements.context: Trump said the war could be over 'pretty quickly' while simultaneously Hegseth warned of the 'most intense day of strikes' yet. Trump said he was 'not happy' with Mojtaba Khamenei and indicated he was open to a Venezuela-style outcome where the regime remains but cooperates.
statements.factCheckDetail: Calling the war 'very complete' contradicts active military operations and Hegseth's announcement of escalated strikes. No ceasefire is in sight and Iran has rejected all mediation attempts.

statements.text: We've destroyed 80 percent of their missile launchers. The war will be over pretty quickly, very soon. But not this week. If they disrupt oil flow any further, we'll increase strikes twentyfold.
statements.context: Trump told Republican Congressional members the war could end 'very soon' while warning Iran against further oil disruption. Called the conflict a 'little excursion.' Oil prices crashed 11-12% on his remarks. Gap widening between US (wanting to wind down) and Israeli (wanting to continue) endgames.
statements.factCheckDetail: 80% of missile launchers claim aligns with CENTCOM's 5,000+ targets struck. However, Iran continues firing projectiles and has rejected all ceasefire overtures. Netanyahu vows 'many surprises' suggesting Israel plans to continue.

statements.text: There's essentially nothing left to target. We've hit 90 percent of their capability. Iran has been set back 50 years.
statements.context: Day 12 comments to reporters. Trump signaled satisfaction with the campaign while Iran deployed Khoramshahr super-heavy missiles — its most powerful conventional weapon — for the first time. 3 more ships attacked at Hormuz. IEA approved record 400M barrel reserve release.
statements.factCheckDetail: While 90% missile reduction aligns with CENTCOM data, Iran demonstrated continued capability by deploying Khoramshahr missiles and attacking 3 more ships. The claim of 'nothing left to target' contradicts ongoing Iranian retaliatory attacks.

statements.text: The war will end soon. There's practically nothing left to target. Anytime I want it to end, it will end.
statements.context: Statement to media. Contradicted by Israeli Defence Minister Katz who said operation has 'no time limit.'
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of 'nothing left to target' contested — Iran retains significant military capability and continues launching missiles. Katz directly contradicted Trump's timeline.

statements.text: The war is going well. We've hit over 6,000 targets. Iran is being set back decades.
statements.context: Day 14 remarks. US claims 6,000+ targets hit, up from 5,000 on Day 13. KC-135 crew all 6 confirmed dead, raising US death toll to 13.
statements.factCheckNote: 6,000+ targets figure reported by NBC. Iran continues retaliatory strikes including cluster missiles that penetrated Israeli defenses.

statements.text: I think he's damaged, but probably alive in some form. The Iranian people should rise up. I'll know when the war is over — I'll feel it in my bones.
statements.context: Trump speaking to Fox News about Mojtaba Khamenei's condition and war timeline. Urged regime change but acknowledged 'very big hurdle.'
statements.factCheckNote: Mojtaba Khamenei's condition unconfirmed. Hegseth separately said 'wounded and likely disfigured.'

statements.text: CENTCOM has 'totally obliterated every MILITARY target' on Kharg Island. Called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' Urged Iranian military to 'lay down their arms, and save what's left of their country.' Warned oil infrastructure is next if Hormuz blockade continues.
statements.context: Truth Social post after Kharg Island bombing raid
statements.factCheckNote: Strike on Kharg Island confirmed. 'Most powerful bombing raid' claim is subjective.

statements.text: We're way ahead of schedule. Iran is totally defeated. They're about to surrender. I've told the G7 — we need China, France, Japan, South Korea, UK to send warships to reopen Hormuz.
statements.context: G7 call and subsequent press remarks; rebuffed ceasefire mediation by Oman and Egypt
statements.factCheckNote: Iran has not signaled surrender. CIA backchannel discussions reported separately suggest ongoing conflict.

statements.text: Iran wants to make a deal, but I'm not ready for one because the terms aren't good enough yet.
statements.context: NBC interview on Day 16 of conflict
statements.factCheckNote: Iran has set specific conditions (guarantees + compensation), not an unconditional desire to 'make a deal' as characterized.

statements.text: The terms are not good enough yet. We may hit Kharg Island a few more times just for fun.
statements.context: Bloomberg interview on Day 16. Trump rejected Iran's ceasefire terms and called on China, France, Japan, South Korea, and the UK to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz to help reopen it.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quotes confirmed by Bloomberg. Call for international naval coalition is a significant escalation in diplomatic pressure.

statements.text: NATO is useless on Hormuz. If they won't help, we'll do it ourselves. Kharg Island seizure is on the table as an economic knockout.
statements.context: FT interview on Day 17. Trump threatened NATO allies over their perceived non-response to Strait of Hormuz disruptions and publicly weighed seizing Iran's Kharg Island, which handles ~90% of Iran's oil exports.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quotes confirmed by NPR and FT reporting. Kharg Island seizure would require major amphibious military operation — unclear if Pentagon has presented such plans.

statements.text: We've gotten some positive response from countries on the Hormuz coalition. But if NATO doesn't help, we may have to rethink what NATO means.
statements.context: Financial Times interview. Also threatened to delay China trip and implied Europe's defense umbrella at risk if warships aren't sent to Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: No country has publicly committed warships. UK, Germany, Australia, Japan have all explicitly declined.

statements.text: Iran's military has been decimated. We've taken out their air force and air defense systems. But I'm not declaring victory yet.
statements.context: Continued mixed messaging — claiming military success while refusing to declare the mission complete.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran continues firing missiles daily. IRGC claims most weapons cache remains intact.

statements.text: I don't think so. But it'll be soon. Won't be long. We're gonna have a much safer world when it's wrapped up. It'll be wrapped up soon.
statements.context: Asked in Oval Office if the war could end this week. Also delayed Xi summit 'a month or so,' said Israel 'would never' use nuclear weapons, and claimed Kharg Island 'literally destroyed' except oil facilities.
statements.factCheckNote: 'Wrapped up soon' contradicts IDF statement of 3+ more weeks of operations through Passover. Kharg Island destruction claim unverified.

statements.text: Israel wouldn't do that. Israel would never do that.
statements.context: Responding to David Sacks warning about Israel potentially using nuclear weapons. Trump said Sacks had not shared that assessment with him.
statements.factCheckNote: Israel maintains a policy of nuclear ambiguity.

statements.text: Scott Bessent is doing a tremendous job. We're opening up the strait for oil. Prices are going to come down very quickly.
statements.context: Commenting on Treasury Secretary Bessent's announcement that US would allow Iranian tankers through Hormuz. Oil dropped 4-6% on the news.
statements.factCheckNote: Oil did drop on the Bessent announcement, but analysts note the strait remains effectively closed to most commercial traffic. Pump price relief likely weeks away.

statements.text: We have totally obliterated their military. Iran is not the same country it was three weeks ago. The war will be wrapped up very soon.
statements.context: Trump claimed Iran's military has been destroyed while delaying his China summit to focus on the war. Pentagon exploring diplomatic off-ramps.
statements.factCheckNote: While significant damage has been inflicted on Iran's military infrastructure, Iran continues to launch missiles and maintain the Hormuz blockade. The claim of total obliteration overstates the situation.

statements.text: Leaving NATO is something to think about. They don't want to help. We do not need the help of anyone. Very bad for the future of NATO.
statements.context: Oval Office remarks alongside Irish PM after NATO allies refused to help secure Strait of Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Trump confirmed remarks to reporters

statements.text: I always thought he was a nice guy. But I always thought he was weak on security, very weak on security.
statements.context: Response to Joe Kent's resignation as NCTC Director
statements.factCheckNote: Trump confirmed response

statements.text: We're knocking them for a loop. They cannot have a nuclear weapon, and they now understand that very strongly. I rebuilt the military in my first term, and I didn't expect to have to use it so much in my second.
statements.context: Evening remarks at the White House alongside Irish PM. Trump praised military success and said Iran now understands it cannot obtain nuclear weapons.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran continues to launch missiles and maintain the Hormuz blockade despite significant damage to its military. The claim of decisive victory overstates the situation.

statements.text: No, I'm not afraid of — I'm really not afraid of anything.
statements.context: When asked if boots on the ground in Iran could become another Vietnam, Trump said he was not afraid. He later added the war would end 'in the very near future' but he wasn't ready to leave yet.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple reporters present. First direct signal Trump is open to ground troops in Iran.

statements.text: We don't know if he's dead or not. A lot of people are saying he's badly disfigured. Nobody's saying he's 100 percent healthy.
statements.context: On Mojtaba Khamenei's status
statements.factCheckNote: No evidence presented for claims about Mojtaba

statements.text: Attended dignified transfer at Dover AFB for 6 service members killed in KC-135 crash. Accompanied by Hegseth, Speaker Johnson, and military families.
statements.context: Dover Air Force Base dignified transfer ceremony
statements.factCheckNote: AP confirmed

statements.text: Israel will NOT again strike the South Pars gas field. But if Iran attacks Qatar again, the United States will massively blow up the entirety of the field.
statements.context: Truth Social post. Axios reports Trump DID coordinate original strike despite denying it.
statements.factCheckNote: Axios: Trump coordinated strike with Netanyahu despite claiming US 'knew nothing'

statements.text: The United States knew nothing about this particular attack on the South Pars Gas Field. If Iran attacks Qatar again, we will massively blow up the entirety of the South Pars Gas Field.
statements.context: Truth Social post after Israel struck South Pars without notifying US, per Trump. CNN sources say coordination occurred.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN and Jerusalem Post report Israel coordinated with US on the South Pars strike

statements.text: The $200 billion is a small price to pay to make sure we stay tippy top. I thought oil prices would be worse, much worse actually. We are not putting troops anywhere. It will be over pretty soon.
statements.context: Afternoon press conference remarks. Downplaying economic pain while requesting largest war supplemental since Iraq/Afghanistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Gas prices up 20%+, oil hit $119 intraday. 'Not putting troops anywhere' contradicts 2,200 Marines deployment. No timeline given for 'pretty soon.'

statements.text: Israel 'out of anger for what has taken place in the Middle East, has violently lashed out' at South Pars Gas Field. US 'knew nothing about this particular attack.'
statements.context: Truth Social post
statements.factCheckNote: Sources contradicted claim US didn't know in advance

statements.text: Threatened to 'massively blow up the entirety' of South Pars if Iran attacks Qatar again. Said NO US ground troops. Invoked Pearl Harbor during Japan PM meeting.
statements.context: White House remarks with Japan PM Takaichi
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast remarks

statements.text: When asked by a Japanese reporter why allies weren't warned about the Iran strikes, Trump quipped: 'Who knows better about surprise than Japan? Why didn't you tell me about Pearl Harbor?' He added the surprise element meant the US 'knocked out 50% of what we anticipated' in the first two days.
statements.context: Oval Office meeting with Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi; defending decision to launch strikes without notifying allies
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed the remark drew gasps. CNN, CNBC, ABC News all confirmed.

statements.text: On US ground troops in Iran: 'No. I'm not putting troops anywhere.' Also stated: 'We're asking for a lot of reasons, beyond even what we're talking about in Iran, when this is a very volatile world,' regarding the $200B Pentagon request.
statements.context: Press availability on Day 21; ruling out ground invasion
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Newsweek and Fox News live updates.

statements.text: I told Netanyahu to hold off on hitting Iran energy sites. The United States knew nothing about this particular attack [on South Pars]. But if Iran keeps attacking Qatar, we will 'massively blow up the entirety of the South Pars Gas Field.'
statements.context: Truth Social post and public statements on the Israel-Iran South Pars dispute; Nowruz Day 21
statements.factCheckNote: Both Trump and Netanyahu confirmed the conversation

statements.text: NATO allies: COWARDS! Now that fight is Militarily WON, with very little danger for them, they complain about the high oil prices they are forced to pay, but don't want to help open the Strait of Hormuz, a simple military maneuver that is the single reason for the high oil prices. So easy for them to do, with so little risk. COWARDS, and we will REMEMBER! Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!
statements.context: Truth Social post on Day 21 (Nowruz/Eid al-Fitr) attacking NATO allies for refusing to help open the Strait of Hormuz despite six nations offering limited post-ceasefire assistance. Sharpest attack on NATO during the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Truth Social post confirmed by Bloomberg, CNN, Al Jazeera

statements.text: We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East.
statements.context: Truth Social post outlining five core war goals — destroying Iran's defense industrial base, missile capability, nuclear program, oil infrastructure, and leadership chain. Signal of potential operational end.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump claims objectives are met and war is 'militarily won' — but Hormuz remains effectively closed, Iran continues missile attacks on Israel, and Hegseth's own data shows uneven strike frequency.

statements.text: Now that fight is Militarily WON, with very little danger for them, they complain about the high oil prices they are forced to pay, but don't want to help open the Strait of Hormuz... COWARDS, and we will REMEMBER!
statements.context: Trump blasted NATO allies for refusing to commit forces to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Wrote: 'Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!'
statements.factCheckNote: Trump's 'militarily won' claim is disputed. Kallas confirmed EU states 'do not have an appetite' for military involvement; six allies issued a joint statement expressing readiness to 'help' without committing forces.

statements.text: Their leaders are all gone. The next set of leaders are all gone. And the next set of leaders are mostly gone... We want to talk to them and there's nobody to talk to.
statements.context: Speech before Naval Academy midshipmen at Annapolis. Trump said Iran's leadership had been so decimated there was no one left to negotiate with.
statements.factCheckNote: Many senior Iranian officials have been killed, but Mojtaba Khamenei released a Nowruz message on March 20 and Pezeshkian released statements the same day, indicating functional leadership still exists.

statements.text: We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East with respect to the Terrorist Regime of Iran. I think we've won.
statements.context: Trump posted on Truth Social that the US was 'getting very close to meeting objectives' and considering winding down operations. On the South Lawn, he told reporters 'I think we've won' and 'I don't want to do a cease-fire. You don't do a cease-fire when you're literally obliterating the other side.' Contradicted by simultaneous deployment of 2,500 additional Marines and Pentagon ground force planning.
statements.venue: Truth Social + South Lawn press gaggle
statements.factCheckNote: Contradicted by simultaneous deployment of 2,200–2,500 Marines from the 11th MEU aboard USS Boxer. Iran dismissed as 'psychological operations to control the markets.' IDF stated 3+ more weeks of operations needed.

statements.text: Nobody left to talk to [in Tehran]. There is nobody to call. As far as I am concerned, all the leaders of Iran are gone.
statements.context: Trump told reporters outside the White House that Iran's leadership has been eliminated and there is no one left for the US to negotiate with. He simultaneously denied sending ground troops to Iran: "If I were [sending troops], I certainly wouldn't tell you, but I'm not putting troops."
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's government continues to function. President Pezeshkian delivered a live Nowruz address on March 21. FM Araghchi appeared on NBC the same morning. Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued statements March 20. Senior military officials continue to brief press.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz must be protected by other nations who use it. NATO is showing its COWARDICE by not supporting action to free up the world's most important shipping lane.
statements.context: Posted on Truth Social after UK authorized Diego Garcia for US Hormuz operations. Trump expressed frustration that NATO allies have not contributed militarily to re-opening the Strait of Hormuz, calling them cowards. Also said he would not put ground troops into the conflict.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 22 liveblog. 'Other nations have to protect Hormuz from Iran' per liveblog headline.

statements.text: If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST!
statements.context: Truth Social post at 7:44 PM ET on March 21. The 48-hour deadline expires ~7:44 PM ET Monday March 23. Earlier the same day Trump had said he was 'considering winding down' military operations — this ultimatum reversed that signal. Trump also stated: 'You don't do a ceasefire when you're literally obliterating the other side.'
statements.factCheckNote: Post confirmed by Fox News, Bloomberg, CNN, NBC. The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed since Day 3 (March 3). Iran has not agreed to open it.

statements.text: Why should we have a ceasefire when we are defeating Iran? Their leadership is gone, their navy and air force are dead, they have absolutely no defense. We are weeks ahead of schedule.
statements.context: Day 23 remarks rejecting ceasefire calls, shortly after issuing the 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran retains ballistic missile capacity (demonstrated by Arad/Dimona strikes Day 22). Iran's navy largely destroyed per CENTCOM — but claim of "no defense" disputed by Iranian missile activity.

statements.text: We are weeks ahead of schedule on Iran. Doing a great job. No deals. Iran must open the Strait of Hormuz, and open it now. The United States will not be stopped.
statements.context: Trump press remarks Day 23, claiming progress in the war effort.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN data analysis found pace of operations has 'ebbed and flowed' rather than consistently accelerating as claimed. Strike frequency claims previously overstated by Hegseth.

statements.text: If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST!
statements.context: 48-hour ultimatum to Iran issued on Truth Social at 7:44 PM ET. Iran responded with full defiance, threatening to 'completely close' Hormuz and attack Gulf energy infrastructure if power plants are struck.
statements.factCheckNote: Ultimatum expires Monday March 23 ~7:44 PM ET. Iran has shown zero compliance signal. Multiple allies (UK PM Starmer, Germany's Merz) have urged restraint.

statements.text: I have met my own goals for this war… and weeks ahead of schedule! Their leadership is gone, their navy and air force are dead, they have absolutely no defense, and they want to make a deal.
statements.context: Claimed on Truth Social that US war objectives against Iran have been met ahead of schedule, contradicted by ongoing missile attacks on Israel and Pentagon's $200B supplemental request to Congress. Iran fired 8 missile salvos at Israel the same day.
statements.factCheckNote: Contradicted by Iran's continuing 8-salvo missile campaign on Israel, Hegseth stating 'no timeframe' for war, and Pentagon's $200B supplemental request. Intel community assessment leaked to WaPo says large-scale war 'unlikely' to oust Iran's regime.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Trump changing messages analysis

statements.text: We can talk, but I don't want a ceasefire. Iran's military has been dismantled. Halting operations now would be premature.
statements.context: Response to Pope Leo XIV's Sunday Angelus ceasefire appeal. Issued via White House spokesperson.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by National Catholic Register and Zenit news agency reporting on Vatican-US exchange.

statements.text: I am leaning toward traveling to Israel in April to receive the Israel Prize at their Independence Day ceremony. We will see.
statements.context: Informal remarks to reporters. Independence Day ceremony is April 22. Trip would be Trump's first to Israel since war began.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Times of Israel liveblog March 22. Described as informal remark; no formal announcement made.

statements.text: Iran defied my 48-hour ultimatum. Their power plants are next. I don't want a ceasefire — we're crushing them. Their military is dismantled. We're weeks ahead of schedule.
statements.context: Day 24 morning statement reaffirming Hormuz stance and rejecting ceasefire appeals. Deadline expires tonight at approximately 7:44 PM ET.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of being "weeks ahead of schedule" disputed by CNN analysis; Iran continues launching 8+ missile salvos/day with 150-180 Israelis wounded on Day 23. "Military dismantled" claim contradicted by ongoing strikes.

statements.text: I AM PLEASED TO REPORT THAT THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, AND THE COUNTRY OF IRAN, HAVE HAD, OVER THE LAST TWO DAYS, VERY GOOD AND PRODUCTIVE CONVERSATIONS REGARDING A COMPLETE AND TOTAL RESOLUTION OF OUR HOSTILITIES IN THE MIDDLE EAST. Based on the tenor and tone of these in depth, detailed and constructive conversations, which will continue throughout the week, I have instructed the Department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period, subject to the success of the ongoing meetings and discussions.
statements.context: Truth Social post before the 48-hour Hormuz deadline expired. Trump also told CNBC: 'this is regime change,' saying many Iranian leaders have been killed and replaced. Iran's government immediately denied any negotiations had occurred.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's FM Araghchi categorically denied any negotiations: 'No direct or indirect contact' with the US. Fars News confirmed Iranian denial. Trump's claim of 'productive conversations' contradicted by Iran's foreign ministry.
statements.source: NPR — Iran defiant as Trump delays striking power plants

statements.text: We're giving it a five-day window and we'll see how it goes. If it goes well, we'll wrap it up. Otherwise, we'll keep bombing. Iran agreed to no nukes, no enrichment — they'll also be surrendering their enriched uranium stockpile. Israel will be very happy. The Strait of Hormuz will be managed by mutual agreement of both sides. We've been speaking to Iran's most respected leader.
statements.context: Trump revealed claimed deal terms to reporters at West Palm Beach airport after announcing 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes.
statements.venue: West Palm Beach International Airport, FL
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Foreign Ministry flatly denied any negotiations or agreements. Trump's claims of 'no nukes, no enrichment' deal cannot be independently confirmed. Iran state media called the claims disinformation designed to lower oil prices.
statements.source: Times of Israel / Axios / Washington Post

statements.text: I am pleased to report that the United States of America, and the country of Iran, have had, over the last two days, very good and productive conversations regarding a complete and total resolution of our hostilities in the Middle East. I have instructed the Department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period.
statements.context: Trump Truth Social post announcing 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes. Came hours before his own 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum was set to expire. Iran immediately denied any talks occurred.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump claims "productive conversations" with Iran — Iran categorically denied any negotiations. The US has not identified the Iranian interlocutor. Third-party intermediaries (Egypt, Pakistan, Turkey) are believed to be relaying messages.

statements.text: They want to make a deal. They have agreed they will not have nuclear weapons. They agreed they will not have enriched uranium. They have to give up their enriched uranium. Hormuz will be jointly managed. At the end of this period I think it could very well end up being a very good deal.
statements.context: Trump press conference remarks outlining claimed Iran deal terms. Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff named as US envoys in talks. Iran denied all these claimed terms and any negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran denied agreeing to any of the stated terms. No independent confirmation of any Iranian nuclear concession. Deal terms remain unverified.

statements.text: We are very intent on making a deal. They will never have a nuclear weapon — that is number one, two and three. They must reopen Hormuz and surrender their enriched uranium stockpiles. We have been having very good and productive conversations.
statements.context: Statement to reporters and on Truth Social regarding US-Iran negotiations. Trump confirmed Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff were leading talks. Announced a 5-day pause on strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure pending outcome of talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran publicly denied any negotiations. Third-party intermediaries (Egypt, Pakistan, Turkey) believed to be relaying messages. Iran International separately reported Araghchi privately signaled readiness.

statements.text: Pete, I think you were the first one to speak up and you said let's do it because you can't let them have a nuclear weapon. We're in negotiations right now. And the other side, I can tell you they'd like to make a deal.
statements.context: Trump credited Hegseth as the primary advocate for the Iran war, deflecting responsibility as the conflict grows politically unpopular. Trump also referenced a mystery Iran "present" worth a "tremendous amount of money" that was "oil and gas related."
statements.factCheckNote: Iran publicly denied any negotiations. The "deal" terms Trump described (no enrichment, surrender uranium) were not confirmed by Iran.

statements.text: WE HAVE WON THIS WAR. Iran has agreed to NEVER acquire nuclear weapons. The Strait of Hormuz is going to open. We have received a very significant prize from Iran. Very good and productive conversations are happening right now — between Kushner, Witkoff, Rubio, Vance, and Iranian officials.
statements.context: Truth Social post (all-caps as originally posted); later elaborated at Tennessee roundtable
statements.factCheckNote: Iran publicly denied direct negotiations. Indirect contacts confirmed via Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt. "Significant prize" unspecified. Iran International reported Araghchi privately signaled deal readiness via back-channel.

statements.text: I am pausing strikes on Iranian power plants for 5 days to give diplomacy a chance. Very significant things are happening. The Iranians are showing great flexibility. We are being very tough, but fair.
statements.context: Announcement of 5-day pause on power plant strikes — pause expires approximately March 28
statements.factCheckNote: Strike pause confirmed by Pentagon briefings. Iranian response to the deadline remains unclear as of this report.

statements.text: We've won this, because this war has been won. They agreed they will never have a nuclear weapon. Iran gave us a big present related to the Strait of Hormuz, worth a tremendous amount of money.
statements.context: Trump declared victory from the Oval Office, claiming productive negotiations with Iran. Iran publicly denied any talks occurred.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's IRGC and parliament speaker called the claims 'fake news.' Indirect contacts via Pakistan confirmed. No direct Iran confirmation of any deal.

statements.text: Pete, I think you were the first one to speak up, and you said 'Let's do it.'
statements.context: Trump appeared to shift blame to Defense Secretary Hegseth for pushing for the war, publicly naming him as advocate. Trump's approval hit record low 36%.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by multiple outlets. Context of blame-shifting noted by analysts.

statements.text: We're in negotiations right now. Iran will never have a nuclear weapon. They agreed to that.
statements.context: Trump claims active negotiations with Iran via Pakistan and that Iran agreed to nuclear concessions — Iran publicly denies all direct talks. US delivered 15-point ceasefire plan. NPR analysis documents shifting war objectives from 'unconditional surrender' to 'war is won.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran has not confirmed agreeing to nuclear concessions. 15-point plan was delivered but Iran publicly denied any direct negotiations. Military spokesman mocked US as 'negotiating with yourselves.'

statements.text: We've won this. This war has been won. Tehran wants to make a deal badly, and a deal could happen within five days. We backed off striking power plants based on the fact we're negotiating.
statements.context: Trump told Fox Business that the war is effectively won and that Iran is seeking a deal. He confirmed Vance, Rubio, Witkoff, and Kushner are all involved in negotiation efforts. Iran fired Wave 80 missile salvos at Tel Aviv metro area hours later. Iran publicly rejected all negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran launched IRGC Wave 80 missile salvos at Tel Aviv metro hours after Trump declared war 'won.' Iran formally rejected US 15-point peace plan with 5 counter-conditions, stating 'no negotiations until our conditions are met.'

statements.text: WRONG!!! They better get serious soon, before it is too late, because once that happens, there is NO TURNING BACK, and it won't be pretty!
statements.context: Truth Social post responding to Iran's rejection of the US 15-point ceasefire plan and Araghchi's statement that Iran was 'only looking at' the proposal.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump posted this on Truth Social on March 26 in direct response to news that Iran had formally rejected or was dismissing the US 15-point plan.
statements.source: NBC News — Iran war live updates

statements.text: They want to make a deal so badly, but they're afraid to say it.
statements.context: Remarks to congressional Republicans during Wednesday evening briefing on the state of Iran war negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's FM Araghchi has publicly stated the opposite — 'we do not want a ceasefire' and 'we do not plan on any negotiations.' The statement may reflect US perception of Iranian backchannels rather than public positions.

statements.text: They're begging to make a deal, not me. We'll just keep blowing them away unimpeded. Iran sent us 8 big boats of oil through the Strait — that's a present. We have other targets we want to hit before we leave.
statements.context: Cabinet meeting, White House. Trump claimed Iran is 'begging' for a deal while simultaneously saying the US would continue strikes if Iran does not permanently abandon nuclear ambitions. He revealed Iran allowed 8-10 tankers through Hormuz as a goodwill gesture. WSJ reported he told aides to 'wrap up the Iran conflict in coming weeks.'
statements.factCheckNote: Trump's 'begging' characterization is disputed. Iranian FM Araghchi explicitly stated Iran is NOT seeking a ceasefire or negotiations. The 8-10 tanker claim was not independently confirmed but IRGC selective blockade policy allowing some 'non-hostile' ships was confirmed by Al Jazeera.

statements.text: As per Iranian Government request, please let this statement serve to represent that I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 P.M., Eastern Time. Talks are ongoing and going very well.
statements.context: Truth Social post extending the pause on striking Iranian energy infrastructure by 10 more days. The announcement came after Iran denied ongoing negotiations, causing Brent crude to surge past $109/bbl and the S&P 500 to fall 1.74% — the worst single-day drop of the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Post confirmed by NPR, Bloomberg, NBC News. Extension is the second since the original energy-strike pause was announced.

statements.text: As per Iranian Government request, please let this statement serve to represent that I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 P.M., Eastern Time. Talks are ongoing and, despite erroneous statements to the contrary by the Fake News Media, and others, they are going very well.
statements.context: Truth Social post extending energy infrastructure strike pause by 10 days to April 6 at 8 PM ET.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump said the extension was at Iran's request; Iran denied formally requesting it. Talks confirmed as indirect via Pakistan, but Iran says they are only message exchanges, not negotiations.
statements.source: NPR — Trump grants Iran another extension on Hormuz deadline

statements.text: Iran is begging to make a deal, not me. I'm the opposite of desperate. I don't care.
statements.context: Said at Cabinet meeting at the White House when asked about desperation for a deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran FM Araghchi explicitly denied Iran is seeking a deal: said US is the one that changed its position. Independent analysts note Iran rejected all 15 US conditions.
statements.source: CNBC — President Trump: Iran is begging to make a deal

statements.text: Speaking at the FII PRIORITY Summit in Miami Beach, I am warning Iran: deal now, before it is too late. To NATO allies who will not send warships to Hormuz — why are we protecting them? Taking Iran’s oil is also an option. Our talks with Iran are going very well.
statements.context: Trump speech at Future Investment Initiative PRIORITY Summit in Miami Beach, Florida
statements.factCheckNote: Trump claim of Iran talks 'going very well' contradicted by Iranian FM Araghchi who stated 'no negotiations have happened with the enemy until now' on March 27. NATO warship threat unprecedented. 'Taking Iran oil' option not elaborated upon.

statements.text: NATO is a paper tiger. I've always said NATO is a paper tiger. And I always said, we help NATO, but they'll never help us. They didn't want to join the fight to stop a Nuclear Powered Iran. Now that fight is Militarily WON...they complain about high oil prices they are forced to pay, but don't want to help open the Strait of Hormuz. The US has thousands of targets left to strike.
statements.context: Truth Social post and public remarks criticizing NATO allies for failing to contribute warships to Hormuz operations
statements.factCheckNote: Trump's Truth Social posts confirmed. NATO has not pledged warships to Hormuz operations. Statement that war is 'militarily won' is disputed by US intel officials.
statements.source: The Hill — Trump calls NATO a 'paper tiger'

statements.text: The war is not finished yet. We still have 3,554 targets left to hit in Iran. But talks are ongoing and they are going very well. I've extended the deadline on Iranian power plants to April 6 to give diplomacy a chance. Iran is begging to make a deal but afraid to say it for fear of being killed.
statements.context: Remarks at the FII Summit in Miami and on Truth Social, announcing a 10-day extension of the deadline on Iranian energy plant strikes while citing ongoing peace talks through Pakistan
statements.factCheckNote: Trump's claim that Iran is 'begging' for a deal cannot be independently confirmed. Iranian officials publicly reject negotiations. The 3,554 targets figure is an unverified claim. April 6 deadline extension confirmed.
statements.source: Al Jazeera liveblog — Iran war March 28

statements.text: They gave us most of the points. I do see a deal in Iran, yeah. Could be soon. Maybe we take Kharg Island, maybe we don't. We have a lot of options.
statements.context: Aboard Air Force One, speaking to reporters about status of US-Iran negotiations through Pakistan, while simultaneously threatening seizure of Iran's main oil export hub Kharg Island (handles ~90% of Iranian crude exports)
statements.venue: Air Force One / Financial Times interview
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's FM Araghchi directly contradicted Trump on same day, calling US demands 'unreasonable.' Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf also rejected any deal. Trump's claim that Iran agreed to 'most of' 15 points is disputed by Iranian officials.
statements.source: CNN — Day 30 Iran war live updates

statements.text: We've had regime change, if you look already, because the one regime was decimated, destroyed, they're all dead. The next regime is mostly dead, and the third regime, we're dealing with different people than anybody's dealt with before. It's a whole different group of people.
statements.context: Financial Times interview published March 30, 2026. Trump also posted on Truth Social that the US is 'in serious discussions with A NEW, AND MORE REASONABLE, REGIME to end our Military Operations in Iran.' Experts at INSS Tel Aviv and other think tanks disputed the claim.
statements.factCheckNote: The Islamic Republic has not fallen. Mojtaba Khamenei continues to issue statements as Supreme Leader. The IRGC is intact. Experts say Trump is conflating leadership decapitation with structural regime change — a 'new regime' in the sense of new faces, not a different political system.
statements.source: Time Magazine — Trump Seems to Embrace 'Regime Change' in Iran

statements.text: To be honest with you, my favorite thing is to take the oil in Iran but some stupid people back in the U.S. say: 'Why are you doing that?' But they're stupid people. I told Scott to cut off all dealings with Spain. Spain has been terrible.
statements.context: Same FT interview in which Trump threatened to seize Kharg Island (Iran's main oil terminal, ~90% of exports). Separately threatened trade war with Spain after Spain formally closed its airspace to US military planes in the Iran campaign — the first NATO ally to do so.
statements.venue: Financial Times interview / Air Force One
statements.factCheckNote: Both statements confirmed by FT, Bloomberg, NBC News, and Times of Israel.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Trump wants to invade Iran to seize oil, calls US objectors 'stupid people'

statements.text: Iran must immediately open the Strait of Hormuz. If they do not, the United States will be forced to completely obliterate all of Iran's electric generating plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and quite possibly, desalination plants. Do it NOW! They have until April 6th. Iran knows what they have to do. I am pretty sure we are going to have a deal.
statements.context: Trump's Truth Social post followed by interview statements on March 30. Sets hard April 6 deadline for full Hormuz reopening. Simultaneously claims to be 'pretty sure' of a deal — classic Trump simultaneous maximum pressure/olive-branch messaging.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran has not publicly agreed to reopen Hormuz. Trump's simultaneous claims of 'regime change achieved' and 'deal coming' while threatening to obliterate civilian infrastructure are internally contradictory. April 6 is stated deadline but credibility depends on follow-through.
statements.source: CNBC — Trump threatens to obliterate Kharg Island if no Hormuz deal

statements.text: [Per WSJ] Trump told aides he is willing to end the military campaign even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed. He assessed that reopening Hormuz would push the conflict beyond his 4-6 week timeline. Would leave the complex reopening operation for a later date.
statements.context: WSJ exclusive report on March 31. Represents significant strategic shift from April 6 ultimatum and threats to 'obliterate' Iran unless Hormuz reopened. Contradicts previous demands for immediate reopening. Brent crude dipped ~1% on the report.
statements.venue: Private remarks to aides (reported by WSJ)
statements.factCheckNote: WSJ exclusive confirmed by Haaretz and US News. Directly contradicts Trump's own April 6 deadline set the previous day. PolitiFact documented 12 times Trump signaled war was about to end.
statements.source: Haaretz — Trump tells aides willing to end without reopening Hormuz

statements.text: I will blow up and completely obliterate Iran's electric plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and desalination plants if a deal is not reached shortly.
statements.context: Escalating threats against Iran's energy infrastructure amid stalled ceasefire talks
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, WaPo, CBS News.

statements.text: They gave us most of the points. Why wouldn't they? We've had regime change if you look already because the one regime was decimated, destroyed, they're all dead.
statements.context: Claiming Iran agreed to most of US 15-point demands and that regime change already achieved through leadership decapitation
statements.factCheckNote: Iran denies agreeing to demands. 'Regime change' refers to killing leaders, not government change — IRGC hardliners empowered per NYT.

statements.text: The war is coming to an end. Iran's military has been decimated and its current leadership is much more reasonable and less radicalized.
statements.context: NBC News interview, March 31 afternoon
statements.factCheckNote: Iran continues to launch missiles and drones. IRGC still operational despite heavy losses. 'Coming to an end' contradicts Hegseth's 'decisive days ahead' phrasing.

statements.text: Go get your own oil! Build up some delayed courage, go to the Strait, and just TAKE IT!
statements.context: Truth Social posts targeting UK and allies; separately called France 'VERY UNHELPFUL' for refusing military overflights
statements.factCheckNote: Allies like UK and France have sent naval forces but disagree on scope of operations. France clarified it restricted only strike-involved aircraft, not all US military.

statements.text: We'll leave when we feel that they are for a long period of time put into the stone ages. Whether we have a deal or not, it's irrelevant. Two or three weeks.
statements.context: Told reporters US forces will leave Iran in 2-3 weeks. Said Iran doesn't need to make a deal. Will address the nation Wednesday April 2 at 9 PM ET with 'an important update on Iran.'
statements.venue: Remarks to reporters / White House announcement
statements.factCheckNote: NPR, CBS, Axios confirmed. Address announced by press secretary Leavitt. Timeline contradicts Hegseth's 'decisive days' framing.

statements.text: Iran doesn't have to make a deal, no. We'll be leaving very soon. All I have to do is leave Iran, and gas prices will come tumbling down. Build up some courage, go to the Strait, and just TAKE IT.
statements.context: Reiterated 2-3 week exit timeline. Told NATO allies to take the Strait of Hormuz. Hit France for being 'VERY UNHELPFUL.' WSJ reports willing to end war without reopening Hormuz.
statements.venue: White House remarks to reporters / Truth Social
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, NPR, WaPo confirmed. WSJ reported willingness to leave without Hormuz resolution.

statements.text: Iran's New Regime President has asked the United States for a Ceasefire. We will only consider it once the Strait of Hormuz is open and clear. I am absolutely considering leaving NATO. NATO is a paper tiger.
statements.context: Confirmed primetime address at 9 PM ET. Told Reuters he is 'absolutely' considering pulling US out of NATO over allies' refusal to join Iran war. Iran denied requesting ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: NPR, NBC, CNN confirmed address and NATO threat. Iran FM called ceasefire claim 'false and baseless.'

statements.text: We are gonna finish the job. We are getting very close. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages, where they belong. Core strategic objectives are nearing completion — 2-3 more weeks of active combat. Iran's navy absolutely destroyed, air force in ruins, missile capability dramatically curtailed. Regime change was not our goal, but regime change has occurred. If no deal, we will hit each and every one of their electric generating plants simultaneously. Countries receiving oil through the strait must take care of that passage — build up some delayed courage. The increase in gas prices was entirely the result of the Iranian regime.
statements.context: Primetime address to the nation (~19 min). Key topics: war 'nearing completion,' regime change claim, threatened Iran power grid, NATO pullout threat (told Telegraph 'beyond reconsideration'), shifted Hormuz to allies, blamed Iran for gas prices, nuclear justification. Did NOT mention negotiations, ceasefire, or 13 US service members killed. Market reaction: Brent +4%, Dow -260 pts, S&P -0.7%, Nikkei -1.4%, Kospi -2.82%. Public opinion: 60% disapprove of war, 67% oppose higher gas prices for it.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran denied requesting ceasefire. PolitiFact rated 'regime change' claim Mostly False. Nuclear claims disputed by arms control experts. FactCheck.org found multiple false/misleading claims about economy, Iran missile threat to US homeland, and oil production. Analysts concluded speech contained no new substance.
statements.source: NPR / CNBC / Al Jazeera / CBS / Time / WaPo / PBS / White House

statements.text: Our military hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants! The Iranian leadership knows exactly what they need to do.
statements.context: Truth Social post on Day 35, escalating infrastructure threats ahead of April 6 deadline
statements.factCheckNote: Power grid strikes were previously postponed to April 6 amid diplomatic talks. This post signals they remain on the table.

statements.text: Israel will do what I tell them. They will stop attacking Iran when I stop.
statements.context: Statement asserting full US control over Israeli military operations in Iran, made on Day 35
statements.factCheckNote: CNBC and Times of Israel confirmed Trump's statements.

statements.text: The US hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants! The New Regime leadership knows what has to be done, and has to be done, FAST!
statements.context: Truth Social post following US strike on B1 Bridge in Tehran-Karaj
statements.factCheckNote: Claim that US 'hasn't even started' is misleading — Day 35 of continuous strikes. Bridge strikes confirmed by NPR, Fox News.

statements.text: With a little more time, we can easily OPEN THE HORMUZ STRAIT, TAKE THE OIL, & MAKE A FORTUNE. IT WOULD BE A 'GUSHER' FOR THE WORLD???
statements.context: Truth Social post amid continuing Hormuz blockade and $109+ Brent prices
statements.factCheckNote: Hormuz Day 32 of blockade with no sign of reopening. 'Taking the oil' would violate international law. CNBC confirmed.

statements.text: Time is running out — 48 hours before all Hell will reign down on them. MAKE A DEAL or OPEN UP THE HORMUZ STRAIT!
statements.context: Truth Social post reiterating April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz or face strikes on energy infrastructure. 10-day energy strike pause expires April 6, 8 PM ET.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump simultaneously claims talks are 'going very well' while threatening 'all hell.' Previous April 1 address contained 5 false claims per FactCheck.org.

statements.text: Talks are going very well. We'll see what happens. But they have to open up that strait.
statements.context: Remarks to reporters contradicting the 'all hell' ultimatum. Claims progress in indirect negotiations via Pakistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran FM Araghchi says 'trust level is at zero' and negotiations are not happening. Iran rejected US ceasefire proposal.

statements.text: One of the most daring Search and Rescue Operations in U.S. History. Our brave airman was rescued from behind enemy lines in Iran after evading capture for over 24 hours. The CIA ran a brilliant deception campaign. GOD BLESS OUR TROOPS!
statements.context: Trump announced the successful rescue of the missing F-15E WSO via commando raid inside Iran near Isfahan. US special forces set up a makeshift airbase inside Iran, then destroyed 2 C-130s to prevent capture. CIA spread false information that the airman had already been found, buying time for the actual rescue.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, Axios, NBC News, NPR, Al Jazeera all confirmed the rescue operation. Iran also confirmed 5 killed during the rescue firefight.

statements.text: Remember when I gave Iran ten days to MAKE A DEAL or OPEN UP THE HORMUZ STRAIT. Time is running out - 48 hours before all Hell will reign down on them. Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran.
statements.context: Trump reiterated his 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning that Tuesday (April 7) would see strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges if the deadline passes without a deal. The deadline expires Monday April 6 at 8 PM ET.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Fox News, Al Jazeera confirmed the threat. Deadline aligned with his March 26 10-day extension.

statements.text: Open the F***in' Strait, you crazy bastards, or you'll be living in Hell - JUST WATCH! We sent guns to the protesters, a lot of them... We sent them through the Kurds. There's a good chance of a deal by Monday.
statements.context: Easter Sunday profanity-laden Truth Social rant followed by Fox News interview with Trey Yingst. Trump revealed US covertly armed Iranian protesters via Kurdish channels and claimed Iran killed ~45,000 demonstrators (widely disputed). Set explicit April 7 8PM ET deadline.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Axios, The Hill. The 45,000 protester death claim is widely disputed — HRANA documents ~7,000; Iran government says ~3,100. Arming-via-Kurds claim is new revelation, not independently verified.

statements.text: We sent guns to the protesters, a lot of them... We sent them through the Kurds. If they don't make a deal and fast, I'm considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil.
statements.context: Fox News interview with Trey Yingst on Easter — revealed US armed Iranian protesters through Kurdish channels; also threatened to 'take over the oil' if no deal
statements.factCheckNote: PDKI denied receiving any weapons. Trump also claimed 45,000 protesters killed by Iranian security — Iranian authorities reported ~3,100.

statements.text: Tuesday, 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time! Open the F***in' Strait, you crazy bastards, or you'll be living in Hell — JUST WATCH! I am blowing up everything over there.
statements.context: Truth Social post extending Hormuz deadline to Tuesday 8PM ET — third extension. Profanity-laden Easter weekend threats.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump posted exact text. Deadline extended from original April 2, then April 4, now April 7.

statements.text: Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran. Open the F***in' Strait, you crazy bastards, or you'll be living in Hell - JUST WATCH!
statements.context: Easter Sunday profane social media post threatening to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges if Hormuz not reopened by Tuesday 8PM ET
statements.factCheckNote: Published on Truth Social. NPR, Time, Washington Post, The Hill confirmed. Iran's UN Mission called it 'incitement to war crimes.'

statements.text: The entire country can be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night.
statements.context: White House press conference. Extended Hormuz deadline to Tuesday April 7 at 8pm ET. Threatened "Power Plant Day and Bridge Day" against civilian infrastructure. Called Iran's 10-point counterproposal "significant" but "not good enough."
statements.factCheckNote: While US has capability for massive strikes, "taking out" an entire country of 88 million people in one night is hyperbolic. Experts note logistics of destroying all power plants and bridges would require sustained campaign.

statements.text: A whole civilization will die tonight. Every bridge in Iran will be decimated by 12 o'clock tomorrow night and every power plant in Iran will be out of business, burning, exploding and never to be used again. The entire country can be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night.
statements.context: White House press conference ahead of 8PM ET Hormuz deadline. Also said he is 'not at all' concerned about committing possible war crimes.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, Al Jazeera, NPR, Washington Post confirmed direct quotes.

statements.text: Iran put forward a 10-point plan. Significant, but not good enough. A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again.
statements.context: White House remarks ahead of 8pm ET deadline. Also claimed 'numerous intercepts' showing Iranians saying 'Please keep bombing. Do it.'
statements.factCheckNote: 10-point plan confirmed by Al Jazeera and Gulf News. 'Intercepts' claim unverified.

statements.text: I have agreed to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks, subject to Iran agreeing to the COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING of the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Posted on Truth Social after 8PM ET deadline passed. Pakistan PM Sharif mediated. Bloomberg: 'two-week ceasefire to finalize talks.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by US News, CNBC, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, NBC News.

statements.text: A big day for World Peace! Just like we are experiencing in the US, this could be the Golden Age of the Middle East! Total and complete victory. 100 percent. Iran can begin reconstruction.
statements.context: Morning-after statement following ceasefire announcement. Claiming complete victory.
statements.factCheckNote: Both sides claiming victory. Ceasefire is 2-week pause, no demands met yet.

statements.text: The United States will work closely with Iran, which we have determined has gone through what will be a very productive Regime Change!
statements.context: Truth Social post after ceasefire announcement
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's government has not changed. Pezeshkian and military leadership remain in place. No evidence of regime change.

statements.text: There will be no enrichment of Uranium, and the United States will, working with Iran, dig up and remove all of the deeply buried Nuclear 'Dust.'
statements.context: Truth Social post demanding nuclear concessions
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's version of ceasefire terms claims they retain uranium enrichment rights. Trump called Iran's Farsi version 'fraudulent.'

statements.text: A Country supplying Military Weapons to Iran will be immediately tariffed, on any and all goods sold to the United States of America, 50%, effective immediately.
statements.context: Truth Social threat targeting Iran arms suppliers
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote from Truth Social. Implementation and targets unclear.

statements.text: All U.S. Ships, Aircraft, and Military Personnel will remain in place in and around Iran until the REAL AGREEMENT is fully complied with. There will be no enrichment of Uranium. If not complied with, 'the Shootin Starts, bigger, and better, and stronger than anyone has ever seen before.'
statements.context: Truth Social post late April 8/early April 9, warning Iran of consequences for non-compliance with ceasefire terms.
statements.factCheckNote: Military deployment confirmed. Iran disputes 'no enrichment' was agreed.

statements.text: If for any reason it is not... then the 'Shootin' Starts,' bigger, and better, and stronger than anyone has ever seen before. NATO wasn't there when we needed them, and they won't be there if we need them again. Remember Greenland, that big, poorly run, piece of ice!!
statements.context: Warning about ceasefire compliance; slamming NATO at Rutte meeting; reviving Greenland threat.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump's claim of 'decisive victory' disputed by WaPo — officials say claims 'contradict reality.' NATO friction confirmed by Rutte.

statements.text: I spoke with Bibi and he's going to low-key it. I just think we have to be sort of a little more low-key.
statements.context: Phone call with Netanyahu about Lebanon strikes during ceasefire
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News, Axios, and Israeli officials

statements.text: I'm very optimistic. Iran's leaders talk much differently when you're at a meeting than they do to the press. They're much more reasonable.
statements.context: On upcoming Islamabad peace talks
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote from NBC interview

statements.text: We're thinking of doing it as a joint venture. It's a way of securing it — also securing it from lots of other people.
statements.context: On potential US-Iran joint management of Strait of Hormuz tolls
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote from ABC interview

statements.text: I spoke with Bibi and he's going to low-key it. I just think we have to be sort of a little more low-key. We have to be smart about this.
statements.context: Trump told NBC he asked Netanyahu to reduce Lebanon attacks during phone call. Comes as Operation Eternal Darkness killed 254+ in Lebanon hours after ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News, CBS News.

statements.text: Iran is doing a very poor job of letting oil through the Strait of Hormuz. They agreed to let it flow freely. Not happening!
statements.context: Trump criticized Iran for failing to reopen Hormuz despite ceasefire agreement. Zero oil tankers have transited since the truce.
statements.factCheckNote: Accurate that Hormuz remains effectively closed with zero oil tanker transits since ceasefire. However, ceasefire terms required 'phased reopening' not immediate flow.

statements.text: Our warships are being reloaded with the best ammunition and the best weapons ever made. Even better than what we did previously and we blew them apart.
statements.context: Trump warned via NY Post interview that US military is ready for fresh strikes if Islamabad peace talks fail. VP Vance en route to Pakistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Manila Times, Asharq Al-Awsat. Escalatory rhetoric consistent with pre-negotiation posturing.

statements.text: We're going to find out in about 24 hours. We're going to know soon. Iran has no cards beyond the Strait. The Strait will reopen fairly soon — with or without Iran's cooperation.
statements.context: Trump setting de facto 24-hour deadline for Islamabad talks. Also said Iran 'doing a very poor job, dishonorable some would say' on Hormuz. Escalatory warning as Vance departs for Pakistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Express Tribune, Gulf News, Newsweek.

statements.text: We're now starting the process of clearing out the Strait of Hormuz as a favor to Countries all over the World, including China, Japan, South Korea, France, Germany, and many others. All 28 of their mine dropper boats are also lying at the bottom of the sea.
statements.context: Trump announced Hormuz mine clearing via social media as USS Peterson and USS Murphy transited the strait — first US warships since war began.
statements.factCheckNote: Mine clearing confirmed by CENTCOM. Claim about 28 mine-dropper boats unverifiable but consistent with prior strike claims.

statements.text: Whether we make a deal or not makes no difference to me. Regardless what happens, we win. Our warships are loading up with the best weapons ever made.
statements.context: After Islamabad talks Day 1 ended with no deal. Trump downplayed significance, threatened further military action.
statements.factCheckNote: Quote confirmed by NBC News, Gulf News. Claim 'we win regardless' is opinion/posturing, not factual assertion.

statements.text: Effective immediately, the United States Navy, the Finest in the World, will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz. I have also instructed our Navy to seek and interdict every vessel in International Waters that has paid a toll to Iran. Additionally, I have instructed them to begin the process of destroying the mines the Iranians laid in the Straits. At an appropriate moment, we are fully LOCKED AND LOADED, and our Military will finish up the little that is left of Iran.
statements.context: Truth Social post announcing US naval blockade of Hormuz after Islamabad peace talks collapsed. Major escalation from mine-clearing to full counter-blockade. Came hours after Vance departed Pakistan without a deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg, CNBC, CBS News, Jerusalem Post, NBC News, Al Jazeera. Implementation timeline and scope unclear.

statements.text: Leo should get his act together as Pope, use Common Sense, stop catering to the Radical Left, and focus on being a Great Pope, not a Politician. He is WEAK on Crime, and terrible for Foreign Policy. If I wasn't in the White House, Leo wouldn't be in the Vatican.
statements.context: Trump escalated feud with Pope Leo XIV after the Pope's April 11 prayer vigil at St. Peter's Basilica denounced the 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling the Iran war. Trump also posted AI image of himself as Jesus-like figure and called Pope's brother 'all MAGA.'
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote from Truth Social post. Confirmed by Axios, Al Jazeera, NPR, Bloomberg.

statements.text: We're not going to let Iran make money on selling oil to people that they like and not people that they don't like. It's going to be all or none and that's the way it is. I predict they come back and they give us everything we want. I want everything. They have no cards.
statements.context: Fox News 'Sunday Morning Futures' interview. Outlined 5-point demands: dismantle nuclear facilities, hand over 400+ kg enriched uranium, accept security framework, end proxy funding, open Hormuz with no tolls. Confirmed minesweepers heading to Hormuz. Said oil prices could be 'a little bit higher' ahead of midterms.
statements.factCheckNote: Claims Iran has 'no cards' contradicted by Iran's continuing ability to block Hormuz and retain 1,000+ medium-range missiles per US intelligence. Nuclear demands far exceed any prior negotiation framework.

statements.text: Something could be happening over the next two days. Iranian officials have called and want to work a deal. I thought she had courage, but I was wrong — referring to Italy's Meloni defending Pope Leo.
statements.context: NY Post interview. Hinted at Round 2 US-Iran talks in Islamabad. Also attacked Italian PM Meloni for defending Pope Leo XIV. Rejected reports of 20-year enrichment suspension demand.
statements.factCheckNote: Axios and NYT confirmed 20-year enrichment proposal exists despite Trump denial. Iran talks timeline unverified.

statements.text: I think it's close to over, yeah. I view it as close to over. If I pulled up stakes right now, it would take them 20 years to rebuild that country. And we're not finished. I had to divert because if I didn't do that, right now, you'd have Iran with a nuclear weapon.
statements.context: Fox Business 'Mornings with Maria' interview aired April 15. Also said he is not thinking about extending the ceasefire (expires April 21).
statements.venue: Fox Business Interview — Mornings with Maria
statements.factCheckNote: PBS fact-checked earlier claims of US victory as 'contradicting reality.' Ceasefire is fragile, talks failed, blockade escalating. No permanent deal in sight.

statements.text: China is very happy that I am permanently opening the Strait of Hormuz. I am doing it for them, also - And the World. This situation will never happen again. They have agreed not to send weapons to Iran... President Xi will give me a big, fat, hug when I get there in a few weeks. We are working together smartly, and very well!
statements.context: Truth Social post. Frames US blockade as permanent strategic realignment, not just war-related. Claims bilateral agreement with China on no weapons to Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: China has not publicly confirmed any agreement to halt weapons to Iran. Trump's claim of 'permanently opening' Hormuz contradicts the current US blockade which is closing it further. No official US-China joint statement issued.

statements.text: Something could be happening over the next two days in Pakistan. Asim Munir is doing a great job.
statements.context: Trump told NY Post Round 2 talks likely in Pakistan. Initially said talks unlikely to return there but called back minutes later to say it was 'more likely' because of Munir's mediation.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UPI, Tribune India, Xinhua. Pakistan army chief Munir confirmed in Tehran on April 15.

statements.text: We've beaten them militarily, totally. I think it's close to over, I view it as very close to over. We'll see what happens, I think they want to make a deal very badly.
statements.context: Fox Business 'Mornings with Maria' interview. Also said stock market 'going to boom' once deal is reached. Hinted deal possible before King Charles US visit. Posted AI image of Jesus embracing Trump on Truth Social.
statements.factCheckNote: Military damage to Iran confirmed by Pentagon. 'Close to over' characterization disputed — 3 sticking points remain (nuclear, Hormuz, $270B compensation). WH separately denied formally requesting ceasefire extension.

statements.text: Prime Minister Netanyahu and President Aoun will be speaking tomorrow. I'm trying to get a little breathing room between Israel and Lebanon.
statements.context: Truth Social announcement of first Israel-Lebanon leader-to-leader phone call in 34 years, scheduled for April 17. Follows Rubio-hosted trilateral meeting between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors on April 14.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, CNBC, CBS News. Both Israeli and Lebanese officials confirmed the call.

statements.text: I just had excellent conversations with the Highly Respected President Joseph Aoun, of Lebanon, and Prime Minister Bibi Netanyahu, of Israel. These two Leaders have agreed that in order to achieve PEACE between their Countries, they will formally begin a 10 Day CEASEFIRE at 5 P.M. EST.
statements.context: Announced 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. First direct diplomatic engagement between Israel and Lebanon since 1983. Directed VP Vance and SecState Rubio to work with both countries toward lasting peace. Invited Aoun and Netanyahu to White House.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, NBC News, Bloomberg, PBS. However, Israel says IDF will NOT withdraw from southern Lebanon. No Hezbollah confirmation.

statements.text: I think it's close to over, I view it as very close to over. We've beaten them militarily, totally. I think they want to make a deal very badly.
statements.context: Interview on Fox Business Network. Markets rallied on optimism. S&P 500 hit record above 7,000.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, Fox Business.

statements.text: If there's no deal, fighting resumes. I'm not sure we'll need to extend the ceasefire.
statements.context: Comments on Iran ceasefire expiring April 22. Mixed signals on extension.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News.

statements.text: Probably, maybe over the weekend. The war is going swimmingly. The blockade is very routine.
statements.context: Asked when next round of Iran talks would happen. Said he would go to Islamabad if a deal is signed there. Continued false claim that Pope Leo supports Iran having nuclear weapons.
statements.factCheckNote: Talks timing unconfirmed. Pope nuclear weapons claim rated FALSE by PolitiFact, PBS, CNN.

statements.text: May have been a historic day for Lebanon. Good things are happening!!! I hope Hezbollah acts nicely and well during this important period in time. It will be an GREAT moment for them if they do. No more killing. Must finally have PEACE!
statements.context: Truth Social posts announcing 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Said he had 'excellent conversations with President Aoun of Lebanon and PM Netanyahu of Israel.' Described deal with Iran as 'very close.'
statements.factCheckNote: Ceasefire confirmed by State Department, NBC, CNN, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: We have a very good relationship with Iran right now, as hard as it is to believe.
statements.context: Expressing optimism about Iran deal prospects. Markets rallied on his comments. Second round of talks expected this weekend in Islamabad.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran still under US blockade. Iran Deputy FM rejected 'temporary ceasefire' at Antalya. Relationship characterization disputed.

statements.text: Israel will not be bombing Lebanon any longer. They are PROHIBITED from doing so by the U.S.A. Enough is enough!!!
statements.context: Truth Social post that caught Netanyahu off guard. Israeli officials asked White House for clarification — post 'contradicted the agreement.' US official later clarified ceasefire 'preserves Israel's right to self-defense against planned, imminent or ongoing attacks.'
statements.factCheckNote: US does not have legal authority to 'prohibit' Israeli military operations. Ceasefire agreement preserves self-defense right. Netanyahu and team blindsided.

statements.text: Iran, with the help of the U.S.A., has removed, or is removing, all sea mines! The naval blockade will remain in full force and effect as it pertains to Iran, only, until such time as our transaction with Iran is 100% complete.
statements.context: Clarifying that US blockade of Iranian ports continues while Hormuz opens for commercial traffic. Also said Iran agreed to 'never again shut the Strait of Hormuz.'
