statements.factCheckNote: Iran has NOT publicly agreed to 'never close Hormuz again.' Araghchi specified opening for 'remaining period of ceasefire' only. Mine removal claim unverified by CENTCOM.

statements.text: This will be a great and brilliant day for the world because Iran has just announced that the Strait of Hormuz is fully open and ready for business and full passage. No money will exchange hands in any way, shape or form.
statements.context: At Turning Point USA rally in Phoenix, AZ. Claim was already outdated — Iran had reversed the Hormuz opening hours earlier.
statements.venue: Turning Point USA Rally, Phoenix, AZ
statements.factCheckNote: Iran reversed the Hormuz opening within hours. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf scrapped the deal citing US 'untrustworthiness.' Trump's claim was outdated by the time he made it.

statements.text: We had some pretty good news 20 minutes ago. Our blockade will remain in full force until our transaction with Iran is 100% complete and fully signed. Talks are going on and will go on over the weekend.
statements.context: On Air Force One after Arizona rally. Declined to elaborate on 'good news.' Warned ceasefire may not extend past Wednesday without deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post, Al-Monitor. Blockade statement consistent with prior messaging.

statements.text: They got a little cute... they wanted to close up the Strait again. You can't blackmail us.
statements.context: After convening White House Situation Room meeting with Vance, Rubio, Hegseth, Bessent, Wiles, Witkoff, Ratcliffe, and JCS Chairman Caine to discuss renewed Hormuz crisis. Trump then went golfing with no further public comment.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, CNBC, ANI News.

statements.text: We're offering a very fair and reasonable DEAL, and I hope they take it because, if they don't, the United States is going to knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran. NO MORE MR. NICE GUY!
statements.context: Truth Social post in afternoon as Iran continued to refuse setting a date for Round 2 talks. Accompanied by accusation of 'total violation' of ceasefire. Ceasefire expires April 22.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by TIME, Fortune, CBS News.

statements.text: Our representatives — Vice President Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner — will travel to Pakistan on Monday to continue the negotiations. We will see if Iran is serious.
statements.context: Confirmed US delegation for Round 2 Iran talks in Islamabad, mediated by Pakistan Army Chief Gen. Asim Munir.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Times, Axios, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: Today, an Iranian-flagged cargo ship named TOUSKA, nearly 900 feet long and weighing almost as much as an aircraft carrier, tried to get past our Naval Blockade, and it did not go well for them.
statements.context: Truth Social post announcing USS Spruance's seizure of Iranian cargo ship Touska in Gulf of Oman. First ship physically boarded and seized under US blockade.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, NPR, Al Jazeera, CNN.

statements.text: Iran decided to fire bullets yesterday in the Strait of Hormuz — A Total Violation of our Ceasefire Agreement!
statements.context: Truth Social post accusing Iran of ceasefire violation after IRGC fired on commercial vessels in Strait of Hormuz on April 18.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran says Hormuz actions are in response to US blockade of Iranian ports, not a ceasefire violation. Confirmed IRGC fired on ships by CNN, NBC.

statements.text: The concept of the deal is done. I think we have a very good chance to get it completed.
statements.context: Stated US negotiators heading to Islamabad for new round of talks. Also claimed blockade costs Iran '$500 Million Dollars a day' while US 'loses nothing.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's IRNA officially declined Pakistan talks same day. Ghalibaf said Trump made 'seven claims in one hour, all seven false.'

statements.text: We're offering a very fair and reasonable DEAL, and I hope they take it because, if they don't, the United States is going to knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran.
statements.context: Truth Social post threatening Iran's infrastructure if deal rejected. Also said: 'Maybe I won't extend [ceasefire], so you have a blockade, and unfortunately we have to start dropping bombs again.' Confirmed sending Vance/Witkoff/Kushner to Islamabad.
statements.factCheckNote: Threat of attacking civilian infrastructure (bridges, power plants) could constitute violations of international humanitarian law. Previous strikes already destroyed significant Iranian infrastructure.

statements.text: Today, an Iranian-flagged cargo ship named TOUSKA, nearly 900 feet long and weighing almost as much as an aircraft carrier, tried to get past our Naval Blockade, and it did not go well for them.
statements.context: Truth Social post boasting about USS Spruance disabling Iranian cargo vessel Touska in Gulf of Oman April 19. First seizure of Iranian vessel since blockade began April 13.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CENTCOM press release, CNN, Al Jazeera. USS Spruance fired three 5-inch rounds into Touska's engine room after 6-hour standoff; 31st MEU Marines rappelled aboard.

statements.text: [Ceasefire extension] is highly unlikely. Iran decided to fire bullets yesterday in the Strait of Hormuz — A Total Violation of our Ceasefire Agreement!
statements.context: NewsNation interview stating the two-week ceasefire that expires Wednesday April 22 will likely not be extended. Cited Iranian warning shots on CMA CGM Everglade and other vessels as truce violations.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran disputes warning shots as violations — claims shots were enforcement of its own counter-blockade after US seized Touska. Ceasefire terms ambiguous on Hormuz shipping enforcement.

statements.text: The Anti-America Fake News Media is rooting for Iran to win, but it's not going to happen, because I'm in charge! My deal will be FAR BETTER than Obama's terrible 2015 deal.
statements.context: Truth Social post railing against media coverage of the war. Also stated he is under 'no pressure' to make a deal with Iran. Vance, Witkoff, and Kushner departing for Islamabad despite Iran's refusal to attend.
statements.factCheckNote: Personal opinion/political statement. No factual claim to verify.

statements.text: The blockade, which we will not take off until there is a 'deal,' is absolutely destroying Iran. They are losing $500 million a day, an unsustainable number, even in the short run.
statements.context: Truth Social post on April 21. Also warned 'lots of bombs start going off' if no deal by Wednesday evening. Said ceasefire extension 'highly unlikely.' Posted 900+ words on Truth Social by midday.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran revenue loss estimate is within the $400-500M/day range per IEA data. 'Lots of bombs' threat confirmed by PBS interview.

statements.text: Based on the fact that the Government of Iran is seriously fractured...we have been asked to hold our Attack on the Country of Iran until such time as their leaders and representatives can come up with a unified proposal...I will therefore extend the Ceasefire until such time as their proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other.
statements.context: Truth Social post extending ceasefire indefinitely. Dramatic same-day reversal after morning CNBC interview where he said extension was 'highly unlikely' and he 'expects to be bombing.' Decision at request of Pakistan PM Sharif and Army Chief Asim Munir. Directed military to continue blockade and remain ready.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, CBS, NPR, Fortune. Fortune headline: 'Trump extends ceasefire after insisting he wouldn't.'

statements.text: Iran is collapsing financially and starving for cash, losing $500 million per day. Military and Police complaining that they are not getting paid. People approached me four days ago, saying, 'Sir, Iran wants to open up the Strait immediately.'
statements.context: Truth Social post claiming Iran's financial collapse under blockade. Axios reports Trump giving Iran '3-5 days' to unify behind a counter-offer. Bessent separately warned Kharg Island storage will be full in days.
statements.factCheckNote: The $500M/day figure is plausible if based on Iran's ~1.3M bpd pre-war exports at ~$100/bbl, but 'military not getting paid' and backroom approach claims are unverified. Washington Examiner and ANI reported the claims.

statements.text: There is no time frame for the conflict with Iran. We're going to end up with a great deal. Iran has no choice but to send a delegation to Pakistan.
statements.context: Trump told reporters there is no deadline for the war and denied midterm elections are influencing his approach. Separately, White House spokesperson said Trump does not view Iran's seizure of two Hormuz ships as a ceasefire violation.
statements.factCheckNote: Contradicts his own officials — Leavitt stated a 3-5 day window for Iran to respond. PBS compiled a timeline showing Trump has shifted positions on war duration multiple times.

statements.text: Eight women in Iran who were arrested during protests will not face execution. I personally asked for this and it was granted. A big step forward!
statements.context: Trump claimed credit for saving 8 Iranian women from execution. Iran's Mizan judiciary news agency dismissed this as 'false news,' stating the women were never scheduled for execution and at least two had already been released when Trump made his plea.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's judiciary stated: 'Trump's empty-handedness in the battlefield has pushed him towards fabricating achievements from false news.' Rights groups could not verify the execution claim.

statements.text: We have total control over the Strait of Hormuz. No ship can enter or leave without the approval of the United States Navy. It is 'Sealed up Tight,' until such time as Iran is able to make a DEAL!!!
statements.context: Truth Social post declaring total US control of Hormuz. Same day, Trump ordered Navy to 'shoot and kill' any Iranian boat laying mines. Pentagon told Congress mine clearing could take 6 months. Iran banks first Hormuz toll revenue the same day.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN and Al Jazeera.

statements.text: Iran is having a very hard time figuring out who their leader is! They just don't know! I have all the time in the World, but Iran doesn't — The clock is ticking!
statements.context: Truth Social post on alleged Iranian leadership infighting between 'hardliners' and 'moderates.' Same day third US carrier (USS George H.W. Bush) enters CENTCOM AOR.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News and Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: Don't rush me... I don't want to rush myself. You know who's under time pressure? They are. Because if they don't get their oil moving, their whole oil infrastructure is going to explode.
statements.context: White House press remarks when asked about timeline for ending Iran war. Denied midterm elections are driving decisions.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote confirmed by CNN/ABC. Claim about Iran infrastructure is a threat, not statement of fact.

statements.text: There is to be no hesitation. Shoot and kill any boats laying sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz. Minesweepers to continue at a tripled up level.
statements.context: Truth Social post after Axios reported IRGC laid more mines in Hormuz this week.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct Truth Social quote confirmed by Al Jazeera, Time, CNBC.

statements.text: Americans should expect higher gas prices for a little while in exchange for an Iran without a nuclear weapon. Iran must stop funding Hezbollah as part of any deal — that's a must.
statements.context: White House remarks alongside Lebanon ceasefire extension announcement.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ABC News, CNBC.

statements.text: I have all the time in the World, but Iran doesn't — The Clock is ticking! I won't use nuclear weapons against Iran. Israel is going to have to defend itself if they are shot at... but they're going to do it carefully, and they'll be surgical.
statements.context: Truth Social post and remarks to press. Explicitly ruled out nuclear weapons. Affirmed Israel's right to strike Lebanon in self-defense.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, Times of Israel liveblog April 24.

statements.text: I could make a deal right now, but I want an everlasting deal. Iran is collapsing financially. They're losing millions of dollars a day.
statements.context: White House remarks confirming Witkoff and Kushner would travel to Islamabad Saturday for direct talks with Iran FM Araghchi; Vance on standby. Said clock is ticking on Iran as US blockade tightens.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera liveblog April 24. Axios on Witkoff/Kushner travel.

statements.text: Why would I use a nuclear weapon? We've totally, in a very conventional way, decimated them without it. A nuclear weapon should never be allowed to be used by anybody. They're making an offer and we'll have to see.
statements.context: White House remarks ruling out nuclear weapons against Iran. Separately told Reuters that Iran plans to 'make an offer' aimed at satisfying US demands. Also said 'Don't rush me' when asked about timeline for ending the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PBS, Washington Times, US News, Daily Caller.

statements.text: I have authorized the US Navy to shoot to destroy any Iranian fast boats that attempt to lay mines or disrupt passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Trump authorized 'shoot to destroy' rules of engagement for US Navy against Iranian mine-laying boats in Hormuz. Also set April 25 deadline for Iran to present unified proposal via Israeli Channel 12.
statements.factCheckNote: CBC News, Fox News. All verified.

statements.text: I just cancelled the trip of my representatives going to Islamabad, Pakistan, to meet with the Iranians. Too much time wasted on traveling, too much work! Besides which, there is tremendous infighting and confusion within their 'leadership.' Nobody knows who is in charge, including them. Also, we have all the cards, they have none! If they want to talk, all they have to do is call!!
statements.context: Truth Social post Saturday morning April 25 cancelling the Witkoff/Kushner mission to Islamabad. Reverses Karoline Leavitt's Friday Fox News announcement that the envoys would depart Saturday. Marks abrupt diplomatic reversal.
statements.factCheckNote: Verified by CBS News, NPR, Times of Israel. Direct quote from Truth Social post.

statements.text: The Iranians gave us a paper that should have been better and interestingly, the minute I cancelled it, within 10 minutes, we got a new paper that was much better… They offered a lot but not enough.
statements.context: Trump press remarks following Truth Social cancellation of Pakistan trip. Suggests Iran responded immediately to the cancellation with a more favorable proposal but Trump deemed it 'still not adequate.'
statements.venue: White House Press Remarks / Truth Social
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog April 25, NPR. Verified.

statements.text: We have all the cards. We're not going to spend 15 hours in airplanes all the time going back and forth. If they want to talk, all they have to do is call. Iran will not have a nuclear weapon.
statements.context: Trump told reporters talks could continue by telephone rather than in-person Pakistan summits. Iran has demanded lifting of naval blockade as precondition. War Powers Act deadline May 1 (5 days).
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill, CNBC, Fox News.

statements.text: Iran sent us a revised proposal within 10 minutes of our cancelling the Pakistan trip. It's much better. But it's not enough. We have all the cards; they have none. By the time they get there, it's hours and hours and hours of flying.
statements.context: Trump remarks to reporters on Truth Social and during press scrum, doubling down on cancellation of Witkoff/Kushner Pakistan trip and rejecting in-person travel for negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: Time, CNBC, The Hill. Confirmed.

statements.text: If they want to talk, they can come to us, or they can call us. You know, there is a telephone. We have nice, secure lines.
statements.context: Fox News 'The Sunday Briefing' interview. Trump reiterated Iran must come to the US, dismissing the need for in-person Pakistan talks. Also said the war could 'come to an end very soon' and the US would be 'very victorious.'
statements.factCheckNote: US News, NPR. Confirmed.

statements.text: I don't think so, but you never know. It will not stop me from winning the war in Iran.
statements.context: Asked whether the WHCD shooting was motivated by the Iran war. Trump dismissed the connection but vowed to continue the war effort.
statements.factCheckNote: NPR, CNN. Confirmed.

statements.text: They can come to us, or they can call us. We have nice, secure lines.
statements.context: Fox News 'The Sunday Briefing' interview, explaining why he canceled the Witkoff/Kushner trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera liveblog, Korea Times, US News. Confirmed.

statements.text: We're going to take Iran's nuclear dust.
statements.context: Referring to Iran's enriched uranium stockpile as part of any potential deal. Emphasizing 'Washington holds all the cards.'
statements.factCheckNote: 'Nuclear dust' is not a recognized nuclear term. Refers to enriched uranium. Iran's Qalibaf has said Iran will not hand over enriched uranium.

statements.text: Iran has 4 days. The War Powers nonsense in Congress doesn't apply to defending American shipping from terrorist regimes. May 1 means nothing. We will keep the Strait OPEN!
statements.context: Truth Social post issued mid-day Apr 27 PT, framing the May 1 War Powers Resolution vote as irrelevant and signaling continued kinetic posture against Iranian Hormuz interference.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote from Truth Social account. May 1 vote is a deadline for Senate action on War Powers Resolution; presidential veto expected if it passes.

statements.text: We have all the cards, they have none! If they want to talk, all they have to do is call!!!
statements.context: Truth Social post after Iran's Hormuz reopening proposal was transmitted. Trump also told reporters Iran 'offered a lot, but not enough' and cited 'tremendous infighting and confusion' in Iran's leadership, adding 'Nobody knows who is in charge, including them.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, Fox News, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: Iran has just informed us that they are in a 'State of Collapse.' They want us to 'Open the Hormuz Strait,' as soon as possible, as they try to figure out their leadership situation. The Chancellor of Germany, Friedrich Merz, thinks it's OK for Iran to have a Nuclear Weapon.
statements.context: Truth Social post on April 28 escalating both anti-Iran rhetoric and a transatlantic attack on German Chancellor Merz, who said Iran's negotiators were 'clearly stronger than one thought' and that the US was being 'humiliated.' Posted hours after Rubio publicly rejected Iran's Hormuz reopening proposal.
statements.factCheckNote: Quote verified via Fox News live coverage. Iran has not publicly described itself as in a 'state of collapse.' Merz did not say it would be 'OK' for Iran to have a nuclear weapon — Trump mischaracterizes Merz's comments.

statements.text: Iran can't get their act together. They don't know how to sign a nonnuclear deal. They better get smart soon!
statements.context: Posted on Truth Social with AI-generated image of himself holding an automatic rifle with explosions, captioned 'NO MORE MR. NICE GUY.' National security team had presented him with military options for Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump's claim Iran 'reached out' is partially accurate — Iran submitted a formal proposal via Pakistani mediators, but framing suggests supplication rather than structured diplomacy.

statements.text: The Chancellor of Germany, Friedrich Merz, thinks it's OK for Iran to have a Nuclear Weapon. He doesn't know what he's talking about! No wonder Germany is doing so poorly, both Economically, and otherwise!
statements.context: Response to Merz saying the US is 'being humiliated' by Iran and comparing the situation to Afghanistan. Merz did NOT say Iran should have nuclear weapons — Trump's characterization is a strawman.
statements.factCheckNote: FALSE: Merz never said Iran should have nuclear weapons. He criticized the lack of a clear US strategy, not the goal of denuclearization.

statements.text: They have to cry uncle. They have to say we give up. Militarily we've wiped them out. The blockade is genius. It's 100% foolproof.
statements.context: During Oval Office event with Artemis II astronauts. Rejected Iran's latest peace proposal to reopen Hormuz. Demanded nuclear deal guarantees first. Ordered national security team to prepare for prolonged blockade.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim Iran is 'militarily wiped out' is exaggerated — Iran retains significant ballistic missile stockpiles and proxy capabilities per Pentagon testimony same day.

statements.text: The blockade is somewhat more effective than the bombing. They are choking like a stuffed pig.
statements.context: Axios exclusive interview. Trump rejects Iran's latest peace proposal, says blockade stays until nuclear deal. Also confirmed CENTCOM briefing on new military options scheduled for April 30.
statements.factCheckNote: Crude metaphor. Blockade economic impact confirmed by Treasury ($170M/day Iran revenue loss). However, Iran's proxy operations and missile capability remain largely intact.

statements.text: The Chancellor of Germany should focus on the Russia-Ukraine War, and the rampant problems facing his own Country, including Crime and Migration, and less time on interfering with those that are getting rid of the Iran Nuclear threat.
statements.context: Truth Social post following Chancellor Merz's criticism of US Iran policy. Trump previously threatened to reduce US troop presence in Germany.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via Al Jazeera live blog (April 30, 2026).

statements.text: Iran should just give up. The blockade has been a great success — they should agree to our terms now.
statements.context: Trump comments on April 30 reiterating that the US naval blockade is succeeding and urging Iran to capitulate ahead of the May 1 War Powers deadline. Said as CENTCOM was preparing to brief him on three new military options.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Al Jazeera live blog and NBC News on April 30. Hormuz transit at 5-8% of normal confirms blockade impact, but Iran has not capitulated.

statements.text: We might need to restart operations against Iran. They're not getting the message. The blockade is working but we may need more.
statements.context: Trump remarks on May 1 as administration simultaneously declared war 'terminated' for War Powers purposes. Administration laying groundwork for extended blockade and possible resumption of strikes.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Al Jazeera May 1 liveblog. Statement contradicts simultaneous 'terminated' declaration.

statements.text: I don't know that we need it. We might need it. Iran wants to make a deal badly.
statements.context: Trump briefed by US commanders on new military options against Iran including ground deployments to Hormuz islands, special forces operation to remove enriched uranium from Isfahan, and rapid heavy strikes.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera Day 63 reporting and CNN live news confirmed Trump's remarks after CENTCOM briefing.

statements.text: Either we blast the hell out of them and finish them forever, or we want to try and make a deal — those are the options. They're asking for things that I can't agree to. The leadership is very disjointed. It's got two to three groups, maybe four... they all want to make a deal, but they're all messed up.
statements.context: Trump press gaggle following CENTCOM Adm. Bradley Cooper briefing on military options. Rejected Iran's Pakistan-mediated proposal delivered evening April 30.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Fox News, CNN cross-verified Trump remarks.

statements.text: [The War Powers Act is] totally unconstitutional. For War Powers Resolution purposes, the hostilities that began on Saturday, February 28, have terminated.
statements.context: Trump's formal letter to Congress, May 1, marking the 60-day deadline. Takes legal position that the April 7 ceasefire stops the WPR clock.
statements.factCheckNote: WaPo, CNN Politics, Al Jazeera confirmed letter and remarks. Sen. Kaine and most legal scholars dispute interpretation.

statements.text: It's actually, I believe it's treasonous [to say the US is not winning the war with Iran].
statements.context: Rally at The Villages, Florida, May 1 evening. Came hours after his War Powers letter declaring hostilities 'terminated' — creating contradiction of simultaneously claiming the war is over while calling criticism of war progress treasonous.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, The Independent confirmed remarks. PBS fact-check noted contradiction with 'terminated' letter.

statements.text: We could be better off not making a deal at all. It's been going on too long. We either go and just blast the hell out of them and finish them forever, or we want to try and make a deal.
statements.context: May 2 remarks escalating rhetoric after rejecting Iran's latest Pakistan-mediated peace proposal the previous day. Signals growing frustration with diplomatic track.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN Live Updates, Washington Examiner confirmed Trump's May 2 remarks.

statements.text: We took over the ship, we took over the cargo, we took over the oil. It's a very profitable business.
statements.context: Describing the US Navy's blockade of Iranian ports, calling US forces 'like pirates.' Framed the blockade as a revenue-generating operation rather than purely military.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed remarks. PBS fact-checkers note seized oil goes to US custody, not revenue. Legal status of seizures contested.

statements.text: If Iran attacks US ships, they will be blown off the face of the Earth.
statements.context: Fox News interview after Project Freedom launch. US Navy had just sunk 6 Iranian boats in Hormuz. Iran fired missiles and drones at US-escorted ships. Trump declined to confirm ceasefire still in effect.
statements.venue: Fox News interview with Trey Yingst
statements.factCheckNote: Fox News, CNN, multiple outlets confirmed direct quote from interview.

statements.text: Iran is now much more malleable in talks. Their enriched uranium — from a standpoint of value, it's not very valuable. Probably can't be used.
statements.context: Same Fox News interview. Trump downplayed Iran's nuclear leverage while claiming diplomatic progress despite Hormuz clashes.
statements.factCheckNote: ABC News confirmed quote. Claim about uranium value is misleading — Iran's 60% HEU stockpile is assessed by IAEA as 'breakout capable' within weeks.

statements.text: Our military just shot down seven Iranian boats. Iran will be blown off the face of the Earth if they target our ships in the Strait of Hormuz or Persian Gulf.
statements.context: Truth Social post on May 4 after Iranian small boats engaged US Project Freedom escort. CENTCOM later confirmed 6 boats destroyed by Apache and Seahawk helicopters; CBS reported 7. Mediaite captured the warning during a Fox News phone interview.
statements.venue: Truth Social / Fox News phone interview
statements.factCheckNote: Mediaite, Haaretz, CNBC all confirmed direct quote. Boat count varies between 6 (CENTCOM) and 7 (CBS/Trump).

statements.text: Pope Leo is endangering Catholics around the world with his weak comments on Iran. He should focus on faith, not on undermining American policy.
statements.context: Truth Social post escalating Trump's rift with the Vatican over Pope Leo XIV's criticism of the Iran war. State Department announced Secretary Rubio will travel to Rome and the Vatican May 7-8.
statements.factCheckNote: TIME magazine and Newsmax confirmed the post. Pope Leo had previously said 'God does not bless any conflict' and warned of a 'delusion of omnipotence.'

statements.text: Great Progress had been toward a Complete and Final Agreement with Representatives of Iran. Project Freedom will be paused for a short period of time.
statements.context: Truth Social post announcing pause of Operation Project Freedom — the US Navy escort operation through the Strait of Hormuz. Blockade of Iranian ports remains in place.
statements.factCheckNote: NPR, CNN, Bloomberg confirmed the pause announcement. However, no independent confirmation of 'great progress' in negotiations. Iran has not confirmed any deal framework.

statements.text: The ceasefire stops the 60-day clock. Other presidents also considered the War Powers Act unconstitutional.
statements.context: Trump told Congress the ceasefire pauses the War Powers Act 60-day authorization clock. Sen. Blumenthal countered: 'There's no pause button in the Constitution, or the War Powers Act.'
statements.factCheckNote: PBS and The Hill confirmed the claim. Legal experts dispute whether a ceasefire pauses the War Powers Act clock. Sen. Blumenthal called it constitutionally baseless.

statements.text: If Iran doesn't agree to the deal, the bombing starts, and it will be, sadly, at a much higher level and intensity than it was before.
statements.context: Trump issued ultimatum to Iran via Truth Social as the US-Iran 14-point peace memorandum negotiation reaches climax. Said Operation Epic Fury would 'be at an end' if Iran complies but threatened resumption of strikes if Iran rejects MOU.
statements.factCheckNote: CNBC and Al Jazeera confirmed the statement. Trump made it during press encounter and on Truth Social same day.

statements.text: Operation Project Freedom is paused. We're making great progress on a deal with Iran.
statements.context: Trump announced he was pausing the US Navy escort operation through the Strait of Hormuz to give negotiations more time. Coupled with the 'higher level bombing' ultimatum, signals carrot-and-stick approach toward Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and NPR confirmed the announcement. CNN reported negotiations approaching framework agreement.

statements.text: Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon, and they won't, and they've agreed to that, among other things.
statements.context: Trump claimed Iran has agreed to give up its nuclear weapons program as part of the proposed 14-point MOU. However, Iran's Atomic Energy Organization has said Iran will NOT accept limits on enrichment, and Iran's FM previously denied Trump's April claim about Iran agreeing to hand over nuclear material.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran has not confirmed any such agreement. FactCheck.org and Political Wire noted the claim lacks evidence. Iran FM previously said enriched uranium 'is not going to be transferred anywhere under any circumstances.'

statements.text: They want to make a deal. We've had very good talks over the last 24 hours, and it's very possible that we'll make a deal.
statements.context: Trump told reporters amid growing expectations Iran will respond to the US 14-point MOU via Pakistani mediators. Qatar PM also said there is a 'high probability' of a deal. Oil markets partially recovering from previous day's crash.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, Al Jazeera. Qatar PM statement confirmed by Times of Israel.

statements.text: Pope Leo is endangering a lot of Catholics with his position on the Iran war. He should be more thoughtful about supporting our efforts.
statements.context: Trump struck back at Pope Leo XIV after the pontiff publicly clarified that neither he nor the Catholic Church supports nuclear weapons in Iran or anywhere. Trump's earlier claim that the Pope endorsed nuclear weapons in Iran was rated FALSE by PolitiFact in April. Rubio scheduled a Vatican meeting for May 7.
statements.factCheckNote: PolitiFact rated Trump's earlier claim about Pope Leo and nuclear weapons as FALSE (April 13, 2026).

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury will be at an end, and the highly effective Blockade will allow the Hormuz Strait to be OPEN TO ALL, including Iran.
statements.context: Trump on Truth Social, framing the eventual deal — assuming Iran agrees to the 14-point MOU. Comes alongside Trump's threat that 'the bombing starts, and it will be, sadly, at a much higher level and intensity than it was before' if Iran rejects.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera live blog reporting on Trump Truth Social posts.

statements.text: If they don't agree, the bombing starts, and it will be, sadly, at a much higher level and intensity than it was before.
statements.context: Trump gave Iran one-week ultimatum on Fox News after US-Iran naval clash in Hormuz. Called the strikes on Qeshm/Bandar Abbas 'just a love tap.' Despite exchange of fire, told ABC News 'the ceasefire is going. It's in effect.'
statements.factCheckNote: Quotes confirmed across Fox News, CNN Day 69 liveblog, CNBC.

statements.text: We've had very good talks over the last 24 hours, and it's very possible that we'll make a deal. They trifled with us today. We blew them away.
statements.context: Trump in Oval Office remarks to reporters after CENTCOM struck Iranian military sites in retaliation for IRGC attacking 3 US destroyers. Simultaneously projecting optimism about deal and warning of escalation.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN Day 69 liveblog and Al Jazeera.

statements.text: Just like we knocked them out again today, we'll knock them out a lot harder, and a lot more violently, in the future, if they don't get their Deal signed, FAST! That was just a love tap.
statements.context: Remarks to reporters at Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool renovation site. Also warned without ceasefire there would be 'one big glow coming out of Iran.' ABC News phone interview.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ABC News, Fox News, Time.

statements.text: Iran is playing games. They will be laughing no longer.
statements.context: Posted on Truth Social after Iran submitted 10-point counter-proposal via Pakistan. Iran rejected US demand for immediate nuclear enrichment moratorium and demanded end to war on all fronts including Lebanon.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran did submit a formal response — characterizing it as 'playing games' is Trump's interpretation. Iran called the response 'realistic and positive.'

statements.text: The ceasefire is on massive life support. Iran's response was totally unacceptable. It was garbage. It was stupid.
statements.context: Rejected Iran's 10-point counter-proposal. Said he is 'more seriously considering a resumption of major combat operations.' Held emergency Situation Room meeting with VP Vance, Rubio, Hegseth, Gen. Caine, CIA Ratcliffe. Discussed resuming Project Freedom and airstrikes on remaining Iranian targets.
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple sources confirm Trump's rejection and Situation Room meeting. Axios, CNN, NBC all corroborate.

statements.text: I'm going to be suspending the federal gas tax for a period of time to help Americans shoulder the higher fuel prices.
statements.context: National average gas price at $4.52/gallon, up 48% from pre-war. Sen. Hawley announced he would introduce legislation to suspend the gas tax. Fox News interview also discussed reviving Project Freedom maritime operation.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, CBS News. Gas tax suspension requires Congressional action.

statements.text: They'll either do the right thing, or we'll finish the job.
statements.context: Before departing for Beijing state visit with Xi Jinping. Reiterated ceasefire on 'massive life support.' Delegation includes Tim Cook, Elon Musk. Said he doesn't 'think we need any help with Iran' from China. Two US officials said strikes unlikely before Trump returns from China.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, NPR, CNBC. Multiple officials confirm Trump considering resuming combat operations.

statements.text: We're going to have a long talk about it. I think he's been relatively good, to be honest with you. I wouldn't say Iran is one of them, to be honest with you, because we have Iran very much under control.
statements.context: Contradictory remarks made before boarding Air Force One for Beijing, first saying he would discuss Iran with Xi, then minutes later downplaying the need. Arrived in Beijing for May 14-15 summit focused on trade, Iran oil purchases, and Hormuz mediation.
statements.venue: White House / Air Force One departure statement
statements.factCheckNote: Both quotes confirmed by AP and Al Jazeera pool reporters. NPR reported full departure remarks.

statements.text: I don't think we need any help with Iran. We'll win it one way or the other, peacefully or otherwise.
statements.context: Told reporters he did not need Xi to help on Iran, even as his administration was counting on China to pressure Tehran on reopening Hormuz. Analysts say China — Iran's biggest oil customer — holds significant leverage.
statements.venue: White House departure statement, Washington DC
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, NPR. CNBC noted the contradictory messaging.

statements.text: We're either going to make a deal or they're going to be decimated, one way or the other. It won't be long.
statements.context: Reiterated his ultimatum framing in multiple public appearances as he departed for Beijing, signaling growing impatience with stalled Iran negotiations and threatening renewed military action if a deal is not reached.
statements.venue: Remarks to reporters, en route to Beijing
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ABC News Iran live updates and Fox News.

statements.text: They are aiding and abetting the enemy! All it does is give Iran false hope when none should exist. These are American cowards that are rooting against our Country. Iran had 159 ships in their Navy — Every single ship is now resting at the bottom of the sea. They have no Navy, their Air Force is gone, all Technology is gone.
statements.context: Trump lashed out at reporters and intelligence officials who contradicted his claims about Iran's military defeat. He made the sweeping claim that all of Iran's 159 navy ships were destroyed, which directly contradicts classified intelligence showing Iran retained significant military capacity. At the Beijing summit, Trump also said Xi pledged not to provide military equipment to Iran, and that the US-China relationship will be 'better than ever.'
statements.venue: Beijing, China — Trump-Xi summit + social media posts
statements.factCheckNote: Intelligence assessments reported by NYT and WaPo show Iran retained ~70% of its pre-war missile arsenal and restored access to ~30 of 33 missile sites. Trump's claim that every Iranian navy ship sank is a significant exaggeration.

statements.text: He said he's not going to give military equipment to Iran. He said that strongly. He said, 'If I can be of any help at all, I would like to be of help.' Anybody that buys that much oil has obviously got some kind of relationship, but he'd like to see the Hormuz Strait open. We'll win it one way or the other, peacefully or otherwise.
statements.context: Trump's remarks following the second day of the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing. He confirmed Xi offered to help broker peace with Iran and pledged not to supply military equipment to Tehran. Trump also said Xi told him China intends to continue purchasing Iranian oil regardless. Trump described the summit as productive on Iran, trade, and Taiwan.
statements.venue: Beijing, China — Trump-Xi Summit Day 2 press remarks
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Time, CBS News, Al Jazeera, and CNBC. White House readout confirms the Hormuz agreement and Xi's military equipment pledge.

statements.text: We talked about that, and I'm going to make a decision over the next few days. [On 20-year nuclear suspension vs. permanent ban:] Yeah, I'd be OK with that if it's real.
statements.context: Trump's remarks on Air Force One departing Beijing after the Trump-Xi summit. Signaled willingness to accept a 20-year Iranian nuclear suspension (previously demanded permanent ban) if it's 'real.' Also weighing lifting sanctions on Chinese companies buying Iranian oil — a significant potential concession. Summit produced no binding Iran deal. Congressional opposition: 52 senators + 177 House members demanded Trump reject any deal allowing enrichment.
statements.venue: Air Force One — Departing Beijing, China
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC and Times of Israel from Air Force One press remarks, May 15, 2026. Significant policy shift from prior permanent-ban position.

statements.text: The military decimation of Iran... to be continued! [Separate Fox News interview:] I am not going to be much more patient. They should make a deal.
statements.context: Trump posted on Truth Social on May 15, 2026 — his most direct public implication of resumed strikes since the April 8 ceasefire. In a separate Fox News interview with Hannity, Trump warned Iran to make a deal soon or face consequences.
statements.venue: Truth Social / Fox News Hannity interview
statements.factCheckNote: Both statements confirmed: Times of Israel confirmed Truth Social post. Fox News confirmed Hannity interview quote. France24 liveblog headline: Trump says he won't be much more patient with Iran.

statements.text: I am losing patience. I did not ask China for any favors. Xi agreed with me that Iran must reopen the Strait of Hormuz — it has to open. We agree almost entirely on the situation in Iran.
statements.context: Trump told reporters on May 15-16 he was losing patience with Iran as ceasefire talks stalled post-Beijing summit. Confirmed Xi Jinping offered diplomatic help but made no binding commitments. Trump insisted he did not ask Xi for favors on Iran to avoid reciprocal obligations. Ceasefire with Iran expires May 17 with no deal in sight.
statements.venue: Remarks to reporters — Air Force One / White House
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Detroit News, Fox News liveblog and CNBC from May 15, 2026. Xi offer of diplomatic help confirmed by Al Jazeera and Time magazine.

statements.text: If Iran gives a real commitment, I would be fine with a 20-year suspension of their nuclear enrichment program. A real guarantee — not a paper guarantee.
statements.context: Trump told reporters aboard Air Force One departing China on May 15, 2026, signaling a policy shift: he would accept a 20-year suspension of Iran's uranium enrichment rather than the permanent halt he had previously demanded. This represents a significant flexibility offer as the ceasefire expiry looms on May 17.
statements.venue: Remarks to reporters aboard Air Force One departing Beijing
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel and CNBC, May 15, 2026.

statements.text: We sent them a proposal. They need to move quickly, or something bad is going to happen.
statements.context: Trump confirmed on May 16 that the United States had formally sent Iran a written nuclear deal proposal — the latest in a series of proposals exchanged via Pakistani mediators. Trump's warning that something 'bad' would happen signals he is approaching an ultimatum threshold. Araghchi said Iran had not received any written proposal, creating a public dispute over the exchange.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog May 16, 2026. Araghchi denial also confirmed.

statements.text: We stopped striking Iran at the request of a very nice group of people from Pakistan, who are very close to Iran.
statements.context: Trump confirmed on Fox News's 'Special Report' that Pakistan's intervention was the reason the US paused airstrikes on Iran. Pakistan's role as primary diplomatic back-channel and ceasefire broker is now publicly confirmed by Trump himself. Trump also said Pakistan wanted to help bring a deal, and he respected their request.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Breitbart (May 15), Fox News, and multiple outlets. Pakistan previously played down its role as a mediator.

statements.text: We could destroy their entire infrastructure — every element — in two days. I've chosen not to. That's not something I want to do. But I want a deal.
statements.context: Trump made the 'two days' destruction threat in remarks to reporters, maintaining maximum pressure while keeping the door open to a deal. Comes as ceasefire formally expired (Iran disputes this) and Pakistan Interior Minister is in Tehran mediating. Haaretz confirmed the quote from the May 16 liveblog.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz May 16 liveblog and Fox News.

statements.text: It was the calm before the storm.
statements.context: Trump posted AI-generated war imagery on Truth Social showing himself on a warship with Iranian vessels in stormy seas, captioned 'It Was The Calm Before The Storm.' A second image showed Trump ordering naval fire: 'Okay, we have it in our sight. Fire!' A third showed a 'space laser' targeting Iranian aircraft: 'Lasers: Bing, Bing, GONE!' Israeli media immediately reported Trump was expected to decide on resumed Iran strikes within 24 hours. This is widely interpreted as the clearest escalation signal since the April 8 ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: Truth Social posts confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 17, IANS Live, Arab Times Online, Ynet News. Israeli media sourced Pentagon officials saying 24-hour decision window is real.

statements.text: Iran will have a very bad time if they don't make a deal. A very, very bad time.
statements.context: Trump told BFM TV (French broadcaster) that Tehran would face a 'very bad time' if it failed to reach an agreement. Trump also responded to Iran's PGSA Hormuz toll announcement by ordering the US Navy to 'destroy the mines Iranians laid in the Straits' and stating the US would blockade any ship that paid Iranian tolls. His BFM TV appearance came hours after his Truth Social 'calm before the storm' posts and Israeli media reports of a 24-hour strike decision window.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera Iran war liveblog May 17, Iran International liveblog. CBS News confirmed Trump's order regarding US blockade of ships paying Iranian tolls.

statements.text: You know what you are? You're a treasonous reporter. I'll tell you that. I don't believe in what you're doing.
statements.context: Trump called New York Times national security reporter David Sanger 'treasonous' aboard Air Force One after Sanger asked why the US continues to attack Iran without achieving its stated political goals. Trump boasted the US could destroy Iran's infrastructure 'in two days' if it wanted to. The confrontation came as Trump returned from Beijing after China summit talks failed to produce any Iran-related breakthroughs.
statements.venue: Air Force One — aboard press pool gaggle
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz May 16 liveblog. Trump calling journalists 'treasonous' has no legal basis — the First Amendment protects national security reporting. Classified information was not involved in Sanger's question.

statements.text: For Iran, the Clock is Ticking, and they better get moving, FAST, or there won't be anything left of them. TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE!
statements.context: Trump posted this ultimatum on Truth Social after speaking by phone with Netanyahu and as the ceasefire deadline passed on Day 79. Trump also scheduled a Situation Room meeting for Tuesday (May 19) with VP Vance, Witkoff, Rubio, Hegseth, Gen. Caine, and Ratcliffe. Trump previously told Fox News' Hannity on May 15: 'Now they can make a deal or they get annihilated.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News, CBS News, Washington Examiner, and Al Jazeera May 17 liveblog.

statements.text: For Iran, the Clock is Ticking, and they better get moving, FAST, or there won't be anything left of them. TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE!
statements.context: Trump posted this ultimatum to Truth Social after speaking by phone with Netanyahu on May 17. He also told BFM TV (France) that Iran would have 'a very bad time' if no deal was reached, and said the US could destroy Iran's entire infrastructure 'in two days.' Trump's national security team meeting is scheduled for Tuesday May 19 to discuss military options. US-Iran ceasefire deadline has passed.
statements.factCheckNote: 'Two days' infrastructure claim disputed by CENTCOM's own assessment; Adm. Cooper testified Iran has restored 30 of 33 Hormuz-area missile sites. Truth Social post confirmed verbatim.

statements.text: They better get moving, FAST, or there won't be anything left of them. I am meeting with my national security team Tuesday — they know what that means.
statements.context: Trump reiterated his ultimatum to Iran on May 18, Day 80, as Iran announced it had submitted a response to the latest US proposal through Pakistan. The response defended Iran's demand for war reparations, which Trump and the US team immediately rejected as a dealbreaker. Trump's May 19 Situation Room meeting with Vance, Rubio, Hegseth, Gen. Caine, Ratcliffe, and Witkoff will review military options including resumed strikes. Iran's stock market reopened the same day, signaling Tehran's domestic normalization posture.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump ultimatum confirmed by CNN, NBC News, UPI May 18. Iran's response through Pakistan confirmed by Arab News, Al Jazeera liveblog May 18. Situation Room meeting confirmed by Axios, Fox News.

statements.text: I have been asked by the Emir of Qatar, the Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia and the President of the United Arab Emirates to hold off on our planned Military attack of the Islamic Republic of Iran, which was scheduled for tomorrow... serious negotiations are now taking place, and that, in their opinion, as Great Leaders and Allies, a Deal will be made, which will be very acceptable to the United States of America... I have also instructed the United States Military to be prepared to go forward with a full, large scale assault of Iran, on a moment's notice.
statements.context: Hours after posting his 'Clock is Ticking' ultimatum, Trump made the extraordinary disclosure that a US military strike on Iran had been scheduled for May 19 — and that he agreed to postpone it at the personal request of Gulf leaders. The simultaneous instruction to keep the military at full readiness maintained maximum pressure. This was the second time Trump held off from striking Iran during the conflict (the first was in early March). Analysts noted the disclosure itself — confirming a specific scheduled strike — was unprecedented.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump Gulf states postponement post confirmed by CNN, PBS NewsHour, CBS News, Axios, CNBC, Al Jazeera, The Hill. Gulf states request confirmed by Qatar, Saudi, UAE diplomatic sources.

statements.text: For Iran, the Clock is Ticking, and they better get moving, FAST, or there won't be anything left of them. TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE!
statements.context: Trump posted this stark warning on Truth Social on Day 81 (May 19) as US-Iran nuclear deal negotiations via Pakistani mediators remained deadlocked over enrichment duration. Iran had submitted a revised proposal offering to freeze enrichment for 5 years — far short of the US demand for 20 years. The post preceded Trump's NSC Situation Room meeting with Vance, Rubio, Hegseth, Gen. Caine, Ratcliffe, and Witkoff to review military options. European leaders and Gulf allies urged continued diplomacy; Pezeshkian responded with 'dialogue does not mean surrender.'
statements.factCheckNote: Trump 'Clock is Ticking / TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE' post confirmed by CNN, NBC News, Al Jazeera liveblog, France 24, Euronews May 18-19.

statements.text: I have instructed Secretary of War, Pete Hegseth, The Chairman of The Joint Chiefs of Staff, General Daniel Caine, and The United States Military, that we will NOT be doing the scheduled attack of Iran tomorrow, but have further instructed them to be prepared to go forward with a full, large scale assault of Iran, on a moment's notice, in the event that an acceptable Deal is not reached. This Deal will include, importantly, NO NUCLEAR WEAPONS FOR IRAN!
statements.context: Truth Social post announcing postponement of scheduled May 19 Iran strike at the request of Gulf state leaders (Qatar Emir, Saudi Crown Prince MBS, UAE President). Pentagon ordered to remain ready for 'full, large scale assault on a moment's notice.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, PBS NewsHour, CBS News, Axios, CNBC, Al Jazeera, The Hill.
statements.source: CNN — Trump holds off on Iran attacks at request of Gulf states

statements.text: We were getting ready to do a very MAJOR attack tomorrow. I've put it off for a little while, hopefully, maybe forever, but possibly for a little while. I was an hour away from making the decision to strike Iran.
statements.context: Trump to reporters during White House ballroom tour on May 19, elaborating on his Truth Social post about calling off the planned Iran strike. He added: 'Everyone tells me it's unpopular, but I think it's very popular.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, ABC News, Time, CNBC May 19 reporting.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Trump delays scheduled attack on Iran crediting serious negotiations

statements.text: President Xi Jinping was very gracious. He told me that China would not be sending weapons to Iran. I believe him.
statements.context: Trump confirmed that Chinese President Xi Jinping personally pledged during diplomatic communications that China would not supply weapons to Iran during the ongoing conflict. The statement came as Xi and Putin held a summit in Beijing where Xi called for a 'comprehensive ceasefire.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera May 19 reporting.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Trump says Xi assured him China would not send weapons to Iran

statements.text: We're going to end that war very quickly. They want to make a deal so badly. You are going to see oil prices plummet. There's so much oil out there.
statements.context: Speaking to reporters outside the White House after calling off the May 19 strike. Trump expressed confidence Iran would capitulate and predicted oil prices would collapse once the war ended.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's FM simultaneously stated 'we cannot trust the Americans at all' — Iran's public posture does not reflect Trump's claimed desperation. Brent fell slightly to $111.22 on the statement but remains near 3-month highs.

statements.text: I'm saying two or three days, maybe Friday, Saturday, Sunday, something — maybe early next week.
statements.context: Trump specifying the calendar window of his 2–3 day deadline for Iran to produce an acceptable deal before resumed US strikes would be ordered. Delivered standing in front of White House construction.
statements.venue: White House South Lawn, Washington DC
statements.factCheckNote: Specific calendar framing confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 82 liveblog and WION News. Sets latest deadline of approximately May 24-25.
statements.source: Al Jazeera Iran war Day 82 liveblog

statements.text: The only question is, do we go ahead and finish it up or are they going to be signing a document? Let's see what happens.
statements.context: Trump at US Coast Guard Academy commencement ceremony in New London, Connecticut, telling graduates they would be 'tested' in their military careers and referencing the ongoing Iran war. A separate remark to reporters indicated Trump believes the US is 'in no hurry' due to midterm election considerations.
statements.venue: US Coast Guard Academy Commencement, New London, CT
statements.factCheckNote: CGA commencement speech confirmed by TurnTo10, C-SPAN, and US News & World Report. Quote reflects Trump's dual framing — military finish vs. diplomatic deal — without specifying which he prefers.

statements.text: I was an hour away from making the decision to go today. We hit them very hard, but we may have to hit them even harder.
statements.context: Trump revealing to reporters how close the US came to launching a new military strike on Iran before Gulf state leaders (Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE) called to request a 2-3 day pause for serious negotiations. Delivered at the White House.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News live updates, Time magazine, and Arab News. Multiple outlets reported the 'hour away' framing from the same White House press availability on May 20.
statements.source: CBS News live updates — Iran war

statements.text: We hit them very hard, but we may have to hit them even harder. People don't want them to have a nuclear weapon, and they will not have a nuclear weapon. And they want to make a deal so badly.
statements.context: Trump addressed the US Coast Guard Academy commencement in New London, Connecticut on Day 83, delivering his most direct public military threat since the ceasefire began while simultaneously pursuing Pakistan-mediated diplomacy. Reports from NYT/Times of Israel cited two Middle Eastern officials saying US and Israel are in their 'most intense preparations yet' for war renewal, possibly within days, including options to capture Kharg Island and extract nuclear material from Iran.
statements.venue: US Coast Guard Academy Commencement, New London, Connecticut
statements.factCheckNote: Speech confirmed by Fox News live coverage May 21. Consistent with Trump's May 16 'Clock is Ticking' Truth Social post and May 19 strike cancellation announcement.
statements.source: Fox News — Iran war live coverage May 20-21

statements.text: He's fine, he'll do whatever I want him to do.
statements.context: Trump's remark about Israeli PM Netanyahu after a 'lengthy and dramatic' phone call in which the two clashed over Iran policy. Netanyahu reportedly pushed to resume the war; Trump was pursuing a 30-day letter of intent via Qatar-Pakistan mediation. Sources described Netanyahu's 'hair on fire' during the call.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Mediaite, and Axios. Axios independently confirmed the tense nature of the call and Netanyahu's skepticism of the deal framework.
statements.source: Mediaite — Trump boasts Netanyahu will do whatever I want after lengthy dramatic call

statements.text: We will get it. We don't need it, we don't want it. We'll probably destroy it after we get it, but we're not going to let them have it.
statements.context: Trump told reporters at the White House that the US will retrieve and destroy Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium, setting a direct collision course with Iran's offer to "downblend" domestically.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Times of Israel, Al-Monitor, Washington Times liveblog May 21.

statements.text: I was an hour away from hitting Iran again. But people came to me — very smart people — and said 'give it a few more days.' So I'll give it a few more days.
statements.context: Trump revealed to reporters that on May 19 he was approximately one hour from ordering new military strikes on Iran before Gulf allies intervened and asked for more time to allow diplomacy to proceed. He said Iran has "a few days" to make a deal.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News confirmed Trump told reporters he was 'an hour away' from striking Iran on May 19. CNBC confirmed the 'few days' deadline. Multiple outlets corroborate Gulf state intervention.
statements.source: CBS News — Trump says he was 'an hour away' from striking Iran

statements.text: Iran reviews US peace proposal as Trump says he's willing to wait 'a few days.' Negotiations are in the final stages.
statements.context: Trump told reporters talks with Iran are in the 'final stages' while simultaneously warning of military action if Iran doesn't engage, saying he would allow 'two or three days — maybe Friday, Saturday, Sunday, maybe early next week' for a deal.
statements.factCheckNote: 'Final stages' characterization contested by Iranian officials who say core issues (uranium enrichment, HEU disposition) remain unresolved. Multiple outlets confirmed the 'final stages' claim was disputed.
statements.source: CNBC — Iran reviews US peace proposal, Trump willing to wait 'a few days'

statements.text: Netanyahu will do whatever I want him to do on Iran. I'm in no hurry. I think we can save people from getting killed by waiting a couple of days. If we don't get the right answer, it could start up very quickly.
statements.context: Trump told reporters he is not rushing toward a deal with Iran while affirming Netanyahu's alignment with US policy. He said the war could resume 'very quickly' if no deal is reached but he preferred to give diplomacy 'a couple of days.' A new poll shows 60% of Americans oppose the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by CBS News live updates and Axios. Trump's 'in no hurry' framing confirmed by multiple outlets.
statements.source: CBS News — Iran war live updates: Iran mulling latest US peace proposal

statements.text: We will get it. We don't need it, we don't want it. We'll probably destroy it after we get it, but we're not going to let them have it.
statements.context: Trump told reporters the US would seize or destroy Iran's near-weapons-grade enriched uranium stockpile (~440 kg at 60% enrichment). The statement directly contradicts Iran's position — confirmed the same day by Mojtaba Khamenei's directive — that the uranium must remain inside Iran. The declaration represents Trump's clearest statement yet that HEU export/destruction is a non-negotiable US condition.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Washington Times and CNBC May 21. Consistent with Trump's repeated demands that Iran surrender its nuclear stockpile.
statements.source: Washington Times — Trump says US wants Iran to hand over enriched uranium as mediators work on peace

statements.text: Iran is dying to make a deal. We have stopped them. They are never going to have a nuclear weapon. And will have that over with soon. It will be over with soon.
statements.context: Trump spoke at a rally in Suffern, New York while his administration was simultaneously preparing for a potential fresh round of Iran strikes if no deal materialized. He canceled his Memorial Day weekend trip to Bedminster to hold NSC meetings on Iran. Trump's statement of Iran 'dying to make a deal' was notably more optimistic than Iran's own characterization of the talks as having 'deep and significant' differences.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Foreign Ministry (Baghaei) simultaneously characterized gaps as 'deep and significant.' Trump's framing contradicted by Iran's public position. His claim of stopping Iran's nuclear program is disputed — uranium stockpile remains inside Iran.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Trump says Iran war will be over with soon as progress reported

statements.text: I have a thing called Iran. There are only two options — they make a deal, or we make the deal for them. My priority right now is to be in Washington doing my job.
statements.context: Trump remained at the White House instead of attending his son's Memorial Day wedding, signaling an imminent decision on Iran. Trump convened an NSC war council (Vance, Hegseth, Ratcliffe, Wiles) to weigh whether to resume military strikes. Source: Axios reported Trump discussed a "decisive" operation after which he could declare victory.
statements.factCheckNote: White House confirmed Trump remaining in Washington. NSC meeting confirmed by Axios. Quote is a paraphrase of multiple on-record statements.

statements.text: It's a solid 50/50. Either we make a good deal or we blow them to kingdom come.
statements.context: Trump exclusive interview with Axios on Day 85, describing the likelihood of a US-Iran deal vs. resumed military strikes. He said he was meeting with envoys Witkoff and Kushner Saturday to review Iran's latest proposal and would decide by Sunday. He separately told reporters talks were 'getting a lot closer.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios on-record interview published May 23, 2026. Multiple outlets confirmed the quote.
statements.source: Axios — Trump says he's '50/50' on Iran deal or bombs

statements.text: I will only sign a deal where we get everything we want. Every day it gets better and better. We want Hormuz open — free, without tolls. It's an international waterway.
statements.context: Trump statement to reporters on Day 85 reiterating US non-negotiable demands: toll-free Strait of Hormuz and transfer of Iran's highly enriched uranium. He said talks were 'getting a lot closer' while holding a conference call with Gulf leaders (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Egypt, Pakistan, Turkey) who urged him to hold off strikes.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News and multiple pool reporters on May 23, 2026. Gulf leader conference call confirmed by CNN.
statements.source: CBS News — Trump says US is 'getting a lot closer' to agreement with Iran

statements.text: An Agreement has been largely negotiated, subject to finalization between the United States of America, the Islamic Republic of Iran, and the various other Countries. In addition to many other elements of the Agreement, the Strait of Hormuz will be opened. Final aspects and details of the Deal are currently being discussed and will be announced shortly.
statements.context: Truth Social post on Day 86 evening after calling Gulf leaders (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan, Bahrain) and Netanyahu. Pakistan Army Chief Gen. Asim Munir completed a 24-hour marathon mediation in Tehran. NSC war council convened with VP Vance, Hegseth, Ratcliffe, and Wiles. The statement represents Trump's most optimistic public framing of the deal to date, marking an evolution from his '50/50' interview with Axios earlier in the day.
statements.factCheckNote: Post confirmed by NPR, CNBC, PBS, CBS News, Al Jazeera, and Axios on May 23, 2026. Munir's mediation confirmed by Tribune India and Pakistani sources.
statements.source: NPR — Trump says Iran deal including opening of Strait of Hormuz is 'largely negotiated'

statements.text: We're not in a rush. Time is on our side. There can be no mistakes! Our relationship with Iran is becoming a much more professional and productive one. The leadership is very disjointed — it's got 2 to 3 groups, maybe 4.
statements.context: Trump walked back his overnight 'largely negotiated' optimism in afternoon remarks to reporters on Day 86. He simultaneously confirmed the White House would not announce a deal that day. The shift reflected unresolved disputes over HEU disposal — a top Iranian official told Fox News there was 'no agreement' on surrendering enriched uranium — and Iran's insistence on retaining Hormuz 'management rights.' Trump's 'disjointed leadership' remark acknowledged the structural challenge of getting Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, President Pezeshkian, and the IRGC aligned on deal terms.
statements.venue: White House — Remarks to reporters, Day 86
statements.factCheckNote: Trump 'not in a rush' and 'time is on our side' quotes confirmed by NBC News, Al Jazeera liveblog May 24. 'Disjointed leadership' quote confirmed by OSINTtechnical X post via NBC. White House 'not signed today' confirmed by CNN live updates.
statements.source: NBC News — Trump says he won't 'rush into' a deal with Iran as hopes of imminent agreement cool

statements.text: Negotiations with Iran are proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner. Iran must understand, however, that they cannot develop or procure a Nuclear Weapon or Bomb. The deal will either be a great and meaningful one, or there will be no deal. I have spoken with the Prime Minister of Israel, Benjamin Netanyahu (went very well!) as well as Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan and Bahrain.
statements.context: Day 87 Truth Social post as Iran's delegation arrived in Doha for Qatar-mediated talks. Trump's 'great deal or no deal' ultimatum eliminates the middle-ground MOU-deferral option while also signaling talks remain actively engaged. The list of leaders called underscores the regional scope of his diplomatic engagement.
statements.factCheckNote: Truth Social post confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 25 and CNBC. Regional leader calls confirmed by White House readouts.

statements.text: In Operation Epic Fury, we lost 13 wonderful souls, wonderful special people. These incredible men and women gave their lives to ensure that the world's number one state sponsor of terror will never have a nuclear weapon. Oh, and they won't. They will never have a nuclear weapon.
statements.context: Memorial Day speech at Arlington National Cemetery, honoring the 13 US service members killed in Operation Epic Fury. Trump's framing positioned the deaths as purposeful and the war's goal as achieved in principle — even as Iran still holds enriched uranium and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed on Day 84. Trump simultaneously demanded that Egypt, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia normalize ties with Israel as part of any Iran settlement.
statements.venue: Arlington National Cemetery — Memorial Day, Day 87
statements.factCheckNote: Arlington speech confirmed by Fox News Digital and ABC News live updates May 25. Abraham Accords expansion demand confirmed by NPR May 25.
statements.source: Fox News — Iran war news Trump Hormuz ceasefire peace deal May 25

statements.text: The United States naval blockade on Iranian ships and ports will remain in full force and effect until an agreement is reached, certified, and signed. Iran must understand that they cannot develop or procure a Nuclear Weapon or Bomb. Time is on our side.
statements.context: Day 87 — Trump posted to Truth Social walking back Saturday's 'largely negotiated' deal optimism, making clear the blockade (Hormuz Day 84) stays until a deal is fully formalized. He also told his negotiators 'not to rush into a deal,' signaling he had overruled any premature announcement.
statements.venue: Truth Social — Day 87/88, Memorial Day weekend
statements.factCheckNote: Truth Social posts confirmed by NPR, Al Jazeera Day 87 summary, and CNBC May 25.
statements.source: NPR — US Iran negotiations updates May 25

statements.text: Negotiations with the Islamic Republic of Iran are proceeding nicely! It will only be a Great Deal for all or, no Deal at all — Back to the Battlefront and shooting, but bigger and stronger than ever before — And nobody wants that! After all the work done by the United States to try and pull this very complex puzzle together, it should be mandatory that all of these Countries, at a minimum, simultaneously, sign onto the Abraham Accords.
statements.context: Day 88 — Trump posted to Truth Social an upbeat negotiation update alongside a new demand: all Muslim nations involved in the Iran deal (Egypt, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia) must simultaneously sign the Abraham Accords normalizing ties with Israel. Trump added that Iran itself could sign the Accords, calling it 'an Honor.' The new condition came hours after CENTCOM conducted self-defense strikes near Bandar Abbas and drew sharp criticism from deal partners and US Republicans.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, CNN, Washington Post (May 26). Abraham Accords condition is new — not in original ceasefire framework.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Trump says mandatory for Muslim nations in Iran deal to join Abraham Accords

statements.text: The Blockade will remain in full force and effect until an agreement is reached, certified, and signed. We are working on it — The deal is not 'largely negotiated' but we are getting there. Iran knows what has to happen.
statements.context: Day 89 — Trump walked back his previous 'largely negotiated' claim less than 24 hours after making it. The reversal confused US allies and deal partners and was widely covered as a sign of disorganization in the US diplomatic effort. Iranian state media cited the walkback as evidence the US was acting in 'bad faith.' Doha MOU talks continued despite the confusion.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump walkback confirmed by NBC News (May 27). Iranian dismissal confirmed by Fars News Agency.
statements.source: NBC News — Deal or no deal? Trump's social media posts add confusion to Iran conflict

statements.text: Both sides are close to finalizing an agreement involving strong inspections of Iran's nuclear program.
statements.context: Day 89 — Trump told reporters on May 27 that the US and Iran were close to finalizing a deal with robust nuclear inspections — his most optimistic public statement on the negotiations yet. The claim was immediately rejected by Iranian Supreme Leader advisor Ali Shamkhani as a 'fantasy,' reflecting the core gap between US demands for unprecedented nuclear access and Iranian red lines on sovereignty. Cabinet meeting was moved from Camp David to the White House citing weather.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump statement confirmed by CBS News liveblog (May 27). Shamkhani 'fantasy' response confirmed by same source.
statements.source: CBS News liveblog — Iran war Trump US strikes peace talks ceasefire

statements.text: The Iranian regime is the single greatest state sponsor of terrorism in the world and must be confronted.
statements.context: As a senator on the Foreign Relations Committee, Rubio delivered a floor speech advocating for stronger measures against Iran, aligning with his long-standing hawkish position.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. State Department does designate Iran as a state sponsor of terrorism, the characterization of 'single greatest' is a subjective ranking. Iran's role is significant but other state actors also contribute to global instability.

statements.text: Any deal with Iran that does not address ballistic missiles and regional aggression is worse than no deal at all.
statements.context: Rubio criticized ongoing European diplomatic efforts to revive nuclear talks with Iran, arguing that a narrow focus on nuclear enrichment ignored larger security threats.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a policy opinion rather than a factual claim. Nonproliferation experts are divided on whether linking missile programs to nuclear talks helps or hinders diplomatic progress.

statements.text: Iran's proxies have American blood on their hands. Hezbollah, Hamas, the Houthis — all roads lead back to Tehran.
statements.context: Following attacks on U.S. military positions in Syria and Iraq by Iran-backed militias, Rubio called for direct retaliation against Iranian assets.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran provides support to these groups, each operates with varying degrees of autonomy. Characterizing all proxy actions as directly ordered by Tehran oversimplifies complex regional dynamics. Responsibility for specific attacks requires individual attribution.

statements.text: As Secretary of State, I will ensure that America's adversaries understand that the era of appeasement is over.
statements.context: In his first major address after confirmation as Secretary of State, Rubio signaled a dramatic shift in diplomatic posture, explicitly naming Iran, China, and Russia.
statements.factCheckDetail: The characterization of previous administrations' policies as 'appeasement' is a political framing. The JCPOA and related diplomatic efforts are viewed by their architects as strategic engagement, not appeasement.

statements.text: We have exhausted every diplomatic avenue with Iran. They have chosen escalation at every turn.
statements.context: Rubio defended the administration's decision to end all back-channel communications with Iran, a move criticized by career diplomats as premature.
statements.factCheckDetail: Multiple diplomatic channels remained available but were not pursued. European allies and Oman had offered to mediate, and Iran's foreign ministry had signaled openness to preliminary discussions. The claim of exhausting every avenue is contradicted by these documented opportunities.

statements.text: Israel has an inherent right to self-defense. We will not second-guess our ally when it faces an existential threat.
statements.context: Responding to IAEA reports of Iran reaching near-weapons-grade enrichment levels, Rubio preemptively endorsed potential Israeli military action.
statements.factCheckDetail: Israel does have a right to self-defense under Article 51 of the UN Charter. However, legal scholars debate whether preemptive strikes against nuclear facilities qualify under this provision without an imminent armed attack.

statements.text: This administration provided intelligence support to Israel as any ally would. We did not plan or direct these operations.
statements.context: After Israeli strikes began, Rubio clarified the U.S. role, attempting to distinguish between intelligence sharing and direct operational involvement amid international criticism.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. may not have directly planned strike packages, extensive intelligence sharing, refueling support for Israeli aircraft, and satellite data constitute significant operational enablement that goes beyond routine allied cooperation.

statements.text: We are urging all parties to exercise restraint while maintaining our ironclad commitment to Israel's security.
statements.context: Rubio's statement attempted to thread the needle between calls for de-escalation from the international community and continued support for Israeli operations, drawing criticism for being contradictory.
statements.factCheckDetail: Critics pointed out the contradiction between calling for restraint while simultaneously authorizing emergency weapons shipments to Israel. The U.S. also vetoed a ceasefire resolution at the same Security Council session.

statements.text: Ordered all embassies to urge allies to pressure Iran 'at highest level' by March 20. Iran 'more sensitive to collective action.'
statements.context: Diplomatic cable
statements.factCheckNote: ABC News obtained cable

statements.text: We assess that the Iranian regime is more sensitive to collective action than unilateral action... We must act while international attention is focused now.
statements.context: Cable to all US diplomatic posts with action deadline of March 20, directing ambassadors to push host governments to designate IRGC and Hezbollah as terrorist organizations. Part of broader diplomatic pressure campaign.
statements.venue: State Department Cable to all diplomatic posts
statements.factCheckNote: Internal diplomatic communications — factual assessment not a public claim.

statements.text: I will travel to Cernay-la-Ville, France this Friday for the G7 Foreign Ministers meeting to coordinate with our allies on the path forward in the Middle East. The diplomatic track is open and active. We are pursuing every avenue to resolve this conflict consistent with the security interests of the United States and Israel.
statements.context: State Department announcement of G7 travel; also participating in Iran back-channel talks per Trump
statements.factCheckNote: G7 foreign ministers meeting confirmed. Rubio participation in Iran talks per Trump statement at Tennessee roundtable.

statements.text: There are intermediary countries that are passing messages and progress has been made — some concrete progress has been made. It is an ongoing and fluid process.
