statements.context: Statement to reporters before departing for G7 foreign ministers meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay, France. Declined to name which countries or the specific nature of progress. Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar simultaneously confirmed on the record that Pakistan is the primary intermediary.
statements.venue: Press remarks en route to G7
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by State Department releases and CNN live blog coverage of the G7.

statements.text: Frankly, I think countries around the world, even those that are out there complaining about this a little bit, should actually be grateful that the United States has a president that's willing to confront a threat like this.
statements.context: Said at G7 foreign ministers meeting at Vaux-de-Cernay Abbey, France, in response to European criticism of the war.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: Reflects US framing that Iran posed existential nuclear threat. European allies dispute this characterization and were not consulted before strikes began.
statements.source: NPR — Rubio heads to Europe to try garner support for Iran war

statements.text: We expect the conflict to end in 2-4 more weeks. Our objectives are achievable without any ground troops. We have had an exchange of messages and indications from the Iranian system about a willingness to talk about certain things.
statements.context: G7 meeting in France. Rubio sparred with European allies, defending the war strategy. Germany called it a 'catastrophe.' France said allies were surprised by US military unilateralism. Rubio revealed Iran officials want negotiations but fear being located and assassinated. Separately, confirmed US takes Russia at its word regarding Kallas's intelligence-sharing claim.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, Bloomberg, NPR. The '2-4 weeks' timeline extends the initial 4-6 week estimate. Rubio's claim of objectives achievable without ground troops contradicts WSJ reporting on 10,000-troop deployment planning.

statements.text: The war is on or ahead of schedule and will end in a matter of weeks, not months. The United States can achieve its objectives without any ground troops. I am pushing allies on a postwar plan to stop Iran from setting up a tolling system in the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Press conference at G7 Foreign Ministers meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: Rubio's 'weeks not months' claim cannot be independently verified. Simultaneously Trump said the US has 'thousands of targets left' — conflicting signals. G7 allies expressed skepticism.
statements.source: Times Live — Rubio says Iran war to last weeks not months

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz will reopen one way or another — either Iran agrees to reopen it, or a coalition will force it open. There are fractures inside the Islamic Republic. People there are talking to us in ways that previous people in charge have not spoken to us. We are talking to people who have the power to deliver.
statements.context: Exclusive interview with Al Jazeera and simultaneously ABC Good Morning America on March 30. Most explicit US statement that the Strait will be forced open if necessary. Also signals diplomatic opening with Iranian figures who may have authority to deliver a deal.
statements.venue: Al Jazeera exclusive interview + ABC Good Morning America
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by State Department transcript. Al Jazeera interview confirmed. ABC GMA interview confirmed. Key diplomatic signal alongside Trump's maximum pressure.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Rubio tells Al Jazeera Strait of Hormuz will reopen one way or another

statements.text: The objectives are achievable in weeks, not months. We don't need ground troops. The Strait of Hormuz will reopen one way or another. Would we be heartbroken if there was a change in leadership? Absolutely not.
statements.context: Al Jazeera interview — confirmed backchannel communication with Iran through intermediaries; criticized NATO allies for refusing US base access
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera interview aired. State Dept published official transcript.

statements.text: Communication with Tehran is ongoing through intermediaries. US war objectives will be achieved in weeks, not months. Fractures have emerged in Iran's leadership.
statements.context: Al Jazeera interview (March 31) and ABC GMA (March 30). Refused to identify who US is engaging with, citing safety concerns.
statements.venue: Al Jazeera interview / ABC Good Morning America
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Washington Examiner.

statements.text: We are going to achieve our objectives in a matter of weeks, not months. We can see the finish line. There are messages being exchanged with Iran and potential for a direct meeting at some point. We will have to reexamine our relationship with NATO once the war concludes.
statements.context: PBS interview. Warned NATO reassessment. Claimed US could achieve goals without ground troops despite Marine deployments.
statements.factCheckNote: PBS, CNN confirmed. NATO reassessment warning notable escalation in allied tensions.

statements.text: We can see the finish line. It's not today, it's not tomorrow, but it is coming. We would not be heartbroken by regime change, but it is not our stated objective.
statements.context: Fox News interview identifying four war goals: destruction of air force, navy, factories, and missile capability
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Times confirmed interview.

statements.text: The United States is going to have to reexamine its relationship with NATO once the war against Iran has concluded. Ultimately that will be a decision for President Trump.
statements.context: Echoing Trump's NATO withdrawal threats over allies' refusal to support the Iran war
statements.factCheckNote: Euronews and Spokesman-Review confirmed.

statements.text: We can see the finish line. It's not today, it's not tomorrow, but it is coming. There are messages being exchanged between Iran and the US, and there is potential for a direct meeting at some point.
statements.context: Fox News Hannity interview, confirming diplomatic exchanges with Iran
statements.factCheckNote: Iran FM Araghchi confirmed receiving messages from US envoy Witkoff but denied formal negotiations.

statements.text: I have revoked the green card of Hamideh Soleimani Afshar, niece of Qasem Soleimani, an outspoken supporter of the Iranian terrorist regime. She and her daughter have been taken into ICE custody.
statements.context: Rubio revoked residency of Soleimani relatives, continuing campaign against Iran-connected individuals in the US
statements.factCheckNote: The Hill, Al Jazeera, RedState confirmed the action.

statements.text: The Trump Administration will not allow our country to become a home for foreign nationals who support anti-American terrorist regimes. This is just the beginning.
statements.context: Follow-up statement after arresting Soleimani family members. Rubio framed it as part of broader crackdown on Iranian regime supporters in the US during wartime.
statements.factCheckNote: RedState, Mediaite confirmed full statement.

statements.text: We can see the finish line. It's not today, it's not tomorrow, but it is coming.
statements.context: Fox News interview. Called the war "a favor" to the US and the world. Identified degradation of Iran's missile capability as primary war objective.
statements.factCheckNote: Characterization of war progress is subjective.

statements.text: Iran is violating every law known by striking commercial vessels. It's a state sponsor of terrorism conducting terrorist operations against commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Press conference accusing Iran of terrorism for shipping attacks.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by SocialNews.xyz and Epoch Times.

statements.text: We are relieved that this American is now freed. The whole world has been impacted because Iran is violating every law known by striking commercial vessels in the Straits of Hormuz.
statements.context: Confirmed release of journalist Shelly Kittleson from Iraq.
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Post, ABC7 confirmed.

statements.text: I hope there will be an update on negotiations later today.
statements.context: Statement on ceasefire talks progress
statements.factCheckNote: ABC7 confirmed statement.

statements.text: We are relieved that this American is now free. I hope to have more news later today on Iran negotiations.
statements.context: Confirming release of American journalist Shelley Kittleson from Iraq and upcoming Iran negotiations
statements.factCheckNote: ABC7 confirmed statement.

statements.text: Whether Iran's supreme leader ordered weaponization doesn't matter — the regime was already pursuing a nuclear weapon. We have the intelligence and the evidence.
statements.context: Rubio sparred with CBS host Margaret Brennan on Face the Nation over US intelligence on Iran's nuclear program. Brennan pressed on whether intelligence showed Mojtaba Khamenei ordered weaponization.
statements.factCheckNote: US intelligence community has not publicly confirmed Iran ordered weaponization. Rubio's framing disputed by host.

statements.text: This is about bringing a permanent answer to 20 or 30 years of Hezbollah's influence in Lebanon. Both sides have agreed to hold further direct talks on a peace agreement.
statements.context: Rubio hosted historic Israel-Lebanon trilateral talks at State Department — first direct meeting since 1993. Israeli Ambassador Leiter and Lebanese Ambassador Moawad attended.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Axios, State Department official statement.

statements.text: This is a historic opportunity. This is a process, not an event.
statements.context: Continuing Israel-Lebanon diplomatic talks. State Dept praised 'productive discussions on steps toward launching direct negotiations between Israel and Lebanon.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill, UPI.

statements.text: Progress has been made. There's a growing amount of energy that's been flowing through the straits. Not as much as should be flowing, but some of it has picked up.
statements.context: At Joint Base Andrews, expressing confidence in reopened negotiations with Iran. US sent draft peace proposal to Tehran. Will chair Israel-Lebanon talks Thursday at State Department with Ambassadors Huckabee and Issa.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of 'growing energy flow' is misleading — only 16 ships transited Hormuz April 21, near-total standstill. Confirmed by The Hill.

statements.text: The president wanted to be personally involved and glad he was, because it made it possible to get this extension, and it gives everybody time to continue to work on what's going to be permanent peace between two countries that want to be in peace.
statements.context: After White House meeting on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote confirmed by CNN.

statements.text: We have not forbidden Iran's participation in the World Cup.
statements.context: Denying reports the administration asked the Iranian team not to come to the 2026 World Cup.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN. FIFA also confirmed Iran will participate.

statements.text: Iranian officials are saying the right things in private. We have to see if these people end up being the ones in charge. President Trump's involvement made the Lebanon ceasefire extension possible.
statements.context: Signaled progress in back-channel Iran talks despite defiant public posturing from Tehran.
statements.factCheckNote: The Hill, Times of Israel.

statements.text: The problem with Iran would be not their athletes. It would be some of the other people they would want to bring with them, some of whom have ties to the IRGC. They can't bring a bunch of IRGC terrorists into our country and pretend that they are journalists and athletic trainers.
statements.context: Alongside Trump, addressed Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup. Said Iranian athletes are welcome but anyone with IRGC ties will be barred. Dismissed Italian replacement proposal as 'pure speculation.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The National, Jerusalem Post, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: The closure of Hormuz is not a negotiating tactic — it is an act of economic warfare against the world. Iran's deputy speaker just admitted the Supreme Leader's office is now setting maritime policy. That tells us everything.
statements.context: State Department briefing reacting to Iranian Deputy Speaker Nikzad's declaration that Hormuz will not return to pre-war state, framing the closure as broader economic aggression.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement consistent with Nikzad's public declaration cited by Iranian state media.

statements.text: If what they mean by opening the straits is, 'yes, the straits are opened, as long as you coordinate with Iran, get our permission, or we will blow you up and you pay us' — that's not opening the straits. We have to ensure that any deal that is made, any agreement that is made, is one that definitively prevents them from sprinting towards a nuclear weapon at any point.
statements.context: Responding to Iran's new proposal to reopen Hormuz while deferring nuclear talks. Rubio acknowledged the proposal 'is better than what we thought they were going to submit' but insisted nuclear issues cannot be separated. Also said the US is 'aware' of Israel's strikes on Lebanon and urged Israel 'to make sure their responses are proportional and targeted.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, Times of Israel, World Oil.

statements.text: What Iran is doing in Hormuz is the use of an economic nuclear weapon. We will not legitimize the practice.
statements.context: Public characterization of Iran's Hormuz blockade in Apr 28 remarks following Iran's conditional offer; reinforces administration position that any deal must address the nuclear program directly. Quote distributed by Russian state outlet RT and confirmed in Al Jazeera analysis of US response.
statements.factCheckNote: Quote reported by RT and corroborated by Al Jazeera analysis of Iran's proposal.

statements.text: Iran's latest proposal is better than what we thought they were going to submit. But we will reject any deal that doesn't prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons. Iran would not be allowed to charge Strait of Hormuz tolls.
statements.context: Rubio acknowledged progress in Iran's proposal but maintained red lines on nuclear issue and Hormuz tolls. Comments made alongside Trump's 'state of collapse' Truth Social post.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News live coverage, Al Jazeera analysis.

statements.text: The question between alive and in power are two different questions. We have indications that he is [alive]. Obviously they claim that he is. We don't have evidence that he's not. But does he have the clerical credentials to actually act as supreme leader?
statements.context: Rubio publicly questioned Mojtaba Khamenei's legitimacy and authority on Fox News. Also questioned whether Iran's Hormuz proposal was submitted by someone with authority to do so.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Arabiya, The Hill, ANI News.

statements.text: They cannot normalize, nor can we tolerate them trying to normalize, a system in which the Iranians decide who gets to use an international waterway and how much you have to pay them to use it. The other thing is, of course, the country's run by radical Shia clerics — that's a pretty big impediment.
statements.context: Rubio rejects Iran's Hormuz proposal, which would have Iran levy 'transit fees' on tankers. Confirms US has 'indications' Mojtaba Khamenei is alive.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill, Bloomberg.

statements.text: If the radical clerical regime remains in charge, they will decide they want a nuclear weapon. That fundamental issue still has to be confronted.
statements.context: Rubio Fox News interview May 1 morning, dismissing Iran's Pakistan-mediated peace proposal and reiterating that regime change remains a precondition for sustainable nuclear non-proliferation.
statements.factCheckNote: ABC News confirmed Rubio Fox News interview content.

statements.text: What they mean by opening the straits is, yes, the straits are open, as long as you coordinate with Iran, get our permission, or we'll blow you up, and you pay us. The nuclear question is the reason why we're in this in the first place.
statements.context: Rubio directly rejected Iran's latest peace proposal terms. Dismissed Iran's Hormuz reopening offer as a protection racket. Insisted nuclear concessions must come first before any deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill.

statements.text: An emergency exists which requires the immediate sale of defense articles. This $8.6 billion in sales to Qatar, Kuwait, Israel, and UAE is critical to the security of our allies in the region.
statements.context: Emergency declaration bypassing Congressional review for arms sales under Arms Export Control Act.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Bloomberg, The Hill.

statements.text: The nuclear question is the reason why we're in this in the first place. Yes, the straits are open, as long as you coordinate with Iran, get our permission, or we'll blow you up, and you pay us. We question whether the person submitting it had the authority to submit that offer.
statements.context: Dismissing Iran's 14-point peace proposal. Insisted nuclear concessions are prerequisite. Mocked Iran's Hormuz reopening terms.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill, Times of Israel, ABC News.

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury is over. There's no shooting unless we're shot at first.
statements.context: Rubio formally declared the main US combat operation against Iran has ended. US shifts to defensive posture with Project Freedom and naval blockade.
statements.factCheckNote: TIME, ABC News, CNN all confirmed Rubio's declaration.

statements.text: A peace deal between Lebanon and Israel is imminently achievable. The problem with Israel and Lebanon is not Israel or Lebanon, it's Hezbollah.
statements.context: Rubio expressed optimism about a Lebanon-Israel peace deal while blaming Hezbollah for blocking progress.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post confirmed the statement.

statements.text: Iran has a choice: a real diplomatic path leading to reconstruction, prosperity and stability, or growing isolation, economic collapse, and ultimately total defeat.
statements.context: Rubio offered Iran a stark binary at the State Department briefing as the 14-point MOU is being finalized. Framed alternatives as inevitable consequences of Iran's choice.
statements.factCheckNote: Mediaite confirmed the statement.

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury is concluded. We achieved the objectives of that operation. We would prefer the path of peace. What the president would prefer is a deal.
statements.context: Rubio formally declared the main US combat operation in Iran over during CNN interview. Said the US would not fire at Iran 'unless we're shot at.' Noted 10 civilian sailors killed in Hormuz violence.
statements.venue: CNN Interview / White House Press Briefing
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, TIME, KCCU/NPR.

statements.text: The top people in that government are, to say the least, you know, they're insane in the brain.
statements.context: Rubio filling in for Karoline Leavitt (on maternity leave) at the White House press briefing. The remark about Iranian leaders drew attention for its casual tone during an active diplomatic process.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill.

statements.text: We had a frank conversation about our disagreements on the war. The United States remains committed to achieving a deal that ensures Iran never acquires nuclear weapons.
statements.context: Rubio met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican for 45 minutes. Pope gave him an olive-wood pen as peace symbol. Meeting came amid escalating tensions between Trump administration and Vatican over Iran war.
statements.venue: Vatican — Meeting with Pope Leo XIV
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, NPR.

statements.text: We expect a response from Iran today. We'll see what the response entails. The hope is it's something that can put us into a serious process in negotiation.
statements.context: Speaking in Rome following Vatican meeting with Pope Leo XIV. Also warned Europe to prepare more than 'strongly worded statements' against Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, Townhall.

statements.text: We are continuing to explore every avenue for a resolution. Qatar's role as a mediator has been constructive.
statements.context: Met with Qatari PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani and special envoy Witkoff in Miami. Qatar is quietly mediating alongside Pakistan's official mediator role.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios.

statements.text: The War Powers Act is 100 percent unconstitutional. Every single president since it was passed has held the same position. We don't acknowledge it as constitutional, but we comply with elements of it for purposes of maintaining good relations with Congress.
statements.context: White House press briefing responding to the Senate war powers vote, where three Republicans joined Democrats in a 50-49 vote — the closest attempt yet but still falling short. Rubio confirmed he has briefed Congress four times on the Iran war.
statements.factCheckNote: Rubio's claim that every president has shared this position is an overstatement — executive branch objections to the WPR have varied widely. Confirmed by The Hill.

statements.text: The Chinese side said they are not in favor of militarizing the Straits of Hormuz, and they're not in favor of a tolling system, and that's our position. We hope to convince them to play a more active role in getting Iran to walk away from what they're doing now in the Persian Gulf.
statements.context: NBC News interview following the Trump-Xi Beijing summit. Rubio confirmed both sides agreed on freedom of navigation — the US was not asking China to help with Iran, but sought Beijing to pressure Tehran to abandon the Hormuz closure. He also noted NATO allies who denied US base access raised questions about alliance purpose.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News and The Hill.

statements.text: He didn't ask them for anything. Every U.S. president over the past two decades has viewed Iran's nuclear program as a major threat — that's not about doing Israel a favor, that's about consistent US policy across administrations.
statements.context: Rubio pushed back on claims Trump attacked Iran solely for Israel's benefit, and clarified that Trump did not formally ask Xi for help with Iran at the summit. He argued US policy toward Tehran has remained consistent regardless of who is in the White House. Rubio also confirmed US and China both agree Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon.
statements.venue: NBC News / Press remarks — Beijing departure
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News, JNS, and Deseret News reporting from the Beijing summit.

statements.text: Iran's leaders are insane in the brain if they think they can test President Trump on this. They need to make a deal — and they know it.
statements.context: Rubio warned Iran's leaders not to miscalculate Trump's resolve as tensions remained high over the Strait of Hormuz and the May 17 ceasefire expiry loomed. Rubio also acknowledged an internal disagreement with Hegseth over the ceasefire status — Rubio declared 'Operation Epic Fury is over' while Hegseth insisted the operation had not ended.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill live updates, May 15, 2026.

statements.text: The Straits have to be reopened. That is non-negotiable. Iran attempting to use US politics — specifically rising energy prices — will not pressure President Trump and lead him into a 'bad' peace deal. If Iran wants a civilian nuclear program, there are countries that have that and don't enrich uranium but import the enriched material — Iran could have that if that's what they wanted.
statements.context: Rubio gave a full interview to NBC's Tom Llamas during the US-China summit in Beijing, outlining US red lines on Iran. He clarified the US position: Iran may have civilian nuclear power but cannot enrich uranium domestically.
statements.venue: NBC News Interview with Tom Llamas — Beijing, China
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News full transcript and The Hill May 15-16, 2026.

statements.text: Iran's leaders are insane in the brain. They should not test President Trump.
statements.context: Rubio warned Iran's leadership not to test Trump as tensions rose over the Strait of Hormuz and the ceasefire frayed. The comment came as Iran resumed strikes on UAE Gulf states and finalized the PGSA Hormuz toll mechanism, while the US-Iran nuclear talks remained deadlocked.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill live Iran war updates.

statements.text: We're not going to let Iran use that as leverage. If the Iranians think that they are going to use our domestic politics to pressure him into a bad deal, that's not going to happen. The red line is clear. They threaten Americans, they are going to be blown up.
statements.context: Rubio pushed back in NBC News interview on fears that Trump might accept a weak deal due to US consumer concern over gas prices. He reiterated that Iran should not test Trump and confirmed the US-Iran talks remain far apart. Trump had publicly dismissed concerns about gas prices as a factor in Iran negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News transcript and The Hill. 'Insane in the brain' comment from May 5 White House briefing cited as consistent pattern.

statements.text: There will be a dramatic reduction of energy prices once the Strait of Hormuz is reopened — but there's a price attached to a nuclear Iran. We're not going to let Iran use rising energy prices as leverage to get a bad deal.
statements.context: Rubio emphasized that domestic US energy price pressure from the Hormuz blockade would not weaken Trump's negotiating position. Statement made as Gulf-state mediators secured a 48–72 hour pause on the planned US strike on Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill May 19. Context: gas $4.515/gal nationally; oil $107-108/bbl.
statements.source: The Hill — Trump, Rubio say Iran negotiations ongoing

statements.text: Iran's 'insane in the brain' leaders should not test President Trump as tensions rise in the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Rubio's sharp warning to Iranian leadership as Trump's 2-3 day ultimatum window was set by Gulf-state mediators. Rubio has consistently emphasized Iran must not miscalculate Trump's resolve to resume military strikes if no deal is reached.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill live updates May 20. Phrase 'insane in the brain' is Rubio's own characterization of Iranian leadership.
statements.source: The Hill live updates — Iran war, Trump, Hegseth, midterms

statements.text: The Maritime Freedom Construct is not a military operation — it is a framework for information-sharing, diplomatic engagement, and economic coordination among allies to address the Hormuz crisis. NATO's role is to support the international framework, not to become a co-belligerent.
statements.context: Rubio attended the NATO foreign ministers' meeting in Helsingborg, Sweden, where the closed Strait of Hormuz dominated the agenda. He promoted an information-sharing and pressure approach rather than NATO offensive operations.
statements.venue: NATO Foreign Ministers Meeting, Helsingborg, Sweden
statements.factCheckNote: Rubio NATO Sweden meeting confirmed by The National, Stars and Stripes, Military.com, May 21.
statements.source: The National — Strait of Hormuz looms large as NATO ministers gather in Sweden

statements.text: There are some good signs, but I don't want to be overly optimistic as well, so let's see what happens over the next few days. If no good deal is reached, the president's been clear he has other options.
statements.context: Rubio offered cautious optimism on the state of US-Iran talks, while maintaining that military options remain on the table. He confirmed Pakistani officials were traveling to Tehran and expressed hope the visit would propel diplomatic efforts forward.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog, ANI News, and Times of Israel liveblog, May 21.
statements.source: ANI News — 'There are some good signs,' US cautious but hopeful on potential Iran deal

statements.text: No one in the world is in favor of the tolling system. It can't happen. It would be unacceptable. It would make a diplomatic deal unfeasible if they were to continue to pursue that. We also want China to take a more active role in helping to resolve this conflict.
statements.context: Rubio explicitly rejected Iran's proposal to charge tolls on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a dealbreaker. He simultaneously urged China to play a more active diplomatic role ahead of the Trump-Xi summit.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by The National, Times of Israel, and Arab News, May 21-22, 2026.
statements.source: The National — Rubio says toll system in Strait of Hormuz would make deal unfeasible

statements.text: I think we've made some progress, but obviously we're dealing with a system that itself is a little fractured. There's been a little bit of movement. I don't want to exaggerate it. You have countries like Spain denying us the use of bases — well then why are you in NATO? That's a very fair question.
statements.context: Rubio spoke at the NATO foreign ministers meeting in Sweden, offering a measured update on Iran talks ('little bit of movement') while criticizing NATO allies who denied base access during Operation Epic Fury. He said Trump is 'very disappointed' in those allies. His 'fractured system' reference pointed to internal divisions within Iran between hardliners (Mojtaba Khamenei/IRGC) and the Pezeshkian government.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by CBS News Iran live updates and RFERL May 22. 'Fractured system' reference and NATO disappointment confirmed by US News and Al-Monitor.
statements.source: CBS News — Iran war live updates: US-Iran peace talks

statements.text: We have to have a plan B for if someone is shooting. The alliance must consider a collective military mission in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's toll system on the Strait is not acceptable — it would make any deal unfeasible. Pakistan is doing an admirable job mediating.
statements.context: Rubio warned NATO foreign ministers at the Sweden meeting to prepare a military 'Plan B' if Iran refuses to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. He simultaneously confirmed 'slight progress' in Iran talks but declined to 'exaggerate' it. His dual message — diplomatic progress + military contingency planning — reflects the administration's 'maximum pressure' posture as the most critical negotiating phase begins.
statements.venue: NATO Foreign Ministers Meeting, Ankatsberg, Sweden
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by France 24, The National, Tribune India, Stars and Stripes. Rubio's 'slight progress' and Pakistan praise confirmed by Washington Post, PBS, Al Jazeera.
statements.source: France 24 — NATO allies sound out US top diplomat after Trump Iran ire

statements.text: There's been a little bit of movement, and that's good. But I don't want to exaggerate it — there is still a significant gap. Iran's proposed tolls on the Strait of Hormuz are not acceptable. We have to have a Plan B if someone is shooting.
statements.context: Rubio at the NATO foreign ministers meeting in Helsingborg, Sweden, on May 22-23. He said the fifth round of Rome talks showed "slight progress" but cautioned against optimism, citing Iran's toll plan and enrichment stance as non-starters. He also warned NATO allies to prepare a collective military "Plan B" for Hormuz if Iran refuses to reopen it.
statements.venue: NATO Foreign Ministers Meeting, Helsingborg, Sweden
statements.factCheckNote: Rubio quote confirmed by Washington Post, The Hill, PBS NewsHour, Times of Israel.
statements.source: Washington Post — US says slight progress in Iran talks

statements.text: There's been some progress done, some progress made. There is a chance that — whether it's later today, tomorrow, in a couple days — we may have something to say. This problem will be solved, as the president's made clear, one way or the other. We hope it's done through the diplomatic route.
statements.context: Rubio speaking to reporters from New Delhi, India, on Day 85. He confirmed diplomatic progress and said there was 'a chance Iran accepts a deal as soon as Saturday.' He reiterated US demands: Hormuz free and open without tolls, no Iranian nuclear weapon, enriched uranium handed over. He also confirmed that 'as I speak to you now, there's some work being done.'
statements.factCheckNote: Quote confirmed by RTE News, Times of Israel liveblog, Al Arabiya. Rubio was in New Delhi for a bilateral meeting with Indian officials when he made these remarks on May 23.
statements.source: RTE News — Rubio says 'chance' of Iran accepting deal as soon as Saturday

statements.text: Significant progress has been made. Not final progress, but significant progress. I do think perhaps there is the possibility that in the next few hours the world will get some good news. The ultimate goal is they never have a nuclear weapon. This problem will be solved, one way or the other.
statements.context: Rubio expanded on US-Iran progress during press remarks in New Delhi on Day 86, hinting specifically at an imminent Hormuz announcement. He restated Trump's three non-negotiable conditions: no nuclear weapon for Iran, Hormuz opens without tolls, Iran surrenders enriched uranium. The 'good news' language was widely interpreted as signaling an imminent deal announcement.
statements.venue: Press remarks, New Delhi, India — Day 86
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, ANI News India, and IANS Live. Rubio was in New Delhi for bilateral meetings while negotiations continued in parallel tracks.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Rubio says significant progress in US-Iran talks

statements.text: We're either going to have a good agreement, or we're going to have to deal with it another way. We'd prefer to have a good agreement. We have what I think is a pretty solid thing on the table in terms of their ability to open up the straits, get the straits open.
statements.context: Day 87 — Rubio, continuing his diplomatic visit to New Delhi, issued the clearest US military threat since Operation Epic Fury ended. The 'another way' phrase was understood as referring to resumed airstrikes.
statements.venue: Press remarks to reporters, New Delhi, India — Day 87
statements.factCheckNote: Quote confirmed verbatim by Al Jazeera, US News, and CNN live updates May 25.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Rubio says US will find 'another way' if Iran talks fail

statements.text: There were some talks going on in Qatar today, so we'll see if we can make progress. I think it's a lot of talking back and forth going on about specific language in the initial document, so it'll take a few days.
statements.context: Day 88 — Rubio, speaking from Jaipur, India during his diplomatic visit, tempered expectations on a quick Iran deal. His remarks came despite — or perhaps because of — the CENTCOM self-defense strikes near Bandar Abbas the previous night. The 'few days' framing was seen as damage control after Trump's earlier 'largely negotiated' optimism, providing space for the Doha MOU talks to continue.
statements.venue: Press remarks to reporters, Jaipur, India — Day 88
statements.factCheckNote: Quote confirmed by Al Jazeera and Irish Times (May 26). Rubio confirmed to be in Jaipur, India as part of Asia diplomatic tour.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Rubio says Iran deal could take days after US forces launch new attacks

statements.text: The straits have to be open. They're going to be open one way or the other. Iran's tolling system for the Strait of Hormuz is unlawful — it's illegal. It's unsustainable for the world; it's unacceptable.
statements.context: Day 88 — In separate remarks to the press, Rubio drew a hard line on Hormuz, making clear that US acceptance of Iranian control or tolling of the strait was not on the table in any deal. This statement directly contradicted Iran's position that the strait falls under Iranian sovereignty.
statements.factCheckNote: Quote confirmed by State Dept transcript (May 26). Iran rejected Rubio's framing as 'interference in Iranian sovereignty' per PressTV.
statements.source: State Dept — Secretary of State Marco Rubio Remarks to Press

statements.text: Our military is the most powerful force in history. Any adversary who tests us will regret it.
statements.context: In his first Pentagon press briefing, Hegseth emphasized military readiness and deterrence, setting a combative tone for his tenure.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. military is widely considered the world's most capable, the characterization of 'most powerful in history' is a subjective claim. Readiness assessments from the GAO have identified specific capability gaps.

statements.text: Iran's air defense systems are outdated Soviet-era relics. They would not survive a serious engagement.
statements.context: In an interview, Hegseth appeared to dismiss Iranian military capabilities, a statement that drew pushback from military analysts who noted Iran's investment in indigenous defense systems.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran has significantly modernized its air defense network, including the domestically produced Bavar-373 system and Russian-supplied S-300 batteries. Military analysts assess these systems as capable of posing meaningful threats to attacking aircraft.

statements.text: We have positioned our assets to ensure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Commerce will not be disrupted.
statements.context: Hegseth announced the deployment of an additional carrier strike group to the Persian Gulf amid rising tensions and Iranian naval exercises near the Strait.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. deployed additional naval assets, Iran's extensive arsenal of anti-ship missiles, fast attack boats, and mine-laying capabilities mean a guarantee of uninterrupted commerce is not militarily supportable. Shipping insurance rates rose 400% in this period.

statements.text: Our troops in the region are positioned defensively. We are not seeking conflict but we are prepared for any scenario.
statements.context: Hegseth sought to reassure Congress after reports indicated U.S. force levels in the Middle East had reached their highest point since 2003.
statements.factCheckDetail: The characterization of deployments as purely 'defensive' was questioned when leaked operational plans showed pre-positioned strike assets and forward-deployed special operations units. The buildup exceeded what military experts considered necessary for a purely defensive posture.

statements.text: We provided no direct military support for Israeli operations. U.S. forces are not engaged in combat operations against Iran.
statements.context: Hours before Israeli strikes commenced, Hegseth denied U.S. combat involvement, though reports later indicated U.S. aerial refueling tankers supported Israeli strike aircraft.
statements.factCheckDetail: While U.S. forces may not have directly fired weapons, aerial refueling of Israeli strike aircraft, intelligence sharing, and AWACS support constitute meaningful military support for combat operations, even if not classified as direct combat engagement.

statements.text: Iranian ballistic missile capabilities have been significantly degraded. The threat to our regional partners is diminished.
statements.context: Hegseth provided a battle damage assessment claiming Israeli and allied operations had destroyed a substantial portion of Iran's missile infrastructure.
statements.factCheckDetail: Independent verification of battle damage claims has not been possible due to access restrictions. Iran maintains dispersed and hardened missile facilities, and intelligence agencies have historically struggled to assess underground facility damage from satellite imagery alone.

statements.text: Force protection of our 45,000 personnel in the region remains my top priority. We are ready for any contingency.
statements.context: As Iranian-aligned militia attacks on U.S. bases in Iraq and Syria intensified, Hegseth confirmed troop numbers that had not been officially disclosed, drawing criticism for the operational security lapse.
statements.factCheckDetail: The troop figure, while not previously confirmed, was broadly consistent with independent estimates. The statement of prioritizing force protection is consistent with official DoD policy.

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury is on track as we enter the second week. We have spared no expense or capability on air defense, but we can't stop everything.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on Day 10. Signaled the conflict could last 4-8 weeks. Dismissed criticisms of the war effort while acknowledging limits of air defense systems.
statements.factCheckDetail: The acknowledgment of air defense limitations is consistent with reports of successful Iranian strikes on Gulf state infrastructure. The 4-8 week timeline represents first official duration estimate.

statements.text: Mojtaba Khamenei is likely disfigured. Every ballistic missile company in Iran has been destroyed. We have hit over 15,000 targets across the theater.
statements.context: Day 14 Pentagon briefing. Hegseth claimed Khamenei was 'likely disfigured' from initial strikes. Stated every ballistic missile production facility destroyed. 15,000+ targets figure is highest official count yet. Also confirmed IDF destroyed 250+ Iranian drones.
statements.factCheckNote: 15,000 targets figure significantly exceeds Trump's 6,000 claim — discrepancy may reflect different counting methodologies (individual aimpoints vs. target complexes). Khamenei's condition unverified independently.

statements.text: This is the highest volume of strikes we've put over Iran's skies. Over 15,000 enemy targets struck. Iran's missile capacity is down 90%, drone capacity down 95%. Ending Iran's nuclear program remains our core mission.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing. Confirmed 2,200 Marines deployed. Would not rule out ground forces to secure enriched uranium.
statements.factCheckNote: Strike volume confirmed. Specific degradation percentages are US claims — independent verification limited.

statements.text: Iran's military is 'destroyed' and 'combat ineffective.' Entire ballistic missile production capacity has been 'eliminated.' Missile volume down 90%, drone volume down 95%. March 14 will see the highest volume of US strikes yet. Confirmed CENTCOM investigation into Minab school strike with outside investigating officer.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing, Day 14
statements.factCheckNote: Missile/drone reduction figures not independently verified. School strike investigation confirmed.

statements.text: New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is 'wounded and likely disfigured.' State Department has offered $10 million reward for information on Khamenei's location. US-Israeli strikes have hit 15,000+ targets across 200+ cities.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on supreme leader targeting and overall strike campaign scope
statements.factCheckNote: Mojtaba Khamenei's condition not independently confirmed. $10M reward confirmed by State Department.

statements.text: Iranian forces launched thousands of drones AND missiles at its neighbours.
statements.context: CENTCOM X post responding to Iranian FM Araghchi's claims. Hegseth/CENTCOM pushed back on Iranian narrative about the scope of Iranian attacks on regional neighbors.
statements.factCheckNote: CENTCOM statement confirmed. Iran has launched missiles at Gulf states, Iraq, and Israel throughout the conflict.

statements.text: We are launching the largest strike package yet on Iran today. It takes money to kill bad guys. That $200 billion number could move.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on March 19. Announced 7,000+ targets struck total, 64 Apache helicopters deployed against Iranian drones.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News confirmed briefing details

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury is not an endless abyss or a forever war or quagmire. Over 120 Iranian naval vessels sunk. 11 submarines eliminated. Iran's surface fleet is no longer a factor. It takes money to kill bad guys.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing with Gen. Caine. Confirmed 5,000-lb penetrators, A-10s hunting boats in Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Naval destruction figures consistent with previous claims. 'Not a forever war' claim undercut by refusal to provide timeline.

statements.text: We will launch the largest strike package yet today. We've struck over 7,800 targets. All 11 submarines destroyed, 120+ warships sunk. It takes money to kill bad guys.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on Day 20 operations and $200B war supplemental request
statements.factCheckNote: Pentagon briefing aired live

statements.text: $200B supplemental: 'That number could move. It takes money to kill bad guys.' NOT a 'forever war or quagmire.' No timeline for end. Over 7,000 targets struck.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing
statements.factCheckNote: Briefing broadcast

statements.text: Announced the US would launch its 'largest strike package yet' against Iran. Said US has struck more than 7,000 targets, destroyed all 11 Iranian submarines, sunk or damaged 120+ warships. Ballistic missile attacks on US forces down 90% since conflict began.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing alongside Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine; Day 21 press conference
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel confirmed the briefing and figures.

statements.text: It takes money to kill bad guys.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing on March 19 when asked about the $200 billion supplemental war funding request — four times the originally floated figure. Hegseth also said the US had struck over 7,000 targets in Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement is rhetorical, not factual. The $200B request figure is confirmed by multiple sources.

statements.text: Iran is not deterred — they are determined. And so are we. The American military will continue to prosecute this campaign until Iran's ability to threaten its neighbors, fund terrorism, and pursue nuclear weapons is decisively degraded. We will not stop.
statements.context: Hegseth's public statement on Day 23 as Iran fired 8 missile salvos at Israel and Trump's power plant ultimatum deadline approached.
statements.factCheckNote: Public statement consistent with US posture throughout the conflict.

statements.text: We negotiate with bombs. You have a choice, as we loiter over the top of Tehran. The Iranian Air Force is destroyed. The Iranian Navy is sunk in the Persian Gulf. We have achieved an 86% reduction in Iranian ballistic missile launches since Day 1, and a 73% reduction in one-way attack drones.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing alongside Gen. Dan Caine on Day 25 operational status
statements.factCheckNote: 86% reduction in missile launches and 73% in drone attacks are self-reported Pentagon figures; not independently verified by external analysts.
statements.source: The Hill / Business Today / Pentagon briefing

statements.text: We negotiate with bombs. You have a choice, as we loiter over the top of Tehran — about your future. We are part of this negotiation as well. The Pentagon is as much a part of these negotiations as the State Department.
statements.context: Statement at NRCC annual fundraising dinner alongside Trump. Hegseth confirmed Trump's claim that negotiations with Iran are ongoing, framing the military campaign as leverage.
statements.venue: National Republican Congressional Committee Annual Dinner
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, NBC News, and multiple outlets. Context: Trump simultaneously claimed 'major points of agreement' with Iran, which Iran denied.

statements.text: Iran now has no navy, no navy leader and the United States' use of slower, low-flying aircraft is proof it has no air defenses left. This is not an endless war. War objectives remain clear: No nukes, no navy, and complete dismantling of their missile program and defense industrial base. This is pure American success.
statements.context: Trump Cabinet meeting, March 26. Hegseth confirmed IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri's death and stated Iran's military had been comprehensively degraded. He also said he would welcome a negotiated deal but warned the Pentagon will keep 'negotiating with bombs.'
statements.factCheckNote: CENTCOM has confirmed destruction of 92% of Iran's largest naval vessels and 2/3 of missile/drone/naval production facilities. Tangsiri's death confirmed. The claim Iran has 'no air defenses left' is likely an overstatement — Iran still fired Wave 82 ballistic missiles into Israel the same day.

statements.text: [FT reports] Hegseth's Morgan Stanley broker attempted to make a multimillion-dollar investment in BlackRock's iShares Defense Industrials Active ETF before the Iran war began. The investment did not go through because the fund was too new. Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell called the report 'entirely false and fabricated.'
statements.context: Financial Times investigation published March 31. ETF contains RTX, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman stocks. Pentagon issued strongest possible denial. Hegseth held first Pentagon briefing in 2 weeks with Gen. Caine on same day.
statements.factCheckNote: FT reports with evidence of broker activity. Pentagon categorically denies. Under scrutiny. BlackRock declined to comment.
statements.source: CNBC — Hegseth defence investments Iran war

statements.text: The upcoming days will be decisive. Iran knows that, and there's nothing they can militarily do about it. We are negotiating with bombs. If Iran is wise, it will cut a deal. Trump doesn't bluff.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing with Gen. Dan Caine. Revealed secret trip to Middle East. Did not rule out ground forces.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Stars and Stripes, Military Times confirmed briefing statements.

statements.text: Iran's defense industrial base is nearing complete destruction. Past 24 hours saw the lowest number of enemy missiles fired by Iran. If Iran doesn't deal, we will continue with even more intensity.
statements.context: Follow-up briefing. Visited pilots 'who deliver bombs deep into Iran.' Said talks 'gaining strength.' CENTCOM claims 75% of Iranian missile launchers destroyed.
statements.factCheckNote: war.gov, CBS News confirmed. 'Lowest missile fire' claim contradicted by IRGC firing 3 waves same day.

statements.text: The upcoming days will be decisive. American firepower is only increasing while Iran's is decreasing. We'll negotiate with bombs. This is not a so-called regime change war — but the regime sure did change.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing with Gen. Dan Caine
statements.factCheckNote: Self-contradictory on regime change. Religious rhetoric noted by NPR.

statements.text: Our adversary right now thinks there are 15 different ways we could come at them with boots on the ground. And guess what? There are.
statements.context: Statement made alongside firing of Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George and two other generals
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Washington Post confirmed. Firings of 3 generals also confirmed.

statements.text: Iran's defense industrial base is nearing complete destruction. The next days of the Iran war will be decisive. Iran knows that, and there's almost nothing they can militarily do about it.
statements.context: Hegseth revealed he visited US troops in the Middle East over the weekend. Said talks on ending conflict are 'gaining strength.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran continues launching missiles despite 'nearing destruction' claim. F-15E shootdown contradicts Iran's inability narrative.

statements.text: [ACTION] Hegseth ousted Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George and two other generals (Gen. David Hodne, Maj. Gen. William Green Jr.) during wartime. Gen. Christopher LaNeve to serve as acting chief.
statements.context: Hegseth fired the Army's top general during an active war. Reports indicate Hegseth and Rubio are 'at each other's throats' over whether to send ground troops to Iran — Hegseth supports it, Rubio is wary.
statements.factCheckNote: Axios, CNN confirmed firings. Middle East Eye confirmed Hegseth-Rubio feud over ground troops.

statements.text: The next days will be decisive. Ceasefire talks are gaining strength.
statements.context: Said he visited US troops in Middle East over the weekend. Refused to rule out ground forces.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by New Arab, NPR.

statements.text: Tuesday will see the most intense day of strikes on Iran since the start of the war. Monday was already the highest volume of strikes. More than 15,000 enemy targets have been hit in this conflict.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing ahead of Trump's 8pm ET Hormuz deadline. Signaling massive escalation.
statements.factCheckNote: PBS, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel confirmed.

statements.text: Today will be the largest number of airstrikes since the very first day of the operation in the Middle East at the direction of the US president, and tomorrow there will be even more than today.
statements.context: Announced escalation of strikes ahead of Trump deadline. Also fired Army Chief of Staff Randy George. House Democrats announced impeachment articles against Hegseth.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, NPR, Axios confirmed.

statements.text: Tehran has been embarrassed and humiliated by US forces. Our goals in Iran are laser-focused.
statements.context: Press conference with Trump where they invoked God and religion. Spokesman-Review reports claims 'contradict reality' per officials.
statements.factCheckNote: Spokesman-Review: 'Hegseth's boastful claims about Iran war contradict reality.' Pentagon officials privately dispute narrative.

statements.text: America's military achieved every single objective on plan, on schedule. Iran can no longer build missiles, build rockets, build launchers or build UAVs. Their factories have been razed to the ground.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on ceasefire Day 1
statements.factCheckNote: WaPo reported officials say Hegseth 'misrepresenting the US position.' More than half of Iran's missiles reportedly remain intact.

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury was a historic and overwhelming victory on the battlefield. In less than 40 days, the US dismantled one of the world's largest militaries. Our forces are prepared to go on offense, prepared to restart at a moment's notice. We will be hanging around.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing declaring victory in Iran war after ceasefire
statements.factCheckNote: WaPo: officials say Hegseth 'misrepresenting US position' — more than half Iran's missiles remain intact. Gen. Caine claims: 95% mines, 80% missile sites, 90% factories destroyed.

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury was a historic and overwhelming victory on the battlefield, a capital V military victory by any measure. CENTCOM used less than 10 percent of America's total combat power to dismantle one of the world's largest militaries.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing declaring victory in Iran campaign following ceasefire announcement.
statements.factCheckNote: Victory claim disputed — nuclear question unresolved, proxies still active, Hormuz still closed.

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury was a historic and overwhelming victory on the battlefield, a capital V military victory. Iran begged for this ceasefire, and we all know it.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing declaring victory
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote from Pentagon briefing, confirmed by NPR

statements.text: By any measure, Epic Fury decimated Iran's military and rendered it combat ineffective for years to come. 90% of their naval fleet is at the bottom of the ocean — 150 ships. We struck 13,000+ targets. 80% of their air defense systems are destroyed. 90% of their weapons factories have been attacked. We're not going anywhere.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing with Joint Chiefs Chair Gen. Caine revealing comprehensive Epic Fury statistics.
statements.venue: Pentagon Briefing — with Gen. Dan Caine
statements.factCheckNote: Military stats are US government claims; US intel separately estimates ~50% of Iran's missile launchers remain intact underground. C-SPAN, CBS News confirmed.

statements.text: We're watching you. The blockade will continue for as long as it takes. We are locked and loaded to attack Iran's energy and electrical power infrastructure if negotiations fail. We are reloading with more power than ever before and better intelligence than ever before.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing with Gen. Caine. Announced US Navy will now intercept Iranian ships anywhere in the world. 13 ships turned back in 3 days. Threatened Iran's electrical grid — new targeting category beyond energy infrastructure.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Newsweek, ABC News, Breaking Defense, Stars and Stripes.

statements.text: Choose wisely. This is not a fair fight.
statements.context: Warning to Iranian leaders at Pentagon press conference. Said US is ready to restart attacks on Iran energy infrastructure if they refuse a deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News, Washington Times.

statements.text: We are locked and loaded on your critical dual-use infrastructure, on your remaining power generation, and on your energy industry. If Iran chooses poorly, then they will have a blockade and bombs dropping on infrastructure, power, and energy. This is the polite way that this can go.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing with Gen. Dan Caine and Adm. Brad Cooper. Threatened to transition from blockade to 'major combat operations very quickly and even more powerfully than ever.' Also said Iran is 'digging out remaining launchers and missiles with no ability to replace them.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News, Al Jazeera, Washington Times, C-SPAN, NBC News, CBS News.

statements.text: Iran is moving around its assets but is unable to replenish its military power almost seven weeks into the war.
statements.context: Statement amid Hormuz re-closure crisis. Iran had briefly opened Hormuz before Ghalibaf/IRGC reversed the decision.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Newsweek.

statements.text: As you expose yourself with your movement to our watchful eye, we are locked and loaded on your critical dual use infrastructure, on your remaining power generation and on your energy industry. Choose wisely.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing urging Iran to 'choose wisely.' Noted US using 'less than 10% of America's naval power' for blockade. Iran cannot replenish military power seven weeks in.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Department of War press release, Al Jazeera, CBS News.

statements.text: Iran is moving assets around but is unable to replenish its military power seven weeks into this war. They should choose wisely.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on Iran's degraded military capacity during Operation Epic Fury ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: War.gov official DOD news.

statements.text: No one sails from the Strait of Hormuz to anywhere in the world without the permission of the United States Navy. Our blockade is only growing and going global. All they have to do is abandon a nuclear weapon and in meaningful and verifiable ways. Or instead, they can watch their regime's fragile economic state collapse under the unrelenting pressure of American power.
statements.context: 9th Pentagon press briefing since war began, alongside JCS Chair Gen. Dan Caine. Announced 34 non-Iranian vessels allowed through, 31 turned back. Second carrier joining blockade.
statements.venue: Pentagon Press Briefing — 9th since war began
statements.factCheckNote: ABC News, C-SPAN, CBS News, war.gov transcript. Blockade claims contested by WaPo fact-checkers.

statements.text: This war is a gift to the world. Iran has an important choice, a chance to make a deal, a good deal, a wise deal. Iran has a historic chance to make a serious deal, and the ball is in their court. We are not counting on Europe, but they need the Strait of Hormuz much more than we do.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing, remarks addressed to European allies amid NATO friction over Iran war. Announced second US aircraft carrier joining blockade as USS George H.W. Bush arrived.
statements.factCheckNote: PBS, Stars and Stripes, C-SPAN official transcript. All verified.

statements.text: Europe and Asia have benefited from our protection for decades, but the time for free-riding is over. America and the free world deserve allies who are capable, who are loyal. If Iran is putting mines in the water or otherwise threatening American commercial shipping or American forces, we will shoot to destroy. No hesitation.
statements.context: Same Pentagon briefing. Dismissed British/French Hormuz conferences as 'not serious efforts.' Slammed European allies for 'free-riding' on US military protection while failing to support Iran operations.
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Times, Fox News, Stars and Stripes. All verified.

statements.text: Our blockade is growing and going global. No one sails from the Strait of Hormuz to anywhere in the world without the permission of the United States Navy. Iran has an important choice, a chance to make a deal, a good deal, a wise deal.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing with Chairman Caine. Announced expansion of blockade enforcement to global Iranian-flagged shipping interdictions, not just Hormuz transits.
statements.factCheckNote: Stars and Stripes, Washington Times, Fox News. All verified.

statements.text: President Trump has authorized the United States Navy to destroy any Iranian fast boats that attempt to put mines in the water. The US war in Iran is a gift to the world. The blockade will continue as long as it takes.
statements.context: PBS interview and Fox News appearance. Called the war a 'gift to the world' drawing widespread criticism. Confirmed shoot-to-destroy orders for Iranian mine-laying vessels in Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: PBS Newshour, Fox News. Pentagon confirmed shoot-to-destroy authorization.

statements.text: CENTCOM has now redirected 38 ships and our Avenger-class minesweepers are operating around the clock in the Strait. Any Houthi crew involved in the Andromeda Star attack will be found.
statements.context: X post (@SecDef) confirming intensification of US naval blockade enforcement in the Strait of Hormuz and pledging accountability for Houthi missile strike on Andromeda Star tanker in Red Sea.
statements.factCheckNote: Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker confirms ~38 ships redirected; Avenger-class operations cited by Pentagon.

statements.text: Who are you cheering for? Fund the fight now, or fold on Iran. The biggest adversary we face is not on the battlefield — it's right here in this chamber.
statements.context: First congressional testimony since war began, before House Armed Services Committee. Questioned Rep. Garamendi's patriotism after being called a liar. Pentagon seeking $200B supplemental + $1.5T FY2027 budget. Democrats challenged contradiction: nuclear program was 'obliterated' in 2025 but 'imminent threat' in 2026.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct testimony quotes confirmed by Al Jazeera, TIME, Fortune, Common Dreams, CNBC.

statements.text: The 60-day War Powers clock pauses during the current ceasefire — the administration does not need new congressional authorization to continue this war.
statements.context: Senate Armed Services Committee testimony, ~6 hours, on eve of May 1 War Powers deadline. Pentagon Comptroller Jules Hurst III confirmed $25B war cost (CNN reports actual $40-50B). White House requesting $1.5T defense budget — largest since WWII. Rep. Garamendi (D-CA): 'You have been lying to the American public about this war from day one.' Democrats exploring lawsuit if Trump continues war past May 1.
statements.factCheckNote: Hegseth's legal claim is contested — most legal scholars say War Powers Resolution clock does not pause during ceasefires absent formal armistice. Congressional Research Service has issued no opinion.

statements.text: US forces have achieved dominant control of the airspace above the Gulf. Project Freedom is about more than oil — it's about restoring deterrence and showing the world that America stands by its friends and keeps the seas open.
statements.context: Defending Project Freedom launch. CENTCOM began escorting ~2,000 stranded ships through Hormuz with destroyers, 100+ aircraft, 15,000 service members. Iran warned ceasefire violation.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News, Conservative Daily News.

statements.text: The ceasefire is not over. We just stopped six more Iranian port-connected ships running the blockade. We are locked and loaded.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing alongside JCS Chair Gen. Caine. Hegseth pushed back on perception that May 4 Hormuz clashes ended the April 8 ceasefire. Caine described Project Freedom as 'defensive in nature, focused in scope, and temporary in duration — with one mission: protecting innocent commercial shipping from Iranian aggression.'
statements.factCheckNote: CNBC, Fox News, RedState all carried Hegseth/Caine quotes. Numbers verified across multiple outlets.

statements.text: If you attack American troops or innocent commercial shipping, you will face overwhelming and devastating American firepower. We're not looking for a fight.
statements.context: Hegseth's Pentagon press briefing alongside Gen. Dan Caine, defending Project Freedom mission as Trump simultaneously announced its pause. Said Iran has fired at commercial vessels 9 times and seized 2 container ships since the April 8 ceasefire began.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News, Al Jazeera. Iranian aggression statistics verified by Pentagon transcripts.

statements.text: The ceasefire paused the 60-day deadline for congressional approval under the War Powers Act. Our forces remain ready to resume operations at a moment's notice.
statements.context: Hegseth faced back-to-back Congressional hearings (House at 8am ET, Senate at 10:30am ET) on the $1.5 trillion defense budget and $29 billion Iran war costs. Sen. Collins (R-ME) voted with Democrats seeking a defined strategy. Bipartisan scrutiny over war costs and lack of exit strategy.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Post, NBC Montana. $29B war cost figure from Pentagon testimony.

statements.text: We have a plan to escalate if necessary, a plan to retrograde if necessary, and a plan to shift assets. The ceasefire with Iran remains in effect despite recent exchanges of fire.
statements.context: House Armed Services Committee testimony. Facing bipartisan questioning over war costs ($29B), strategy, and endgame. PBS and Washington Post noted intense frustration from both parties. Defended FY2027 $1.5 trillion defense budget request.
statements.venue: House Armed Services Committee, Capitol Hill
statements.factCheckNote: C-SPAN broadcast hearing. PBS and Washington Post confirmed quotes.

statements.text: The munitions issue has been foolishly and unhelpfully overstated. Ultimately we control the Strait, because nothing's going in that we don't allow to go in. Trump doesn't need congressional approval to restart strikes on Iran.
statements.context: Senate Armed Services Committee testimony on May 12. Hegseth defended $1.5 trillion Pentagon budget and insisted Iran's missile program is 'functionally destroyed' — contradicting classified intelligence reports obtained by NYT and WaPo showing Iran retained ~70% of its missile arsenal. Faced bipartisan questioning. Asserted presidential authority beyond the 60-day War Powers Act limit.
statements.venue: Senate Armed Services Committee, Capitol Hill
statements.factCheckNote: Intelligence community assessments show Iran retained significant missile capability. WaPo confirmed classified briefings contradict Hegseth's public statements. NYT reported Iran restored access to ~30 of 33 missile sites.

statements.text: We have a plan to escalate if necessary. We have a plan to retrograde if necessary. We have a plan to shift assets. Concerns about weapons stockpiles have been unhelpfully overstated — we have plenty of what we need.
statements.context: Hegseth testified before Congress on May 12-13, 2026, defending the $1.5 trillion Pentagon budget request and the cost of the Iran war ($29 billion and rising). Faced bipartisan frustration. Confirmed Trump would not need Congressional approval to restart strikes, saying the president would have all necessary authorities. Also described "Project Freedom" naval escort operation as "defensive in nature, focused in scope and temporary in duration."
statements.venue: Congressional Testimony — Senate/House Armed Services Committees
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Post, PBS NewsHour, and Al Jazeera from May 12-13 hearings. War cost figure confirmed by Pentagon comptroller.

statements.text: These men and women served their country with honor and distinction through the longest carrier deployment since Vietnam. America is safer because of their sacrifice. The USS Gerald R. Ford is home.
statements.context: Hegseth attended the USS Gerald R. Ford homecoming ceremony at Naval Station Norfolk on May 16, 2026, welcoming 5,000 sailors after the ship's record 11-month deployment supporting Operation Epic Fury strikes on Iran.
statements.venue: USS Gerald R. Ford Homecoming — Naval Station Norfolk, Virginia
statements.factCheckNote: CNN and Washington Post confirmed Hegseth attended the Ford homecoming on May 16. Navy Times confirmed the 11-month deployment record.

statements.text: The ceasefire is holding.
statements.context: Hegseth at a Pentagon press briefing with Joint Chiefs Chair Gen. Dan Caine, maintaining that the overall ceasefire framework remains in force even as both sides have fired at one another in recent days. The claim came amid Israeli strikes killing 8 in southern Lebanon and ongoing Hezbollah counter-attacks.
statements.factCheckNote: The Hill and CBS News reported the statement. Ceasefire 'holding' is disputed — Israel struck Lebanon multiple times on May 20 killing 8, and Hezbollah claimed 26 counter-attacks on IDF forces. The ceasefire extension is technically active but widely described as 'ceasefire in name only.'
statements.source: The Hill live updates — Iran war

statements.text: We are sending you, perhaps, to war, and you are ready. Diversity is not our strength. Unity is our strength.
statements.context: West Point commencement address to graduating cadets on Day 86. Hegseth delivered the speech as Iran peace negotiations were at their most critical juncture — Trump admitted to reporters he was '50/50' on deal vs. resumed strikes shortly after the speech concluded. Hegseth also announced the US Army hit its 2026 recruiting goals four months early. The speech notably did not mention Iran by name.
statements.venue: US Military Academy at West Point, New York
statements.factCheckNote: Speech confirmed by PBS NewsHour, Fox News, Washington Examiner. Army recruiting goals announcement confirmed by Fox News. Context of Trump '50/50' comment timed after speech confirmed by Axios.
statements.source: Fox News — US Army hits 2026 recruiting goals four months early, Hegseth announces at West Point

statements.text: The ceasefire remains in effect. We continue to monitor the situation in the Strait of Hormuz closely. Our forces are positioned and ready.
statements.context: Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine held a Pentagon press briefing on Day 86, confirming the Iran war ceasefire remains in effect despite new IRGC naval activity in the Strait of Hormuz. Hegseth returned early from his West Point commencement address as Trump convened senior national security advisors at the White House. CENTCOM separately reported directing more than 50 vessels away from the Strait since the blockade began.
statements.venue: Pentagon Press Briefing — with Gen. Dan Caine, Day 86
statements.factCheckNote: Briefing confirmed by PBS NewsHour. Return from West Point confirmed by multiple outlets. CENTCOM vessel count confirmed by NewsNation.
statements.source: PBS NewsHour — Hegseth and Caine hold briefing on Strait of Hormuz activity

statements.text: The ceasefire with Iran is not over. We push back on the false premise that somehow President Trump is being pulled in by Prime Minister Netanyahu. We are grateful for Israel's input, their insights, the existential nature of the threat they face from an Iranian bomb.
statements.context: Hegseth at a Pentagon press conference on Day 87 Memorial Day, honoring fallen troops alongside Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine. Statement addressed growing media narrative that Netanyahu was influencing Trump away from the deal. Hegseth's defense of the US-Israel relationship came as Netanyahu had urged Trump to resume strikes rather than sign the MOU.
statements.venue: Pentagon Press Conference — Memorial Day, Day 87
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by CNN May 24 live updates and Fox News Digital May 25.
statements.source: Fox News — Iran war news Trump Hormuz ceasefire May 25

statements.text: Iran is the greatest threat to world peace. A nuclear Iran is an existential threat to the State of Israel and to all civilization.
statements.context: In an address to a joint session of Congress, Netanyahu framed the Iran nuclear issue as a global civilizational threat, seeking to build international support for action.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran's nuclear program poses legitimate proliferation concerns, characterizing it as the 'greatest threat to world peace' and 'all civilization' is considered hyperbolic by most international security analysts, who cite multiple concurrent global threats.

statements.text: Israel will not allow Iran to acquire nuclear weapons. We reserve the right to act alone if necessary.
statements.context: At the UN General Assembly, Netanyahu reiterated Israel's red line on Iranian nuclear capability, signaling willingness for unilateral military action.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents Israel's longstanding stated policy position. Israel's Begin Doctrine, established after the 1981 Osirak reactor strike, holds that Israel will prevent hostile states from acquiring nuclear weapons.

statements.text: Iran's attack on Israeli soil was an unprecedented act of aggression. We will respond at a time and manner of our choosing.
statements.context: Following Iran's direct missile and drone attack on Israel in April 2024, Netanyahu addressed the nation and pledged a calculated response.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran did conduct a direct military strike on Israeli territory in April 2024, the first such direct attack. The characterization of the event as unprecedented and aggressive is factually supported.

statements.text: We welcome the return of President Trump. Israel has never had a greater friend in the White House.
statements.context: After the 2024 U.S. election, Netanyahu praised Trump's victory and anticipated closer coordination on Iran policy.
statements.factCheckDetail: The characterization of Trump as Israel's greatest White House friend is subjective. While Trump recognized Jerusalem and the Golan Heights, other presidents have provided significant support including military aid packages and diplomatic backing.

statements.text: Our intelligence indicates Iran is months away from a nuclear weapon. The world cannot afford to wait.
statements.context: Netanyahu presented intelligence briefings to allied leaders claiming Iran had crossed critical enrichment thresholds, building the case for preventive action.
statements.factCheckDetail: Israeli intelligence on Iran's nuclear timeline has historically been contested. Previous claims of imminent Iranian nuclear capability dating back to the 1990s did not materialize. Independent IAEA assessments noted increased enrichment but did not confirm weaponization timelines.

statements.text: Two years after October 7th, Israel is stronger and more determined than ever. We will eliminate every threat to our existence.
statements.context: On the second anniversary of the October 7 attacks, Netanyahu connected the ongoing Iran threat to the broader security environment, using the memorial to justify expanded military operations.
statements.factCheckDetail: Claims of increased strength are difficult to independently verify. While Israel's military capabilities remain formidable, the prolonged multi-front conflicts have strained resources and personnel according to IDF reserve force assessments.

statements.text: We will not live under the shadow of a nuclear threat. Tonight, Israel acts to ensure its survival for generations.
statements.context: Netanyahu's address to the nation as Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities commenced, framing the operation as an existential necessity.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the nuclear threat concern has some basis, the characterization of the strikes as purely defensive is contested. Critics argue Israel possesses its own undeclared nuclear arsenal, creating a deterrence dynamic that the 'survival' framing does not acknowledge.

statements.text: Our operations have been precise and limited to military and nuclear targets. We have no quarrel with the Iranian people.
statements.context: Amid reports of civilian casualties near targeted facilities, Netanyahu defended the operation's targeting methodology and attempted to separate the conflict from the Iranian civilian population.
statements.factCheckDetail: While strikes may have targeted military and nuclear facilities, independent reports from humanitarian organizations documented civilian casualties in surrounding areas. Infrastructure damage also affected civilian populations through power and water disruptions.

statements.text: We are open to a ceasefire if Iran permanently dismantles its nuclear program and its proxy network. These are reasonable terms.
statements.context: Under growing international pressure, Netanyahu set conditions for a ceasefire that were widely seen as non-starters designed to appear diplomatic while ensuring continued operations.
statements.factCheckDetail: Demanding permanent dismantlement of both nuclear programs and proxy networks as preconditions for ceasefire is considered diplomatically unrealistic. Analysts note these conditions exceed what was achieved through years of JCPOA negotiations and are designed to be unacceptable.

statements.text: Israel will continue wide-scale strikes on Iran's military and energy infrastructure until the threat is eliminated.
statements.context: IDF briefing.
