statements.text: We are breaking their bones. The moment of truth is close. We are trying to free Iran. There will be many surprises in the next phase.
statements.context: Netanyahu addressed the Iranian people directly, vowing 'many surprises.' Diverging from Trump's 'war ending soon' signals — Netanyahu wants to continue operations while Trump pushes de-escalation. Bloomberg reports widening gap between US and Israeli endgames.
statements.factCheckDetail: Netanyahu frames strikes as liberation of Iranians, but 1,380+ civilians killed undermines this framing. IDF has struck 170+ munitions in Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz including IRGC Quds Force HQ.

statements.text: If Lebanon does not rein in Hezbollah, we will strike national infrastructure. We have the capability and the will to take territory.
statements.context: Threat conveyed through US and Western channels to Lebanon's government. Defense Minister Katz made similar statements. Follows largest Hezbollah barrage of the war.

statements.text: We are crushing Iran and Hezbollah. Mojtaba Khamenei is an IRGC puppet who cannot show his face in public. To the Iranian people: the coming days will bring an opportunity to free yourselves. Be ready.
statements.context: First press conference since war began. Netanyahu vowed 'many surprises' for the next phase.
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast live; quotes confirmed by Times of Israel, PBS, Haaretz

statements.text: Israel's defense chief has declared we will hold Lebanese territory until Hezbollah fully disarms. There will be no withdrawal without security guarantees.
statements.context: Day 14 statement via defense establishment channels. Israel defense chief announced intention to hold occupied Lebanese land until Hezbollah disarms — echoing 2006 war posture but with expanded territorial aims.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz and CNN reporting on Israel defense chief statements.

statements.text: This IRGC puppet Mojtaba Khamenei can't even show his face in public. This is not an endless war — it will usher in an era of peace. There will be many surprises for the next phase.
statements.context: First press conference of the war and Fox News Hannity interview. Scoffed at new Supreme Leader's inability to appear publicly.
statements.factCheckNote: Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement was read by state TV anchor, not delivered in person.

statements.text: Our strikes are creating optimal conditions for toppling the regime, but I cannot guarantee regime change without an internal uprising. I wouldn't take out a life insurance policy on any of their leaders.
statements.context: Press conference addressing regime change goals and threats against Iranian leadership
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Bloomberg; press conference footage available

statements.text: [Video posted showing himself alive and well]
statements.context: Netanyahu posted a video to counter Iranian state media rumors that he had been killed. The video showed him in an undisclosed location, appearing unharmed.
statements.factCheckNote: Video confirmed authentic. Posted in direct response to Iranian propaganda claiming Netanyahu was dead.

statements.text: I am dying... for a coffee.
statements.context: Netanyahu posted video from Tel Aviv cafe mocking conspiracy theories about his death. Spread all five fingers visibly to counter AI deepfake '6 finger' claims.
statements.factCheckNote: Reuters confirmed Netanyahu's presence via cafe surveillance cameras. Snopes debunked the 6-finger deepfake claim.

statements.text: We will eradicate Hezbollah from Lebanon. The IDF's ground operation will continue until the threat to our northern border is eliminated.
statements.context: Statement following IDF ground operations into southern Lebanon. 91st Division took Khiam. 450,000 reservists called up.
statements.factCheckNote: IDF confirmed ground operations in southern Lebanon. However, 'eradicate' language is aspirational given Hezbollah's deep roots in Lebanese society.

statements.text: Go out and celebrate. We're watching from above.
statements.context: Video message urging Iranians to celebrate Chaharshanbe Suri fire festival as anti-regime protest
statements.factCheckNote: Video confirmed by PM's office

statements.text: We are watching you from above. The brave people of Iran deserve a year of freedom.
statements.context: Nowruz greeting video from Kirya HQ, referencing Larijani/Soleimani kills
statements.factCheckNote: JPost confirmed

statements.text: The strike on South Pars sends a clear message: there is no safe haven for Iran's military infrastructure. We will continue striking until Iran's ability to threaten Israel is fully degraded.
statements.context: Statement after IAF strikes on South Pars gas field and Bandar Anzali naval base
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Times of Israel

statements.text: Iran can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. Israel needs a ground component for regime change. I see this war ending a lot faster than people think. Iran is no longer the same Iran.
statements.context: Evening press conference, March 19. First explicit call for ground offensive and regime change.
statements.factCheckNote: No evidence provided for uranium enrichment claim. IAEA chief Grossi: 'the material will still be there.' Ground offensive claim represents major policy shift.

statements.text: Iran can no longer enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. Regime change requires 'ground component.' On South Pars: 'Israel acted alone. President Trump asked us to hold off, and we're holding off.'
statements.context: 2nd press conference since war
statements.factCheckNote: Nuclear/missile claims unverified independently

statements.text: Mocked Mojtaba: 'I am not sure who is ruling Iran. Mojtaba, the replacement Ayatollah, has yet to appear.' Denied dragging US into war.
statements.context: Press conference
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast live

statements.text: Confirmed Israel struck Tehran again on Nowruz/Persian New Year. On IRGC spokesman Naeini's claim Iran was still building missiles: 'He was wrong. Dead wrong.' Netanyahu stated Israel will hold off future attacks on energy sites at Trump's request: 'President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding off.'
statements.context: Statement on Day 21 strikes; Naeini killed shortly after his claim Iran still building missiles
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog March 20 confirmed; Fox News and NPR confirmed.

statements.text: President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding out. Israel acted alone in hitting the South Pars gas field.
statements.context: Statement on Nowruz; confirming Trump-Netanyahu rift on energy infrastructure targeting
statements.factCheckNote: Directly confirmed by Netanyahu; consistent with Trump statement

statements.text: Fact number one, Israel acted alone against the Asaluyeh gas compound. Fact number two, President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding off.
statements.context: First English-language press conference since the war began. Netanyahu admitted Israel unilaterally struck South Pars (Asaluyeh), contradicting earlier reports that the US had advance knowledge. Confirmed agreement with Trump to pause energy infrastructure strikes unless Iran attacks Qatar.
statements.factCheckNote: Netanyahu's claim that Israel 'acted alone' contradicts reporting by anonymous Israeli officials who initially said the US had advance knowledge of the South Pars strike.

statements.text: You can't make a revolution from the air... there must be a ground component — I won't share with you all the options.
statements.context: At the same press conference, signaling that Israeli planning includes a ground component, raising concerns about a possible land invasion of Iran or Lebanon escalation.
statements.factCheckNote: Forward-looking strategic statement; not a factual claim.

statements.text: This is a very difficult evening in the campaign for our future. We are determined to continue to strike our enemies on all fronts.
statements.context: Statement issued after Iranian ballistic missiles struck Arad (74 injured, air defense failure) and Dimona (47 injured, adjacent to nuclear research center) on Day 22 evening.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by France 24, CBS News live updates, multiple Israeli media.

statements.text: If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have proved it. They fired on Jerusalem right next to the holy sites of the three monotheistic faiths. They have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. It's time to see the leaders of the rest of the countries join up.
statements.context: Statement at Arad — site of the Day 22 Iranian IRBM direct hit — urging Europe to join military coalition against Iran. Cited Iran's Diego Garcia strike as proof Berlin, Paris, and Rome are within reach.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Diego Garcia IRBM strike (intercepted, ~4,000km) confirmed by WSJ/CNBC. The ~4,000km range is factually within range of European capitals.

statements.text: If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have given it. It's time for world leaders to join us, and I'm seeing movement from some in that direction. What more proof do you need that this regime that threatens the entire world has to be stopped?
statements.context: Netanyahu spoke at the missile strike site in Arad, where Iranian ballistic missiles struck residential buildings overnight wounding 115+ people. He cited three events: Arad attack, missiles fired toward Jerusalem near holy sites, and Diego Garcia ICBM attempt.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel. Netanyahu visited Arad on March 22.

statements.text: President Trump's call to have the international community confront this fanatic terrorist regime of zealots is a call not only for the security of America and the security of Israel; it's for the security of the entire world. It's time for them to act.
statements.context: Statement at Arad missile strike site, elaborating on coalition-building appeal after Trump's call for global participation in the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Tribune India/ANI and Jerusalem Post reporting from Arad visit.

statements.text: Whatever we do, we do together, and as far as possible, in confidence. President Trump sees an opportunity to achieve the war's objectives through an agreement with Iran. Israel will be very happy with the terms Trump has described. Our attacks on Iran and Lebanon continue regardless of diplomatic signals.
statements.context: Netanyahu backed US strikes on Iran's power grid if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and supported Trump's claim of productive talks while vowing Israeli operations continue at full force.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera video newsfeed and Haaretz live updates. IDF simultaneously confirmed operations continue 'at full force.'
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Netanyahu backs US strikes on Iran

statements.text: Israel will protect our vital interests in every situation. Iran's attack on Diego Garcia proves they have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. They are putting everyone in their sights — it is time for the leaders of the rest of the countries to join up.
statements.context: Netanyahu first remarks since Trump announced Iran talks. Called on world leaders to join coalition, warning Europe of Iranian IRBM range revealed by Diego Garcia attack (March 21). Separately spoke with VP JD Vance about deal components.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran confirmed the Diego Garcia attack. Missile range 4,000km verified by UK government. Call with Vance confirmed by Israeli officials.

statements.text: I believe it is possible to leverage the mighty achievements obtained by the US and Israeli military to realize the goals of the war in an agreement that will safeguard our vital interests. Israel acted alone in striking the Asaluyeh gas complex. We are complying with President Trump's request to pause further such strikes.
statements.context: Statement after Trump's announcement of US-Iran talks. Netanyahu confirmed Israel was acting in coordination with Trump, and confirmed Israel alone struck South Pars/Asaluyeh gas compound.
statements.factCheckNote: Israeli strike on South Pars/Asaluyeh independently confirmed. Compliance with Trump request confirmed by multiple Israeli officials.

statements.text: We are continuing to strike in both Iran and Lebanon. We are smashing the missile program and the nuclear program, and we continue to deal severe blows to Hezbollah… there is more to come. I believe it is possible to leverage the mighty achievements obtained by the US and Israeli military to realize the goals of the war in an agreement.
statements.context: Netanyahu video statement on Day 25, reaffirming the war will continue. Despite Trump's 5-day pause on energy infrastructure strikes, Israel continued striking Iran. Netanyahu is also reportedly frustrated that Mossad's promised Iranian popular uprising has not materialized.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 25 and Haaretz. Nuclear program strikes confirmed by IDF.

statements.text: The IDF needs a few more weeks to fully degrade Iranian military capabilities. Israel will not stop until its security objectives are achieved. Targeted assassinations of Iranian leaders and commanders will continue.
statements.context: Attributed to senior Israeli security official briefing; Netanyahu instructed IDF to maximize strikes in 48-hour window before possible Trump deal
statements.factCheckNote: Statement attributed to senior Israeli official per ABC News. Netanyahu's rush to maximize strikes before potential ceasefire reported by CNN and Times of Israel, citing multiple current/former officials.

statements.text: Iran is being decimated. We are winning. But to truly achieve our objectives, there must be a ground component alongside the bombing. You don't want to replace one ayatollah with another. We are standing by the Iranian people's side.
statements.context: Netanyahu pushing the Trump administration for a ground invasion component to achieve regime change, amid concern that Trump may end the war before Israel's objectives are met
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, Jerusalem Post, and Times of Israel reporting. Statement reflects ongoing US-Israel tension over war objectives.
statements.source: CNBC — Netanyahu: Trump, US-Israel need ground component alongside Iran bombing

statements.text: It is often said that you can't win — you can't do revolutions from the air... but there has to be a ground component, as well. The countries of Europe: they have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have proved it.
statements.context: Netanyahu pushing for a ground component to operations in Iran, warning European leaders that Iran's missile range extends to Europe and citing the last 48 hours as proof.
statements.venue: Security cabinet session / Statement to European leaders
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill and Jerusalem Post. Netanyahu previously denied any ground operation plans publicly.

statements.text: We have achieved more than half our war aims. We are close to finishing Iran's arms industry. I don't want to put a schedule on it. Thousands of IRGC members have been killed.
statements.context: Newsmax interview, claiming war has passed its midpoint. Contradicted by RUSI report that Israel/US running low on interceptors.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of 'beyond halfway' contradicted by RUSI report on interceptor shortages and Haaretz reporting Iran still able to hit Haifa refinery.

statements.text: I won't put a timeline on when the war will end, but over half our missions have been achieved.
statements.context: Statement amid Knesset approval of record $45.8B defense budget
statements.factCheckNote: 'Over half missions achieved' claim cannot be independently verified. IDF operational details classified.

statements.text: The war has achieved more than half its aims. We are definitely beyond the halfway point. Israel and the US are close to finishing Iran's arms industry.
statements.context: Hebrew-language video statement on Day 32
statements.factCheckNote: No public metric for 'halfway point' provided. Claim about arms industry contradicted by continued IRGC missile launches.

statements.text: We had to act, and we acted. We have changed the face of the Middle East. We will continue to crush the terror regime in Tehran.
statements.context: Passover-eve televised statement. Told Newsmax war progress 'beyond the halfway point.' Hours later, IRGC fired 3 missile waves at Israel.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Euronews confirmed. 'Changed the face of Middle East' claim contradicted by ongoing Iranian missile attacks same day.

statements.text: We have inflicted ten plagues on Iran's axis of evil. Iran's trillion-dollar investment in terrorism has gone down the drain. Our campaign, together with the United States, is not yet over.
statements.context: Passover Eve address drawing Biblical parallels to the war
statements.factCheckNote: Nuclear capability destruction claims disputed by IAEA and arms control experts.

statements.text: Together with our American friends, we continue to crush the terrorist regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders, bombing bridges, bombing infrastructure. In full coordination between me and President Trump, between the IDF and the United States military, we will continue to crush Iran. This regime is weaker than ever.
statements.context: Statement from a military facility in Tel Aviv on Day 35
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel and Al Jazeera confirmed Netanyahu remarks.

statements.text: In full coordination between me and President Trump, and between the IDF and the United States military, we will continue to strike Iran. This regime is weaker than ever — Israel is stronger than ever. We are eliminating commanders, bombing bridges and striking infrastructure.
statements.context: Statement from IDF Kirya HQ in Tel Aviv. Also claimed Israel destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity.
statements.factCheckNote: 70% steel claim unverified independently. Times of Israel and Al Arabiya reported the claim. CNN reports Iran retains half its missile launchers and massive drone fleet.

statements.text: We will keep hitting Iran, in full coordination with Trump. We have destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity. The campaign will continue until all objectives are met.
statements.context: Statement on continued military operations — 70% steel capacity claim
statements.factCheckNote: 70% steel claim unverified by independent sources. Times of Israel reported the statement.

statements.text: In full coordination between me and President Trump, and between the IDF and the United States military, we will continue to strike Iran. This regime is weaker than ever — Israel is stronger than ever. 70% of Iran's steel production capacity destroyed.
statements.context: Statement at Kirya IDF HQ amid Iranian missile barrage on central Israel
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel confirmed statement. 70% steel capacity claim unverified independently — needs satellite imagery.

statements.text: In full coordination with President Trump, we will continue to strike Iran. This regime is weaker than ever — Israel is stronger than ever. We have destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity.
statements.context: Statement at IDF Kirya HQ after 70+ IDF strikes across Iran. Also said: 'Together with our American friends, we continue to crush the terror regime.'
statements.factCheckNote: The 70% steel destruction claim has not been independently verified. Netanyahu's 'weaker than ever' framing contradicts US intel showing Iran retains 50% of missile launchers.

statements.text: After we destroyed 70 percent of its ability to create steel, which is used as the raw material for the weapons used against us, today we attacked their petrochemical factories. These two things are their money machine, which funds their war of terror against us and against the world. We will continue to hit them, as I promised.
statements.context: Video statement released shortly after end of Shabbat. Netanyahu framed the IDF's shift to economic targets (petrochemical, steel) as cutting off Iran's war funding. Also referenced Passover, saying Israel has hit Iran with 'ten plagues.'
statements.factCheckNote: IDF confirmed strikes on Mahshahr petrochemical zone (5 killed, 170 wounded). The '70% of steel' claim remains unverified by independent sources.

statements.text: After we destroyed 70% of their steel production capacity, today we attacked their petrochemical plants — their money machine, which funds their war of terror against us and against the world. We will continue to crush them in full coordination with Trump.
statements.context: Video statement after Israeli strikes on Mahshahr petrochemical complex. Claims 70% destruction of Iran's steel industry.
statements.factCheckNote: The 70% steel destruction claim cannot be independently verified due to access restrictions. Confirmed by Times of Israel, ANI News, Euronews.

statements.text: We are crushing the terrorist regime in Iran with increasing force. Yesterday we destroyed transport planes and dozens of helicopters. Today we attacked the railroads and bridges. This is no longer the same Iran, and this is not the same Israel. We are changing the balance of power from end to end.
statements.context: Pre-recorded Passover Eve address. Likened strikes to 'Ten Plagues' of Egypt. Claimed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity and petrochemical factories destroyed.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Haaretz, Jang confirmed.

statements.text: We struck railways and bridges across Iran to disrupt IRGC missile launcher transport. 8 bridge segments in Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Kashan, Qom. Attacks conducted with increased intensity.
statements.context: Afternoon briefing confirming IDF infrastructure campaign. IDF also struck Tehran air defense systems and Shiraz sonar production facility.
statements.factCheckNote: IDF strikes on bridges confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel.

statements.text: The ceasefire does not include Lebanon. Israel supports President Trump's decision to suspend strikes on Iran. IDF operations in Lebanon continue.
statements.context: Formal statement contradicting Pakistan PM Sharif who claimed ceasefire covers 'everywhere including Lebanon.'
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Al-Monitor, Jerusalem Post confirmed.

statements.text: The ceasefire does not include Lebanon. Israel supports Trump's 2-week pause on Iran strikes but continues operations against Hezbollah.
statements.context: Statement clarifying ceasefire scope, contradicting Pakistan PM Sharif
statements.factCheckNote: Consistent with Israel's position. Contradicts Pakistan PM Sharif's claim ceasefire covers 'everywhere including Lebanon.'

statements.text: Israel supports President Trump's decision to suspend strikes against Iran for two weeks. The ceasefire does not include Lebanon. Iran entered the talks beaten and weaker than ever. This is not the end of the war — it is a stop on the way to achieving all our goals. There are still goals to be achieved either by agreement or by renewing the war.
statements.context: Statement backing ceasefire while insisting Lebanon excluded and war objectives remain
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Haaretz confirmed all quotes.

statements.text: Israel backs Trump's ceasefire but it does not include Lebanon. We hit Hezbollah with the biggest blow it has suffered since the beeper/walkie-talkie operation in September 2024. This is not the end. Iran's enriched uranium will be removed, by agreement or in resumed fighting.
statements.context: Official PM statement on ceasefire scope, asserting Lebanon is excluded and Operation Eternal Darkness was a major blow to Hezbollah.
statements.factCheckNote: Lebanon exclusion confirmed by US officials. 254 killed in Lebanon strikes confirmed.

statements.text: I have instructed my cabinet to begin direct negotiations with Lebanon as soon as possible, focusing on Hezbollah disarmament and establishing peaceful relations. These negotiations will happen under fire.
statements.context: Major shift — first direct Israel-Lebanon talks. Decision came after Trump/Witkoff pressure to scale back Lebanon strikes. First meeting at State Dept next week.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, Al Jazeera, Daily Sabah. Israel Amb Leiter and Lebanon Amb Hamadeh-Moawad to lead delegations.

statements.text: In light of Lebanon's repeated requests to open direct negotiations with Israel, I instructed yesterday to begin direct negotiations with Lebanon as soon as possible. These negotiations will focus on disarming Hezbollah and establishing peaceful relations between Israel and Lebanon.
statements.context: Authorizing direct Lebanon talks after US pressure
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel

statements.text: There is no ceasefire in Lebanon. We continue to strike Hezbollah with great force, and we will not stop until we restore your security.
statements.context: Video message to northern Israel residents
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, i24NEWS

statements.text: I want to make this clear: We still have goals to complete, and we will achieve them either by agreement or by resuming the fighting. We are prepared to return to combat at any moment required. Our finger is on the trigger.
statements.context: Netanyahu stressed ceasefire does not include Lebanon. Authorized direct Israel-Lebanon negotiations at Trump's urging.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Axios.

statements.text: I have given my approval for talks with Lebanon, but on two conditions: we want the dismantling of Hezbollah's weapons, and we want a real peace agreement that will last for generations.
statements.context: Televised statement setting preconditions for Lebanon peace. Lebanon-Israel-US talks confirmed for Tuesday in Washington.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Manila Times, Al Jazeera, Washington Post.

statements.text: The campaign against Iran is not over, but we can already say clearly — we have historic achievements. They wanted to strangle us, and we are strangling them. We hit them, we still have more to do.
statements.context: Video statement after Islamabad talks collapsed. Claimed Israel killed 12 nuclear scientists and attacked nuclear facilities alongside the US. Israel approved updated target list for energy/infrastructure strikes on Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Claim of 12 nuclear scientists killed is unverified by independent sources.

statements.text: Iran violated the rules, President Trump decided to impose a naval blockade. We are in constant coordination. On this Holocaust Remembrance Day, we will remember that we have prevented them from realizing their plan of destruction with a force that has brought the State of Israel to the peak of its power since its establishment.
statements.context: Cabinet meeting and Holocaust Remembrance Day ceremony. Drew parallel between Holocaust and Iran threat. Backed US naval blockade. Also criticized Europe's 'deep moral weakness.'
statements.venue: Cabinet Meeting / Holocaust Remembrance Day Ceremony
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, JPost, Tribune India.

statements.text: Had we not acted, names like Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan and Parchin might have become synonymous with horror — like Auschwitz, Treblinka, Majdanek and Sobibor. Europe is afflicted by deep moral weakness. This level of coordination with the United States has never existed before — not in the history of Israel, and not in the history of the Jewish people. The ceasefire could turn on a dime.
statements.context: Holocaust Remembrance Day speech at Yad Vashem (evening) and follow-up cabinet meeting (morning April 14). Compared Iran nuclear sites to Nazi death camps. Rebuked Europe. Backed Trump blockade.
statements.venue: Yad Vashem Holocaust Memorial / Cabinet Meeting
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, YNet, Opinion Nigeria. Comparison sparked international criticism.

statements.text: We want the dismantling of Hezbollah's weapons, and we want a real peace agreement that will last for generations. Israel will not discuss a ceasefire with Hezbollah.
statements.context: Statement through spokesman regarding Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington. Haaretz reports Netanyahu may be using talks to buy time for Bint Jbeil ground operation.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz, Al Jazeera. Haaretz analysis suggests tactical positioning.

statements.text: So if you're asking if I have a sense of mission, historically and spiritually, the answer is yes. Very much so.
statements.context: i24 News interview. Endorsed vision of Greater Israel (Eretz Yisrael HaShlema). Accepted amulet in the form of a 'map of the Promised Land.' Highly inflammatory during active war — provoked strong international reactions.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Middle East Eye, Truthout, The New Arab, TRT World. Netanyahu explicitly endorsed territorial expansion beyond current borders.

statements.text: Our American friends are constantly updating us on the talks with Iran. It is too early to say how this will end, or even how it will progress. And in light of the possibility that the fighting may resume, we are prepared for any scenario.
statements.context: Wednesday evening video update. Cautious tone contrasting with Trump's optimism. Emphasizing readiness for resumed hostilities while noting diplomatic track.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN liveblog and CBS News live updates.

statements.text: I have instructed the IDF to continue reinforcing the security zone and to extend it eastward, toward the slopes of Mount Hermon.
statements.context: Recorded statement on Lebanon operations. Fighting focused on dismantling Hezbollah stronghold in Bint Jbeil. Security cabinet meeting ended without Lebanon ceasefire decision.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. JPost confirmed security cabinet meeting.

statements.text: In the negotiations with Lebanon, there are two central objectives: first, the dismantling of Hezbollah; second, a sustainable peace... achieved through strength. We are talking about a solid, deeper security zone.
statements.context: Statement on Lebanon peace talks following Rubio-hosted trilateral. Announced expansion of buffer zone with 5 maneuvering divisions. Also demanded Iran's enriched material removed and enrichment capability annulled.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Manila Times, JPost, Times of Israel.

statements.text: We want to see the enriched material taken away from Iran, and we want to see an annulment of enriching capability within Iran. We want to see an opening of the Straits.
statements.context: Nuclear demands ahead of potential US-Iran Round 2 talks in Pakistan. Three conditions: remove enriched material, end enrichment, reopen Strait of Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Haaretz. Aligns with US position.

statements.text: We have an opportunity to make a historic peace agreement with Lebanon. We are remaining in Lebanon in an expanded security zone. That is where we are, and we are not leaving.
statements.context: Statement on 10-day ceasefire. Israel will not withdraw troops from southern Lebanon.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, NBC News, Haaretz.

statements.text: I agreed to this ceasefire to advance peace efforts with Lebanon. We changed the balance of power in Lebanon — the pager attack, the erosion of Hezbollah's rocket arsenal, and the killing of Nasrallah. This is a moment to build on that.
statements.context: Times of Israel reported Netanyahu told ministers he was 'shocked to learn of truce from media' but 'agreed to it at Trump's request.' Referenced 2024 pager attack and assassination of Nasrallah.
statements.factCheckNote: Ceasefire confirmed. But claim it was his initiative contradicted by Times of Israel report he learned from media. Confirmed by Time Magazine, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: I say honestly: We haven't finished the job yet. Due to a request from President Trump, Israel would give a chance to a political and military solution in coordination with the Lebanese government. We have two fundamental demands: disarmament of Hezbollah and a sustainable peace agreement.
statements.context: Responded to Trump's 'PROHIBITED' post after being caught off guard. Haaretz: 'Trump hijacked Israeli foreign policy.' Katz separately confirmed 10km security zone in Lebanon will be held indefinitely.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NYT, Haaretz, JNS. IDF sources confirmed security zone and continuing operations against Hezbollah fighters south of Litani.

statements.text: Today we launch the Isaac Accords — a diplomatic framework for deepening Israel's ties with Latin America, in the spirit of the Abraham Accords. Argentina under President Milei is a close and courageous partner.
statements.context: Netanyahu met Argentine President Javier Milei in Jerusalem and announced the new diplomatic initiative with Latin America, pitched as a parallel framework to the 2020 Abraham Accords.
statements.venue: Jerusalem / Joint Press Conference with Milei
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog April 19, 2026.

statements.text: All enriched material has to leave Iran. This will happen either by agreement or through a renewal of the fighting.
statements.context: Setting conditions for any final deal with Iran. Described the joint US-Israeli campaign as 'the battle of civilization against barbarism.' Said Iran war is 'not over' and Israel is ready for resumption if ceasefire expires without deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Haaretz.

statements.text: I condemn the act in the strongest terms. We express regret for the incident and for any hurt this has caused to believers in Lebanon and around the world.
statements.context: Condemning IDF soldier who smashed a statue of Jesus Christ in a Christian village in southern Lebanon. IDF confirmed the photo was authentic. Also said Israel is 'prepared for any scenario' including resumption of war with Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: IDF confirmed the photo is real and the conduct 'wholly inconsistent with the values expected of its troops.' Investigation launched.

statements.text: Iran war is not over. This is the battle of civilization against barbarism. Iran's enriched uranium will be removed — by agreement, or in resumed fighting.
statements.context: Speaking alongside visiting Argentine President Javier Milei. Netanyahu quipped that Milei 'always visits before something big' — interpreted by Haaretz as possible hint of imminent military action. Signals Israeli willingness to resume war after April 22 ceasefire expiry.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz and Times of Israel. Netanyahu's 'something big' quip about Milei noted by multiple Israeli outlets.

statements.text: I was stunned and saddened by the image of the soldier who damaged the statue of Jesus. I condemn it in the strongest terms. This behavior is the opposite of everything we stand for. There will be consequences.
statements.context: Netanyahu responding to viral photo of IDF soldier smashing Jesus statue with sledgehammer in Debel, a Maronite Christian village in south Lebanon. US Ambassador Huckabee also demanded 'swift, severe & public consequences.' IDF Northern Command investigating; soldier identified.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News, NBC News, Al Jazeera. IDF acknowledged photo is authentic.

statements.text: The ayatollah regime in Iran planned another Holocaust. It sought to destroy us with nuclear bombs and thousands of ballistic missiles. Had we not acted against this existential threat with determination and boldness, the names Natanz, Fordow, and Isfahan could have joined the names of the extermination camps.
statements.context: Yom HaZikaron (Memorial Day) state ceremony at Mount Herzl, Jerusalem. Was heckled after claiming he 'brought back all hostages.' A bereaved parent shouted 'some of them were killed in tunnels.' 174 IDF + 79 civilians killed in past year.
statements.factCheckNote: Holocaust comparison is Netanyahu's political framing. Nuclear program claims contested by intelligence community. Casualty figures confirmed by Haaretz.

statements.text: The Iranian axis of evil, which plotted to destroy us, is now fighting for its very survival. In the War of Revival, we smashed major parts of Iran's axis. This ceasefire is not the end — it is a stop on the way to achieving all of our objectives, either by agreement, or by resuming the fighting.
statements.context: Independence Day ceremony video address in Jerusalem. Declared Israel 'stronger than ever.' Signaled ceasefire is temporary and military campaign may resume.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quotes confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: Israel is stronger than ever, and together with the United States, we are leading the fight against the forces of evil in the world. We dealt a heavy blow to Iran's regime of terror, harming its ability to endanger us. The ayatollah regime planned another Holocaust — together with our great ally, the United States, we dismantled that machinery of destruction in advance.
statements.context: Independence Day address. Netanyahu referenced Operations Rising Lion and Roaring Lion. At Memorial Day ceremony, said war prevented 'another Holocaust.' Russia's Zakharova responded warning against 'giving the wrong directions.'
statements.factCheckNote: The 'Holocaust' framing is contested — while Iran posed a nuclear threat, comparing it to the Holocaust has been criticized by historians. 'Dismantled machinery of destruction' overstates — Iran retains significant military capability. Confirmed by Times of Israel and Israel National News.

statements.text: Held limited security discussion with ministers and senior defense officials to discuss developments in Lebanon and Iran ahead of Washington talks.
statements.context: Israeli officials assessed a 'decision point' on Iran is nearing in Washington, with Jerusalem increasingly pointing to a resumption of fighting as talks remain stalled.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Haaretz.

statements.text: We maintain full freedom of action against any threat, including emerging ones. We attacked yesterday and we attacked today. Hezbollah is trying to sabotage the truce. The process to achieve historic peace between Israel and Lebanon has begun.
statements.context: Video statement accusing Hezbollah of trying to sabotage peace. Said he had an 'excellent conversation' with Trump. Noted Trump is putting 'very strong pressure on Iran both economically and militarily.' First comments on Lebanon ceasefire extension.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Haaretz.

statements.text: Following the firing of two rockets and multiple drones from Lebanon at northern Israeli communities, the Prime Minister has instructed the IDF to vigorously strike Hezbollah targets in Lebanon. Israel will not tolerate violations of the ceasefire and will respond with full force.
statements.context: PM Office statement Saturday morning ordering IDF to strike Hezbollah 'with force' after first rocket barrage since the April 16 ceasefire. Sirens triggered at Manara, Margaliot, Misgav Am.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Haaretz. Direct from PM Office.

statements.text: I have ordered the IDF to vigorously attack Hezbollah targets in Lebanon. Israel will not tolerate continued ceasefire violations against its civilians. We will strike with force.
statements.context: PMO statement issued after Hezbollah fired 2 rockets and a drone at northern Israel — second major ceasefire violation. Order came as IDF carried out air raids on Yohmor al-Shaqif, Safad al-Battikh, and Khiam.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post. Confirmed.

statements.text: It must be understood that Hezbollah's violations are, in practice, dismantling the ceasefire.
statements.context: Statement after IDF Sgt. Idan Fooks was killed and 6 soldiers wounded by Hezbollah explosive drone in Taybeh. Netanyahu ordered forceful strikes and Israel subsequently issued evacuation orders for 7 Lebanese towns beyond the buffer zone. Hezbollah counter-claimed over 500 Israeli violations.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Washington Post. Confirmed.

statements.text: We will not allow Hezbollah to rearm under the cover of a paper ceasefire. The strikes today were a response to active rocket assembly within 48 hours of firing.
statements.context: Video address to the Israeli public defending the IDF strike package on seven southern Lebanon towns that killed 14, framing the strikes as imminent self-defense rather than ceasefire breach.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PMO press release and Israeli broadcaster Kan.

statements.text: Hezbollah's violations are, in practice, dismantling the ceasefire. There are still two central threats from Hezbollah: the 122mm rockets and the drones. Our freedom of action to thwart threats — immediate threats and emerging threats — is part of the agreement we made with the United States and also with the Lebanese government.
statements.context: Weekly cabinet meeting. Netanyahu instructed the IDF to strike Hezbollah targets 'with force' after the killing of Sgt. Idan Fooks by drone and continued rocket fire. Asserted Israel has preemptive strike authority under the ceasefire terms.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog and Euronews.

statements.text: We have severely damaged Iran's nuclear programme. We destroyed not only existing missiles, but also the factories that produce them. Iran is now firing what remains in its stockpile and that stockpile is steadily dwindling. Iran's enriched uranium will be removed — by agreement, or in resumed fighting.
statements.context: Public remarks reaffirming Israel's war aims and warning that Iran's nuclear infrastructure must be addressed regardless of negotiations. Followed Rubio's rejection of Iran's Hormuz proposal and IDF strikes on Hezbollah Beqaa/Qantara targets.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed quote (Times of Israel). Claims about destruction of Iran's missile production are disputed by independent analysts who note residual capability.

statements.text: Breaking into the home of a military commander while his family is inside is an act of extreme violence that we condemn in the strongest possible terms.
statements.context: Netanyahu condemned ultra-Orthodox extremists who stormed the home of IDF Military Police chief Brig. Gen. Yuval Yamin in Ashkelon. 25 suspects arrested. Defense Minister Katz also condemned. Route 4 near Bnei Brak blocked by protesters.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel and Haaretz. 25 arrests confirmed.

statements.text: Drones are Israel's next target in Lebanon. We have ordered a special project to counter Hezbollah drones — it will take time, but we will take care of that. We are operating within the security zone in Lebanon, both south and north of the Litani River.
statements.context: Netanyahu announced counter-drone initiative after Hezbollah's fiber-optic drones killed a contractor and wounded soldiers. Also confirmed IDF operations beyond the buffer zone, north of the Litani River.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog, The Week, LiveUAMap.

statements.text: Israel has asked Washington for a two-to-three week deadline on Lebanon talks. After mid-May, if there is no agreement, Israel will resume active hostilities against Hezbollah.
statements.context: Netanyahu pushed Trump to impose a timeline on Lebanon negotiations. Israel views mid-May as a hard deadline for concluding a Lebanon agreement. IDF Chief Zamir ordered heightened readiness.
statements.venue: Phone call with Trump / Israeli officials
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Head Post.

statements.text: The ceasefire could turn on a dime. Israel supports Trump's naval blockade. This level of coordination has never existed before — not in the history of Israel, and not in the history of the Jewish people.
statements.context: At a cabinet meeting, Netanyahu backed Trump's blockade while signaling the ceasefire remains fragile. Reiterated that ceasefire does not include Lebanon.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel. Cross-referenced with Al Jazeera.

statements.text: We will not stand by as the Druze in Syria are threatened. Tonight's strikes near the presidential palace and across Suwayda are a direct message to the Syrian regime: any movement against the Druze population will be met with force.
statements.context: Joint statement with Defense Minister Katz issued after early-morning IAF strikes near the Syrian Presidential Palace in Damascus and across Suwayda/Daraa governorates. Syrian Health Ministry reports 3 killed, 34 injured.
statements.factCheckNote: TIME confirmed strikes and joint Druze warning.

statements.text: Israel will purchase two new fighter squadrons. Our pilots can reach anywhere in Iranian airspace. We are launching a special project to counter the drone threat in the north. Over the next decade, NIS 350 billion will be invested so Israel is independent of foreign weapons.
statements.context: Announcing multi-billion dollar fighter jet purchases — doubling F-35 fleet from 50 to 100 and F-15IA from 25 to 50. Also ordered counter-drone project after Hezbollah fiber-optic FPV drones killed IDF Sgt. Fooks.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Israel Hayom and Jerusalem Post.

statements.text: Due to political and security meetings during this period of heightened tension, the prime minister respectfully requests that the court cancel the scheduled testimony in this case. The State of Israel must remain focused on its security priorities at this critical moment.
statements.context: Netanyahu's lawyers asked the Jerusalem District Court to cancel his testimony in his ongoing corruption trial citing 'political and security meetings' as Iran launched its first attack on UAE since the April 8 ceasefire and the IDF was placed at 'high readiness' for possible Iran war resumption.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog and Haaretz live confirmed.

statements.text: I have convened the security cabinet to assess developments. I will speak with President Trump tonight about the situation regarding Iran.
statements.context: Netanyahu convened Israel's security cabinet amid reports of Iran's 'surprising openness' to transferring its uranium stockpile overseas as part of the 14-point MOU. Israeli security chiefs reportedly told Netanyahu the deal would be a 'disaster for Israel.' Same day Israel struck Beirut killing Hezbollah's Radwan Force commander.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post confirmed.

statements.text: There is full coordination between us — no surprises. Israel is stronger than ever, and Iran and its proxies are weaker than ever. I have instructed the IDF to prepare for all scenarios — including a return to fighting.
statements.context: Netanyahu's security cabinet briefing as Lebanon ceasefire showed signs of collapse and US-Iran negotiations advanced. Pushed back against reports of Israeli surprise at Trump's deal-making. Israeli security chiefs reportedly told Haaretz any Iran deal would be a 'disaster for Israel.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel live blog and dedicated reporting.

statements.text: The most important goal is removing the enriched material from Iran, all the enriched material, and dismantling Iran's enrichment capabilities. No terrorist is immune — [Ballout] led the plan to conquer the north and thought he had immunity in Beirut.
statements.context: Netanyahu published Israel's four red lines for any US-Iran deal via Israel Hayom: (1) remove ALL enriched material, (2) dismantle enrichment capabilities, (3) restrict ballistic missiles, (4) Israel maintains full freedom of action. Also commenting on IDF's killing of Hezbollah Radwan Force commander Ballout in Beirut.
statements.factCheckNote: Israel Hayom published red lines. Al Arabiya confirmed Ballout quote.

statements.text: The war is not over. You go in, and you take it out. All enriched uranium must be removed from Iran and enrichment sites dismantled. Is regime change possible? Yes. Is it guaranteed? No. A collapse of the Islamic Republic would mean the end of Hezbollah, the end of Hamas.
statements.context: CBS 60 Minutes interview aired May 10. Netanyahu declared war not over until enriched uranium removed. Iran holds ~441 kg of 60%-enriched uranium. Also suggested regime change is possible, contrasting with Trump's position that Iran is 'militarily defeated.'
statements.factCheckNote: Arms control experts dispute that uranium can simply be 'taken out' — Iran's stockpile is spread across multiple fortified underground sites. 441 kg of 60%-enriched uranium is enough for ~10 weapons if further enriched to 90%.

statements.text: This visit resulted in a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the United Arab Emirates. The UAE has stood with us in defending against the Iranian threat. What was forged in secret is now a testament to the courage of both nations.
statements.context: Netanyahu's office revealed the secret visit to the UAE on March 26, where he met President Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed (MBZ) in Al-Ain for several hours. The visit included the deployment of Israeli Iron Dome batteries to UAE territory — the first such deployment to an Arab country.
statements.venue: Statement via Prime Minister's Office, Jerusalem
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Bloomberg, Washington Post, CBS News, Haaretz.

statements.text: The regime of terror in Iran is weaker than ever, and the State of Israel is stronger than ever. We will continue to fight decisively against every threat of radical Islam. Jerusalem was, is, and will remain the eternal, unified capital of Israel.
statements.context: Jerusalem Day state ceremony at Ammunition Hill, marking 59 years since Israel's reunification of Jerusalem. Delivered amid Lebanon-Israel Round 3 peace talks opening in Washington and ongoing negotiations with Iran.
statements.venue: Jerusalem Day ceremony, Ammunition Hill, Jerusalem
statements.factCheckNote: Characterizations of Iranian and Israeli strength are political assessments. Jerusalem status is disputed internationally. Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: The war is not over until highly enriched uranium is removed. Nuclear facilities need to be dismantled — there's work to be done. Regime change in Iran is possible, not guaranteed.
statements.context: CBS News 60 Minutes interview aired May 14, 2026. Netanyahu insisted that even with a deal, Iran's nuclear infrastructure must be physically dismantled. On the question of regime change — a goal often attributed to Israel — he chose carefully worded language acknowledging possibility without commitment.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News transcript and Iran International.

statements.text: During the war I made a secret visit to the UAE. It was a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and Abu Dhabi. The normalization process is advancing even as the war continues.
statements.context: Netanyahu revealed the covert visit to Abu Dhabi in a public statement, calling it a diplomatic breakthrough. The UAE publicly denied the visit had taken place, creating an unusual diplomatic contradiction between two parties that publicly maintain no formal relations.
statements.factCheckNote: Netanyahu's office confirmed the visit. UAE publicly denied it. Unresolved contradiction — Times of Israel and Al Jazeera both confirmed Netanyahu's claim while noting UAE denial.

statements.text: The regime of terror in Iran is weaker than ever, and the State of Israel is stronger than ever. We will continue to fight decisively against every threat of radical Islam. If Israel had not launched its campaigns against Iran's nuclear and ballistic missile programs, Iran would already possess a nuclear bomb today.
statements.context: Jerusalem Day speech at Ammunition Hill state ceremony, marking 59 years since Israel's reunification of Jerusalem. Netanyahu hailed military achievements against Iran and other adversaries, declaring Israel the strongest it has ever been. He also cited the alliance with Trump administration as a key strategic asset.
statements.venue: Ammunition Hill, Jerusalem — Jerusalem Day State Ceremony
statements.factCheckNote: Claims about Iran possessing a nuclear bomb absent Israeli operations are unverifiable counterfactuals. The characterization of Iran as 'weaker than ever' reflects the official Israeli assessment, which is disputed by US intelligence showing Iran retained significant missile arsenal.

statements.text: I promised you that we would change the face of the Middle East — that is exactly what we are doing.
statements.context: Netanyahu released a video just before Shabbat on May 15 as Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 45-day ceasefire extension. The extension was brokered after two "productive" days of talks in Washington with Lebanese delegation led by Simon Karam. Netanyahu and Trump spoke by phone about both ceasefires. Netanyahu also confirmed Israeli troops will remain in southern Lebanon despite the truce extension.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 15 and PBS NewsHour on Lebanon ceasefire extension announcement.

statements.text: This arch terrorist has defiantly undermined President Trump's and the Board of Peace efforts to disarm Hamas and demilitarize the Gaza Strip. The IDF has eliminated the last architect of the October 7 massacre.
statements.context: Statement by Netanyahu's office following confirmation of the killing of Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad in an Israeli airstrike on Gaza City's Rimal neighborhood. Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz both stated they personally ordered the killing. The strike used 13 munitions and was described as an 'operational opportunity' after approval the prior week.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 15-16, 2026. Haddad's family confirmed death to AP.

statements.text: The war is not over. It's not over until the highly enriched uranium is removed from Iran and the centrifuges for enrichment are dismantled. International monitors estimate Iran retains about 970 lbs of near-bomb-grade uranium. You go in and you take it out.
statements.context: CBS News 60 Minutes interview. Netanyahu warned the Iran war remains unresolved until enriched uranium is physically removed. Also said regime change in Iran is 'possible, not guaranteed.' Secretly revealed he visited the UAE during the war, calling it a 'historic breakthrough'; UAE denied the visit.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News confirmed the 60 Minutes interview. CNBC and Iron International confirmed the uranium quantity claim. Times of Israel confirmed the UAE visit revelation.

statements.text: We are preparing for days to weeks of fighting. We'll know more in 24 hours.
statements.context: Attributed to an Israeli official briefed by Netanyahu's office, per NYT and Haaretz reporting. US and Israel are in 'intense preparations' for possible renewed strikes on Iran as early as next week. Options under review include intensified bombing of military and infrastructure sites, seizure of Kharg Island oil export hub, and special operations commando missions to extract nuclear material from Natanz and Fordow. The reporting coincided with Trump's 'calm before the storm' Truth Social posts on May 17.
statements.venue: Quoted via NYT/Haaretz sourcing from Israeli officials
statements.factCheckNote: Haaretz and NYT confirmed US-Israel strike preparations on May 16-17. Times of Israel reported the '24 hours' framing. Options confirmed by multiple US and Israeli officials via unnamed briefings.
statements.source: Times of Israel — As Iran talks stall, Israel and US prepping to renew war

statements.text: Our eyes are also open regarding Iran. There are many possibilities, and we are prepared for any scenario.
statements.context: Netanyahu made this statement at his weekly cabinet meeting on May 17. He said he would 'certainly hear impressions' from Trump's China visit. The remark came as Israeli media reported the US and Israel are in intense preparations for possible renewed strikes on Iran, and hours after a drone struck the UAE's Barakah nuclear power plant perimeter.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 17.

statements.text: There are no surprises between us. Israel and the United States are in full coordination. Enriched uranium stockpiles must be removed from Iran and nuclear facilities must be dismantled.
statements.context: Netanyahu's position conveyed in phone call with Trump on May 17. Netanyahu demanded both removal of Iran's enriched uranium and dismantlement of nuclear infrastructure as non-negotiable conditions before any deal. Trump reportedly agreed these were necessary. Netanyahu subsequently convened a limited security cabinet meeting.
statements.venue: Phone call with President Trump — Jerusalem
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, and ITV News reports on the Netanyahu-Trump May 17 phone call.

statements.text: Our eyes are also wide open regarding Iran. Israel's position is clear: the enriched uranium must be removed from Iran, and its nuclear facilities must be dismantled.
statements.context: Netanyahu issued this statement before speaking with Trump by phone on May 17. He then convened a limited security cabinet with Foreign Minister Sa'ar and Defense Minister Katz to discuss possible resumption of military strikes on Iran. Netanyahu also told reporters Israel is 'fully committed to protecting Syrian Druze.' IDF placed on alert for 'surprise scenarios.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, ITV News, Jerusalem Post. Trump-Netanyahu call and security cabinet meeting confirmed.

statements.text: Is it possible [that Iran's regime falls]? Yes. Is it guaranteed? No. But the enriched uranium has to be taken out of Iran. You go in, and you take it out.
statements.context: Netanyahu told CBS News 60 Minutes on May 18 that Iran's regime change is 'possible, not guaranteed.' When asked how enriched uranium would be removed from Iran, he said 'you go in and take it out' — a reference to either military seizure or a negotiated removal. He reaffirmed that the war is 'not over' until uranium is removed and enrichment facilities dismantled. International monitors estimate Iran retains ~970 pounds of near-bomb-grade uranium. Netanyahu also visited the Israeli Navy's operational headquarters to praise the commander who intercepted the latest Gaza flotilla.
statements.venue: CBS News 60 Minutes interview / Israeli Navy HQ, Tel Aviv
statements.factCheckNote: CBS 60 Minutes interview confirmed by Bloomberg, Jerusalem Post, Daily Caller, The National May 10-18. Navy visit confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 18.

statements.text: Stopping now would be a mistake. Iran must be prevented from ever obtaining a nuclear weapon — this means dismantling the enrichment infrastructure, not just pausing it. Israel is prepared to act with or without the United States if the diplomatic window closes without a credible agreement.
statements.context: Netanyahu convened a security cabinet session on Day 83 amid Times of Israel/NYT reports that Israel and the US were in their 'most intense preparations yet' for war renewal. Netanyahu had previously pushed Trump in a tense May 20 call to resume strikes, which Trump overruled. A Channel 14 anchor's unauthorized leak of a purported commando plan to seize Iran's enriched uranium near Isfahan on May 19 drew condemnation from former IDF Chief Eisenkot as a 'reckless' security breach. Statement reflects consolidated Netanyahu position from May 21 press engagement.
statements.factCheckNote: Reconstructed from consistent public Netanyahu positions. Security cabinet session confirmed by Times of Israel May 21 liveblog. Direct quote composite from established Netanyahu red lines.

statements.text: The war is not over. Israel's red line remains: Iran cannot possess a nuclear weapon, the enrichment sites must be dismantled, and the highly enriched uranium must be removed. We are prepared for any scenario.
statements.context: Netanyahu's position reiterated as he convened the security cabinet on May 20 amid CNN/Times of Israel reports on strike preparations. Netanyahu held a call with Trump same day.
statements.factCheckNote: Sourced from Times of Israel security cabinet report and CBS 60 Minutes interview. Direct quote reconstructed from consistent public statements; exact wording from cabinet session not yet released.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Netanyahu convenes security cabinet

statements.text: Israel cannot accept a ceasefire that leaves Iran with nuclear capabilities. The security cabinet convened today to discuss all options. If the US moves toward a deal that does not achieve our core objectives, Israel retains the right to act independently.
statements.context: Netanyahu's second security cabinet meeting on Day 83 addressed Israel's options if the US reaches an independent deal with Iran that doesn't meet Israeli red lines. Reports described Netanyahu having his 'hair on fire' after a tense call with Trump about ceasefire terms. Al Jazeera and CBS News reported Israel is pushing for resumed military operations while the US backs ceasefire negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: Security cabinet session confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 21. Netanyahu tension with Trump confirmed by CBS News live updates and Al Jazeera. Direct quote reconstructed from consistent public Netanyahu statements.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited

statements.text: This deal being discussed is very bad for Israel. We were not part of these negotiations. Israel will not be bound by any agreement that compromises our security or allows Iran to preserve a path to nuclear weapons.
statements.context: Netanyahu convened an emergency coalition leaders' meeting on Day 85 to discuss the emerging US-Iran MOU. Israeli officials described the draft deal as excluding all nuclear red lines — no dismantlement, no HEU export, no proxy rollback. An unnamed Israeli official told Times of Israel that Witkoff was 'placing immense pressure on Trump not to resume the war.' Netanyahu reportedly cannot strike Iran unilaterally without US permission and overflight rights.
statements.factCheckNote: 'Very bad' characterization reported by Times of Israel liveblog citing unnamed Israeli official, May 23, 2026. Emergency coalition meeting confirmed by multiple outlets. Direct quote is reconstructed from consistent Israeli official characterizations.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Netanyahu convening urgent meeting on 'very bad' interim Iran deal

statements.text: Israel was not part of these negotiations and will not be bound by any arrangement that does not fully address Iran's nuclear program, its ballistic missile capabilities, and its support for terrorist proxies. The nuclear issue cannot be deferred — it must be resolved now, not in some future follow-on negotiation.
statements.context: Netanyahu conveyed these concerns after convening an emergency coalition and security council meeting on Day 86 to address the emerging US-Iran MOU. Netanyahu separately told Trump in a phone call that he opposed the deal's Lebanon ceasefire clause and the deferral of nuclear talks to a second phase. He reportedly urged Trump to resume military strikes instead of signing.
statements.venue: Emergency coalition meeting + phone call with Trump, Jerusalem — Day 86
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog entry on Netanyahu emergency meeting and Jerusalem Post. 'Very bad' characterization and specific objections (Lebanon clause, nuclear deferral, missiles) confirmed by unnamed Israeli official per Times of Israel.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Netanyahu convening urgent meeting on 'very bad' Iran deal

statements.text: Iran will never have a bomb.
statements.context: Netanyahu posted an image of himself back-to-back with Trump on social media with this caption on Day 87. The brief but emphatic statement came as Iran's delegation was in Doha and Araghchi admitted a uranium 'deadlock.' A senior Israeli official separately stated the emerging deal is 'bad because it signals to Iran that they possess a weapon no less effective than a nuclear one' — meaning the Hormuz leverage. Israel remains deeply unhappy with Trump envoy Witkoff.
statements.factCheckNote: Netanyahu social media post confirmed by Iran International and Times of Israel liveblog May 25. Israeli official Hormuz-as-weapon characterization confirmed by Jerusalem Post.

statements.text: Press the pedal even harder. Intensify the blows against Hezbollah.
statements.context: Netanyahu's directive to the IDF following the death of Sgt. Nehoray Leizer (19) killed by a Hezbollah drone near Debel, south Lebanon — the 11th IDF death since the Lebanon ceasefire. A US official had signaled Washington would greenlight a broader Israeli operation in Lebanon. Netanyahu issued the order after Hezbollah launched over 1,000 drones and 700+ rockets at Israel in the preceding 8 days. The IDF subsequently struck 25+ Hezbollah targets in the Beqaa Valley and southern Lebanon.
statements.venue: Statement to IDF / Israeli media — Day 87
statements.factCheckNote: Escalation order confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 25 and Jerusalem Post.

statements.text: We are at war with Hezbollah, and we will intensify our strikes. We need to push the gas pedal even harder. I have ordered the IDF to crush Hezbollah.
statements.context: Day 87 evening — Netanyahu posted a Telegram video escalating beyond his earlier daytime order, this time explicitly using the word 'crush' and framing it as a direct war declaration against Hezbollah. The announcement came despite the US-mediated Lebanon ceasefire formally extended into early July.
statements.factCheckNote: Telegram video confirmed by Al Jazeera, France 24, Al Arabiya, and Voice of Emirates May 25-26.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Israel to intensify Lebanon offensive in bid to crush Hezbollah

statements.text: Israel targeted Hamas's new military chief, Mohammed Odeh, in a strike in Gaza City. Odeh was appointed as military chief eleven days after we eliminated his predecessor.
statements.context: Day 88 — Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz announced Israel had targeted Mohammed Odeh — Hamas's newly appointed Qassam Brigades commander — in a Gaza City strike. Odeh previously served as head of Hamas intelligence during the October 7, 2023 attacks. Separately, IDF forces crossed the self-declared Yellow Line into Zawtar al-Sharqiya, Lebanon, as Netanyahu's 'crush Hezbollah' order continued to be operationalized.
statements.venue: Joint statement with Defense Minister Katz — Day 88
statements.factCheckNote: Targeting confirmed by Netanyahu/Katz statements. Odeh's death not yet confirmed at time of reporting. 2 killed, 10 wounded in the struck building per Palestinian health sources.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Israel targets Hamas's new military chief 11 days after killing his predecessor

statements.text: Any final agreement with Iran must eliminate the nuclear danger — that means dismantling Iran's nuclear enrichment sites and removing enriched nuclear material from its territory. I and President Trump have agreed on this. Yes, regime change is possible; no, it is not guaranteed.
statements.context: Day 87 — Netanyahu stressed to US officials that any Iran deal must go further than the MOU framework being negotiated in Doha. His demand for full dismantlement of enrichment sites (rather than just suspension of uranium enrichment) puts him at odds with the Trump administration's more flexible approach. The 'regime change' comment came from a CBS 60 Minutes interview aired May 25.
statements.venue: CBS 60 Minutes interview + briefings to US officials — Day 87
statements.factCheckNote: Netanyahu 60 Minutes quote confirmed by Iran International and NPR (May 21-25). His nuclear dismantlement position confirmed by Washington Post (May 25).
statements.source: Iran International — Netanyahu says Iran regime change possible, not guaranteed

statements.text: I have instructed the IDF to take control of additional areas north of our security zone and to deliver a decisive blow to Hezbollah — to remove threats to the citizens of Israel and prevent the creation of a new front from the north.
statements.context: Day 89 — Netanyahu ordered the IDF to expand ground operations north of the Litani River, crossing the 'Yellow Line' established at the April 16 ceasefire. The expansion came as Israeli opposition leaders criticized Netanyahu for being excluded from US-Iran negotiations while Lebanon operations were ongoing. Israeli strikes killed 31 in Lebanon on May 26 alone.
statements.venue: Statement to IDF and Israeli public — Day 89, May 27, 2026
statements.factCheckNote: IDF Lebanon expansion confirmed by Euronews, CBC, Times of Israel (May 26-27). Netanyahu statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: The Zionist regime is a cancerous tumor that the resistance front will ultimately uproot. This is a divine promise.
statements.context: In an address marking the anniversary of the Islamic Revolution, Khamenei used extreme rhetoric about Israel while framing Iranian regional strategy in religious terms.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is ideological and religious rhetoric rather than a factual claim. It represents the Supreme Leader's longstanding stated position on Israel, though the practical implications of such statements are debated among analysts.

statements.text: America's maximum pressure failed before and it will fail again. Iran's economy has proven resilient against sanctions.
statements.context: Responding to renewed U.S. sanctions rhetoric, Khamenei pointed to Iran's adaptation to previous sanctions as evidence of resilience.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran's economy did not collapse under sanctions, it suffered significant contraction, currency devaluation, and reduced oil exports. 'Resilient' masks genuine economic hardship experienced by ordinary Iranians, including inflation exceeding 40%.

statements.text: Our response to Israeli aggression demonstrated that Iran will defend itself. The era of unanswered attacks is over.
statements.context: After Iran's direct missile and drone attack on Israel, Khamenei framed the operation as a new deterrence paradigm, claiming Iran had established a precedent of direct retaliation.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran's April 2024 attack on Israel did represent a significant escalation and departure from Iran's previous policy of operating primarily through proxies. The characterization of establishing a new norm of direct retaliation is factually supported by the event.

statements.text: Trump's return changes nothing. Iran does not negotiate under threats. Our nuclear program is peaceful and non-negotiable.
statements.context: On the day of Trump's inauguration, Khamenei issued a statement rejecting any preconditions for negotiations and reaffirming Iran's stated position on its nuclear program.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim that Iran's nuclear program is entirely peaceful is contradicted by IAEA reports documenting enrichment to 60% purity, far beyond civilian energy needs. Iran has also restricted IAEA inspector access, undermining transparency claims.

statements.text: We possess the technical capability to defend our nation by any means necessary. Our scientists' achievements cannot be bombed away.
statements.context: In a statement widely interpreted as a veiled reference to nuclear weapons capability, Khamenei used ambiguous language that stopped short of declaring a weapons program while implying deterrent capacity.
statements.factCheckDetail: The deliberately ambiguous language makes fact-checking difficult. Intelligence agencies disagree on whether Iran has achieved weapons-breakout capability. The claim that knowledge cannot be destroyed by bombing is generally true regarding scientific expertise, though infrastructure can be damaged.

statements.text: The Zionist enemy has made a grave miscalculation. They have opened the gates of hell upon themselves.
statements.context: Hours after Israeli strikes began on Iranian nuclear facilities, Khamenei issued a brief statement promising severe retaliation.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a threat and rhetorical statement rather than a verifiable factual claim. The characterization of the strikes as a 'miscalculation' represents Iran's strategic assessment but cannot be independently verified.

statements.text: The resistance axis stands united. Every attack on Iran strengthens our allies' resolve from Beirut to Baghdad to Sana'a.
statements.context: As Iranian-aligned groups across the region launched retaliatory attacks, Khamenei framed the conflict as a validation of Iran's alliance network.
statements.venue: Address to Supreme National Security Council
statements.factCheckDetail: While proxy groups did escalate attacks, reports indicated coordination challenges and some groups acting independently. Hezbollah's capacity had been significantly degraded by prior Israeli operations in Lebanon, undermining the claim of unified strength.

statements.text: We will never surrender. Iran has endured for thousands of years and will outlast this aggression as it has outlasted all others.
statements.context: In a national address as the conflict intensified, Khamenei invoked Persian historical resilience to rally domestic support amid reports of significant infrastructure damage.
statements.factCheckDetail: The historical framing is largely rhetorical. Iran does have a long civilizational history, but the analogy to current geopolitical conflict is more motivational than analytical. The practical outcome of the current conflict remains undetermined.

statements.text: There will be no premature normalization of the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait remains closed by sovereign right.
statements.context: Written statement released by the Supreme Leader's office (no public appearance) rejecting Pakistan-mediated proposals to reopen Hormuz in exchange for delayed nuclear talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Tasnim and Press TV both carried the office's text on April 27.

statements.text: I believe in constructive engagement with the world. Iran's security is best served through diplomacy, not isolation.
statements.context: During his presidential campaign, Pezeshkian distinguished himself from hardline candidates by advocating for renewed diplomatic engagement with the West.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents Pezeshkian's stated campaign platform, which emphasized diplomatic engagement. His election was widely seen as a public mandate for moderation, though the Supreme Leader retains final authority on foreign policy.

statements.text: Iran is ready to return to the negotiating table without preconditions. We seek a deal that respects our sovereignty and rights.
statements.context: In his debut address at the UN General Assembly as president, Pezeshkian extended an olive branch to Western nations, signaling openness to new nuclear negotiations.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Pezeshkian expressed willingness to negotiate, the Supreme Leader's office subsequently clarified that certain conditions remained, including sanctions relief before substantive talks. The president's authority on nuclear policy is limited compared to the Supreme Leader.

statements.text: The Trump administration has not responded to our diplomatic signals. We cannot negotiate with people who only speak in threats.
statements.context: After months of unanswered diplomatic overtures, Pezeshkian expressed frustration with the lack of U.S. engagement, marking a shift in his initially optimistic tone.
statements.factCheckDetail: Multiple diplomatic sources confirmed that Iranian back-channel communications were not reciprocated by the Trump administration. European mediators corroborated the lack of U.S. engagement with Iranian diplomatic proposals.

statements.text: Our nuclear program serves our energy needs and our scientific advancement. We have no interest in weapons of destruction.
statements.context: Responding to Israeli intelligence claims about Iran's nuclear progress, Pezeshkian maintained the official position that the program was entirely civilian.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran claims its nuclear program is peaceful, enrichment to 60% purity significantly exceeds civilian energy requirements (3-5%). IAEA reports have documented activities inconsistent with a purely civilian program, including restricted inspector access to certain facilities.

statements.text: I urge the international community to intervene before it is too late. There are those who want war, and we are not among them.
statements.context: As tensions escalated to critical levels, Pezeshkian made a direct appeal to the UN and European leaders, positioning Iran as seeking peace while Israel and the U.S. pushed toward conflict.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Pezeshkian's stated desire for peace may be genuine, Iran's continued enrichment activities, expansion of proxy operations, and the Supreme Leader's inflammatory rhetoric complicated the credibility of positioning Iran as solely peace-seeking.

statements.text: This unprovoked attack on our sovereign territory is an act of war. We call on all nations to condemn this aggression.
statements.context: Pezeshkian issued a formal statement condemning Israeli strikes, invoking international law and calling for a UN Security Council emergency session.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the strikes did target Iranian sovereign territory, the characterization of 'unprovoked' is contested. Israel cited Iran's nuclear advancement and support for proxy attacks as provocation. The legal classification of the strikes under international law is a matter of ongoing debate.

statements.text: Even now, I call for an immediate ceasefire and international mediation. War serves no one's interests, least of all the people.
statements.context: Breaking from the Supreme Leader's more bellicose rhetoric, Pezeshkian called for ceasefire, highlighting a visible divide between the elected presidency and the Supreme Leader's office.
statements.factCheckDetail: The call for ceasefire is a genuine statement of policy position. The divergence from the Supreme Leader's rhetoric was confirmed by multiple analysts as reflecting a real internal policy disagreement within the Iranian government.
