statements.text: Millions of Iranians are displaced and suffering. The humanitarian cost of this conflict demands an immediate end to hostilities.
statements.context: Pezeshkian cited humanitarian concerns as the basis for renewed ceasefire calls, publishing casualty and displacement figures that could not be independently verified.
statements.factCheckDetail: The specific displacement figures cited by Pezeshkian could not be independently verified due to restricted access for international humanitarian organizations. That significant civilian displacement has occurred is widely accepted, but precise numbers remain contested.

statements.text: Any ceasefire must include firm international guarantees that Iran will not be attacked again. We also demand compensation for the assault on our nation and recognition of Iran's right to a full nuclear fuel cycle.
statements.context: Day 12. Pezeshkian publicly laid out Iran's conditions for ending the war, marking the first formal ceasefire demands. Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed back-channel communications with Russia, China, and France. Bloomberg reported the demands are unlikely to be accepted by Washington or Jerusalem.
statements.factCheckDetail: Pezeshkian's conditions confirmed by Bloomberg via officials familiar with the matter; Deputy FM Gharibabadi's statements reported by multiple outlets independently

statements.text: Iran demands three conditions for peace: recognition of our sovereign rights, reparations for the destruction caused by US-Israeli aggression, and binding guarantees against future attacks.
statements.context: First formal articulation of Iran's demands for ending hostilities. Signals Iran open to talks but with high price. Comes as Iran launches largest coordinated attack with Hezbollah.

statements.text: Set terms for ending the war: recognition of Iran's rights, reparations for damage caused, and guarantees against future aggression by the US and Israel.
statements.context: Statement on ceasefire conditions
statements.factCheckNote: Terms reported by Al Jazeera and France 24.

statements.text: Iran has never pursued nuclear arms. We propose the establishment of an Islamic Parliament of the Middle East as a regional security structure.
statements.context: Nowruz message from Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. Called for regional unity and proposed a new 'Islamic Parliament of the Middle East.' The statement came amid reports that Pezeshkian admitted in closed conversations that he is 'completely excluded from military and strategic decisions.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's claim to have never pursued nuclear weapons contradicts longstanding IAEA concerns, enrichment to 60%+ levels, and Netanyahu's claims about nuclear program destruction. However, the specific nuclear fatwa Pezeshkian cites is documented.

statements.text: We do not need the foreigners' presence in the region. We can set up an Islamic assembly in the Middle East with the cooperation of Muslim states, and within that framework, regulate our security, economic, cultural and political relations.
statements.context: Nowruz (Persian New Year) address proposing a new regional security architecture. Pezeshkian called for an "Islamic Assembly of the Middle East" as a framework to replace foreign military presence. He rejected Israel's narrative and called Israel the real source of instability. Also reiterated Iran's rejection of nuclear weapons based on the Supreme Leader's religious fatwa.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ANI and Tribune India citing Iranian state media broadcast.

statements.text: There needs to be an immediate cessation of US-Israeli aggression. We call on BRICS to play an independent role. We can set up an Islamic Assembly in the Middle East with Muslim states to regulate security, economic, and political relations.
statements.context: Phone call with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Nowruz (Persian New Year). Pezeshkian proposed a regional security framework and called on BRICS nations to act independently of Western pressure.
statements.venue: Phone call with PM Narendra Modi of India
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al-Monitor and Madhyamam Online reporting on the Modi-Pezeshkian call.

statements.text: You are our brothers, and we have no dispute with you. We seek to live in peace with our neighbors.
statements.context: Eid al-Fitr message to Islamic and neighboring countries on Day 23. Signals Iran is seeking to prevent the conflict from expanding regionally while continuing to fight US and Israel.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Capital News Point and regional media.

statements.text: The illusion of erasing Iran from the map shows desperation against the will of a history-making nation. Threats and terror only strengthen our unity. The Strait of Hormuz is open to all except those who violate our soil. We firmly confront delirious threats on the battlefield.
statements.context: Posted on X in response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum threatening to obliterate Iranian power plants if Hormuz is not opened. Also called on BRICS to 'play an independent role in halting aggressions against Iran.'
statements.factCheckNote: Post confirmed by Tribune India and Al Jazeera Day 23 explainer.

statements.text: The statements of the US president are made in the context of attempts to reduce energy prices and buy time to implement their military plans. There have been no negotiations with Washington.
statements.context: Pezeshkian Telegram post directly responding to Trump's claim of productive US-Iran talks. Called Trump's announcement an attempt to manipulate oil markets.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran denies all contact with US. However, Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt are known intermediaries — whether formal contact occurred through these channels remains disputed.

statements.text: Iran did not begin this atrocious war. Any end to the fighting is meaningless until we ensure there will be no more attacks, and that our legitimate rights are restored. We insist on war reparations and firm international guarantees. We will not accept a ceasefire that leaves Iran humiliated and unprotected.
statements.context: Statement to French President Macron during a phone call. Pezeshkian also apologized to neighboring Gulf states for damage caused by IRGC attacks — though the IRGC continued attacks independently, exposing a significant civilian-military leadership rift.
statements.venue: Phone call with French President Macron
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera and Middle East Monitor. IRGC-presidential rift analysis confirmed by multiple regional analysts.
statements.source: Middle East Monitor — Pezeshkian sets terms for ceasefire talks

statements.text: Iran seeks a complete end to the war and wants to ensure security and peace in West Asia. We consider the US-Israeli actions against our country unlawful and inhumane.
statements.context: Phone call with Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim. Pezeshkian praised Malaysia's "principled stance" condemning the attacks. Iran simultaneously announced Malaysia's ships are approved for Hormuz transit — following Spain earlier in the day. Iran allowing ships from non-hostile nations while blocking US/Israeli-linked vessels.
statements.venue: Phone call with Malaysian PM Anwar Ibrahim
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Brunei News Net and Al Jazeera reporting on Malaysia-Iran relations during the war.

statements.text: Talking to leaders of Russia and Pakistan, I reaffirmed Iran's commitment to peace in the region. The only way to end this war — ignited by the Zionist regime and the United States — is recognizing Iran's legitimate rights, payment of reparations, and firm international guarantees against future aggression.
statements.context: X post following calls with Russian and Pakistani leaders, ahead of Islamabad peace summit
statements.factCheckNote: Pezeshkian's X post confirmed. Calls with Russia and Pakistan PM confirmed by both sides.
statements.source: Pezeshkian on X via Pakistan Today

statements.text: Iran needs trust and credible guarantees before any talks can succeed. Pakistan's mediation is valuable — but the window for diplomacy requires all parties to act in good faith. Iran has laid out its conditions: recognition of legitimate rights, payment of reparations, and firm guarantees against future aggression.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif on Day 29, discussing Islamabad peace summit and Iran's conditions for ceasefire negotiations
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistani PM Sharif
statements.factCheckNote: Call confirmed by Iranian state media and Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: Iran does not seek nuclear weapons. We have participated in a one-hour call with Prime Minister Sharif. We are open to discussions that respect Iranian sovereignty and result in a permanent end to all attacks. Peace is possible if the conditions are right.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif during Islamabad peace summit preparations; Pezeshkian reportedly signaling Iran could consider ending the war if certain conditions are met, in contrast to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's hardline stance
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistan PM / Iranian state media
statements.factCheckNote: Phone call confirmed by Pakistan PM Sharif and Iranian state media. Pezeshkian signals mark a divergence from the Supreme Leader position on negotiations per CNN analysis.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war live Day 30

statements.text: Iran's economy could collapse within 3-4 weeks without a ceasefire. Civilian government authority must be restored.
statements.context: Privately warned IRGC Chief Ahmad Vahidi of economic collapse. IRGC rejected demand. Inflation at ~120%, ATMs empty, internet at 4%.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Iran International citing unnamed sources. Cannot be independently confirmed due to secrecy of internal meetings.

statements.text: Iran has the necessary will to end the war if essential conditions are met, especially guarantees required to prevent repetition of aggression. European intervention would have dangerous consequences.
statements.context: Phone call with European Council President Antonio Costa. Also warned economy could collapse in 3 weeks to 1 month without ceasefire.
statements.venue: Phone call with EU Council President Costa
statements.factCheckNote: Euronews and CNN confirmed. Separately, Iran International reported serious rift with IRGC chief Vahidi.

statements.text: The Iranian people harbor no enmity toward other nations, including the people of America, Europe or neighboring countries. Which interests are being served by this war? Not America First.
statements.context: Open letter to the American people published via Bloomberg and Dawn. Strongest ceasefire signal yet from Tehran — rejected unconditional surrender, called war 'not serving US interests,' framed Iran's actions as 'legitimate self-defense.'
statements.factCheckNote: Full text published by Bloomberg, Dawn. Confirmed by Al Jazeera, US News.

statements.text: I urge the American people to look beyond the machinery of misinformation. Trump's threat to send Iran back to the Stone Age is an admission of intent to commit a massive war crime. Continuing along the path of confrontation is more costly and futile than ever before.
statements.context: Response to Trump's 'Stone Age' threats, calling them a war crime admission. Published on X as continuation of diplomatic messaging to American public.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and ABC News confirmed Pezeshkian's statements.

statements.text: I call on international health organizations to urgently respond to the criminal targeting of our medical facilities. The destruction of the Pasteur Institute — a century of medical achievement — is a war crime against all of humanity.
statements.context: Appeal after US/Israeli strikes hit Pasteur Institute in Tehran
statements.factCheckNote: Anadolu Agency and Al Jazeera confirmed Pezeshkian appeal. WHO verified 20+ attacks on Iran healthcare since March 1.

statements.text: The Iranian nation has steadfast determination to defend its sovereignty. This anniversary has taken on a deeper meaning than in previous years.
statements.context: 47th anniversary of the Islamic Republic of Iran amid ongoing war
statements.factCheckNote: GlobalSecurity/PressTV confirmed.

statements.text: Trump's threats are born of sheer desperation and anger. The Strait of Hormuz will open when all the damage caused by the imposed war is compensated through a new legal regime.
statements.context: Via presidential spokesperson, responding to Trump's "take out entire country" press conference.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by NPR, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: I am ready to sacrifice my life for Iran. More than 14 million Iranians have declared their readiness to do the same through state media and text message campaigns.
statements.context: Posted on X platform as Trump's 8PM ET deadline approached. Human chains forming around power plants.
statements.factCheckNote: Jerusalem Post, CBS News confirmed.

statements.text: I too have been, am, and will remain devoted to giving my life for Iran. More than 14 million proud Iranians have declared their readiness to sacrifice their lives.
statements.context: Social media post during ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: PBS confirmed.

statements.text: The ceasefire, with the acceptance of Iran's key principles, is the result of the blood of our martyred leader, Ayatollah Khamenei, and the achievement of the presence of all the people on the scene.
statements.context: Statement on ceasefire, confirming Iran will participate in Islamabad talks Friday
statements.factCheckNote: Press TV confirmed statement and Islamabad talks confirmation.

statements.text: The ceasefire is the result of the blood of our martyred leader, Ayatollah Khamenei, and the achievement of the presence of all the people on the scene. From today, we will continue to stand together, whether in the field of diplomacy, defense, on the streets, or in the realm of service.
statements.context: Statement praising ceasefire as national achievement
statements.factCheckNote: Press TV, Tribune India confirmed.

statements.text: This ceasefire is the result of the blood of our martyred leader, Ayatollah Khamenei, and the achievement of the presence of all the people on the scene. From today, we will continue to stand together, whether in diplomacy, defense, on the streets, or service.
statements.context: Social media statement praising ceasefire. Separately called Macron to discuss Lebanon inclusion as 'central condition.'
statements.venue: Social Media / Phone call with Macron
statements.factCheckNote: Press TV confirmed both the statement and Macron call.

statements.text: The ceasefire, with the acceptance of Iran's key principles, is the result of the blood of our martyred leader, Ayatollah Khamenei. From today, we will continue to stand together, whether in the field of diplomacy, defense, on the streets, or in the realm of service.
statements.context: Statement reaffirming Iran's unity following ceasefire
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV, UNN

statements.text: Israeli strikes on Lebanon violate the temporary truce and imperil the upcoming peace talks. A ceasefire that does not include Lebanon is no ceasefire at all.
statements.context: Pezeshkian condemned continued Israeli strikes on Lebanon, insisting ceasefire must include Lebanon. Aligned with Araghchi's position.
statements.factCheckNote: Accurate that Israel explicitly excluded Lebanon from the US-Iran ceasefire and continued striking Lebanon. Netanyahu confirmed Lebanon 'not included.'

statements.text: The biggest obstacle to reaching a fair agreement was the double standards and hegemonic approach of the American side. Iran is fully prepared to reach a balanced and fair agreement. If America is committed to the frameworks of international law, a deal is not unreachable.
statements.context: Pezeshkian called Putin after Islamabad talks collapsed. Blamed US 'excessive demands.' Putin supported Iran's demand for war reparations and offered to mediate.
statements.venue: Phone call with Putin — post-Islamabad collapse
statements.factCheckNote: PressTV and Moscow Times both confirmed the call and statements.

statements.text: We emphasise constructive dialogue, but will not be forced to surrender. Any attempt to impose will or force Iran to surrender is doomed to failure, and the people will never accept such an approach. Iran is not seeking war. With what authorisation and for what crime was the attack on our country carried out?
statements.context: Statement on Day 2 of US naval blockade. Also demanded $270B in war compensation from US, Israel, and 5 Arab states.
statements.factCheckNote: ANI News, Al Jazeera confirmed statement and $270B demand.

statements.text: During this war, powers with extensive claims and prestige stood against the Iranian nation, but the people's steadfastness caused these currents to inevitably retreat. We must produce effective narratives about the realities and achievements created during the 40-day resistance of the Iranian nation.
statements.context: Speaking at Ministry of Culture and Islamic Guidance. Framing the war as a victory narrative for domestic consumption despite ongoing blockade and destruction.
statements.venue: Tehran — Ministry of Culture and Islamic Guidance
statements.factCheckNote: Victory claim disputed — war in ceasefire, not resolved. Iran under full US naval blockade. Confirmed by Press TV, ANI News, Pars Today.

statements.text: Iran harbours no enmity toward ordinary Americans. Portraying Iran as a threat is inconsistent with history and current facts. Our military response was a measured response grounded in legitimate self-defense. Attacks on Iran's infrastructure will have consequences beyond its borders.
statements.context: Second open letter to the American people. Published as US-Iran talks expected Monday in Islamabad. Iran state media walked back Araghchi's Hormuz opening — says he 'misspoke.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's characterization of its military actions as 'purely defensive' is contested. Iran closed Hormuz, launched hundreds of missiles at Israel, and armed proxies. Confirmed by Iran LiveUAMap, Tribune India.

statements.text: Trump has no right to deny Iran nuclear rights. Iran has no intention of attacking any country — we are exercising our legitimate and legal right to self-defense. We do not want to escalate the war and seek to end it with dignity. I apologize to our neighbors: they will no longer be targeted unless attacks originate from them.
statements.context: Afternoon address ahead of Monday Islamabad talks. Direct acknowledgment of prior strikes on Qatar, Kuwait, Iraq from Iranian soil. Reasserts Iran's NPT nuclear rights.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Capital News Point, Middle East Monitor, News on Air. Characterization of all Iranian actions as self-defense is disputed.

statements.text: The US has demonstrated bullying and unreasonable behaviour throughout this conflict. Their actions — seizing our ships, imposing a blockade, making excessive demands — have increased our suspicion that America will betray diplomacy. We seek peace, but not at the cost of our sovereignty.
statements.context: 45-minute phone call with Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif. Discussed Touska seizure and upcoming Islamabad talks. Hours later IRNA announced Iran would not attend Round 2.
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistan PM Sharif
statements.factCheckNote: Pakistan Today and Free Press Journal confirmed the call and statements.

statements.text: Trump has no right to deny Iran nuclear rights. Iran has no intention of attacking any country and is exercising its legitimate and legal right to self-defense. Iran does not want to escalate the war and seeks to end it with dignity.
statements.context: Statement defending Iran's nuclear program while positioning Iran as acting in self-defense. Also said neighboring countries will not be targeted 'unless an attack originates from there.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's nuclear program exceeds JCPOA limits. Iran re-closed Hormuz after brief opening and IRGC attacked Indian tankers — contradicting 'no intention of attacking.'

statements.text: War is in no one's interests. We call for a return to diplomacy to reduce tensions. Trump says Iran cannot make use of its nuclear rights, but doesn't say for what crime. Who is he to deprive a nation of its rights?
statements.context: Apr 20 statement rejecting US preconditions. Rebuke comes as ceasefire approaches April 22 expiry. Iran FM had just announced 'no plans' for Round 2 Islamabad talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Middle East Monitor, Voice of Emirates, Press TV. Consistent with Pezeshkian's prior public positioning.

statements.text: Honoring commitments is the basis of meaningful dialogue. Deep historical mistrust in Iran toward U.S. gov conduct remains, while unconstructive & contradictory signals from American officials carry a bitter message; they seek Iran's surrender. Iranians do not submit to force.
statements.context: X (Twitter) post directly addressing US negotiating posture. Posted same day as Iran FM confirmed no plans for Round 2 talks. Asserts Iran will not capitulate under military pressure.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Tribune India, Middle East Monitor.

statements.text: Deep historical mistrust in Iran toward US government conduct remains, while unconstructive and contradictory signals from American officials carry a bitter message — they seek Iran's surrender. Today, we are endeavouring to manage these difficult circumstances through prudence, wisdom, and the preservation of national honour.
statements.context: Statement on eve of ceasefire expiry. Contradicted by Karoline Leavitt's claim that US is 'never been so close to a truly good deal.' Iran supreme leader gave last-minute approval for Islamabad talks delegation.
statements.factCheckNote: US signals have indeed been contradictory — Leavitt claims deal proximity while Trump threatens 'lots of bombs.'

statements.text: US breach of commitments and its blockade of Iranian ports and threats are the main obstacles to genuine negotiations. Honoring commitments is the basis of meaningful dialogue.
statements.context: Remarks on ceasefire extension and conditions for resumed talks. Echoed by Araghchi's 'act of war' framing and Iran's decision to boycott Islamabad talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Times of Israel liveblog Apr 22 and Voice of Emirates Apr 19 reporting related remarks.

statements.text: The world sees your endless hypocritical rhetoric and contradiction between claims and actions. Iran welcomes dialogue and agreement but breach of commitments, blockade and threats are the main obstacles to genuine negotiations.
statements.context: Online statement accusing the US of inconsistency. Made as Trump extended ceasefire but maintained blockade. Pezeshkian also stated Trump 'cannot deprive Iran of its nuclear rights.'
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by NPR, Times of Israel. Pezeshkian's nuclear rights assertion reported by Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: In Iran, there are no radicals or moderates; we are all 'Iranian' and 'revolutionary,' and with the iron unity of the nation and government, with complete obedience to the Supreme Leader, we will make the aggressor criminal regret his actions.
statements.context: Coordinated unity statement with FM Araghchi, Speaker Qalibaf, VP Aref, Chief Justice Eje'i — all issued within 30 minutes. Response to Trump's claims of Iranian leadership 'fractures.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Bloom Pakistan.

statements.text: Iran will not enter imposed negotiations under pressure, threats, or blockade. The hostile actions of the United States are inconsistent with its stated claims of pursuing a political resolution. Instead of 10 lights, two lights should be turned on in the house.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistani PM Sharif rejecting talks under duress. Separately urged Iranians to conserve electricity, warning US/Israel aim to sow 'dissatisfaction.'
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistan PM / Public address
statements.factCheckNote: PressTV, Pakistan Today. Electricity comments confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: The United States must learn its historic lesson from crushing defeats in Tabas and Isfahan. Iran will not negotiate under threats or siege.
statements.context: Social media post invoking the failed 1980 US Operation Eagle Claw rescue mission in Tabas and a referenced encounter at Isfahan. Comes amid US naval blockade and Trump rejecting revised Iranian proposal.
statements.factCheckNote: PressTV. Confirmed.

statements.text: Iran will not enter into forced negotiations while facing pressure and threats. Washington must first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistan PM Sharif. Pezeshkian reiterated Iran's precondition that the US blockade must be lifted before talks resume. Also called on Iranian citizens to reduce electricity consumption amid the blockade.
statements.factCheckNote: Press TV, Express Tribune. Confirmed.

statements.text: Iran remains open to honest dialogue but will not negotiate at gunpoint. Goldman Sachs price forecasts are psychological warfare, not market analysis.
statements.context: Cabinet remarks aired on state TV IRIB rejecting Goldman's $90 Q4 Brent forecast as economic intimidation, while keeping the door open to mediated talks.
statements.factCheckNote: IRNA carried the cabinet readout on April 27.

statements.text: Iran will not enter into negotiations under pressure, threats or siege. Washington cannot pursue negotiations while increasing pressure on Iran — such actions disrupt the necessary atmosphere for diplomacy.
statements.context: Criticised US 'maritime restrictions' as 'clear breach of ceasefire understandings' inconsistent with the UN Charter. Warned renewed US-Israel confrontation 'could have serious consequences for both regional and global stability.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Nation (Pakistan) / Mehr News Agency.

statements.text: The United States should first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade, before any new round of negotiations can occur. Without that, talks will not produce results.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistani PM Sharif on April 28 in which Pezeshkian set conditions for resuming talks with Washington after Rubio's rejection of Iran's Hormuz proposal. Sharif had been mediating an indirect channel.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via Boston Globe US-Iran war updates blog.

statements.text: What is being done under the guise of a naval blockade is an extension of military operations… Continuation of this oppressive approach is intolerable. Any attempt to impose a maritime blockade or restrictions is contrary to international law and is doomed to fail. Iran will respond.
statements.context: Persian Gulf Day statement posted on X. Pezeshkian condemns US naval blockade as illegal warfare and warns of Iranian response if continued.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Gulf Today, ANI News, BSS.

statements.text: Any attempt to impose a naval blockade and restrictions is contrary to international law... and is doomed to failure. The continuation of this unjust approach is intolerable.
statements.context: Persian Gulf National Day speech, May 1. Reiterated condemnation of US blockade and called it 'extension of military operations.'
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Tribune India, Pajhwok cross-verified.

statements.text: Iran will not hold talks under pressure, threats, or blockade.
statements.context: Told Pakistan PM Sharif that Iran refuses to negotiate under duress. Also reportedly seeking to oust FM Araghchi for taking orders from IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi without presidential authorization.
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistan PM Sharif
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed. Iran International, Jerusalem Post confirmed Araghchi ouster reports.

statements.text: The IRGC strikes on the UAE are completely irresponsible and carried out without the government's knowledge or coordination. This is madness with potentially irreversible consequences.
statements.context: Pezeshkian publicly condemned IRGC's missile/drone attacks on UAE as unauthorized. Requested emergency meeting with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei to halt attacks on Gulf states. Deepest public rift between civilian government and IRGC since war began.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran International confirmed Pezeshkian statement and emergency meeting request.

statements.text: The US is pursuing a policy of maximum pressure and expects Iran to submit to their unilateral demands. This is impossible.
statements.context: Pezeshkian publicly rejected US negotiating stance, directly contradicting Trump's claim of 'great progress' toward a deal. Statement comes as Araghchi meets Wang Yi in Beijing and Trump pauses Project Freedom.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera liveblog May 6 confirmed.

statements.text: The IRGC attacks on the UAE were madness and completely irresponsible. They were carried out without the government's knowledge. If they speak to us with logic, dialogue is possible.
statements.context: Pezeshkian publicly branded IRGC strikes on UAE as unauthorized, revealing deepest internal rift between Iran's civilian government and military since war began. Also spoke with Iraq's new PM-designate.
statements.venue: Phone call with Iraqi PM-designate / public remarks
statements.factCheckNote: Iran International confirmed. Press TV corroborated broader remarks.

statements.text: Our problem is that on the one hand, America is pursuing a policy of maximum pressure against our country, and on the other hand, it expects Iran to come to the negotiating table and submit to their unilateral demands. Such an equation is impossible.
statements.context: Pezeshkian rejected US approach as contradictory — maximum pressure combined with demands for negotiation. Stated Iran is 'ready for any dialogue' but 'will never submit to force.' Comments came as Iran reviewing US 14-point MOU proposal.
statements.factCheckNote: Dawn and Iran International confirmed.

statements.text: I recently met with the Supreme Leader for two and a half hours. The atmosphere was humble and deeply cordial.
statements.context: Pezeshkian confirmed first known in-person meeting with Mojtaba Khamenei since his appointment as Supreme Leader in March. Provided no details on timing, location, or substance discussed. Statement serves as first proof of life for the reclusive new Supreme Leader.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Arabiya and Euronews. No independent verification of meeting details.

statements.text: Lifting the US naval blockade is a precondition for any negotiations on Strait of Hormuz transit. Iran is ready to pursue diplomacy if our rights are safeguarded.
statements.context: Pezeshkian conditioned engagement on the 14-point MOU. Position aligns with hardliner consolidation under IRGC commander Vahidi (per ISW). Comes a day after Pezeshkian called Trump's policy 'stabbing Iran in the back' in a call with French President Macron.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Voice of Emirates, National Herald India.

statements.text: Washington's conduct has diverted the path of diplomacy toward threats, pressure and sanctions. We no longer trust Washington. Claims of resignations and rifts are fake news — I and IRGC commanders make decisions in joint meetings.
statements.context: In call with French President Macron, Pezeshkian accused US of 'stabbing from behind.' Also pushed back on Iran International reports of internal power struggles. Macron told Pezeshkian that attacks on UAE infrastructure and Hormuz ships were 'unjustified.'
statements.venue: Phone call with Macron / ISNA statement
statements.factCheckNote: ANI confirmed call quotes. Al Jazeera confirmed unity messaging.

statements.text: We will never bow down to the enemy, and if any talk of dialogue or negotiation arises, it does not mean surrender or retreat. We will defend our national interests with strength.
statements.context: Posted on X as Iran submitted 10-point response to US peace proposal via Pakistan. Also criticized US for pursuing 'a policy of maximum pressure' while expecting Iran at the negotiating table.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Press TV and DAWN.

statements.text: Iran urges BRICS to defend nations against monopoly, sanctions and coercion. The BRICS platform must speak with one voice against unilateral economic pressure that violates international law.
statements.context: FM Araghchi delivered these messages at the BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting in New Delhi. Pezeshkian endorsed the BRICS approach as Iran sought a united bloc statement condemning US/Israeli actions. India, as BRICS chair, struggled to forge consensus given deep divisions between Iran and UAE members.
statements.venue: BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting, New Delhi (via FM Araghchi)
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV and BusinessWorld. Araghchi arrived Delhi May 13 for the meeting.

statements.text: Our beloved people have, in an almost unbelievable way, ensured the security of our country. They have shown the world the firmness, unity, and solidarity of our nation. National unity is the key to overcoming hardship.
statements.context: Pezeshkian released a video praising Iran's resilience during what he called the 'Third Imposed War' — the 40-day US-Israeli conflict — and subsequent reconstruction period. He honored doctors, engineers, soldiers, teachers, and workers for their contributions. The statement comes as Iran continues ceasefire negotiations with the US through Pakistani mediators.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV on May 14, 2026.

statements.text: We will not bow our heads before the enemy. If talk of dialogue or negotiation arises, it does not mean surrender or retreat. The goal is to uphold our rights and national interests. Lifting the blockade is our condition for any talks on the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Pezeshkian public statements following his reported 2.5-hour private meeting with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Set explicit precondition that the US naval blockade must be fully lifted before Iran will enter Strait of Hormuz talks. Iran International separately reported Pezeshkian privately called IRGC attacks on UAE "madness" — in contrast to his public defiance posture.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Voice of Emirates and PressTV. IRNA cited statement on blockade-lifting precondition. Iran International reported private "madness" comment.

statements.text: We will not bow. I will never bow before any power. We will not sacrifice the country's dignity for the sake of comfort-seeking and our own worldly desires, but we must manage the country with prudence and wisdom. We are fighting, and we must accept the hardships that come with it.
statements.context: Speaking at a gathering of public relations directors from executive branch agencies. Pezeshkian called the conflict the 'Third Imposed War' and prepared Iranians for continued shortages and inflation. The statement came as Iran submitted a revised peace proposal to the US via Pakistani mediators, and as Trump issued a 'Clock is Ticking' escalation warning.
statements.venue: Gathering of public relations directors, executive branch agencies, Tehran
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV report and Iran International. Consistent with Pezeshkian's pattern of public defiance while pursuing back-channel diplomacy.

statements.text: Iran's right to enrichment is recognized under the NPT... This right already exists. Rights are not something we are to negotiate or compromise over.
statements.context: Pezeshkian publicly staked out a firm position on nuclear enrichment rights as US-Iran negotiations via Pakistan reached a critical impasse over the duration of a potential enrichment freeze. The US was demanding a 20-year moratorium; Iran offered 5 years. His statement made clear Iran views enrichment as a non-negotiable right rather than a concession. Trump responded hours later by calling off a planned military strike but keeping military options active.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog entry May 18 and Iran International reporting on nuclear rights stance.

statements.text: Dialogue does not mean surrender. The Islamic Republic of Iran enters into dialogue with dignity, authority, and the preservation of the nation's rights, and under no circumstances will it retreat from the legal rights of the people and the country.
statements.context: Pezeshkian's second major May 18 statement distinguished Iran's back-channel diplomatic engagement from capitulation — a framing designed for both domestic and international audiences. It came as Pakistan's FM Mohsin Naqvi personally facilitated a new Iranian written response to US proposals. The statement signaled Iran would continue talks but would not accept US demands for a full enrichment freeze as a condition of ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV May 18 and Al Jazeera May 19 liveblog coverage of Iran negotiating posture.

statements.text: If there is any talk of dialogue or negotiation, it does not mean surrender or backing down, but rather the goal is to secure the rights of the Iranian nation and powerfully defend national interests.
statements.context: Pezeshkian's May 19 post on X (Persian) reinforcing that Iran's engagement with US proposals through Pakistan is from a position of strength, not weakness. He met with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and confirmed the Supreme Leader is alive and well amid persistent health rumor reports.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by JNS, Jerusalem Post, and Tribune India. Statement is consistent with Pezeshkian's May 18 'dialogue does not mean surrender' framing — each reiteration is slightly different in wording.
statements.source: JNS — Iranian president: Dialogue does not mean surrender

statements.text: All paths to a diplomatic solution remain open from our side. Forcing Iran to surrender through coercion is nothing but an illusion. We will never bow our heads before the enemy, and if talk of dialogue or negotiation arises, it does not mean surrender or retreat.
statements.context: Day 83 morning statement as Pakistan Interior Minister Naqvi arrived in Tehran for two-day mediation and message exchanges with the US continued. Pezeshkian framed Iran's diplomatic posture as strength-based, not concession-driven. Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed Iran is 'reviewing' the latest US proposal while rejecting any framing of the talks as capitulation.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera May 21 liveblog. Consistent with Pezeshkian's May 18-19 statements but with new specific framing of 'all paths remain open' alongside the anti-surrender language.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war live: Tehran says no surrender, diplomacy is wiser than war

statements.text: The Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran demonstrated the country's defensive authority during the recent developments and threats through high operational readiness, intelligence superiority, command cohesion, and effective combat capability. We will use all available capacities to modernize and strengthen our armed forces.
statements.context: Pezeshkian pledged full government support for the military while meeting Army Commander Major General Amir Hatami, signaling continued military build-up even as back-channel diplomacy proceeds.
statements.venue: Meeting with Army Commander Major General Amir Hatami, Tehran
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by PressTV, May 21, 2026.
statements.source: PressTV — Pezeshkian commits full capacity to modernization of Iran Armed Forces

statements.text: If there is any talk of dialogue or negotiation, it does not mean surrender or backing down. The goal is to secure the rights of the Iranian nation and powerfully defend national interests. Iran is ready to end the war but wants guarantees.
statements.context: Pezeshkian made these remarks during a meeting with Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir in Tehran on May 23. Foreign Minister Araghchi was also present. The meeting was part of an intensified Pakistan-Qatar-Oman mediation effort as the US considers resuming strikes. Munir met separately with Araghchi and Ghalibaf on the same day.
statements.venue: Meeting with Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir, Tehran, May 23, 2026
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Tribune India and Times of Israel liveblog. Pakistan Army confirmed Munir's meetings with all three Iranian officials.
statements.source: Tribune India — Pakistan Army Chief Munir, Iran FM Araghchi hold talks in Tehran

statements.text: Iran is ready to assure the world that we are not seeking nuclear weapons. The one seeking to destabilize the region is Israel, not Iran. We are not seeking unrest in the region. Any decision will require the approval of the Supreme Leader and the Supreme National Security Council.
statements.context: Pezeshkian made this nuclear assurance statement to IRIB state broadcaster on Day 86 as Trump-Iran deal negotiations reached a critical juncture. His caveat that any decision requires Khamenei's approval was widely noted as a reminder that the Supreme Leader — not the president — holds ultimate authority over Iran's nuclear stance.
statements.venue: IRIB state television interview, Tehran — Day 86
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Times of Israel Day 86 liveblog and CNN Iran war live updates May 24.

statements.text: The Islamic Republic will under no circumstances surrender to pressure and excessive demands.
statements.context: Pezeshkian's Day 87 statement came as his FM Araghchi was in Doha admitting a 'deadlock' on Iran's enriched uranium stockpile. The firm statement was interpreted as a signal to domestic audiences that Iran was not capitulating — and to the US that the uranium issue remains a red line.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by CBS News live updates and NBC News May 25.
statements.source: CBS News Live — Iran war US peace talks

statements.text: I am ordering the restoration of international internet access for the Iranian people. The 87-day blackout has served its purpose. Iranians deserve to reconnect with the world.
statements.context: Day 87 — Pezeshkian ordered Iran's international internet restored after an unprecedented 87-day wartime blackout that began February 28, Day 1 of the war. The blackout was the longest national internet shutdown on record. The Ministry of Communications had not yet provided a reconnection timeline. Analysts saw the order as signaling Iranian government preparation for post-conflict normalization, and as a domestic pressure-relief measure while Araghchi leads MOU talks in Doha.
statements.factCheckNote: Internet restoration order confirmed by Sunday Guardian Live and Haaretz live blog May 25.
statements.source: Sunday Guardian Live — Iran moves to restore global internet access after 87-day blackout

statements.text: We are ready to assure the world that we are not seeking nuclear weapons and are not seeking unrest in the region. Israel, not Iran, is the main source of war and insecurity in West Asia. Our negotiators will never retreat from the country's dignity and honor.
statements.context: Day 86 — Pezeshkian reaffirmed Iran's non-nuclear-weapons posture during a visit to Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting (IRIB), amid active MOU negotiations in Doha. The statement was a direct attempt to reassure the international community while domestic hardliners signaled resistance to any deal that compromised Iranian sovereignty.
statements.venue: Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting (IRIB), Tehran
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by PressTV (May 24) and Global Security.org.
statements.source: PressTV — Pezeshkian: Iran ready to assure world not seeking nuclear weapons

statements.text: The erosion of the nuclear nonproliferation framework is deeply alarming. The JCPOA remains the best available path to preventing a nuclear crisis.
statements.context: In his annual report on disarmament, Guterres warned about the consequences of the JCPOA's collapse and urged renewed diplomatic engagement.
statements.factCheckDetail: The assessment that the JCPOA framework was eroding is supported by IAEA reports documenting reduced monitoring access and increased Iranian enrichment. The characterization of the JCPOA as the best available path reflects the consensus view of the nonproliferation community.

statements.text: I condemn the Iranian attack on Israel and call on all parties to step back from the brink. The Middle East cannot afford another war.
statements.context: Guterres responded to Iran's direct attack on Israel with a call for de-escalation, attempting to maintain the UN's position of neutrality while condemning the specific act of aggression.
statements.venue: UN Headquarters Press Statement, New York
statements.factCheckDetail: The condemnation and the characterization of the regional security situation are consistent with documented events. The call for restraint represents the official UN position.

statements.text: The failure of diplomacy is not inevitable. It is a choice made by leaders who prefer confrontation to compromise.
statements.context: Guterres expressed growing frustration with the lack of diplomatic engagement between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, placing responsibility on political leaders.
statements.venue: Address to UN General Assembly Special Session
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a normative statement reflecting the Secretary-General's assessment of diplomatic dynamics. While the characterization of diplomatic failure as a political choice is shared by many analysts, others argue structural factors beyond individual leaders' choices contributed to the breakdown.

statements.text: I am deeply concerned by reports of near-weapons-grade enrichment. I urge Iran to cooperate fully with IAEA inspectors.
statements.context: Responding to an IAEA report indicating enrichment levels approaching weapons-grade, Guterres issued a pointed statement calling for transparency.
statements.factCheckDetail: The IAEA did report enrichment levels nearing weapons-grade. Guterres's call for cooperation with inspectors reflected the official UN position and was supported by the IAEA Board of Governors' own resolutions.

statements.text: Military strikes on nuclear facilities risk catastrophic environmental and humanitarian consequences. I call for an immediate cessation of hostilities.
statements.context: Within hours of the first Israeli strikes, Guterres issued an urgent statement warning about the potential for radiological contamination and calling for a ceasefire.
statements.venue: Emergency UN Press Conference, New York
statements.factCheckDetail: The warning about environmental and humanitarian consequences of striking nuclear facilities is supported by scientific assessments from the IAEA and independent nuclear safety experts. Radiological contamination from damaged enrichment facilities is a documented risk.

statements.text: The Security Council's inability to act is a failure of the international system. Civilians are dying while permanent members exercise vetoes.
statements.context: After the U.S. vetoed a ceasefire resolution, Guterres took the unusual step of publicly criticizing the Security Council, reflecting deep frustration with institutional paralysis.
statements.venue: UN General Assembly Emergency Special Session
statements.factCheckDetail: The U.S. did veto a ceasefire resolution. Guterres's characterization of institutional failure reflects factual events. The criticism of the veto mechanism, while politically charged, is grounded in the documented inability of the Council to pass a binding resolution.

statements.text: International humanitarian law applies to all parties in this conflict. Targeting civilian infrastructure is a violation regardless of the justification offered.
statements.context: Guterres responded to reports of strikes hitting power plants, water treatment facilities, and transportation infrastructure in both Iran and Israel.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflects international humanitarian law, specifically the Fourth Geneva Convention and Additional Protocol I, which prohibit attacks on civilian infrastructure. The legal principles cited are well-established in international law.

statements.text: I am appointing a Special Envoy and proposing a 72-hour humanitarian pause. The suffering must stop. We need space for diplomacy.
statements.context: Guterres announced concrete UN-led de-escalation measures, including the appointment of a Special Envoy and a proposed humanitarian corridor agreement.
statements.factCheckDetail: The appointment of a Special Envoy and the proposal for a humanitarian pause are concrete actions within the Secretary-General's authority. Whether parties will accept the proposal remains to be seen.

statements.text: Arrived in Beirut for solidarity visit. The conflict has caused 'immense suffering.' 'De-escalation and dialogue are the only way out.' 773 killed in Lebanon, 800,000+ displaced.
statements.context: Arrival statement in Beirut
statements.factCheckNote: Visit confirmed by UN News. Casualty figures align with known data.

statements.text: Directly addressed the warring parties from Brussels: 'To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control.' Also told Iran: 'Stop attacking your neighbors. They were never parties to the conflict.' Warned the Hormuz closure 'causes enormous pain in so many people around the world that have nothing to do with this conflict.'
statements.context: Briefing European Council leaders in Brussels; strongest UN statement since war began
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera video confirmed; UN News transcript confirmed.

statements.text: Stop attacking your neighbours; they were never parties to the conflict. The world cannot afford a war that spirals completely out of control. The Strait of Hormuz must be opened immediately. The United Nations stands ready to help facilitate safe passage.
statements.context: Guterres addressed the UN Security Council on March 21, calling for Iran to stop attacks on Gulf states and for the US/Israel to halt strikes before the conflict spirals further.
statements.factCheckNote: UN official statement. Confirmed by UN News and UN Press.

statements.text: To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control. The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz causes enormous pain in so many people around the world that have nothing to do with this conflict. It's time for the force of the law to prevail over the law of force.
statements.context: Guterres made the remarks outside the European Council meeting in Brussels, calling for an immediate end to the conflict and warning of global economic consequences from the Hormuz closure.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by UN News and Arab News.

statements.text: This war is out of control. This has gone too far. The world is staring down the barrel of a wider war. A direct US-Iran meeting could be the last opportunity to avoid a fully fledged escalation. I am appointing veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as my personal envoy for the Middle East conflict.
statements.context: Guterres made his strongest statement yet on the conflict, declaring it 'out of control' and announcing the appointment of Jean Arnault as personal envoy. He called on US/Israel to end the war and Iran to stop attacking neighbors. This comes on Day 26 as Iran formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement and Arnault appointment confirmed by UN News, Al Jazeera Day 26, and Pravda EN.

statements.text: I am appointing Jean Arnault as my personal envoy to support a negotiated end to this conflict. The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz is choking the movement of oil, gas, and fertilizer at a critical moment in the global planting season. This war is out of control. The world cannot afford this to continue.
statements.context: Statement announcing appointment of veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as UN personal envoy for the Iran-Israel conflict. Arnault previously served as UN envoy to Colombia, Afghanistan, and Myanmar.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by UN News official release. Jean Arnault appointment confirmed.

statements.text: It is high time to end the war — as human suffering deepens, civilian casualties mount, and the global economic impact is increasingly devastating. I have appointed veteran diplomat Jean Arnault as my personal envoy. The world cannot afford a prolonged conflict of this nature.
statements.context: UN Secretary-General statement and announcement of personal envoy appointment
statements.factCheckNote: Guterres statement confirmed via UN Press release. Jean Arnault appointment confirmed.

statements.text: To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control. To Iran: stop attacking your neighbors. They were never parties to the conflict. The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz causes enormous pain to so many people around the world who have nothing to do with this conflict. It is time for the force of the law to prevail over the law of force.
statements.context: Guterres made his most direct appeal to all parties at the EU Council in Brussels, calling on both sides to end the war while simultaneously condemning Iran's attacks on neighboring states.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News and Al Jazeera. Guterres's statements to EU Council are on-the-record.

statements.text: I strongly condemn the killing of UNIFIL peacekeepers. Attacks on peacekeepers are grave violations of international humanitarian law and may amount to war crimes.
statements.context: Response to 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in two separate incidents in southern Lebanon on March 30 — deadliest day for UN forces in the conflict. France called emergency UN Security Council meeting.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News and Al Jazeera.

statements.text: It's time for the force of the law to prevail over the law of force. It's time for diplomacy to prevail over war.
statements.context: Following deaths of 3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers in south Lebanon — called on Iran to stop attacking Gulf neighbors
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News.

statements.text: These incidents are grave violations of international law. This is just one of a number of recent incidents that have jeopardized the safety and security of peacekeepers.
statements.context: After 3 Indonesian UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in Lebanon. Called for accountability in phone call with Indonesian FM.
statements.venue: Public statement / phone call with Indonesian FM
statements.factCheckNote: UN News and Euronews confirmed.

statements.text: I am setting up an initiative to allow humanitarian assistance to move through the Strait of Hormuz to prevent a global food crisis.
statements.context: Humanitarian initiative for Strait of Hormuz passage
statements.factCheckNote: AP/KSAT confirmed.

statements.text: The world is on the edge of a wider war with catastrophic global implications. To the United States and Israel, it is high time to stop the war that is inflicting immense human suffering and already triggering devastating economic consequences. Conflicts do not end on their own. They end when leaders choose dialogue over destruction.
statements.context: Guterres issued strongest warning yet as Day 34 saw escalating strikes, 40-nation Hormuz summit, and Baghdad militia threat. Announced dispatch of Personal Envoy Jean Arnault to the region.
statements.venue: Press statement outside UN Security Council, New York
statements.factCheckNote: UN News confirmed statement and Arnault dispatch.

statements.text: The world is on the edge of a wider war. To the United States and Israel, it is high time to stop the war. When the Strait of Hormuz is strangled, the world's poorest and most vulnerable cannot breathe. Conflicts do not end on their own — they end when leaders choose dialogue over destruction.
statements.context: Major press statement outside UN Security Council
statements.factCheckNote: UN News and The National confirmed Guterres statement.

statements.text: The world is on the edge of a wider war with catastrophic global implications. To the United States and Israel, it is high time to stop the war. To Iran, you must stop attacking your neighbours.
statements.context: Strongest statement yet from UN leadership — warning of wider war
statements.factCheckNote: UN News published full text.

statements.text: When the Strait of Hormuz is strangled, the world's poorest and most vulnerable cannot breathe. Conflicts do not end on their own. They end when leaders choose dialogue over destruction.
statements.context: Continuing warning about catastrophic global implications of Iran war. Addressed both US/Israel and Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Hormuz blockade has disrupted WFP and humanitarian aid deliveries. FAO confirms food price increases.

statements.text: The world is on the edge of a wider war. When the Strait of Hormuz is strangled, the world's poorest and most vulnerable cannot breathe. I call on all parties to choose dialogue over destruction.
statements.context: Warning of wider war as Hormuz blockade continues and ceasefire talks falter
statements.factCheckNote: UN News confirmed.

statements.text: The world is on the edge of a wider war. It is high time to stop the war. When the Strait of Hormuz is strangled, the world's poorest and most vulnerable cannot breathe.
statements.context: Warned of wider war. Announced dispatch of Personal Envoy Jean Arnault to the region.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via UN News.

statements.text: Once again, the secretary-general reaffirms that it's high time for the parties to stop this conflict as there is no viable alternative to the peaceful settlement of international disputes. Attacking civilian infrastructure including energy infrastructure may not be attacked under international law.
statements.context: UN SG statement via spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric warning about Trump's threats against Iran power plants and bridges.
statements.factCheckNote: CGTN, UNN confirmed.

statements.text: Even if specific civilian infrastructure were to qualify as a military objective, international humanitarian law would still prohibit attacks against them if they may be expected to cause excessive incidental civilian harm.
statements.context: UN Spokesperson Dujarric expanding on Guterres warning about attacking Iranian civilian infrastructure.
statements.factCheckNote: UN News and PM News Nigeria confirmed remarks.

statements.text: There is no military objective that justifies the wholesale destruction of a society's infrastructure or the deliberate infliction of suffering on civilian populations. I call on leaders to choose dialogue over destruction.
statements.context: UN SG statement after Trump's 'civilization will die' threat and ahead of 8PM ET deadline.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN Press release SG/SM/23033.

statements.text: An end to hostilities is urgently needed to protect civilian lives. I call on all parties to abide by the ceasefire terms.
statements.context: Welcomed ceasefire, thanked Pakistan mediators.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera confirmed.

statements.text: I call on all parties to abide by ceasefire terms in order to pave the way toward a lasting and comprehensive peace in the region.
statements.context: Statement welcoming US-Iran ceasefire, thanking mediating nations
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera confirmed statement.

statements.text: I call on all parties to abide by ceasefire terms to pave the way toward a lasting and comprehensive peace. An end to hostilities is urgently needed to protect civilian lives.
statements.context: UN statement welcoming ceasefire. Also launched $308.3M flash humanitarian appeal for Lebanon following 254 killed in Israeli strikes.
statements.factCheckNote: UN News, UN Press confirmed.

statements.text: The Secretary-General unequivocally condemns the strikes and calls for an end to the hostilities, which pose a grave risk to the ceasefire.
statements.context: Condemning ceasefire violations after Lebanon strikes
statements.factCheckNote: Official UN statement

statements.text: The ongoing military activity in Lebanon poses a grave risk to the ceasefire and to regional stability. I call on all parties to comply with their obligations under international law.
statements.context: Guterres warned Israeli strikes in Lebanon threaten the fragile US-Iran truce. Condemned strikes that killed hundreds of civilians.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: It is highly probable that peace talks between the US and Iran will restart before the ceasefire expires next week. It would be unrealistic to expect that such a complex problem, long-lasting problem, could be resolved in the first session of a negotiation.
statements.context: Guterres commenting after Islamabad talks ended in stalemate. Ceasefire expires April 21. Pakistan pushing for Round 2.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Middle East Eye, UN News, Washington Times.

statements.text: There is no military solution to this crisis. Peace agreements require persistent engagement and political will. Serious negotiations must resume. The ceasefire must be preserved and all violations must cease.
statements.context: Statement on April 15 calling for continued talks. Thanked Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Türkiye for mediation efforts.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News, Sahara Reporters.

statements.text: The ceasefire must absolutely be preserved and all violations must cease. It is highly likely peace talks will restart before the ceasefire expires.
statements.context: Press conference at UN HQ. Thanked Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Turkiye as mediators.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News.

statements.text: I welcome the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and urge full respect and compliance by all parties.
statements.context: Formal statement welcoming the 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Commended the US role in facilitating the deal. Urged compliance with international humanitarian law.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Manila Times, ANI News.

statements.text: The opening of the Strait of Hormuz is a step in the right direction. Together with the ceasefire, this measure will contribute to creating confidence between the parties and strengthen the ongoing dialogue.
statements.context: Welcomed Iran's declaration that Hormuz is 'completely open' for commercial traffic during ceasefire period.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News.

statements.text: I call for the full restoration of international navigational rights and freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. The ceasefire must be preserved. All violations must cease.
statements.context: Statement on April 21 amid escalating restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz. Addressed the dual blockade (Iran Day 50 + US CENTCOM Day 8) and its impact on 40,000+ stranded seafarers.
statements.factCheckNote: Navigational rights claim aligns with UNCLOS. 40,000 seafarers figure confirmed by UN reports.

statements.text: The ceasefire extension will create critical space for diplomacy and confidence-building between Iran and the United States. There is no military solution to this conflict. I fully support Pakistan's facilitation efforts.
statements.context: Reacting to Trump's indefinite ceasefire extension. UN spokesman encouraged all parties to 'refrain from actions that could undermine the cease-fire and engage constructively in negotiations.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News, NBC Washington.

statements.text: This is an important step toward de-escalation and creating critical space for diplomacy and confidence-building between Iran and the United States. I encourage all parties to refrain from actions that could undermine the cease-fire.
statements.context: Cautiously welcoming Trump's ceasefire extension. Expressed full support for Pakistan's mediation. Made as IRGC seized 2 ships in Hormuz and Israel killed journalist in Lebanon — both potential ceasefire-undermining actions.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News and Middle East Eye.

statements.text: An important step toward de-escalation and creating critical space for diplomacy. All parties must refrain from actions that could undermine the ceasefire and engage constructively in negotiations.
statements.context: Welcoming the 3-week Lebanon ceasefire extension. Expressed full support for Pakistan's mediating role.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News.

statements.text: The ceasefire must absolutely be preserved and all violations must cease. The Islamabad discussions underscored the seriousness of their engagement and constituted a positive and meaningful step toward renewed dialogue.
statements.context: Called for continued talks and thanked mediators: Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Turkiye. Noted UN warning that Israeli strikes in Lebanon and Hezbollah rockets into Israel may both breach international humanitarian law.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News.

statements.text: Today's Israeli airstrikes in the Beqaa and southern Lebanon are deeply alarming. The Lebanon ceasefire is hanging by a thread. We need immediate, verifiable de-escalation in both Hormuz and Lebanon.
statements.context: UN noon briefing readout responding to Israeli strike package on seven southern Lebanon towns and concurrent Iranian Hormuz hardening, calling for parallel de-escalation tracks.
statements.factCheckNote: UN spokesperson Stephane Dujarric read the SG's statement at the noon briefing April 27.

statements.text: I appeal to the parties: Open the Strait. Let ships pass. No tolls. No discrimination. Let trade resume. Let the global economy breathe. Safe, unimpeded passage is an economic and humanitarian imperative.
statements.context: Strong statement calling for immediate restoration of free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, warning the blockade risks triggering a global food crisis as one-third of global fertilizer trade transits Hormuz and planting season begins in vulnerable countries.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News, ANI News, Deccan Chronicle.

statements.text: The Hormuz closure is strangling the global economy. There is no military solution. I call for continued talks and an immediate end to ceasefire violations on all sides.
statements.context: Guterres warned the ongoing Hormuz blockade and ceasefire violations in Lebanon are compounding economic damage. Called for diplomatic solution.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News.

statements.text: Open the Strait. Let all ships pass. Let the global economy breathe again. Prolonged closure could push tens of millions into poverty, trigger global hunger, and cause global recession.
statements.context: Urgent call for reopening of Strait of Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News, C-SPAN, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: My message to all parties is clear: Navigational rights and freedoms must be restored immediately, in line with Security Council Resolution 2817. Open the Strait. Let all ships pass. Let the global economy breathe again.
statements.context: Statement expressing deep concern over Hormuz developments and Project Freedom escalation risk. Warned prolonged closure could push inflation above 6%, global growth to 2%, risking global recession.
statements.factCheckNote: Economic warning supported by IMF/World Bank projections. UNSC Resolution 2817 confirmed to demand restoration of navigation.

statements.text: My strong appeal is for the negotiations to go on until that diplomatic solution is found, the ceasefire to be maintained, and in between, the Strait of Hormuz to be completely open. Any restart of the fighting would have terrible consequences.
statements.context: Speaking in Nairobi, Kenya. Warned that 13% of Africa's imports move through the Strait of Hormuz. Urged both sides to keep negotiating despite Trump's rejection of Iran's counter-proposal.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by GlobalSecurity/UN News. 13% Africa import figure supported by UNCTAD data.

statements.text: The force of the law must prevail over the law of force. Any restart of fighting would have catastrophic consequences. The negotiations must continue, the ceasefire must hold, and the Strait of Hormuz must be opened completely.
statements.context: Statement issued as ceasefire remains in jeopardy. Guterres responding to Iran's vow of 'no retreat' and Trump's Beijing departure. Previously warned from Nairobi on May 11. UN Security Council has remained deadlocked due to US veto power.
statements.factCheckNote: UN News confirmed. Aligns with earlier May 11 Nairobi statement.

statements.text: There must be no further attacks near civilian infrastructure, including nuclear power plants. Attacks on nuclear infrastructure are unacceptable and risk dangerous escalation across the region.
statements.context: Guterres led international condemnation of the May 17 drone strike on the UAE's Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant — the first attack on any nuclear facility in the war. IAEA Director General Grossi simultaneously expressed 'grave concern' and dispatched a monitoring team to verify no radiological release. The UAE intercepted 2 of 3 drones; the third struck an external electrical generator. No group claimed responsibility. The UN chief's statement came as global actors including the EU, India, Canada, Pakistan, and Gulf states expressed solidarity with the UAE.
statements.factCheckNote: Guterres quote confirmed by The National UAE, Voice of Emirates, and Al Jazeera May 18. IAEA Grossi 'grave concern' confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: The use of force against any state's sovereignty must be rejected. Diplomacy must be given the chance to restore stability in the region.
statements.context: Guterres made these remarks in a telephone call with Iranian FM Araghchi on May 23, during which Araghchi accused the US of 'betrayals, contradictory stances, and excessive demands.' Guterres is actively pressing both sides to preserve the diplomatic track as the US weighs resuming strikes and Iran signals it has rebuilt military capabilities during the ceasefire.
statements.venue: Telephone call with Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi, May 23, 2026
statements.factCheckNote: Guterres statement confirmed by Press TV (May 23) citing the UN chief's readout of the call with Araghchi. Also confirmed by ANI News.
statements.source: Press TV — US betrayals and excessive demands source of disruption: Iran FM to Guterres

speakers.name: Kaja Kallas
speakers.title: EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs

statements.text: Europe must develop an independent strategic posture on Iran. We cannot simply follow Washington's lead on every crisis.
statements.context: As incoming EU High Representative, Kallas signaled a more autonomous European approach to the Iran file, distinguishing EU policy from the incoming Trump administration.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflects the EU's stated intent to maintain strategic autonomy on foreign policy. The EU had already diverged from U.S. Iran policy during the first Trump administration by creating the INSTEX mechanism to preserve trade.

statements.text: The JCPOA was imperfect but it was working. Europe remains committed to a diplomatic solution based on its framework.
statements.context: Kallas reaffirmed European commitment to the diplomatic approach embodied in the JCPOA, contrasting the EU position with the U.S. withdrawal and maximum pressure strategy.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim that the JCPOA 'was working' is partially supported by IAEA data showing Iran was compliant before the U.S. withdrawal. However, the deal had acknowledged limitations including sunset clauses, and Iran had begun exceeding limits even before full U.S. withdrawal took effect.

statements.text: We have opened a direct diplomatic channel with Tehran. Europe will not stand by while others march toward conflict.
statements.context: Kallas announced EU-Iran diplomatic talks, positioning Europe as an active mediator while the U.S. refused direct engagement with Iran.
statements.factCheckDetail: The EU did establish direct diplomatic contacts with Iran through the EEAS. This was confirmed by both European and Iranian diplomatic sources and represented a significant divergence from U.S. policy.

statements.text: Europe will not participate in or support military action against Iran. We believe this path leads only to catastrophe.
statements.context: As military planning accelerated, Kallas explicitly ruled out European participation, creating a significant rift with the U.S. within the NATO alliance.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents the official EU position as communicated to NATO allies. The statement created documented tensions within the alliance, with the U.S. expressing disappointment at the lack of allied solidarity.

statements.text: Our mediation efforts have produced a framework for talks. All we need is political will from both sides to take the final step.
statements.context: Kallas revealed that European shuttle diplomacy had produced a draft framework for negotiations, but neither Israel nor Iran had committed to participating.
statements.factCheckDetail: The existence of a draft framework was confirmed by European diplomatic sources but its contents were not made public. Claims of being close to a breakthrough were disputed by both Israeli and U.S. officials who described the framework as inadequate.

statements.text: We condemn these strikes in the strongest possible terms. Unilateral military action undermines international law and regional stability.
statements.context: The EU issued one of the sharpest criticisms of Israeli military action, straining EU-Israel relations and drawing criticism from the U.S. for failing to support an ally.
statements.venue: EU Emergency Foreign Affairs Council Statement
statements.factCheckDetail: The condemnation reflects the official EU position. The legal assessment that unilateral strikes may violate international law is supported by many international law scholars, though Israel disputes this characterization based on self-defense provisions.

statements.text: Europe is imposing targeted sanctions on entities involved in the escalation. We are also tripling our humanitarian aid to affected civilian populations.
statements.context: The EU announced a dual-track response combining punitive measures with humanitarian support, positioning Europe as a distinct voice from both the U.S. and China/Russia.
statements.factCheckDetail: The EU did announce a sanctions package and increased humanitarian aid. The specific aid figure and sanctions targets were detailed in official EU Council documents.

statements.text: We are working with the UN Special Envoy on a European peace initiative. A ceasefire must be the first step, not the last.
statements.context: Kallas announced EU coordination with the newly appointed UN Special Envoy, presenting a joint European-UN diplomatic framework as an alternative to the stalled U.S.-led process.
statements.venue: Joint Press Conference with UN Special Envoy, Geneva
statements.factCheckDetail: EU-UN coordination on a peace initiative was confirmed by both organizations. The existence of a diplomatic framework document was verified, though its prospects for acceptance by the warring parties remained uncertain.

statements.text: We propose a Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz through the UN. Without action, we face food deprivation next year.
statements.context: Kallas proposed a UN-brokered Hormuz shipping arrangement modeled on the 2022 Black Sea Grain Initiative, warning of catastrophic food security consequences if Hormuz remains disrupted.
statements.venue: EU Foreign Affairs Council / Al Jazeera interview
statements.factCheckNote: Kallas's proposal confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog. The Black Sea grain deal comparison reflects a concrete diplomatic model. Food security warnings align with UN FAO assessments.

statements.text: There is no appetite among European nations for military involvement in the Strait of Hormuz. We propose a Black Sea grain deal model — a humanitarian oil corridor under UN auspices.
statements.context: EU High Representative rejected military involvement in Hormuz, proposing instead a diplomatic framework modeled on the Black Sea grain deal. EU foreign ministers voted against expanding Aspides naval mission.
statements.factCheckNote: EU rejection of Hormuz military role confirmed. Germany's Pistorius explicitly stated 'This is not our war.'

statements.text: We have been consulting with Gulf countries, Jordan, Egypt about whether we could bring forward proposals for Iran, Israel and the US to get out of this situation so that everybody saves face.
statements.context: Kallas called on US and Israel to end the war in a Reuters exclusive. Said EU is consulting regional governments on a face-saving diplomatic exit. Also discussed with UN Secretary-General Guterres a Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement for Hormuz oil transit.
statements.factCheckNote: Reuters exclusive confirmed by Fox News and Al-Monitor. Kallas confirmed consulting with Gulf states on diplomatic off-ramp.

statements.text: There is no appetite in Europe to expand our naval presence or join this military campaign. Europe will not allow itself to be blackmailed.
statements.context: EU summit in Brussels; EU refuses to join US military campaign or secure Hormuz. Kallas refers to Austrian Chancellor Stocker's wording.
statements.factCheckNote: WaPo confirmed

statements.text: Iran bears a heavy responsibility for the current escalation.
statements.context: Statement at UNSC during Resolution 2817 debate. France voted in favor of condemning Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Official statement at UN, consistent with EU position

statements.text: Member states do not have an appetite to go to this war.
statements.context: After EU Foreign Ministers decided against expanding Aspides naval mission to Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple EU press pool reports

statements.text: EU 'no appetite' to join military campaign. European Council: moratorium on energy/water strikes, reopen Hormuz. ECB: 'material impact on near-term inflation.'
statements.context: European Council summit
statements.factCheckNote: Council conclusions published

statements.text: Iran is responsible for decades of violence and the fewer options Iran has to terrorise the region, the better. The regime is weaker than it has been for many years, but there is no clear trajectory how this war will end.
