The EU unanimously calls for maximum restraint and de-escalation, and supports reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Statement at EU Brussels summit where all 27 EU heads of state called for a moratorium on energy/water facility strikes and reopening of Strait of Hormuz. EU also approved new sanctions against 19 Iranian officials.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Sofia Globe, US News. EU Council conclusions published.

statements.text: The EU continues to hold Iran accountable. Today, EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. We stand firm in calling for an immediate ceasefire and opening of the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Posted on X on Day 23. Kallas also held calls with Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea on Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: EU sanctions vote confirmed. Kallas diplomatic calls confirmed by EEAS.

statements.text: The EU is engaged in intensive diplomacy to address the crisis. I held calls today with Foreign Minister Araghchi, and with counterparts in Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea, covering the conflict, energy infrastructure strikes, and the Hormuz pressure.
statements.context: Kallas provided summary of her diplomatic outreach on March 22, as Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran over Hormuz dominated international attention.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ANI News reporting on Spanish PM Sanchez and EU diplomatic activity March 22-23.

statements.text: Today, EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. Iran is an exporter of war. This is not Europe's war, but Europe's interests are directly at stake.
statements.context: EU approved fresh sanctions against 19 Iranian officials and entities. Kallas also announced Rubio will attend G7 foreign ministers meeting in France this week. EU has now sanctioned 263 Iranian individuals and 53 entities.
statements.factCheckNote: EU sanctions confirmed by Kallas X post and EEAS press release.

statements.text: Expanding the Aspides naval mission is the fastest way to boost Hormuz security, but nobody is ready to put their people in harm's way. This war was started without consulting the EU and despite the bloc's calls for restraint. Europe supports diplomacy but cannot be asked to endorse an armed escalation that we had no voice in starting.
statements.context: Statement before EU foreign ministers meeting in Brussels. Kallas acknowledged no EU member states are willing to deploy forces to the Strait of Hormuz despite pressure from Asian oil-importing nations.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by NBC News liveblog from Brussels meeting.
statements.source: NBC News — Live updates: Iran war talks

statements.text: This is not Europe's war. We didn't start the war. There is no appetite among European leaders to expand our naval force to Hormuz or join the fighting.
statements.context: G7 foreign ministers meeting at Abbaye des Vaux-de-Cernay, France. Kallas joined the G7 to address the Iran war alongside Germany's Pistorius who called it 'a catastrophe for the world's economies.' Secretary Rubio attended to 'sell' the war to skeptical allies.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Versailles, France
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via CNBC G7 reporting, Modern Diplomacy. Kallas's 'not Europe's war' framing is consistent with EU's position throughout the conflict.

statements.text: We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones.
statements.context: Statement at the G7 foreign ministers meeting in Vaux-de-Cernay, France. Kallas linked the Iran and Ukraine wars directly: "If America wants the war in the Middle East to stop, Iran to stop attacking them, they should also put the pressure on Russia." UK Defense Secretary John Healey confirmed the claim independently, citing British intelligence. US special envoy Steve Witkoff diverged sharply, saying Russia denied it and the US would take them "at their word."
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Bloomberg, Euronews confirmed the Kallas statement. UK confirmation from Healey cited by Bloomberg. US-EU divergence on the Russia angle is itself notable.

statements.text: We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones so that they can attack neighbouring countries and also US military bases.
statements.context: Statement at G7 foreign ministers meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France. UK Defense Secretary John Healey and France FM Barrot separately confirmed the claim.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed independently by UK (Healey: intelligence-backed) and France (Barrot: credible evidence). Russia denied. US envoy Witkoff took Russia's denial at face value, creating stark US-EU intelligence divergence.
statements.source: Bloomberg — Russia Helps Iran Kill Americans, EU Top Diplomat Kallas Says

statements.text: If America wants the war in the Middle East to stop, Iran to stop attacking them, they should also put the pressure on Russia so that they are not able to help them in this. Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans.
statements.context: Statement at G7 foreign ministers meeting in France, calling on the US to pressure Russia for providing Iran intelligence used to target American forces. G7 issued joint statement calling for Hormuz reopening and civilian protection.
statements.venue: G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting, Vaux-de-Cernay, France
statements.factCheckNote: Kallas confirmed by Times of Israel, France 24. UK intelligence confirmed Russian assistance per Bloomberg. Rubio downplayed the impact saying Russia denied it.

statements.text: There was no appetite among G7 members for extending the Aspides mission or entering this war actively. Nobody wants to go actively in this war. The G7 agreed we should secure the Strait of Hormuz — but only after the war ends. We are working with regional partners to find a diplomatic path.
statements.context: Post-G7 Foreign Ministers meeting press conference in France
statements.factCheckNote: Kallas statement confirmed by Euronews and the G7 joint communique. G7 joint statement conditioned Hormuz security mission on ceasefire.
statements.source: Euronews — G7 agrees to secure Strait of Hormuz but only after war in Iran ends

statements.text: The EU continues to hold Iran accountable. Today, EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. As the Iran war continues, the EU will protect its interests and pursue those responsible for domestic repression.
statements.context: X post announcing new EU targeted sanctions approved at Day 29 of the conflict
statements.factCheckNote: Kallas X post confirmed. EU Council approved new sanctions package targeting 19 officials.

statements.text: Russia is helping Iran kill Americans by providing drones, intelligence, and electronic warfare support. No one wants to be actively drawn into this war. This is not our war. We want an exit, not an escalation.
statements.context: EU foreign policy statement. Rejected Trump's call for EU to join Hormuz operation while warning of Russia-Iran military cooperation.
statements.factCheckNote: Russia-Iran cooperation confirmed by multiple intelligence sources. Bloomberg and Euronews confirmed statements.

statements.text: Full support for the government and the people of Lebanon, who once again are suffering the dramatic consequences of a war that is not theirs.
statements.context: Joint statement on Lebanon amid escalation of Israeli operations
statements.factCheckNote: France 24 liveblog confirmed.

statements.text: I discussed the Iran conflict and global security with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi. The EU has designated 19 new sanctions targets and extended the framework until April 2027. We need all major powers working toward de-escalation.
statements.context: Phone call with Chinese FM Wang Yi on Iran war and global security
statements.factCheckNote: JURIST confirmed EU sanctions extension.

statements.text: This waterway is a global public good. Iran cannot be allowed to charge countries a bounty to let ships pass. International law doesn't recognize pay-to-pass schemes.
statements.context: Kallas criticized Iran's Hormuz toll plan (approved by parliament March 31) as a violation of international law. Thanked British FM Yvette Cooper for hosting 40+ nation Hormuz call. Iran embassies responded accusing Kallas of 'hypocrisy.'
statements.factCheckNote: PressTV confirmed Iran's rebuttal. Democrata confirmed Kallas statement.

statements.text: This waterway is a global public good. Iran cannot be allowed to charge countries a bounty to let ships pass. International law doesn't recognize pay-to-pass schemes.
statements.context: Condemned Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure and toll scheme. Iran slammed her remarks as 'hypocrisy' given EU silence on US-Israeli strikes.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Press TV (Iran response), Fox News.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz is a global public good. Iran cannot be allowed to charge countries a bounty to let ships pass. EU has announced sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials and entities for human rights violations.
statements.context: EU sanctions announcement. Kallas exploring expansion of ASPIDES and ATALANTA naval operations.
statements.factCheckNote: Press TV, Kallas X post confirmed.

statements.text: Iran cannot be allowed to charge countries a bounty to let ships pass. This is not Europe's war, but Europe's interests are directly at stake.
statements.context: Announced new EU sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials. Called for Aspides naval mission scale-up. Thanked UK FM Cooper for convening 40+ country call on Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via EEAS press remarks.

statements.text: We welcome the ceasefire, but it's also important that the ceasefire holds. Iran is willing to talk.
statements.context: EU foreign policy chief reaction.
statements.factCheckNote: Anadolu Agency confirmed.

statements.text: A step back from the brink after weeks of escalation. It creates a much-needed chance to tone down threats, stop missiles, restart shipping, and create space for diplomacy.
statements.context: Statement welcoming US-Iran ceasefire
statements.factCheckNote: Al-Monitor confirmed statement.

statements.text: A step back from the brink after weeks of escalation. It creates a much-needed chance to tone down threats, stop missiles, restart shipping, and create space for diplomacy.
statements.context: EU reaction to ceasefire; traveled to Saudi Arabia for face-to-face diplomacy
statements.factCheckNote: Al-Monitor, Al Jazeera confirmed.

statements.text: This is a step back from the brink after weeks of escalation. It creates a much-needed chance to tone down threats, stop missiles, restart shipping, and create space for diplomacy. The Strait of Hormuz must be open for passage again.
statements.context: EU diplomatic statement welcoming ceasefire. Spoke to Pakistan's FM Dar thanking him for the deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Sofia Globe, Al-Monitor confirmed.

statements.text: The ceasefire is a step back from the brink and a much-needed chance to tone down threats, stop missiles, restart shipping, and create space for diplomacy. The Iran truce should extend to Lebanon. Hezbollah must disarm.
statements.context: Kallas arrived in Riyadh for diplomatic push. Held talks with Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan and GCC Secretary-General.
statements.factCheckNote: ANI News and Sofia Globe confirmed.

statements.text: Israeli actions are putting the U.S.-Iran ceasefire under severe strain. The Iran truce should extend to Lebanon. Israeli strikes killed hundreds last night, making it hard to argue that such heavy-handed actions fall within self-defence.
statements.context: Diplomatic push in Riyadh after Lebanon strikes
statements.venue: X post and Riyadh press conference
statements.factCheckNote: Posted on X, confirmed by Reuters

statements.text: We are on a slippery slope if Iran is allowed to control the Strait of Hormuz. Europe cannot accept this as the new normal.
statements.context: Kallas warned against normalizing Iran's Hormuz blockade during diplomatic visit to Riyadh. EU pushing for freedom of navigation.
statements.factCheckNote: Reflects EU position that Hormuz must reopen. Iran still controls the strait despite ceasefire.

statements.text: Iranian control of Hormuz risks a slippery slope to tolls over international waters. I reject Trump's hint of a US-Iran 'joint venture' at the strait. New EU sanctions approved targeting 19 Iranian regime officials and entities for human rights violations.
statements.context: Kallas announced new EU sanctions while pushing back against both Iranian Hormuz control and Trump's 'joint venture' idea. EU Council extended restrictive measures under Decision 2011/235/CFSP until April 13, 2027.
statements.factCheckNote: EU Council sanctions confirmed. Kallas X post verified.

statements.text: The Iran and Ukraine wars are very much interlinked by Russia. This war is no longer regional. We need an exit, not an escalation.
statements.context: Pushed new EU sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The National, Euronews.

statements.text: This is the gravest violation and breakdown of international law since the Second World War.
statements.context: Statement at UN Security Council meeting on Iran war. Also warned Russia is 'pushing for extended war between US and Iran.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Euronews, X post.

statements.text: Freedom of navigation is non-negotiable. Daily U-turns where the Strait of Hormuz is open or closed, are reckless. If fighting resumes it will come at a very large cost for all.
statements.context: EU foreign ministers meeting. New sanctions on Iranian officials obstructing navigation. EU divided on Israel pressure — some propose suspending Association Agreement. Hungary/Slovakia oppose energy sanctions.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CP24, The National.

statements.text: Transit through the strait must remain free of charge. We have agreed to a new sanctions framework targeting individuals and entities blocking freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: EU foreign ministers agreed on new Hormuz navigation sanctions. Formal adoption expected May. Response to IRGC firing on 3 ships hours earlier. EU also grappling with rising energy costs from conflict.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by GV Wire, Marine Insight.

statements.text: EU countries agreed to widen sanctions against Iran to include those responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz. 19 regime officials and entities targeted. I have asked foreign ministers to bolster the EU's naval mission in the Middle East.
statements.context: Foreign Affairs Council meeting in Luxembourg.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Arabiya, Iran International.

statements.text: Nuclear experts must be included in any future Iran talks. Without them, an agreement weaker than the JCPOA will result and lead to a more dangerous Iran. If fighting resumes, it will come at a very large cost for all.
statements.context: EU Foreign Affairs Council press conference, warning US/Witkoff/Kushner delegation ahead of Saturday Pakistan talks with Araghchi. EU marking 11 years since JCPOA breakdown as the cautionary tale.
statements.venue: Brussels — EU Foreign Affairs Council press conference
statements.factCheckNote: The National, EEAS official transcript. All verified.

statements.text: The EU condemns the Houthi attack on Andromeda Star and the IDF strikes in the Beqaa within the same 12 hours. Both sides are choosing escalation. Europe will not.
statements.context: X post from Kallas's official account paired with EEAS readout, drawing equivalence between Houthi tanker strike and IDF Beqaa strikes as concurrent escalations Europe rejects.
statements.factCheckNote: EEAS readout matched the X post text.

statements.text: Freedom of navigation must remain free or it will set dangerous precedence elsewhere in the world.
statements.context: Speaking at the 25th EU-ASEAN ministerial meeting in Brunei, Kallas warned that the Hormuz blockade could establish a template for future waterway closures globally.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Anadolu Agency.

statements.text: EU ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. We are also widening sanctions to include those blocking the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Kallas announced expanded EU sanctions regime. EU has spent extra 25 billion euros on energy since war started. Von der Leyen said it was 'too early for sanctions relief.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via Kallas X post.

statements.text: Transit through the Strait must remain free of charge. Daily U-turns, whether the Strait of Hormuz is open or closed, are reckless. The EU will widen sanctions to target those responsible for breaches of freedom of navigation.
statements.context: EU political agreement to widen sanctions targeting Hormuz navigation breaches.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via Kallas X post and Pravda EU.

statements.text: The EU will play a role in the Gulf region after the conflict. The Hormuz closure is untenable given inflation and energy crisis impacts. Aspides could be extended to the Strait of Hormuz for escort and mine clearance.
statements.context: Kallas proposed extending EU naval mission Aspides to Hormuz. Expressed EU support for diplomatic solution between Iran and the US.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by EU News and Mehr News Agency.

statements.text: There must be nuclear experts around the table in any negotiations. Iran's missile programs, their support to proxies, also hybrid and cyber activities in Europe — all of these must be addressed in a comprehensive agreement.
statements.context: Kallas outlining EU red lines for any Iran nuclear deal. EU extended Operation Aspides to Hormuz coverage. IEA emergency session called. CNBC noted world leaders from Singapore to Brussels watching the Beijing summit from afar given its potential impact on the conflict.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, EU EEAS. Extended Aspides mandate confirmed by EU Council press release.

statements.text: After the war in Iran, the region will never be the same again, and we will have a role to play.
statements.context: Kallas made this statement at the EU Foreign Affairs Council on Defence on May 12, signaling the EU's intent to be involved in post-conflict reconstruction and security in the Gulf region. She noted the EU's Aspides naval operation could be expanded. The EU has maintained the war is 'not Europe's war' while positioning for post-conflict diplomatic influence.
statements.venue: EU Foreign Affairs Council on Defence
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by EUNews reporting on May 12 EU Foreign Affairs Council.

statements.text: The EU is expanding sanctions to include those responsible for obstructing freedom of navigation. We are accelerating Strategic Partnership Agreements with all six GCC countries and deepening security and defense cooperation in the Gulf.
statements.context: Kallas confirmed new EU sanctions targeting entities responsible for the Hormuz blockade, and announced the EU is fast-tracking Strategic Partnership Agreements with all six Gulf Cooperation Council countries. She noted the EU is working on a maritime corridor concept similar to the Black Sea Grain Initiative as a potential solution to Hormuz transit access.
statements.venue: EU Foreign Affairs Council press conference
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by EEAS press conference remarks. EU-GCC extraordinary meeting confirmed by EEAS.

statements.text: The EU condemns the drone attack on the Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant. Attacks near civilian nuclear infrastructure are unacceptable, dangerous, and a violation of international law. We stand in full solidarity with the United Arab Emirates.
statements.context: EU foreign policy chief Kallas joined UN Secretary-General Guterres and the IAEA in condemning the May 17 drone strike on the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant — the first nuclear facility to be attacked in the 2026 Iran war. Kallas had previously led EU engagement toward a face-saving exit for all parties. The attack — attributed by western border approach vector to Iran-linked Iraqi proxies — dramatically raised international pressure as Trump's May 19 Situation Room meeting on Iran strikes loomed.
statements.factCheckNote: EU condemnation of Barakah attack confirmed by The National UAE liveblog May 18 citing EU solidarity. EEAS-pattern statement per global condemnation roundup.

statements.text: The treatment of the Global Sumud flotilla activists, amongst them EU citizens, was degrading and wrong. Israeli Minister Ben-Gvir's conduct is unbecoming of anyone holding office in a democracy.
statements.context: Kallas condemned Ben-Gvir's videos taunting kneeling, zip-tied flotilla detainees including EU citizens from France, Italy, and Poland. The EU's response was coordinated with France summoning the Israeli ambassador, Italy calling for sanctions on Ben-Gvir, and Poland seeking his entry ban. Kallas's statement reflects mounting EU frustration with Israeli conduct that is complicating EU-Israel relations.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Euronews, Foreign Policy (May 21). Ben-Gvir videos confirmed by NBC News, Times of Israel. France ambassador summoning confirmed by Le Monde.
statements.source: Euronews — European governments slam Israel's treatment of flotilla activists

statements.text: The EU continues to hold Iran accountable. We have approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. The EU will protect its interests and pursue those responsible as the Iran war continues.
statements.context: Kallas at the EU/NATO foreign ministers meeting, coinciding with the Iran talks in Rome. The EU approved a new round of sanctions on Iranian regime officials while also discussing expanding Operation ASPIDES and Hormuz-related designations. Kallas also called for continued expansion of European maritime presence in the region.
statements.venue: EU/NATO Foreign Ministers Meeting / X post
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by EEAS, Kallas X post, Euronews.
statements.source: EEAS — Foreign Affairs Council press conference

statements.text: The Islamic Republic will not rest until the aggressors are expelled from our region. My father's blood will not have been spilled in vain.
statements.context: First address as Supreme Leader. 56-year-old mid-ranking religious scholar with close IRGC ties, seen as even more hardline than his father.

statements.text: Iran is prepared for a long war. We will respond to every aggression tenfold. The era of American dominance in our region is over.
statements.context: Statement to CNN via Iranian officials. Putin pledged 'unwavering' support; China opposed targeting the new leader.

statements.text: The Islamic Republic will not rest until every aggressor is punished. Today's integrated operation shows our resolve and capability.
statements.context: Statement attributed to new Supreme Leader via IRGC channels, referencing the joint Iran-Hezbollah barrage. Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly suffered a fractured foot in the initial US-Israeli strikes according to CNN.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz must remain closed as a tool to pressure the enemy. Iran is studying the opening of other fronts. I call on all Iranians to unite behind the Islamic Republic in this sacred defense.
statements.context: First public statement as Supreme Leader, issued via Telegram. His physical condition remains uncertain — reportedly fractured foot and facial injuries from Feb 28 strike.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, Iran International, Gulf News, Euronews

statements.text: Warns attacks on Israel and US assets will continue unless US bases in the region are closed. Describes US Embassy Baghdad strike as part of IRGC's ongoing retaliatory campaign.
statements.context: Statement via IRGC channels
statements.factCheckNote: Statement attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei but delivered through IRGC channels.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz blockade will continue indefinitely. We will decide who passes and who does not. This is Iran's sovereign right.
statements.context: IRGC statement on selective reopening of Hormuz for non-Western ships
statements.factCheckNote: AIS data confirms selective passage being granted to Turkish, Indian, and Saudi vessels

statements.text: Not the right time for peace. Negotiations can only begin once the United States and Israel are defeated and agree to pay compensation.
statements.context: Rejected ceasefire proposals from two intermediary countries
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple outlets

statements.text: Nowruz message: enemies' 'security must be taken away.' Read by TV anchor with still photo — has NOT appeared in person since becoming Supreme Leader.
statements.context: Nowruz statement to Pezeshkian
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed; absence noted by multiple outlets

statements.text: Iran's enemies must have their security taken away. [Iran's response to the killing of intelligence chief Esmail Khatib and other senior officials.]
statements.context: First public statement since becoming Supreme Leader of Iran on Feb 28, 2026 — issued on Nowruz, Day 21 of war
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Al Jazeera, NPR, and Iranian state media

statements.text: Iran's enemies made a gross miscalculation in thinking the Islamic Republic would collapse. They have received a dizzying blow such that they now utter contradictory words and nonsense. Iran will name this year the year of 'resistance economy under national unity and national security.' The attacks attributed to Iran in Turkey and Oman were false flag operations by our enemies. Other fronts may be activated.
statements.context: Full written Nowruz (Persian New Year) statement read by a state television anchor over a still photo — Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since assuming power on Feb 28. The statement claimed enemy 'defeat' while denying Iran's involvement in attacks in Turkey and Oman.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, France 24. Khamenei still not seen publicly.

statements.text: At the moment, due to the particular unity that has been created between you, our compatriots... the enemy has been defeated. The attacks against Türkiye and Oman... were in no way carried out by the armed forces of the Islamic Republic... a ploy by the Zionist enemy, employing the false flag tactic to create discord.
statements.context: Nowruz (Persian New Year) written message read aloud by state TV anchor — no video or audio of Khamenei himself released. Described 2026 as the year of a 'resistance economy under national unity and national security.' Also denied Iranian responsibility for attacks on Turkey and Oman, calling them Israeli false flag operations.
statements.venue: State TV (written message, no public appearance)
statements.factCheckNote: Khamenei's claim that the enemy is 'defeated' is contradicted by ongoing Israeli airstrikes on Tehran. The 'false flag' claim regarding Turkey attacks is disputed: Turkey's MND said NATO intercepted a ballistic missile launched from Iran.

statements.text: I thank the Iraqi people, their religious leadership, and all Iraqi supporters, for their steadfast support of the Iranian people and the Islamic Republic in facing this aggression.
statements.context: Latest written statement from Iran's Supreme Leader, issued March 29, 2026. Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly on video or in person since assuming leadership on February 28. Trump said on March 29 that Khamenei is 'seriously wounded' — a claim not independently verified. Telegraph reported a leaked audio claiming only a 'minor leg injury.'
statements.venue: Official website / State media (written statement, no public appearance)
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Iranian state media and noted by Times of Israel and WION News. Continued absence from public view remains a major open question.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Iran's new supreme leader issues fresh statement as questions linger over his health

statements.text: A courageous son of Tangestan, a soldier of Iran, and a guardian of Islam.
statements.context: 4th public written message since succeeding his father, mourning IRGC Navy Chief Tangsiri who was killed at Bandar Abbas.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Xinhua, Republic World, and Tasnim News.

statements.text: The leverage of blocking the Strait of Hormuz should still be used. Dear warrior brothers! The demand of the masses is the continuation of effective and regret-inducing defense.
statements.context: Contradicted President Pezeshkian's ceasefire signals. Praised Hezbollah and vowed continued support. Shows power split between elected president and supreme leader.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, France 24 confirmed. Analysts noted direct contradiction with Pezeshkian's position.

statements.text: Message to Hezbollah's Sheikh Naim Qassem pledging continued support for resistance movements.
statements.context: Health confirmation via FM Araghchi — 'in good health, expected to appear soon.' Message sent to Hezbollah.
statements.factCheckNote: Health claim unverifiable independently. Life News Agency reported FM's statement.

statements.text: The unbroken ranks of the combatants and fighters on the path of truth in Islamic Iran form such a towering, deeply rooted front that terrorism and crime cannot even crack their resolve.
statements.context: Statement after assassination of IRGC Intelligence Chief Maj. Gen. Khademi. First public communication in weeks despite 30-day absence from public view since March 8 election.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN live updates April 7.

statements.text: Our armed forces are not deterred by the assassinations of commanders. They form such a towering, deeply rooted front that terrorism and crime cannot even crack their resolve.
statements.context: Written statement after IRGC intel chief Khademi killed. Reports persist Mojtaba Khamenei may be gravely ill/unconscious in Qom — only written/AI-generated statements attributed to him.
statements.venue: Written statement — attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei
statements.factCheckNote: CNN liveblog attributed statement. Open The Magazine reports Khamenei unconscious in Qom — raises questions about authorship.

statements.text: This is not the end of the war but all military branches should follow the Supreme Leader order and cease their fire.
statements.context: Ceasefire order broadcast on state TV. SNSC: 'Nearly all objectives achieved.'
statements.factCheckNote: Organiser, Axios confirmed.

statements.text: This is not the end of the war, but all military units must obey orders and uphold the ceasefire.
statements.context: Written message broadcast on state TV ordering military compliance
statements.factCheckNote: Israel Hayom confirmed broadcast. Health concerns remain — US-Israeli intel claims Khamenei 'unconscious' since Feb 28 strike.

statements.text: This is not the end of the war, but all military units must obey orders and uphold the ceasefire.
statements.context: Written directive (not filmed appearance) authorizing ceasefire compliance. Reportedly communicating via runners/written notes due to security fears. First directive since war began.
statements.factCheckNote: Israel Hayom, Fortune reported. Mojtaba alive and communicating but not appearing on camera.

statements.text: This is not the end of the war, but all military units must obey orders and uphold the ceasefire.
statements.context: Written statement read on Iranian state TV. First communication since start of war. Reportedly communicated via runners passing notes. Confirmed alive by Axios sources.
statements.factCheckNote: Israel Hayom, Fortune, Gulf News confirmed. Authenticity not independently verified but accepted by IRGC.

statements.text: We will take revenge for our father and all those killed in this war. Iran will bring management of the Strait of Hormuz into a new phase. Our Gulf neighbors: Iran is still awaiting an appropriate reaction from you.
statements.context: Statement read on state TV. First public threat since ceasefire. Signals Iran may permanently alter Hormuz transit regime.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN and Republic World confirmed broadcast.

statements.text: This is not the end of the war, but all military units must obey orders and uphold the ceasefire. We will never retreat, and vow to avenge the blood of our martyrs. Our revenge will be never ending.
statements.context: First public statement since becoming Supreme Leader — read on state TV
statements.factCheckNote: Read on Iranian state TV, confirmed by CBS, NBC, CNN

statements.text: Iran has achieved a decisive victory. We are entering a new phase in the management of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran will not forfeit its rights.
statements.context: Mojtaba Khamenei's first major post-ceasefire address, broadcast on state TV. Framed ceasefire as validation of Iran's strategy. Chinese state media amplified the message.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of 'decisive victory' disputed — PolitiFact rated Trump's similar claim false. Iran suffered massive infrastructure damage, thousands of casualties, and lost Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

statements.text: Our hands are on the trigger. We will certainly not leave the criminal aggressors who attacked our country unpunished. Iran has achieved a final victory and astonished the world.
statements.context: Marking 40 days since father Ali Khamenei killed. Threatened 'new phase' without elaboration. Statement broadcast during Islamabad talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of 'final victory' and 'astonished the world' disputed. Iran suffered massive damage. 'Hands on trigger' threatens ceasefire. Confirmed by The News Pakistan, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: Iran is courageously defending the land, water, and flag. Our drones strike the US and the Zionist murderers like lightning. The navy is ready to inflict new bitter defeats on its enemies. The 40-day war has exposed the weakness and humiliation of our adversaries.
statements.context: First Army Day (Farvardin 29) message as Supreme Leader. Also marks birth anniversary of his father Ali Khamenei (killed Feb 28). Published via Telegram.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Iran International, ANI News, Free Press Journal, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: Iran is now courageously defending the land, water, and flag that belong to it. Our valiant Navy stands ready to make the enemies taste the bitterness of new defeats.
statements.context: Written-only Army Day statement — still no video/audio appearances since becoming Supreme Leader (~6 weeks). Statement reportedly dictated by IRGC. Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen publicly since selection.
statements.factCheckNote: Written statement only — no video or audio. ISW and analysts question whether Mojtaba Khamenei is truly in control or a figurehead for IRGC Commander Vahidi.

statements.text: The bank of targets is specified, fingers are on the trigger, and our backing is the unity of the streets and the cohesion of officials.
statements.context: IRGC Aerospace and Navy commanders issued joint 'eye for head' reprisal threat. Reports indicate Mojtaba Khamenei is sidelined — IRGC generals led by Ahmad Vahidi are making key wartime decisions. Mojtaba receiving news through handwritten notes. CNN reports Trump administration reviewing options including targeting Vahidi.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV, Kurdistan24.

statements.text: By God's help and power, the bright future of the Persian Gulf region will be a future without America. A new chapter has opened — Iran will determine new legal rules in the Strait of Hormuz. We will protect our nuclear and missile capabilities as national assets.
statements.context: Persian Gulf Day message. Comes hours after Sec. State Rubio said the US has 'indications' Mojtaba is alive after months of conflicting reports about his status. The message reasserts Iran's intent to permanently change Hormuz governance.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV (official translation), The Week India, ANI News.

statements.text: Foreigners have no place in the Gulf except at the bottom of its waters. We will launch long and painful strikes against US positions if they renew attacks.
statements.context: Continuation of Persian Gulf Day defiance. IRGC Aerospace Commander Mousavi amplified with threat: 'We've seen what happened to your regional bases, we will see the same thing happen to your warships.' Mojtaba not seen in public since Feb 28; reportedly awaiting prosthetic leg.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Post, Fortune, Townhall. Mousavi threat confirmed by Arab News and Free Malaysia Today.

statements.text: Two months after the largest military deployment and aggression by the world's bullies in the region, and the United States' disgraceful defeat in its plans, a new chapter is unfolding for the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Written statement read on state television marking Persian Gulf Day. Framed the US campaign as a failure despite massive Iranian casualties and infrastructure damage.
statements.factCheckNote: US suffered significant losses but Iran's infrastructure damage far exceeds US losses. 'Disgraceful defeat' claim disputed by Western analysts.

statements.text: Iran has proven to the world in a military battle with the enemies its progress and excellence, but must also disappoint and defeat them in the stage of economic and cultural jihad. I call on business owners to avoid, as much as possible, layoffs and separation of their workforce.
statements.context: Called for 'economic and cultural jihad' amid collapsing rial (1,776,000/USD) and severe economic pressure from blockade. Urged prioritizing domestic manufacturing. First acknowledgment of economic pain from war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Middle East Eye, BSSNEWS.

statements.text: By God's help and power, the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz now enter a new chapter — under management that will bring calm, stability, and economic benefit to the Gulf nations. We promise a bright future without US presence.
statements.context: Broadcast on state TV May 2 amid IRGC announcement of new Hormuz management rules and parliament's 12-article Hormuz sovereignty law. Frames blockade-induced disruption as opportunity for permanent regional restructuring under Iranian leadership.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Week India, BSS News, Daily Post UK.

statements.text: Iranians must wage economic and cultural jihad — prioritize domestic goods over foreign imports. The closing of US bases in our region is essential to our long-term security.
statements.context: Statement following day-of UAE attacks. Combined call for economic resistance with explicit demand for closure of all US bases in the region — sharper escalation of post-ceasefire rhetoric.
statements.factCheckNote: Daily Post UK, BSS News confirmed.

statements.text: A new management approach to the Strait of Hormuz will bring calm and benefit Gulf nations economically. The US has suffered a disgraceful defeat. I call on Iranians to wage economic and cultural jihad — prioritize domestically manufactured goods.
statements.context: Written statement broadcast on state TV. Khamenei remains unseen publicly since assuming power, communicating only via statements and state media.
statements.factCheckNote: Disgraceful defeat claim is propaganda — US killed Ali Khamenei, destroyed nuclear infrastructure, maintains dominant military position. Hormuz new management = Iran toll/sovereignty claim rejected by international community.

statements.text: Iran will not submit to coercion. Threats and bullying lead nowhere. The era of US dominance over our region is ending.
statements.context: Written statement broadcast amid US-Iran 14-point MOU negotiations and Trump's 'much higher level bombing' ultimatum.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement issued in defiance of US negotiation pressure.

statements.text: The armed forces of the Islamic Republic are ready to crush any aggression. New guiding measures have been issued to pursue military operations and firmly confront adversaries.
statements.context: Met with Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbiya HQ commander). Meeting designed to show Khamenei is alive and in command. US Ambassador Waltz stated Khamenei is 'severely injured and in hiding' — conflicting claims unverifiable.
statements.venue: Meeting with Maj. Gen. Abdollahi — Tehran
statements.factCheckNote: Iran state media claims meeting occurred; Waltz claims Khamenei is injured/hiding. No independent verification. Khamenei has never appeared publicly since becoming Supreme Leader.

statements.text: The only place for the United States in the Persian Gulf is at the bottom of its waters.
statements.context: Defiant statement released via Iranian state media amid stalled ceasefire negotiations and the shaky US-Iran truce. Mojtaba Khamenei has still not appeared publicly since being declared Supreme Leader — statement was read on state TV. The Times of Israel reported this as a signal of Iran's continued hard line on Hormuz control despite diplomatic back-channels.
statements.venue: Iranian State TV broadcast — attributed statement
statements.factCheckNote: Statement reported by Times of Israel and attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei via state media. Khamenei has made no verified public appearance since his appointment as Supreme Leader.

statements.text: Now, we stand at the forefront of an unprecedented historical epic of resistance by the Iranian people against the two global terrorist armies — the United States and Israel. Officials must fulfill their responsibilities for the people who are standing against attacks.
statements.context: Statement issued on the 2nd anniversary of former President Raisi's death, calling US and Israel 'global terrorist armies' and framing the conflict as an 'unprecedented historical epic of resistance.' Iran's ISNA simultaneously reported that the latest US ceasefire proposal had 'narrowed the gaps to some extent' — revealing the Supreme Leader's continued belligerent public posture even as diplomatic channels remained active.
statements.venue: Official statement via Iranian state media
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Asiae.co.kr Korean news service citing official Iranian state media on May 21. Issued on occasion of 2nd anniversary of Raisi's death in helicopter crash.
statements.source: Asiae English — Mojtaba Khamenei statement May 21, 2026

statements.text: Among the most valuable achievements of the Third Sacred Defense against the American and Zionist invasion is the emergence of Iran at the level of a major, influential power.
statements.context: Statement posted on Mojtaba Khamenei's official X account on Day 82. US counterterrorism analyst Dr. Omar Mohammed (GWU Program on Extremism) told Fox News: 'Strip the euphemism away, and what Khamenei is invoking here is jihad — sacred religious war.' The framing of the conflict as 'Third Sacred Defense' mirrors Iranian clerical framing of the 1980s Iran-Iraq war as 'Sacred Defense.'
statements.venue: Official X account / Iranian State Media
statements.factCheckNote: Statement posted on official Mojtaba Khamenei X account confirmed by Fox News Day 82 liveblog. 'Third Sacred Defense' framing is a new rhetorical escalation — the first two 'Sacred Defenses' refer to the Iran-Iraq war (1980-88) and 2006 Lebanon war in Iranian clerical tradition.
statements.source: Fox News Iran war Day 82 liveblog

statements.text: Iran's enriched uranium is a sovereign strategic asset and will remain on Iranian soil. It cannot and will not be transferred abroad under any framework.
statements.context: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly issued an internal directive that Iran's approximately 440 kg of uranium enriched to 60% must remain inside Iran and cannot be transferred abroad, directly contradicting Trump's core demand. The directive was confirmed by multiple senior Iranian sources. Iran's Foreign Ministry officially denied the report as 'enemy propaganda.' The directive signals the IRGC — led by General Ahmad Vahidi — controls Iran's nuclear red lines, not civilian President Pezeshkian.
statements.venue: Internal directive — reported via Iranian state media
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Kathmandu Post/Reuters, CDM Press, Geo.tv citing multiple senior Iranian sources. Officially denied by Iranian FM as 'enemy propaganda.' Classified as 'likely' — multiple senior officials confirm, one official denies.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Iranian sources say regime decided to keep highly enriched uranium in Iran

statements.text: The only place that Americans belong in the Persian Gulf was at the bottom of its waters.
statements.context: Mojtaba Khamenei issued this threatening statement directed at the US as Iran-US peace negotiations intensified. The statement underscores the hardline faction's rejection of any accommodation with the US.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement reported by Iran International and multiple OSINT sources. Not independently confirmed by Reuters or AP wires.
statements.source: Iran International — Mojtaba Khamenei uranium directive and US threat

statements.text: The region will not return to past conditions, and Washington will no longer have a safe place to station military bases in the region. The clock cannot be turned back. 'Death to America' and 'Death to Israel' will continue to be the slogans of the Islamic Ummah, especially its youth. Israel will not live to see another 15 years.
statements.context: Day 88 — Mojtaba Khamenei's first extensive written public statement since succeeding his father Ali Khamenei (killed Feb 28, Day 1). The Hajj statement — released on the occasion of the annual pilgrimage in Mecca — was his most confrontational communication since the war began, broadcast on Iranian state TV. It was issued simultaneously as Iran's negotiating delegation (Araghchi, Ghalibaf, Hemmati) remained in Doha for MOU peace talks, suggesting deliberate hardline signaling to the domestic audience and Islamic world even as pragmatic negotiations continued. The '15 years' framing echoes his late father's 2015 statement that Israel 'would not exist in 25 years'; the Israeli state is now 78 years old.
statements.venue: Written statement for Hajj pilgrimage — Day 88, May 26, 2026 (broadcast Iranian state TV)
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Bloomberg, Washington Times, Euronews, and Jerusalem Post (May 26). The '15 years' claim: Israel was founded in 1948 (78 years ago), and Ali Khamenei's 2015 '25 years' statement is historical record.
statements.source: Bloomberg — Iran's Khamenei says no going back for Middle East rocked by war

statements.text: There will be no ceasefire. There are no contacts with the United States. Iran will defend itself by any means necessary.
statements.context: Press conference in Tehran.

statements.text: Iran has no interest in negotiations while under attack. Mediation requests from other countries have been rejected.
statements.context: Foreign Ministry spokesperson statement. Despite Oman pushing for mediation and President Pezeshkian acknowledging 'some countries have begun mediation efforts.'

statements.text: We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people. Iran will not accept any ceasefire while our territory is under attack.
statements.context: NBC News interview broadcast March 9. Araghchi rejected ceasefire demands and emphasized Iran's commitment to continued resistance.

statements.text: This is Operation Epic Mistake — a misadventure engineered by Israel and paid for by ordinary Americans. We have very bitter experience with US negotiations. Iran will fight as long as it takes.
statements.context: PBS NewsHour interview. Araghchi called the strikes a failure, ruled out negotiations with the US, and vowed continued missile strikes. Also stated: 'They failed at rapid regime change.'
statements.factCheckDetail: Calling it a 'failure' is contradicted by CENTCOM data showing 5,000+ targets struck and 90% reduction in Iranian missile launches. However, regime change has not occurred and Iran continues retaliating.

statements.text: Iran has now deployed its most powerful weapons including the Khoramshahr super-heavy missile. The enemy should understand this is only the beginning of our response capabilities.
statements.context: Day 12 statement following Iran's 37th wave of attacks using Khoramshahr super-heavy ballistic missiles, Iran's most powerful conventional weapon.

statements.text: We are not asking for ceasefire. Iran's war of resistance will continue until the aggression stops.
statements.context: Statement rejecting ceasefire calls. Contradicts Pezeshkian's peace conditions, aligning more with Mojtaba Khamenei's hardline position.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Iranian state media and international outlets

statements.text: UNESCO's silence on the destruction of Iran's historical and cultural landmarks by Israeli bombs is unacceptable. The international community stands complicit.
statements.context: Day 14 statement condemning UNESCO for not speaking out against Israel bombing Iranian cultural sites and landmarks during the campaign.
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's statement confirmed via Iranian state media. UNESCO has not issued a formal response to cultural site damage claims.

statements.text: Mocked US on X: 'After two weeks of war with Iran, White House is now begging the world to buy Russian crude.' Referenced the US waiver of Russia oil sanctions as evidence of economic desperation.
statements.context: Post on X/Twitter
statements.factCheckNote: US did waive Russia oil sanctions. 'Begging' characterization is editorial.

statements.text: Trump is begging others to send warships. This shows the failure of American power. We urge our Gulf neighbors to expel these foreign aggressors from our region.
statements.context: Response to Trump's call for international naval coalition to reopen Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Public press conference, statements confirmed by multiple outlets

statements.text: The end of the war depends on guarantees that attacks will not be repeated and payment of compensation for the damage caused during the conflict.
statements.context: Public statement setting conditions for ending hostilities
statements.factCheckNote: Public statement confirmed by multiple outlets.

statements.text: We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans.
statements.context: Time Magazine interview. Araghchi rejected ceasefire negotiations, stating Iran is ready for a long war. Dismissed US-brokered talks entirely.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote confirmed by Time Magazine interview.

statements.text: We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans. We never asked for a ceasefire.
statements.context: CBS Face the Nation interview. Araghchi reiterated Iran's refusal to negotiate with the US and explicitly stated Iran never requested a ceasefire, contradicting Trump administration claims of Iranian overtures.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote confirmed by CBS News transcript. Contradicts Trump's claim that Iran was 'begging' for talks.

statements.text: The strait is open, but closed to our enemies, to those who carried out this cowardly aggression against us and to their allies. We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation.
statements.context: Tehran press conference. Also revealed Iran receiving 'military cooperation' from Russia and China.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement broadcast live. Russia-China military cooperation claim is new and significant.

statements.text: Iran has never asked for a ceasefire, and we will not. But this cycle of war must end through negotiations, not through surrender.
statements.context: FM Araghchi addressed reports of ceasefire talks, insisting Iran would not request a ceasefire but remained open to diplomatic resolution. Revealed Russia and China providing 'military cooperation.'
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Iran International and multiple outlets. Iran's refusal to request ceasefire while leaving door open to negotiations reflects a face-saving diplomatic posture.

statements.text: Claims of contact between me and the US envoy are geared solely to mislead oil traders.
statements.context: Publicly denied back-channel communication with US envoy Witkoff, despite Axios report confirming contact resumed
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple sources including Axios confirm contact did occur; Araghchi's denial appears strategic

statements.text: The Islamic Republic has a strong political structure. The presence or absence of a single individual does not affect this structure.
statements.context: On Larijani killing
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote

statements.text: This war is not our war. We did not start it. The United States started it and is responsible for all consequences.
statements.context: Talk to Al Jazeera interview
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Hormuz blockade preceded US strikes

statements.text: We don't ask for ceasefire. We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes. The attack on South Pars is a new phase of war.
statements.context: Statement rejecting calls for ceasefire after Gulf energy strikes
statements.factCheckNote: NPR, PressTV confirmed

statements.text: This war is not our war. We did not start it. The United States started it and is responsible for all the consequences. We will end this war when we are certain it will not be repeated. Iran will demand reparations.
statements.context: Al Jazeera interview, March 19. Also stated: 'Naturally we will not allow our enemies to use this waterway' regarding Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Reparations demand is new. Hormuz statement confirms Iran's intent to maintain blockade.

statements.text: Our response to Israel's attack on our infrastructure employed a FRACTION of our power. The ONLY reason for restraint was respect for requested de-escalation. Zero restraint if infrastructure targeted again.
statements.context: Warning after South Pars strikes and Gulf retaliation
statements.factCheckNote: Posted on official Araghchi account

statements.text: Rejected ceasefire: 'We do not want this scenario repeated.' Nuclear material 'under the rubbles' with 'no plan to recover.' Negotiations 'off the table.'
statements.context: Talk to Al Jazeera interview
statements.factCheckNote: Interview broadcast live

statements.text: Signaled possible new rules for navigation through the Strait of Hormuz: rejected ceasefire in favor of a permanent end to attacks. Stated Iran will demand 'assurances that all attacks will stop' and reparations before agreeing to end the conflict. IRGC disputed Netanyahu's claim that Iran cannot build missiles.
statements.context: Day 21 — Iran's defiant posture on Nowruz; IRGC spokesman Naeini made same claim before being killed in airstrike
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and Times of Israel confirmed IRGC statement and Naeini's death.

statements.text: Our missile industry continues to function at full capacity. Iran's production capabilities have not been degraded. We will continue to respond to every Israeli attack.
statements.context: Statement echoing IRGC spokesman Naini's final claims about missile production; issued after Naini's death
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of unaffected missile production is disputed — Netanyahu said Iran 'can no longer manufacture ballistic missiles'

statements.text: We seek to end the war permanently. This requires a decisive and final end to the attacks — and war reparations. There is no good experience in talking with the Americans, so what good will come of it if we talk again?
statements.context: Araghchi statement reported by Al Arabiya as Iran's first signal of potential war-end conditions. Simultaneously, reports emerged of a CIA-Iranian intelligence backchannel through a third country. Araghchi set two conditions: permanent end to all attacks and reparations. Not yet confirmed by official Iranian state media as of publish time.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Al Arabiya — not independently confirmed by official Iranian statement as of press time. Described as likely by Al Arabiya's sourcing.

statements.text: We welcome any initiative that can fully end this war; we are ready to listen and consider. However, at present, the United States does not appear ready to halt its aggression. Therefore, we will continue to defend ourselves. We need a permanent, complete, and lasting end — not a temporary ceasefire.
statements.context: Araghchi appeared on NBC's Meet the Press rejecting all ceasefire proposals. He also denied any contact with US envoy Steve Witkoff since the war began: "My last contact with Witkoff was prior to his employer's decision to kill diplomacy with another illegal military attack on Iran." On Hormuz: "The strait is only closed to enemy ships."
statements.factCheckNote: NBC confirmed broadcast. Araghchi's claims about Witkoff contact independently reported by Drop Site News.

statements.text: PM Starmer is putting British lives in danger by allowing UK bases to be used for aggression against Iran. Iran will exercise its right to self-defence.
statements.context: Direct warning to UK Prime Minister Starmer after UK authorized use of Diego Garcia base for US Hormuz strike operations. Warning conveyed on X and separately by phone to UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper.
statements.venue: Post on X and phone call with UK FM Cooper
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News live updates and ITV News reporting.

statements.text: We don't believe in a ceasefire. We believe in ending the war on all fronts — in Lebanon, Yemen, Iraq, Iran and other countries of the region. We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation.
statements.context: Statement rejecting Trump's ceasefire narrative and Araghchi's public response to reports of Iranian backchannel contact with the CIA. Simultaneously demanded reparations for war damages as a precondition for any talks.
statements.venue: NBC News / CBS Face the Nation
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's rejection of ceasefire directly contradicts Trump's claim Iran was "ready to make a deal." Araghchi also separately demanded reparations — confirmed by multiple outlets.

statements.text: Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran. No insurer — and no Iranian — will be swayed by more threats. Try respect. Freedom of Navigation cannot exist without Freedom of Trade. Respect both — or expect neither.
statements.context: Posted on X in response to Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum. Also appeared on NBC's Meet the Press rejecting ceasefire calls and confirming Russian support 'in many different directions.' Sent letter to UN Security Council calling power plant threats 'inherently indiscriminate and clearly disproportionate.'
statements.factCheckNote: X post confirmed. NBC Meet the Press interview confirmed. UN letter confirmed by IRNA.

statements.text: Consequences of a US strike on the Bushehr nuclear power plant specifically would be very severe. The international community must understand what is at stake.
statements.context: Warning issued in response to Trump's threat to strike Iranian power plants if Hormuz remains closed. Bushehr NPP contains Russian-supplied nuclear reactors.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill and Times of Israel reporting on Iran's response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum.

statements.text: Attacks on power plants are inherently indiscriminate and clearly disproportionate. I urge the Security Council to compel the aggressors to immediately cease all unlawful attacks and pay reparations. Israel must also place its nuclear facilities under IAEA monitoring.
statements.context: Formal letter sent to UN Security Council documenting Iran's legal case against US-Israeli strikes. Also demanded that the Security Council compel Israel to place its nuclear facilities under IAEA safeguards.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by APA.az and NPR reporting on Araghchi's UN letter.

statements.text: Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran. No insurer — and no Iranian — will be swayed by more threats. Try respect.
statements.context: Response to Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum, issued on Day 24 morning via social media.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran maintains Hormuz was declared a "closed military zone" on March 2. CNBC vessel tracking shows ~13 ships/day transiting vs 153+ pre-war — effectively closed regardless of framing.
statements.source: The Hill — Araghchi Iran US conflict

statements.text: My last contact with Mr. Witkoff was prior to his employer's decision to kill diplomacy with another illegal military attack on Iran. Any claim to the contrary appears geared solely to mislead oil traders and the public. There is no direct or indirect contact with the United States.
statements.context: Araghchi's categorical denial of Trump's Truth Social claim of 'very good and productive conversations' with Iran. Issued hours after Trump's 5-day pause announcement. Iran's Fars News cited an unnamed government source confirming no US contact.
statements.venue: X (Twitter) post / Iranian Foreign Ministry statement
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's denial confirmed by Republic World, Euronews liveblog, and Al Jazeera. Iran's Fars News independently confirmed 'no direct or indirect contact.' Sharp contradiction with Trump's claim of productive talks.
statements.source: Republic World — Iran rejects Trump's productive talks claim

statements.text: Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran. Freedom of Navigation cannot exist without Freedom of Trade. Respect both — or expect neither.
statements.context: Araghchi X post pushing back on Trump's Hormuz ultimatum framing. Part of Iran's broader diplomatic counter-narrative on Day 24.
statements.factCheckNote: While technically the strait is not formally closed, AIS data shows less than 2% of pre-war transit levels — the IEA has characterized it as the largest oil supply disruption in modern history. Iran's mines and active IRGC deterrence are the primary cause of the shipping halt, not insurer decisions alone.

statements.text: Iran has the approval and authority to close this issue quickly if our conditions are met. But no agreement has been reached. Significant gaps remain.
statements.context: Reported private signal to US envoys Witkoff and Kushner per Iran International. Araghchi reportedly cited approval from Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Simultaneously, Iran's public statements denied all negotiations.
statements.venue: Private communication with US envoys (reported by Iran International)
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Iran International citing sources. Iran's public statements directly contradict — Ghalibaf called talks claims fabricated. Dual-track public/private diplomacy is consistent with historical Iranian negotiating patterns.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed to vessels linked to the United States and Israeli military aggression. This is a direct result of American and Israeli military aggression against the Iranian people.
statements.context: Araghchi's public statement to Malaysia's FM maintained Hormuz closure while Iran International simultaneously reported Araghchi privately told Witkoff and Kushner that Mojtaba Khamenei approved deal talks if conditions are met.
statements.factCheckNote: Hormuz closure is confirmed fact. Dual-track (public hardline / private signals) is a consistent Iranian diplomatic pattern.

statements.text: We have approval from the highest levels to close the issue quickly if conditions are met.
statements.context: Iran International reported Araghchi privately signaled readiness for a deal in a back-channel call with Witkoff, saying he had approval from Mojtaba Khamenei. This contradicts Iran's public denial of any negotiations.
statements.venue: Back-channel call with Witkoff (reported by Iran International via Ynet)
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Iran International citing Ynet. Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf publicly denied all talks, calling them 'fake news to manipulate oil markets.'

statements.text: Iran will sustain defensive measures until the United States and Israel regret their aggression against our nation.
statements.context: Araghchi told Chinese FM Wang Yi in a call that Iran will continue defensive operations. Wang Yi urged end to 'bullying behavior.' Iran simultaneously issued counter-demands including US base closures, war reparations, and missile program retention — US called demands 'ridiculous.'
statements.venue: Phone call with Chinese FM Wang Yi
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by PressTV and Chinese Foreign Ministry.

statements.text: Iran will not negotiate until: the aggression and assassinations halt; there is a binding guarantee the war will not resume; war reparations are paid; attacks on Hezbollah and Iraqi factions cease; and Iran's natural, legal right over the Strait of Hormuz is internationally recognized. No negotiations will be held prior to that.
statements.context: Iran's formal response to the US 15-point ceasefire proposal delivered via Pakistan. Araghchi issued five counter-conditions that effectively make negotiations impossible in the short term. The Hormuz sovereignty demand was described by US officials as 'ridiculous and unrealistic.' Iran also demanded JD Vance personally replace Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner as US negotiator — rejected by White House.
statements.factCheckNote: 5 counter-conditions confirmed by PressTV state news, Anadolu Agency, NPR, CNBC. Vance demand confirmed by Daily Beast. White House rejection confirmed by NBC News.

statements.text: Iran does not plan to negotiate and has no intention of ceasefire. Speaking of negotiations now is an admission of defeat. Our five conditions stand: halt all aggression and assassinations; binding guarantee of non-resumption; payment of war reparations; end attacks on Hezbollah and Iraqi militias; international recognition of Iran's legal authority over the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Statement on Iranian state television formally rejecting the US 15-point peace proposal transmitted via Pakistan. Araghchi also denied any direct or indirect US-Iran talks are underway, calling Trump's claims false.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by NPR, Al Jazeera, and PressTV. Iran's 5 counter-conditions confirmed by multiple independent outlets.
statements.source: NPR — Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war

statements.text: We do not want a ceasefire. We want the war to end in a way that it does not repeat, on our own terms. The damages to the people of Iran must also be compensated. We have not engaged in talks to end the war, and we do not plan on any negotiations.
statements.context: Public statement responding to reports of Iran-US ceasefire talks via Pakistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's formal position as stated publicly by FM Araghchi.

statements.text: The American proposal is maximalist and unreasonable. Messages being conveyed through our friendly countries and us responding by stating our positions or issuing the necessary warnings is not called negotiation or dialogue.
statements.context: Statement on the US 15-point ceasefire plan delivered via Pakistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and Times of Israel reporting on Iran's formal response to the US proposal.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran calls US proposal to end war maximalist, unreasonable

statements.text: Didn't the Americans say 'unconditional surrender'? So why are they talking about negotiation now? This is an admission of defeat. We do not want a ceasefire. Iran's conditions include: end to aggression with concrete guarantees against recurrence, recognized Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and full compensation for war damages.
statements.context: Statement via Iranian state media and interviews, Day 27. Araghchi confirmed Iran had received US messages via 'friendly countries' but framed them as having been rejected in line with Iran's 'principled positions.' Iran's 5 counter-conditions include Hormuz sovereignty recognition — which the US has called a 'non-starter.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's formal rejection of the US 15-point peace plan confirmed by multiple sources. Araghchi's quotes verified by Al Jazeera, NBC News, NPR. Iran's 5 counter-conditions including Hormuz sovereignty demand confirmed by IDN Financials and Times of Israel.

statements.text: Didn't the Americans say unconditional surrender? So why are they talking about negotiation now? The fact that they are talking about negotiation now is exactly an admission of defeat.
statements.context: Televised interview on Iranian state TV rejecting claims of ongoing negotiations with the US.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's characterization of its counter-conditions (ending strikes, reparations, Hormuz rights) as non-negotiable suggests it is indeed using diplomatic language but demanding terms the US will not accept.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iranian foreign minister rejects talks with US

statements.text: No negotiations have happened with the enemy until now, and we do not plan on any negotiations. Exchange of messages through mediators does not mean negotiations. Iran will exact a heavy price for Israeli crimes targeting nuclear facilities and civilian steel plants.
statements.context: Araghchi explicitly rejected characterization of Pakistan-mediated message exchanges as 'negotiations,' directly contradicting Trump's optimistic framing. Also condemned Israeli strikes on the Arak reactor, Ardakan yellowcake facility, and Isfahan steel plants on Day 28, alleging Israel claimed coordination with the US during Trump's stated diplomatic pause.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, The Hill, PBS. Araghchi's denial of negotiations is on-record and unambiguous.

statements.text: No negotiations have happened with the enemy until now, and we do not plan on any negotiations. Messages are being exchanged through friendly countries, but this is not negotiation. Iran will exact a HEAVY price for Israeli crimes — strikes on our nuclear sites and steel factories are an act of war against our civilian industrial base.
statements.context: Statement to Iranian state television, March 27. Also posted on social media noting Israel strikes on nuclear sites contradict Trump's extended diplomatic deadline.
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, NBC News, CBS News. The distinction between 'messages via intermediaries' and 'negotiations' aligns with Iran's official position rejecting direct talks.

statements.text: Israel's attacks on our nuclear facilities and industrial plants directly contradict President Trump's extended deadline for diplomacy. Israel will pay a heavy price for these crimes. Iran will not be swayed by threats. The strait of Hormuz is not closed — ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated, not Iran.
statements.context: Statement following Israeli strikes on Arak heavy water reactor and Ardakan yellowcake plant on Day 28
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi statements confirmed via OneNewsPage, The Hill, and Al Jazeera. Iranian FM has consistently denied direct US-Iran negotiations.
statements.source: OneNewsPage — Iran FM Abbas Araghchi gives warning to Israel

statements.text: Didn't the Americans say 'unconditional surrender'? Why are they talking about negotiation now? That's an admission of defeat. We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people. There will be a heavy price for Israeli crimes.
statements.context: Rejecting US calls for direct negotiations at the Islamabad summit, while confirming Iran submitted a 5-point counter-plan through Pakistan mediators
statements.venue: Press statement / NBC Meet the Press
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's comments confirmed by Al Jazeera and NBC. Iran simultaneously submitted a 5-point counter-plan through Pakistan — suggesting a gap between public rhetoric and back-channel diplomacy.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iranian FM rejects talks with US

statements.text: The deliberate targeting of journalists is a flagrant violation of international law. This is not a battlefield mistake — it is a targeted assassination policy. Iran will not negotiate under bombardment. We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people. There will be a heavy price for Israeli crimes against Iranian civilians.
statements.context: Araghchi issued two statements on March 29: condemning Israel's killing of journalists in Lebanon on Telegram, and reaffirming Iran's rejection of talks under fire.
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's Telegram statement confirmed by Al Jazeera. Statement on negotiations confirmed by NBC News.

statements.text: Tehran is skeptical about diplomatic efforts. The US is making unreasonable demands and contradictory actions. The messages received from friendly countries do not represent real negotiations with the United States.
statements.context: Phone call with Turkish Foreign Minister, relayed by Iranian state media. Iran's FM denied direct US-Iran talks are occurring despite Trump's claims of productive discussions.
statements.venue: Phone call with Turkish FM Hakan Fidan
statements.factCheckNote: Iranian state media IRNA reported the call. The Euronews reported Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt and Saudi Arabia seeking de-escalation as intermediaries.
statements.source: Euronews — Pakistan, Turkiye, Egypt and Saudi seek to de-escalate

statements.text: Israel and its partner-in-crime believe that knowledge can be bombed away. It's reflected in the murders of Iran's scientists and attacks on Iran's nuclear sites, and now universities. Your actions ooze of desperation.
statements.context: Statement posted on X (formerly Twitter) in response to US-Israeli strikes on the University of Science and Technology in Tehran and Isfahan University of Technology. Araghchi directed the statement directly at Israel and the US.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by ANI News and multiple Iran-monitoring outlets.
statements.source: ANI News — 'Your actions ooze of desperation' — Araghchi on US-Israeli strikes on Iranian universities

statements.text: Iran respects the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and considers it a brotherly nation. High time to eject US forces. Just look at what we did to their aerial command.
statements.context: Posted on X, urging Saudi Arabia to expel US forces from its territory. Also boasted about Iranian strikes on US/Israeli air defense systems.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple outlets monitoring Araghchi's social media.

statements.text: This is in fact a war on the entire Islamic region. We demand a permanent end to attacks and reparations before any agreement. The regime enjoys complete stability and strength.
statements.context: Rejecting US ceasefire terms, insisting Lebanon must be included in any deal
statements.factCheckNote: 'Complete stability' contradicts reports of IRGC hardliner empowerment and leadership decimation (NYT, JPost).

statements.text: We have received direct messages from US envoy Steve Witkoff but these are not negotiations. Iran has never had a good experience negotiating with the US. Only for the ships of those who are at war with us, this strait is closed. That is normal during war.
statements.context: Al Jazeera exclusive interview
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Times of Israel.

statements.text: We do not have any faith that negotiations with the US will yield any results. The trust level is at zero. Iran is completely ready to confront any sort of ground attack. We are prepared for at least six months of war. Russia is helping us in many different directions.
statements.context: Extended Al Jazeera interview. Confirmed Russian assistance. Demanded Lebanon included in ceasefire. Said Iran seeks complete end to war with reparations and sovereignty guarantees.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera broadcast confirmed. NBC News corroborated key claims.

statements.text: We do not have any faith that negotiations with the US will yield any results. The trust level is at zero. We are prepared for at least six months of war. We seek a complete end to the war — not a ceasefire.
statements.context: Al Jazeera exclusive interview. Confirmed contact with US envoy Witkoff but denied formal negotiations. On Hormuz: 'Only for ships of those at war with us, this strait is closed.' On ground invasion: 'We are waiting for them.'
statements.factCheckNote: PBS, Investing Live confirmed key quotes.

statements.text: Striking civilian structures, including unfinished bridges, will not compel Iranians to surrender. It only conveys the defeat and moral collapse of an enemy in disarray. Every bridge and building will be built back stronger.
statements.context: Response to US strike on B1 Bridge (Tehran-Karaj) and expanded infrastructure targeting
statements.factCheckNote: TASS and Al Jazeera confirmed Araghchi statement.

statements.text: The future of the Strait of Hormuz should be decided by Iran and Oman alone. We have direct contact with US envoy Witkoff but this does not constitute negotiations.
statements.context: Phone call with Philippine FM on Hormuz transit + comments on US contact
statements.factCheckNote: Al Arabiya, GlobalSecurity confirmed.

statements.text: Radioactive fallout will end life in GCC capitals, not Tehran. We will not accept a ceasefire but rather seek a complete end to the war, not in Iran alone but in the entire region.
statements.context: Multiple statements on April 4: warning about Bushehr nuclear plant risks to Gulf states, rejecting ceasefire in favor of complete war end, and stating trust with US is 'at zero.'
statements.factCheckNote: Bushehr radiation risk to GCC capitals is overstated per nuclear experts — prevailing winds blow east, not west toward Gulf states. However, any nuclear facility damage carries genuine risk.

statements.text: We are deeply grateful to Pakistan for sustained mediation efforts. Western media claims that Iran refused Pakistan-hosted talks are fabrications. We are completely ready to confront any sort of ground attack.
