statements.context: Praising Pakistan's mediation role while dismissing claims Iran refused talks. Also warned against US ground invasion.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran FM says trust is 'at zero' but credits Pakistan mediation. Multiple reports confirm Iran DID reject a specific 48-hour ceasefire via Fars agency.

statements.text: Iran never refused to go to Islamabad for peace talks. We thank Pakistan for its sincere mediation efforts.
statements.context: Clarifying Iran's position on Pakistan-mediated peace negotiations amid reports Iran had rejected talks
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Daily Pakistan.

statements.text: Iran's reaction would be one of reciprocating any such attack. Trump's ultimatum constitutes incitement to war crimes.
statements.context: FM spokesperson Baghaei responding to Trump's 'blowing up the whole country' threat. Senior official says Hormuz won't reopen until Iran 'fully compensated.'
statements.factCheckNote: ABC News, Al Jazeera reported.

statements.text: Iran has formulated its response to the ceasefire proposals. Negotiation is in no way compatible with ultimatum, crime, or threats. Iran demands a complete end to the war including Lebanon. We are working on the Oman protocol for the Strait.
statements.context: Response to Islamabad Accord ceasefire proposal. In overnight contact with Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, NPR, Al-Monitor confirmed.

statements.text: We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans. We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.
statements.context: CBS Face the Nation interview. Also confirmed April 5 phone call with Russian FM Lavrov.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement verified via CBS News broadcast.

statements.text: We won't merely accept a ceasefire. We only accept an end of the war with guarantees that we won't be attacked again.
statements.context: Iran formally rejects 45-day ceasefire proposal from Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey mediators via diplomatic mission in Cairo. Demands permanent end to war.
statements.factCheckNote: AP, NPR, Bloomberg confirmed.

statements.text: As of now, there is NO 'agreement' on any ceasefire or cessation of military operations.
statements.context: Posted on X after 72 hours of phone calls with counterparts from India, Russia, Turkey, Japan, Qatar, France, Egypt, and Pakistan. Iran's 10-paragraph response demands: end to regional conflicts, Hormuz safe passage protocol, war reparations, lifting sanctions.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via Araghchi's X account and Dropsite News.

statements.text: Israeli-US aggressors have bombed the MIT of Iran — targeting Sharif University of Technology. We emphasize the responsibility of the international community to confront violations of international law and prevent the normalization of war crimes.
statements.context: Condemned strike on Sharif University. Called Indian FM Jaishankar and Russian FM Lavrov about de-escalation.
statements.factCheckNote: GlobalSecurity, Blitz India Media confirmed.

statements.text: Pezeshkian's openness to de-escalation was immediately killed by President Trump. We engaged Qatar, Pakistan in overnight mediation efforts.
statements.context: Phone calls with Qatar FM Al Thani (who condemned Iran's Gulf attacks) and Pakistan's Asim Munir for mediation.
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi X post confirmed. Qatar call confirmed by Times of Israel.

statements.text: For a period of two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces and with due consideration of technical limitations. If attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations.
statements.context: Confirmed Iran's acceptance of the two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan. Iran retains coordination authority over Hormuz transits.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PBS, Al Jazeera, Axios.

statements.text: If attacks against Iran are halted our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations. Safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.
statements.context: SNSC statement accepting ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, NBC News confirmed.

statements.text: If attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations. For a period of two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces and with due consideration of technical limitations.
statements.context: Official statement laying out Iran's ceasefire terms for Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera confirmed statement. Note: 'technical limitations' and 'coordination' requirements may limit actual transit.

statements.text: If attacks against Iran are halted our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations. Safe passage through Hormuz is possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces and with due consideration of technical limitations.
statements.context: Statement on ceasefire terms and Hormuz conditions
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, NPR confirmed.

statements.text: The ball is in the US court, and the world is watching whether it will act on its commitments. I raised Israeli ceasefire violations in Iran and Lebanon with Pakistan's army chief.
statements.context: Phone call with Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir raising alleged ceasefire violations including Lebanon strikes and Lavan refinery attack
statements.factCheckNote: Al Arabiya, CNBC confirmed.

statements.text: If attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations. Iran will provide safe passage through Strait of Hormuz for 14 days in coordination with Iranian armed forces.
statements.context: Posted on X confirming ceasefire terms. Thanked Pakistan's PM Sharif for mediation. Key negotiator alongside Pakistan's Asim Munir.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, CNBC confirmed. Iran's interpretation of Hormuz terms disputes US 'complete opening.'

statements.text: The ball is in the U.S. court, and the world is watching whether it will act on its commitments. The Iran-US Ceasefire terms are clear and explicit: the U.S. must choose — ceasefire or continued war via Israel. It cannot have both.
statements.context: Araghchi raising ceasefire violations with Pakistani mediators. Iran considering withdrawal from agreement.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News and Al Arabiya confirmed.

statements.text: If attacks against Iran are halted our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations. For a period of two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.
statements.context: Iran's formal response to ceasefire proposal
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, NPR

statements.text: We expressed appreciation for the constructive role of Pakistan. For two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.
statements.context: Araghchi outlined ceasefire terms. Also held first phone call with Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan since war began — discussed reducing tensions.
statements.venue: Foreign Ministry Statement / Phone call with Saudi FM
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The National, Al Arabiya.

statements.text: It would be dumb for the US to let Netanyahu kill diplomacy via continued Lebanon strikes. The ceasefire must cover Lebanon — this was part of Iran's 10-point proposal. Three parts of our 10-point ceasefire have already been violated.
statements.context: Araghchi criticized continued Israeli strikes on Lebanon as threatening to destroy the ceasefire. Also warned three violations of Iran's 10-point proposal have occurred (Lebanon strikes, drone in Iranian airspace, enrichment denial).
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera. Lebanon inclusion remains contested — US/Israel say it was never part of the deal.

statements.text: Iranian delegation (Ghalibaf, Araghchi, SNSC Sec. Ahmadian, CB Gov. Hemmati) arrived in Islamabad under aerial escort. Ghalibaf set two preconditions: Lebanon ceasefire and release of frozen Iranian assets BEFORE negotiations begin. Accused US/Israel of three ceasefire violations.
statements.context: Iranian delegation arrived at Nur Khan Airbase late April 10. Ghalibaf's preconditions could stall talks before they begin. Indirect format planned at Serena Hotel with Pakistani mediators.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by IranWire, CNBC, PressTV, Daily Signal.

statements.text: Iran enters these negotiations with complete distrust toward Washington due to its repeated breaches of commitment and treachery against diplomacy. The US must choose — ceasefire or continued war via Israel. Tehran could abandon the ceasefire if Israeli strikes on Lebanon continue.
statements.context: Araghchi in phone call with German FM Johann Wadephul ahead of Saturday talks at Serena Hotel. Iran's 'complete distrust' message signals extremely difficult negotiations ahead.
statements.venue: Phone call with German FM Wadephul
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV, Middle East Eye.

statements.text: We engaged with the US in good faith in intensive talks at the highest level in 47 years to end the war, but when we were just inches away from an Islamabad MoU, we encountered maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade. Zero lessons learned.
statements.context: Araghchi posted on social media after 21 hours of failed Islamabad negotiations. Also called Qatar and Saudi FMs to warn of 'dangerous consequences of provocative US actions in the Persian Gulf.'
statements.venue: Social media / Phone calls with Qatar and Saudi FMs
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Aaj English, MENAFN, Open The Magazine, Defapress.

statements.text: We thanked Oman for its responsible, principled stance and commitment to safeguarding regional peace without external interference. The dangerous repercussions of US provocations in the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz threaten the entire region.
statements.context: Phone calls with Omani FM and Russian FM Lavrov. Also spoke with Egyptian FM Abdelatty about Islamabad negotiations. Iran in continuous message exchange with Pakistan for Round 2.
statements.venue: Phone calls with Omani, Russian, Egyptian FMs
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV.

statements.text: Tehran came to talks with the US in good faith but was met with maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade. We remain open to constructive engagement but will not negotiate under duress.
statements.context: Statement reflecting on failed Islamabad Round 1 talks amid escalating US blockade. Iran rejected US demand for 20-year enrichment moratorium; countered with 5-year proposal (also rejected).
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

statements.text: Our commitment to promoting peace and stability in the region remains firm.
statements.context: Met Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir in Tehran. Expressed appreciation for Pakistan's hosting of talks, calling it a reflection of 'depth and strength' of bilateral relations. Separately spoke with Chinese FM Wang Yi by phone — briefed on negotiations; said Iran willing to seek 'rational and practical solution.'
statements.venue: Tehran — Meeting with Pakistan Army Chief Munir
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV, Xinhua, People's Daily.

statements.text: The Iran-U.S. Ceasefire terms are clear and explicit: the U.S. must choose — ceasefire or continued war via Israel. It cannot have both. The world sees the massacres in Lebanon. The ball is in the U.S. court, and the world is watching whether it will act on its commitments.
statements.context: Posted on X responding to Israeli ceasefire violations in Lebanon. Raised violations with Pakistan's army commander. Separately, Deputy FM Khatibzadeh at Antalya Diplomacy Forum stated: 'We are not accepting any temporary ceasefire' — demands comprehensive end 'from Lebanon to the Red Sea.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, CBS News, Anadolu Agency.

statements.text: In line with the ceasefire in Lebanon, the passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open for the remaining period of ceasefire, on the coordinated route as already announced by Ports and Maritime Organisation of the Islamic Republic of Iran.
statements.context: MAJOR development: Iran declares Hormuz completely open for commercial traffic during ceasefire. Dow futures rallied 500+ points. Oil dropped sharply. Markets see possible resolution path.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg, CNBC, Washington Times, NBC News.

statements.text: Iran's enriched uranium stockpile will not be transferred anywhere. Any agreement must respect Iran's sovereign rights to peaceful nuclear technology.
statements.context: Iran FM rejected Trump's claims that Iran agreed to hand over enriched uranium. Key sticking points: US demands 20-year moratorium vs Iran's 5-year offer; disposition of ~450kg at 60% enrichment.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Tribune India, Bloomberg.

statements.text: The United States is not serious about resolving the conflict. While claiming diplomacy, the US carries out behaviours — ship threats, unlawful blockade, excessive demands — that in no way indicate seriousness. As of now we have no plans for the next round of negotiations. Iran does not accept any deadlines or ultimatums.
statements.context: FM Spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei press briefing, delivered Araghchi's position. Comes after US seizure of M/V Touska (Apr 19) and Iran re-closure of Hormuz (Apr 18). Effectively kills Round 2 Islamabad talks scheduled for week of Apr 21.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Asharq Al-Awsat, Free Malaysia Today, ANI News.

statements.text: I briefed Foreign Minister Lavrov on the latest developments. The American naval blockade is illegal under international law. Russia stands with Iran's sovereignty and territorial integrity.
statements.context: Phone call with Russian FM Lavrov on April 21. Russia condemned US naval blockade. Russian oil sanctions waiver extended through mid-May. Both sides discussed ceasefire status and Touska seizure.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera live updates April 21.

statements.text: The US naval blockade is an act of war and thus a violation of the ceasefire. Striking a commercial vessel and taking its crew hostage is an even greater violation. Iran knows how to neutralize restrictions, how to defend its interests, and how to resist bullying.
statements.context: Posted on X after US seized M/T Tifani. In phone call with Pakistan FM Dar, said US 'pursues malicious goals and lacks the necessary seriousness in its engagement.'
statements.venue: Social Media / Phone Call with Pakistan FM
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Global Security, CBS News live updates, Shillong Times.

statements.text: Iran knows how to neutralize restrictions, how to defend its interests, and how to resist bullying. There is no prospect for participation in talks while the blockade continues.
statements.context: Reiterated position on April 22 as IRGC fired on 3 ships in Hormuz. Iran's UN envoy Iravani said talks could resume 'as soon as Washington ends the naval blockade.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Pravda Australia, Global Security / PressTV.

statements.text: The aggressors are fully responsible for any instability in the Strait of Hormuz. The current situation stems directly from US lawlessness and its illegal military strikes against a sovereign UN member.
statements.context: Meeting with South Korea FM's Special Envoy Chung Byung-ha in Tehran. Araghchi also told Italy's FM that Hormuz instability 'stems directly from US lawlessness.' Follows IRGC seizure of 2 ships.
statements.venue: Tehran — meeting with South Korean envoy
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by ANI News and Press TV. Meeting with South Korean envoy confirmed.

statements.text: The aggressors are fully responsible for any instability in the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Part of coordinated Iranian unity statement rejecting claims of leadership fractures.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct X post confirmed.

statements.text: My trip to Islamabad is for bilateral consultations with Pakistan regarding the ongoing developments in the region, as well as the latest status of the war imposed by the United States and the Israeli regime against Iran. I will continue to Moscow and Muscat.
statements.context: Statement to IRNA on arriving in Islamabad with small delegation Friday evening. Tasnim: 'no negotiations with the Americans on the agenda.' US Witkoff, Kushner arrive Saturday for direct talks; Vance on standby.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera feature, BSS News, Press TV. All verified.

statements.text: Iran's observations will be conveyed to Pakistan. No meeting is planned to take place between Iran and the US.
statements.context: Arrived in Islamabad Friday evening for regional tour to Pakistan, Oman, and Moscow. Received by Pakistan Deputy PM Ishaq Dar and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir. Iran FM spokesperson Baghaei denied any planned talks with the US despite White House confirming Witkoff/Kushner headed to Pakistan Saturday.
statements.venue: Islamabad, Pakistan — via FM spokesperson Baghaei
statements.factCheckNote: Arrival confirmed by Dawn, PressTV. However White House says Iran 'reached out and asked for in-person conversation' — direct contradiction with Iran's denial.

statements.text: I have explained Iran's positions regarding the latest developments related to the ceasefire and the complete end of the imposed war against Iran. Pakistan plays a vital role of mediation and good offices. We are en route to Muscat and then Moscow.
statements.context: Iranian FM departure remarks from Islamabad after meeting PM Sharif, Army Chief Munir, and FM Dar. Continuing regional tour to Muscat (Oman) then Moscow. Trump cancelled Witkoff/Kushner trip later same day.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Press TV, Dawn.

statements.text: Our neighbours are our priority. I briefed His Majesty on Iran's viewpoints concerning regional developments and the path toward ending the imposed war. We have yet to see if the United States is truly serious about diplomacy.
statements.context: Araghchi met Sultan Haitham bin Tarik at Al Barakah Palace in Muscat — his first visit to the Gulf since the war began. Expected to return to Pakistan then travel to Moscow.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The National, PressTV, Muscat Daily.

statements.text: Consulted with Russian officials on the latest status of the negotiations, the ceasefire and surrounding developments. Russia remains a key partner in our effort to end the imposed war.
statements.context: Araghchi met President Putin, FM Lavrov, and NSA Ushakov at the Kremlin on April 27. Briefed Russian officials on Iran's red lines including nuclear program and Hormuz. Attended by Iranian delegation including Jalali and Gharibabadi.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Kremlin.ru, Euronews, Sputnik.

statements.text: The US approaches caused the previous round of negotiations, despite progress, to fail to reach its goals because of the excessive demands.
statements.context: Statement upon arrival in St. Petersburg for Putin meeting. Araghchi completing 3-country diplomatic tour: Pakistan → Oman → Russia. Also told mediators there is 'no consensus inside the Iranian leadership' about how to address US demands.
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Moscow Times, NPR, RFE/RL. Confirmed.

statements.text: Reports of a Pakistan-mediated agreement to reopen Hormuz in exchange for delayed nuclear talks are not authorized by Tehran. They are speculation by foreign press.
statements.context: Statement carried by Tasnim and on Araghchi's Telegram channel denying Axios reporting that a quid-pro-quo Hormuz-for-nuclear-delay arrangement had been brokered through Pakistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Tasnim carried the denial; Pakistani MFA neither confirmed nor denied a mediation role.

statements.text: Iran is stable, solid and powerful. The world has now realised Iran's true power. Slow progress in any talks is the result of Washington's destructive habits — unreasonable demands, frequently changing positions and threatening rhetoric.
statements.context: Remarks during Araghchi's regional tour following his April 27 meetings with Putin and Lavrov in St. Petersburg. Pushed back on the Trump 'state of collapse' narrative.
statements.venue: Press Pool / Saint Petersburg & Pakistan tour
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and Washington Post coverage of Araghchi's diplomatic tour.

statements.text: Iran has great friends and allies like Russia. Our strategic partnership continues to deepen in the face of American aggression.
statements.context: After 90-minute meeting with Putin in St. Petersburg, Araghchi described Iran-Russia ties as a 'strategic partnership.' Then traveled to Islamabad for continued Pakistan-mediated talks. Putin told Araghchi Russia was ready to do 'everything that serves your interests.'
statements.factCheckNote: NPR, Al Jazeera, Kremlin.ru confirmed.

statements.text: We discussed the latest ceasefire developments and the situation in the Strait of Hormuz. India is an important partner and we will remain in close touch.
statements.context: Phone call with India's External Affairs Minister Jaishankar to discuss Hormuz and ceasefire developments. Follows Araghchi's diplomatic tour to Pakistan, Oman, and Russia — signaling Iran expanding its support network beyond traditional allies.
statements.venue: Phone call with Indian FM Jaishankar
statements.factCheckNote: India TV News, Tribune India confirmed.

statements.text: Iran remains open to diplomacy if Washington alters its expansionist approach and threatening rhetoric. We have briefed our friends in the region on Iran's latest position.
statements.context: Phone calls with the foreign ministers of Turkey, Egypt, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Azerbaijan briefing them on Iran's new peace proposals delivered via Pakistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Mehr News confirmed Araghchi calls May 1.

statements.text: Iran is open to diplomacy if the US drops its excessive demands and stops using the language of threats.
statements.context: Follow-up to May 1 diplomatic blitz. Also held call with EU High Representative Kallas on Hormuz security arrangements. Iran's proposal: open Hormuz first, then negotiate nuclear program separately.
statements.venue: Phone call with EU HR Kallas
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV, APA (Azerbaijan Press Agency).

statements.text: Netanyahu's gamble has directly cost America $100 billion so far, four times what is claimed by the Pentagon. The Pentagon is lying about war costs.
statements.context: Accusation that Pentagon is understating war costs. Part of broader diplomatic offensive.
statements.factCheckNote: Pentagon official figure is $25B. No independent verification of Araghchi's $100B claim.

statements.text: The ball is in the United States' court to choose the path of diplomacy or the continuation of a confrontational approach. Iran is prepared for both paths.
statements.context: Deputy FM Gharibabadi speaking on behalf of Iran's foreign ministry after US rejected 14-point proposal. Signaling readiness for either outcome.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Manila Times, PressTV.

statements.text: Iran's 14-point proposal focuses solely on ending the war and contains nothing pertaining to the nuclear field. Today's scheduled meeting is postponed for technical reasons.
statements.context: Briefed Omani and German counterparts on Iran's diplomatic efforts; cancelled scheduled May 4 US-Iran negotiation meeting citing 'technical reason.' Reaffirmed Iran's position that nuclear talks are separate from war-ending negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: GlobalSecurity, Pravda EN coverage confirmed cancellation and 14-point plan framing.

statements.text: Events in the Strait of Hormuz make clear that there's no military solution to a political crisis. Project Freedom is Project Deadlock. As talks are making progress with Pakistan's gracious effort, the U.S. should be wary of being dragged back into quagmire by ill-wishers.
statements.context: Statement after speaking with Pakistan DPM/FM Ishaq Dar on May 4. Iran remains open to diplomacy if US drops excessive demands.
statements.factCheckNote: Characterization of Project Freedom as failure is opinion. Pakistan-mediated talks confirmed ongoing by both sides.

statements.text: I am traveling to Beijing for talks with my Chinese counterpart Wang Yi on bilateral relations and regional developments. Strategic partners must coordinate as the United States escalates risks in the Gulf.
statements.context: First visit to China since the war began. Bloomberg confirmed travel; the trip lands amid Project Freedom escort operations and new EU sanctions on 19 Iranian officials.
statements.venue: Iranian Foreign Ministry — Beijing trip announcement
statements.factCheckNote: Bloomberg confirmed Araghchi's first China trip since war began; The Hill carried complementary remarks on Pakistan-mediated talks 'making progress.'

statements.text: Events in the Strait of Hormuz make clear that there's no military solution to a political crisis. Project Freedom is Project Deadlock. Tehran's diplomacy will continue from a position of strength.
statements.context: Araghchi mocked US escort operation as a failure after only 2 ships transited Day 1. Warned US of being dragged into a 'quagmire.'
statements.factCheckNote: The National, Al Jazeera confirmed Araghchi's 'Project Deadlock' framing.

statements.text: China is a close friend and our cooperation will even become stronger. Iran has attained an elevated international standing after the war. We only accept a fair and comprehensive agreement.
statements.context: Araghchi met Chinese FM Wang Yi in Beijing — first visit to China since war began. Comes one week before Trump-Xi summit on May 14-15. Discussed Hormuz, nuclear program, and sanctions.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, CNBC, CNN, Washington Post confirmed meeting and statements.

statements.text: Iran calls for dialogue and diplomacy. Tehran is finalising its views on the US proposal and will respond once a position has been reached.
statements.context: Araghchi in a phone call with Pakistani counterpart as Tehran reviews the US 14-point peace MOU. The MOU envisions Iran easing Hormuz grip and US gradually lifting blockade over 30 days. Key sticking point: enrichment moratorium duration (Iran proposed 5 years, US demanded 20, sources expect 12-15 year landing).
statements.venue: Phone call with Pakistani Foreign Minister
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera live blog and Foreign Ministry spokesman statement.

statements.text: The US has abandoned diplomacy in favor of a reckless military adventure. Iran attacked US destroyers only after the US struck an Iranian oil tanker near Jask port. We only accept a fair and comprehensive agreement.
statements.context: Araghchi accused US of provocation in Hormuz incident. Earlier visited Beijing May 6 where he endorsed China's 4-point peace plan with Wang Yi. Meeting one week before Trump-Xi summit.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and Chinese MFA.

statements.text: Ending the war and lifting the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz are prerequisites to any nuclear discussions. The US demands complete surrender — not genuine dialogue. Iran is ready to prove the peaceful nature of its nuclear program, but only after the aggression stops.
statements.context: Remarks at BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting in New Delhi. Araghchi met Indian FM Jaishankar on sidelines. FM spokesman Baghaei reinforced the same preconditions in a separate India Today interview. Iran International reported hardliners are pushing against any compromise.
statements.venue: BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting, New Delhi
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV, GlobalSecurity/PressTV wire, BusinessWorld. Baghaei quotes confirmed by India Today Global.

statements.text: The US and Israel continue their policy of expansionism and warmongering. Instability in the Middle East is a lose-lose proposition for all sides — including the aggressors. Collusion with Israel will not be forgotten.
statements.context: Araghchi posted on X and issued statements denouncing the alleged Netanyahu secret UAE visit and the Trump-Xi Beijing summit as they unfolded. He warned the UAE against what he called 'collusion with Israel,' claiming Iranian intelligence knew of the alleged Netanyahu-UAE meeting before Israel made it public.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog May 14 and Day 76 summary.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran War Day 76 liveblog

statements.text: We Iranians never bow to any pressure or threat, but we reciprocate the language of respect. As much as our powerful armed forces remain ready to exact devastating retribution on foreign aggressors, my people are peace-loving and do not seek war. Imperial power in decline wants to turn back the clock, and is desperately lashing out on its way down.
statements.context: Araghchi addressed the BRICS foreign ministers' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15. He urged member states to unequivocally condemn US-Israeli 'illegal aggression' against Iran. He accused the UAE — a fellow BRICS member — of being directly involved in military operations against Iran, causing tension within the bloc. BRICS failed to reach a unified position on the conflict due to disagreement between Iran and the UAE.
statements.venue: BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting — New Delhi, India
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by PressTV, Al Jazeera, The Tribune India, and WANA News Agency. Araghchi's exact quote confirmed by multiple outlets.

statements.text: Tehran has no trust in the United States and will only negotiate if it's serious. The enriched uranium issue has been postponed — it is not under discussions. The contradictory messages from Washington make Iran doubt US intentions. We never wanted nuclear weapons and never will.
statements.context: Araghchi's press statements at the BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting sidelines in New Delhi, May 15. Confirmed US-Iran nuclear talks in 'deadlock' over enriched uranium — Russia's offer to store Iran's enriched uranium 'appreciated but not on agenda.' Also held a bilateral meeting with Indian FM Jaishankar. Context: Trump simultaneously told reporters he was open to a 20-year nuclear suspension (policy shift).
statements.venue: Press statements — BRICS sidelines, New Delhi, India
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by WION and Al Jazeera liveblog. Jaishankar bilateral confirmed by Republic World.

statements.text: To virtually everyone in this room, our resistance against US bullying is not an unfamiliar battle. So many of us encounter slight variations of the same repugnant coercion. It is high time for us to jointly step up and work towards making clear that those practices belong in the dustbin of history.
statements.context: Araghchi addressed BRICS Foreign Ministers in New Delhi, May 15, 2026, calling on bloc nations to unite against US pressure. He also singled out the UAE for supporting Israel and the US in their aggression against Iran. The BRICS summit ended without a joint statement for the first time in BRICS history due to Iran war divisions.
statements.venue: BRICS Foreign Ministers Meeting, New Delhi
statements.factCheckNote: Quote confirmed by Al Jazeera, Democracy Now, and India.com from Araghchi's BRICS speech on May 15, 2026.

statements.text: We received messages again from the Americans saying they are willing to continue talks. But we have no trust in them. The ceasefire is very shaky. We are trying to keep it to give diplomacy a chance. Iran doubts US seriousness about talks amid nuclear deadlock.
statements.context: Araghchi spoke to reporters May 15-16 following BRICS sideline meetings in New Delhi. Confirmed Iran has received new US messages expressing willingness to negotiate but reiterated deep distrust of Washington. Described current ceasefire as "very shaky." After meeting Indian FM Jaishankar, also stated the Strait of Hormuz remains open to all nations except those at war with Iran.
statements.venue: Press statements — New Delhi, India / media interviews
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ANI News (May 15 interview), Al Jazeera (May 15 report on Iran doubting US seriousness), and The Wire (Jaishankar bilateral).

statements.text: Iran has not received any written nuclear proposal from the United States. Americans warn of consequences but have not delivered a formal document. We await substance, not ultimatums.
statements.context: Araghchi publicly contradicted Trump's May 16 claim that the US had sent Iran a written nuclear deal proposal. The public dispute over whether a formal proposal was transmitted signals the breakdown in the diplomatic channel. Iran FM also warned US of continued economic costs of the 'war of choice' on Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's denial confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog May 16, 2026. Contradicts Trump's public statement about sending a proposal.

statements.text: We are in doubt about their seriousness, but the moment we feel that they are serious and they are ready for a fair and balanced deal, we will certainly proceed in the course of negotiations. Contradictory messages from the US have made us reluctant about the real intentions of Americans.
statements.context: Araghchi speaking at BRICS Foreign Ministers Meeting in New Delhi. Said Iran has 'no trust in Americans' and characterized this as the 'main obstacle in any diplomatic effort.' Also acknowledged both sides are 'almost in a deadlock' on the enriched uranium stockpile question.
statements.venue: BRICS Foreign Ministers Meeting — New Delhi, India
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fortune, Al Jazeera, HuffPost May 15-16, 2026.

statements.text: Americans are told that they must absorb rocketing costs of war of choice on Iran. Put aside gas price hike and stock market bubble, real pain begins when US debt and mortgage rates start to jump.
statements.context: Araghchi posted to social media targeting US domestic economic opinion directly — a deliberate attempt to use rising US gas prices ($4.52/gal), inflation, and war costs ($29B+) as diplomatic leverage against the Trump administration.
statements.factCheckNote: Social media post confirmed by Al Jazeera article 'Iran warns of readiness for war and economic costs as US talks falter' May 16, 2026.

statements.text: All countries now acknowledge that the Islamic Republic of Iran was the victor in this war. Iran was able to prevent its enemies from achieving their objectives.
statements.context: Araghchi declared on Iranian state television that Iran 'won' the war against the US and Israel. The claim came on Day 79 as Iran formally unveiled the PGSA Hormuz toll mechanism and Trump posted escalatory 'calm before the storm' imagery. Araghchi's 'victor' framing is contested: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper testified Iran's military was degraded by 85%+, but Iran prevented regime change, retained nuclear infrastructure, and forced a ceasefire. Both sides claim partial objectives achieved.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by CBS News Iran war live updates Day 79. 'Victor' claim is contested: CENTCOM confirmed Iran's military degraded by 85%+ (Cooper Senate testimony). However, Iran did prevent regime change and retain nuclear capability. Both sides have legitimate partial-victory claims.
statements.source: CBS News — Iran war live updates Day 79

statements.text: We are in doubt about their seriousness. But the moment we feel that they are serious and ready for a fair and balanced deal, we will certainly proceed in the course of negotiations. We have no trust in the U.S.
statements.context: Araghchi spoke at the BRICS Foreign Ministers meeting in New Delhi, describing US-Iran negotiations as being in 'difficulty' rather than failure. He cited 'contradictory messages' from Washington and described the nuclear enriched material issue as a 'deadlock' likely to be postponed to later negotiating stages. He also told reporters all vessels except those at war with Tehran can transit Hormuz if they coordinate with Iran's navy. The BRICS meeting ended without a joint communique for the second consecutive time due to war-related divisions.
statements.venue: BRICS Foreign Ministers Meeting — New Delhi, India
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News (rcna345281) and Al Jazeera (May 15 report).

statements.text: The U.S. has failed to offer any real concessions in its latest response. Iran remains prepared to maintain the ceasefire — but is also prepared to go back to fighting if necessary.
statements.context: Araghchi's position on Day 79 as Trump posted his 'Clock is Ticking' ultimatum on Truth Social. Iran's Mehr News Agency carried the statement as Trump-Netanyahu coordination on war renewal was reported. Araghchi also told reporters Iran's patience with stalled negotiations is not unlimited.
statements.venue: Statement to Mehr News Agency / Press
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera May 17 liveblog and CBS News live updates citing Mehr News Agency.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war liveblog May 17

statements.text: All countries acknowledge Iran was the victor in this war. We prevented the enemy from achieving their goals.
statements.context: Araghchi made this declaration on Iranian state television on Day 80, as Trump issued his 'clock is ticking' ultimatum. The statement represents Iran's official framing of the war outcome — positioning the ceasefire as a victory for Iran, not a defeat.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's 'victor' claim is contested by US and Israeli officials who point to significant degradation of Iran's military and nuclear infrastructure.

statements.text: We have no trust in the US. The moment we feel they are serious and ready for a fair and balanced deal, we will proceed with negotiations. Enmity with the Great People of Iran is a foolish gamble. Collusion with Israel in doing so: unforgivable.
statements.context: Araghchi addressed the BRICS foreign ministers meeting in New Delhi, stating Iran's negotiating posture. He also issued a thinly veiled warning to the UAE following the drone strike on the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant perimeter, calling UAE 'collusion with Israel' unforgivable — signaling Iran may view the UAE as a legitimate target.
statements.venue: BRICS Foreign Ministers Meeting — New Delhi, India
statements.factCheckNote: BRICS statement confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 79 liveblog. UAE warning confirmed by Euronews and Mehr News.

statements.text: BRICS countries look at Iran with different eyes after the American failure in its attack on the country. Iran has been able to establish itself as a power and actor in the region — an actor who has the ability to confront the greatest powers. We are in doubt about their seriousness when it comes to reaching a fair deal.
statements.context: Araghchi gave an interview to Fortune magazine at the BRICS Foreign Ministers Summit in New Delhi (India, May 14-16), where he declared Iran victorious in the war and expressed doubt about US sincerity in negotiations. BRICS talks ended without a joint statement due to internal divisions — the UAE (understood to be the blocker) opposed key parts of India's proposed draft.
statements.venue: Fortune magazine interview / BRICS Summit, New Delhi
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fortune magazine and Scheer Post / Juan Cole. BRICS summit failure to issue joint statement confirmed by Al Jazeera.

statements.text: The process of talks through the Pakistani mediator is ongoing. Iran has responded to the latest US proposal. Our demands — ending the blockade, releasing frozen assets, war reparations, Hormuz sovereignty — are not maximalist. They are Iran's legitimate rights. At the current stage, our focus is on ending the war.
statements.context: Iranian FM Araghchi confirmed through Iranian state media and Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei that Iran submitted a response to the latest US proposal via Pakistan. Iran defended the demand for war reparations, which the US immediately rejected as a 'dealbreaker.' Araghchi noted Iran's nuclear enrichment status was not the focus at this negotiating stage. Iran's stock market reopened May 19 — a signal of regime confidence amid military standoff.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Arab News, Al Jazeera liveblog, Times of Israel liveblog May 18. Baghaei quotes confirmed by Al Jazeera video report and Iran International.

statements.text: A return to war will feature many more surprises. Iran is prepared and stronger than before. The US must choose — ceasefire or continued war via Israel. It cannot have both.
statements.context: Araghchi issued the warning as Trump's 2–3 day Gulf-mediated diplomatic window began counting down. The Iranian FM signaled readiness to escalate significantly beyond Phase 1 if negotiations collapse. Iran had submitted a revised proposal via Pakistani mediator Naqvi on May 18.
statements.venue: Iranian Foreign Ministry / Al Jazeera liveblog
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera May 20 liveblog.
statements.source: Al Jazeera liveblog — Tehran warns of many more surprises if conflict resumes

statements.text: We cannot trust the Americans at all. We are trying to maintain the shaky ceasefire to give diplomacy a chance. Every day brings a different message, sometimes even two different messages in a single day, which deepens mistrust. The lack of trust in the US is the main obstacle to any diplomatic effort.
statements.context: Araghchi's most explicit trust-breakdown statement of the conflict, issued the same morning Trump claimed Iran was 'desperate' for a deal. Directly contradicts the optimistic US framing.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ABC News live updates and CBS News war coverage. Consistent with Araghchi's May 16 Fortune interview saying 'We are in doubt about their seriousness.'

statements.text: Events in Hormuz make clear that there is no military solution to a political crisis. Iran's conditions for a sustainable peace are clear: an end to fighting on all fronts, exit of US forces from areas adjacent to Iran, reparations for war damage, removal of all sanctions, and release of frozen assets.
statements.context: Araghchi posted on X that the Hormuz situation demonstrates military force cannot resolve what is fundamentally a political dispute. Iran confirmed it had sent a formal response to the latest US proposal via Pakistan on May 18. Iran's conditions — which require a full US military departure and reparations — remain far from what Washington has offered.
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi X post confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog May 21. Iran response sent via Pakistan confirmed by Al Jazeera May 18 report. Conditions listed are Iran's publicly stated positions confirmed by multiple sources.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran sends response to US proposal to end war via Pakistan

statements.text: Iran maintains its dual posture: ready to negotiate a just and lasting peace, but equally prepared to resume the fight if the United States abandons its commitments. Hormuz control is proof that military coercion cannot resolve the core political dispute — only diplomacy with mutual respect can do that.
statements.context: Araghchi maintained Iran's dual diplomatic-military posture on May 22 as Pakistan's Munir arrived in Tehran and Qatar joined mediation efforts. Rubio had said 'slight progress' while Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman Baghaei simultaneously described gaps as 'deep and significant.' Araghchi framed Hormuz control as evidence of Iran's leverage.
statements.venue: Al Jazeera liveblog / Iranian Foreign Ministry
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog May 22. Araghchi's dual posture framing consistent with prior statements. Baghaei's 'deep and significant' description confirmed separately by Times of Israel liveblog.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war live: signs of progress amid ceasefire efforts

statements.text: Figuring out the path to a deal is not rocket science. Time to decide. If the US insists on zero enrichment, we do NOT have a deal — enrichment is a very firm red line.
statements.context: Araghchi posted this before entering the fifth round of nuclear talks in Rome on May 23. The statement signals Iran's maximum flexibility has been reached — the Islamic Republic will not surrender enrichment rights. Iran's delegation was led by Araghchi; the US side by envoy Witkoff and Michael Anton. The talks ended without breakthrough.
statements.venue: Posted on X before Rome talks; confirmed post-talk statements
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi X post confirmed by Outlook India, CNBC, Al Jazeera. Post-talk statement confirmed by PBS, RFERL.
statements.source: Outlook India — Fifth round of nuclear talks between Iran and US in Rome

statements.text: Washington's repeated betrayals of diplomacy, military aggression against Iran, contradictory positions, and recurring excessive demands are the source of disruption in talks. Iran has again entered the diplomatic process seriously and with a responsible approach.
statements.context: Araghchi made these remarks in a phone call with UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres on May 23, positioning Iran as the responsible party and blaming the US for the stalled negotiations. He also spoke by phone with Omani FM Badr al-Busaidi on the same day. The statement reflects Iran's broader narrative heading into the critical post-Rome period.
statements.venue: Telephone call with UN Secretary-General Guterres, May 23, 2026
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Press TV (May 23) and Business Standard citing Iranian FM official readout of the Guterres call.
statements.source: Press TV — US betrayals, contradictory stances, excessive demands source of disruption in talks

statements.text: Differences remain deep and significant. Iran is engaged in diplomacy seriously but our positions on core issues, including the status of enriched uranium, have not changed. The Strait of Hormuz is open to all except enemies — Iran is within its rights as a coastal nation to impose restrictions.
statements.context: Araghchi's statements following a 24-hour marathon mediation session with Pakistan Army Chief Gen. Asim Munir in Tehran on May 22-23. Munir met separately with Araghchi, President Pezeshkian, and Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf. He departed carrying a fresh Iranian counter-proposal. The 'deep and significant differences' framing reflects Iran's continued insistence on HEU sovereignty despite Trump's demand for uranium transfer.
statements.venue: Tehran — bilateral meetings with Pakistan Army Chief Munir, May 22-23, 2026
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi 'deep and significant differences' quote confirmed by Al Jazeera May 23 liveblog. Munir's marathon mediation confirmed by Tribune India. Hormuz sovereignty statement confirmed by India.com.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war live: Iran weighs US peace proposal despite deep and significant disagreements

statements.text: Iran never wanted nuclear weapons. The enriched uranium issue has created a deadlock with Washington — this issue is being postponed to later stages of the talks. We are here in Doha to work toward a framework that addresses the Strait of Hormuz and the broader ceasefire.
statements.context: Araghchi arrived in Doha on Day 87 with a high-level Iranian delegation — including Majles Speaker Ghalibaf and Central Bank Governor Hemmati — for Qatar-mediated talks. His open admission of a uranium 'deadlock' is significant: it confirms both sides have agreed to defer the central sticking point. Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly issued an internal directive barring any removal of enriched uranium from Iran.
statements.venue: Doha, Qatar — Qatar-mediated US-Iran talks, Day 87
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi 'deadlock' and 'postponed' language confirmed by Tribune India and CBS News live updates May 25. 'Iran never wanted nuclear weapons' statement confirmed by same sources.
statements.source: Tribune India — Iran FM Araghchi admits enriched uranium deadlock with Washington

statements.text: The United States committed a gross violation of the ceasefire in the Hormozgan region. Iran holds the US regime responsible for all consequences of these aggressive and unjustified actions. The Islamic Republic of Iran will leave no act of aggression unanswered. These actions demonstrate US dishonesty and unreliability as a negotiating partner.
statements.context: Day 88 — Araghchi issued this statement via the Iran Foreign Ministry in direct response to CENTCOM's self-defense strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats and a SAM battery near Bandar Abbas on May 25. Despite this rhetoric, Araghchi and the Iranian delegation remained in Doha for MOU talks — sending a contradictory signal that Iran prioritizes the economic track over military escalation.
statements.venue: Iran Foreign Ministry statement — Day 88/89, May 26
statements.factCheckNote: Iran FM statement confirmed by Newsweek, Press TV, Al Jazeera, Antiwar.com (May 26). US characterizes strikes as self-defense against mine-laying boats — a ceasefire violation or legitimate self-defense is disputed. The claim the strikes were 'aggressive and unjustified' reflects Iran's position, not a verified fact.
statements.source: Press TV — Iran says latest US ceasefire violations show ill-will and bad faith

statements.text: I urge an immediate end to violence in Iran and across the Middle East. War is always a defeat for humanity.
statements.context: Address from Vatican.

statements.text: I call for the roar of bombs to cease. Humanity must choose dialogue over destruction.
statements.context: Vatican Angelus address. Called for 'a space for dialogue to open.' Tone described as 'relatively restrained' compared to John Paul II on Iraq War.

statements.text: I express deep sorrow for all those killed and wounded. The tragedy unfolding must stop before it becomes an irreparable abyss.
statements.context: New Vatican appeal March 10. Cardinal McElroy (Washington) stated the war does not meet 'minimum criteria' for being morally just. Pope Leo XIV has become a leading voice calling for cessation of hostilities.

statements.text: Cease fire!
statements.context: Strongest papal appeal yet on the Iran conflict. Pope Leo XIV directly addressed war leaders and referenced the school strike that killed 165+ children. Described as the most forceful Vatican intervention since John Paul II on Iraq.

statements.text: In the name of God, I ask: stop the bombing. Stop killing children. The school strike that took 165 young lives cries out to heaven.
statements.context: Sunday noon blessing from Vatican. Pope Leo XIV delivered his strongest ceasefire appeal yet, directly referencing the school strike that killed 165+ children. Called on all parties to begin dialogue immediately.
statements.venue: Sunday Noon Blessing, St. Peter's Square, Vatican
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Vatican News. The school strike reference aligns with confirmed reporting on the Minab girls' school attack.

statements.text: On behalf of the Christians of the Middle East and all women and men of good will, I appeal to those responsible for this conflict. Cease fire so that avenues for dialogue may be reopened.
statements.context: Sunday noon blessing. Strongest statement yet. Vatican Secretary of State Cardinal Parolin rejected 'preventive war' claim. US Cardinals McElroy and Cupich also publicly criticized the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UPI, Washington Post, and Newsweek

statements.text: Cease the fire! In the name of God, I beg you — cease the fire. Every bomb that falls, every missile that strikes, kills a child of God.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV's third and most forceful appeal for ceasefire, delivered during Sunday Angelus address. Vatican confirmed direct phone calls to both Trump and Mojtaba Khamenei.
statements.factCheckNote: Vatican Press Office confirmed the Pope's direct calls to both heads of state. This represents an unprecedented level of papal diplomatic engagement.

statements.text: Cardinal Parolin: Would urge Trump 'to stop as soon as possible, danger of escalation imminent.' Rejected 'preventive war' justification.
statements.context: Vatican Secretary of State remarks
statements.factCheckNote: CathNews, NCR confirmed

statements.text: Stability and peace are not achieved through mutual threats, nor through the use of weapons, which sow destruction, suffering, and death, but only through reasonable, sincere, and responsible dialogue. Persevere in prayer, so that hostilities may cease and paths of peace may finally open up.
statements.context: Sunday Angelus address at St. Peter's Square, Rome. Pope Leo XIV's second personal appeal for ceasefire since the Iran War began. He said he continues to follow the situation 'with dismay.' Trump responded hours later by rejecting the appeal.
statements.venue: Sunday Angelus, St. Peter's Square, Vatican
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill, ABC7, and PhilNews reporting on Pope Leo XIV's March 22 Angelus address.

statements.text: On behalf of the Christians of the Middle East and all women and men of good will, I appeal to those responsible for this conflict. Cease fire so that avenues for dialogue may be reopened. Violence can never lead to the justice, stability, and peace that the people are waiting for. Some claim to involve the name of God in these deadly decisions, but God cannot be enlisted by darkness.
statements.context: Strongest papal ceasefire appeal to date, delivered during Sunday March 22 Angelus address. Referenced missile strike on Iranian elementary school. Did not name US or Israel directly. Trump responded: "I don't want a ceasefire."
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Vatican News and multiple Catholic media outlets.
statements.source: Vatican News — Pope Leo ceasefire appeal

statements.text: Hatred is increasing, and the violence is getting worse and worse. Not with weapons — rather through dialogue. More than a million displaced people and many dead.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV renewed his ceasefire appeal on Day 25, lamenting the escalating violence and displacement.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al-Monitor.

statements.text: Hatred is increasing, and the violence is getting worse and worse. I renew my appeal for an immediate ceasefire. The world cannot watch this destruction in silence.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV's 12th ceasefire call in 26 days. Speaking from Castel Gandolfo. Trump told EWTN on March 20: 'I don't want a ceasefire.'
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Crux and Philstar.

statements.text: I want to renew my call for a ceasefire, to work toward peace, not through weapons, but through dialogue, and to genuinely seek a solution that benefits everyone.
statements.context: Remarks to journalists at Castel Gandolfo, March 24. Pope Leo renewed his calls for a ceasefire after earlier Angelus remarks calling for guns to 'fall silent.' The White House responded that Trump has 'no interest in papal peace counsel.' Trump: 'I don't want a ceasefire.'
statements.venue: Castel Gandolfo, Vatican — Remarks to journalists
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed via Vatican News. The White House dismissal of papal counsel confirmed by EWTN/Trump interview.

statements.text: Stability and peace are not achieved through mutual threats or weapons. The leaders of nations must find the courage to talk to each other, not destroy each other.
statements.context: Remarks widely covered by The Hill and NCR on March 26. Pope Leo has increasingly escalated his rhetoric as the war drags into its 27th day. He has not named the US or Israel directly by name in any statement but has described the war as "a catastrophe for humanity" and repeatedly called for unconditional ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: The Hill confirmed the statement. Pope Leo has made multiple similar statements across the conflict — see pope_leo-011 through pope_leo-013 for prior context.

statements.text: I want to renew my call for a ceasefire, to work toward peace, not through weapons, but through dialogue, and to genuinely seek a solution that benefits everyone. Right now, hatred is on the rise, violence is getting worse, there are over a million displaced people, and so many have lost their lives.
statements.context: Remarks to journalists at Castel Gandolfo, renewing ceasefire appeal as conflict enters its fourth week
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Melbourne Catholic and The Hill.

statements.text: This is our God: Jesus, King of Peace, who rejects war, whom no one can use to justify war. He does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war, but rejects them.
statements.context: Palm Sunday Mass homily, St. Peter's Square, Vatican City — 12th Vatican appeal for peace since war began Feb 28.
statements.venue: Palm Sunday Mass, St. Peter's Square
statements.factCheckNote: Exact quote confirmed by Vatican News and NPR from the Palm Sunday homily, March 29, 2026.
statements.source: NPR — Pope Leo rejects claims God justifies war on Palm Sunday

statements.text: He does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war, but rejects them, saying: 'Even though you make many prayers, I will not listen: your hands are full of blood.'
statements.context: Palm Sunday homily at St. Peter's Square, directly rebuking Hegseth's use of Scripture to justify the war. White House responded: 'It's not wrong to call on the American people to pray for troops.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, The Hill, and IBTimes UK.

statements.text: This is our God: Jesus, King of Peace, who rejects war, whom no one can use to justify war. He does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war, but rejects them.
statements.context: Palm Sunday address before tens of thousands in St. Peter's Square — widely seen as rebuke of Trump's use of religious justification for Iran war
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Axios. FDD published counterargument citing Catholic Just War doctrine.

statements.text: Brothers and sisters, this is our God: Jesus, King of Peace, who rejects war, whom no one can use to justify war.
statements.context: Easter appeal to end the Iran war, following Palm Sunday address. Vatican Secretary of State Parolin said war 'does not seem to meet the conditions' for a just war.
statements.venue: Vatican Easter appeal / Palm Sunday Mass
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Post, NPR, Salt Lake Tribune confirmed.

statements.text: I'm told that President Trump has recently stated that he would like to end the war. I hope he is looking for an off-ramp. I call on all world leaders to return to dialogue.
statements.context: Remarks to reporters leaving Castel Gandolfo. Explicitly called for Easter ceasefire. Most direct naming of Trump by the first American pope.
statements.factCheckNote: Manila Times, ABC, Washington Post confirmed.

statements.text: I'm told that President Trump has recently stated that he would like to end the war. I hope he is looking for an off-ramp. I call on all world leaders to return to dialogue.
statements.context: Castel Gandolfo doorstep remarks. Called for Easter ceasefire in the US-Israel war on Iran. Condemned those who 'wage war.' Also sent private message to Hegseth about war and religion (per Bloomberg).
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, EWTN, Manila Times, ABC7 confirmed.

statements.text: I hope that he's looking for an off-ramp. We call for an Easter ceasefire. This war does not seem to meet the conditions for a just war.
statements.context: Easter ceasefire appeal during Holy Week, criticizing religious justification for war
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, Manila Times, Bloomberg confirmed.

statements.text: God does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war. The Lord sees. The Lord hears. But He does not answer those who cry out for 'overwhelming violence of action against those who deserve no mercy.'
statements.context: Washington Post profile on Pope Leo XIV taking aim at Trump administration's religious framing of conflict, directly countering Hegseth's prayer
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Post and National Catholic Reporter confirmed.

statements.text: God does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war. On this Good Friday, I appeal directly to President Trump and all leaders: stop the bombing, start the talking. I have spoken with President Herzog and urged immediate humanitarian corridors.
statements.context: Good Friday peace appeal — called Trump directly, spoke with Israeli President Herzog
statements.venue: Good Friday address, Vatican / phone calls
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Post confirmed Pope Leo's statement and phone calls.

statements.text: God does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war. I urge all sides to reopen all paths of dialogue.
statements.context: Good Friday ceasefire appeal + phone call with Israeli President Herzog
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Post, US News confirmed.

statements.text: On Good Friday, I call on all parties to cease fire so that avenues for dialogue may be reopened. Stability and peace are not built with mutual threats, nor with weapons, but only through a reasonable, authentic, and responsible dialogue.
statements.context: Easter ceasefire appeal. Also called Israeli President Herzog and Ukrainian President Zelensky urging diplomatic solutions. CNN noted push-back on divine justification for war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by America Magazine, CNN, Manila Times.

statements.text: Let those who have weapons lay them down! Let those who have the power to unleash wars choose peace! People are growing accustomed to violence, resigning ourselves to it, and becoming indifferent.
statements.context: Easter Sunday Urbi et Orbi message — unusually brief and direct, focused almost entirely on the Iran conflict. Announced April 11 prayer vigil for peace at St. Peter's Basilica. First American pope's most forceful antiwar statement yet.
statements.venue: St. Peter's Square, Vatican City — Urbi et Orbi
statements.factCheckNote: Al-Monitor, NCR, Athens Times confirmed.

statements.text: Let those who have weapons lay them down! Let those who have the power to unleash wars choose peace! The world is growing accustomed to violence, resigning ourselves to it, and becoming indifferent.
statements.context: Easter Sunday address at St. Peter's Square, calling for end to Iran-Israel war
statements.venue: St. Peter's Square, Vatican City — Easter Address
statements.factCheckNote: NBC News, Al-Monitor, Axios confirmed.

statements.text: Let those who have weapons lay them down! Let those who have the power to unleash wars choose peace! Not a peace imposed by force, but through dialogue!
statements.context: First Easter address as Pope. Called for peace amid Iran-Israel war. Announced April 11 peace vigil.
statements.factCheckNote: NBC News, Washington Post, Axios confirmed.

statements.text: Let those who have weapons lay them down! Let those who have the power to unleash wars choose peace! Jesus is the King of Peace... whom no one can use to justify war.
statements.context: First Easter Urbi et Orbi address. Hoped Trump would find an "off-ramp." Warned world is "becoming indifferent" to violence.
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast live globally.

statements.text: Trump's threat to the Iranian people is truly unacceptable. It is a moral question concerning the good of the people as a whole. Come back to the table. Let's talk.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV addressed journalists at Castel Gandolfo, condemning Trump's 'civilization will die' threat.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Vatican News and Axios.

statements.text: Today as we all know there was this threat against all the people of Iran. This is truly unacceptable.
statements.context: Condemned Trump's threat hours before ceasefire.
statements.factCheckNote: The Hill, PBS, Fox News confirmed.

statements.text: I welcome the two-week ceasefire with great satisfaction.
statements.context: Statement welcoming ceasefire after previously condemning Trump's civilization destruction threat
statements.factCheckNote: Al-Monitor confirmed statement.

statements.text: The ceasefire is a sign of living hope. Only through a return to negotiation can the war come to an end.
statements.context: Weekly general audience at Vatican, welcoming Iran-US ceasefire
statements.factCheckNote: National Catholic Register, Crux confirmed.

statements.text: I welcome the ceasefire with satisfaction and as a sign of living hope. Only through a return to negotiation can the war come to an end.
statements.context: Vatican statement welcoming ceasefire. Previously called Trump's threat against Iranian civilization 'truly unacceptable.'
statements.factCheckNote: National Catholic Register, Catholic World Report, America Magazine confirmed.

statements.text: We welcome this ceasefire with satisfaction and as a sign of living hope. Only through a return to the negotiating table can we bring the war to an end.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV announced Prayer Vigil for Peace in St Peter's Basilica on Friday April 11 at 6pm Rome time.
statements.factCheckNote: Catholic Herald and NCR confirmed.

statements.text: I welcome with satisfaction and as a sign of living hope the announcement of an immediate two-week truce. Only through a return to negotiation can the war come to an end.
statements.context: Weekly general audience at St. Peter's Square
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Vatican News

statements.text: We welcome with satisfaction and as a sign of living hope the announcement of an immediate two-week truce. Only through a return to negotiation can the war come to an end. We have a worldwide economic crisis, an energy crisis, and a situation in the Middle East of great instability.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV welcomed ceasefire while criticizing Trump's earlier threat to wipe out Iran's civilization as 'truly unacceptable.' Invited prayer vigil at St. Peter's Saturday.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NCR, Reuters.

statements.text: Led Prayer Vigil for Peace at St. Peter's Basilica with meditations from Church Fathers and the Lamp of Peace from Assisi. Called ceasefire a 'sign of living hope' and urged all parties to pursue lasting peace.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV held prayer vigil at 6PM Rome time on April 11 at St. Peter's Basilica. Vigil attended by thousands. Comes ahead of critical Islamabad talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Vatican Press Office, EWTN, National Catholic Register.

statements.text: It is here that we find a bulwark against that delusion of omnipotence that surrounds us and is becoming increasingly unpredictable and aggressive. Praying for peace is a way to break the demonic cycle of evil.
statements.context: Strongest statement yet on the war. Evening Prayer Vigil for Peace at St. Peter's Basilica. Did not name Trump or the US directly but widely interpreted as directed at Washington.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by AP/WSLS, ABC News, Daily Caller. Quote verified in multiple translations.

statements.text: I have no fear of the Trump administration or speaking out loudly of the message of the Gospel. To put my message on the same plane as what the president has attempted to do here, I think is not understanding what the message of the Gospel is.
statements.context: Aboard papal plane en route to Algeria for 11-day Africa tour. Response to Trump calling him 'WEAK on Crime' and 'terrible for Foreign Policy.' Previously at Saturday prayer vigil denounced 'delusion of omnipotence' and called Iran blockade 'truly unacceptable.'
statements.venue: Papal Plane — En route to Algiers, Algeria
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Time, Bloomberg, CBS News.

statements.text: I have no fear of neither the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message in the Gospel. Warmakers have hands full of blood.
statements.context: Continuing public criticism of US-Iran war. Called out "delusion of omnipotence." Trump fired back calling Pope Leo "weak on crime."
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Fox News, Vatican News.

statements.text: The world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants.
statements.context: Speech in Cameroon widely interpreted as reference to Trump and other wartime leaders. Vowed to continue peace appeals. Trump responded by falsely claiming Pope supports Iran having nuclear weapons.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Foreign Policy, NPR.

statements.text: The world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants, yet it is held together by a multitude of supportive brothers and sisters. Woe to those who manipulate religion and the very name of God for their own military, economic and political gain, dragging that which is sacred into darkness and filth.
statements.context: Full speech at St. Joseph's Cathedral in Bamenda, Cameroon during African trip. Widely interpreted as directed at Trump administration and Iran war. NPR, Al Jazeera, PBS, Euronews all reported on the remarks.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Al Jazeera, PBS, Euronews.

statements.text: It is not in my interest at all to debate with Trump. But I will keep preaching peace. His threat to annihilate Iranian civilization is truly unacceptable.
statements.context: Aboard papal plane from Cameroon to Angola. Declined to engage in direct debate with Trump over Iran war but reiterated peace message. Said preaching reflects Gospel, not directed at any individual.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News, CBC, Washington Times.

statements.text: It was not in my interest at all to debate Trump. I will continue to speak out loudly against war, looking to promote peace, promoting dialogue and multilateral relationships among states to find just solutions to problems. I have no fear of the Trump administration.
statements.context: Remarks aboard papal plane returning from Africa (Cameroon to Angola). Refusing to engage in direct confrontation with Trump while maintaining anti-war stance.
statements.factCheckNote: Verified by multiple outlets covering the papal trip.

statements.text: A handful of tyrants are spending billions on this war while children go hungry. The threat against the entire Iranian people is truly unacceptable. Peace is not weakness — it is strength.
statements.context: General audience remarks criticizing US and Israeli leadership over Iran war spending and civilian threats.
statements.factCheckNote: NPR reporting of April 16 remarks; in line with April 11 NPR coverage of Pope's denunciation of 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling the war.

statements.text: I cannot be in favor of war. We need a new attitude and culture for peace. I urge the United States and Iran to return to peace talks.
statements.context: Press conference aboard papal plane en route to Rome from Africa trip. Pope called for US-Iran dialogue, condemned capital punishment, and rejected regime change. Came hours after Trump ordered Navy to 'shoot and kill' mine-layers.
statements.venue: Papal plane press conference en route to Rome
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by America Magazine and Columbian.

statements.text: As a pastor, I cannot be in favor of war. I would like to encourage all to make efforts to look for answers that come from a culture of peace and not from a place of hate and division.
statements.context: Press conference aboard papal plane returning from Africa. Urged US and Iran to return to negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: America Magazine, CNN.

statements.text: I condemn the Iranian regime's killing of anti-regime protesters. But as a pastor, I cannot support the US-Israeli war with Iran. Come back to the table. Let's talk. Let's look for solutions in a peaceful way.
statements.context: Extended remarks on papal plane distinguishing condemnation of Iranian regime repression from support for the war. Echoes April 11 'delusion of omnipotence' critique. Vance had told Pope to 'stick to matters of morality.'
statements.venue: Papal plane press conference en route to Rome
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, NPR, America Magazine. All verified.

statements.text: The question is not whether there is regime change or no regime change; the question is how to promote the values in which we believe without the death of so many innocent people. I condemn all actions that are unjust. I condemn the taking of people's lives.
statements.context: Aboard papal plane, addressing the regime-change debate around the Iran war. Distinguishes moral concern for innocents from political question of regime change.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN, America Magazine. Confirmed.

statements.text: I renew my appeal for a Eucharistic truce during the upcoming month of May. Let our prayers reach Lebanese civilians and the seafarers trapped on idle ships in the Gulf.
statements.context: General audience remarks at the Vatican, calling for a month-long ceasefire pause in May, naming Lebanese civilians under IDF strikes and seafarers stranded by the Hormuz blockade.
statements.factCheckNote: Vatican News carried the audience text on April 27.

statements.text: It is the delusion of omnipotence that fuels this war. The threat against the entire Iranian people is truly unacceptable. We must rebuild a culture of peace — and the United States and Iran must return to the table.
statements.context: Remarks at St. Peter's Basilica peace vigil following Rubio's rejection of Iran's Hormuz proposal and Trump's 'state of collapse' Truth Social post. The Pope's intervention adds Vatican pressure to a growing international chorus (UN, EU, GCC, Pakistan).
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News and PBS NewsHour coverage of the vigil.

statements.text: I condemn unequivocally the killing of Iranian protesters by the regime. But as a pastor, I cannot support the US-Israeli war against Iran. I cannot be in favor of war.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV's most pointed statement yet on the dual moral crisis of the war. He simultaneously condemned the Iranian regime's domestic crackdown (per UN reports of 21 executions and 4,000+ arrests) while refusing to endorse the US-Israeli military campaign. Trump had previously criticized the Pope.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, NPR, Times of Israel.

statements.text: President Trump's threat to destroy Iranian civilization is truly unacceptable. I appeal for prayer that the roar of bombs might cease, that weapons might fall silent. I will not be deterred by criticism.
statements.context: Vatican response after Trump warned Iran would be 'blown off the face of the Earth' if it attacked US ships. Pope Leo XIV directly rebuked the rhetoric and reiterated his peace appeal despite previous Trump criticism of the pontiff.
statements.factCheckNote: NPR confirmed prior 'truly unacceptable' framing on April 23. CNN reaffirmed peace appeal. Pope Leo's posture toward Trump remarks consistent across April-May statements.

statements.text: I said, 'Peace be with you,' and the Church's mission is to preach the Gospel, to preach peace. It's much better to enter into dialogue than to look for arms. Violence is a last resort.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV rejected Trump's claim that he supports Iran having nuclear weapons. Reaffirmed his peace stance ahead of Rubio's Vatican visit May 7.
statements.factCheckNote: National Catholic Register, Al Jazeera confirmed the statement.

statements.text: Pray with me so that peace prevails. God willing, peace is near.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV surprised 13 priests from southern Lebanon with a video call. Met Rubio at Vatican May 7 for 45-minute 'frank conversation' on Iran war disagreements. Gave Rubio an olive-wood pen as peace symbol.
statements.venue: Video call to Lebanese priests / Vatican meeting with Rubio
statements.factCheckNote: Washington Times, CNN, America Magazine confirmed video call and Rubio meeting.

statements.text: Many innocent people have died. One day Iran says yes, the United States says no, and vice versa. God does not bless any conflict. The bombing of Iran has created a chaotic situation for the global economy.
statements.context: Pope Leo vented frustration over the war's ongoing deadlock in media interviews and at his weekly audience. The statement comes as Trump and Xi were meeting in Beijing while peace talks remained stalled on Iran's 5 preconditions.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NBC News and NPR coverage of Pope Leo's ongoing statements on the Iran war.

statements.text: I cannot be in favor of war. When asked about regime change in Iran — I am not in favor of war.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV spoke to reporters en route home from Africa, urging the US and Iran to return to the negotiating table. Also condemned capital punishment in the wide-ranging airborne press conference. His statements on Iran have been consistent since Easter: calling Trump's annihilation threat "truly unacceptable," urging dialogue over force, and demanding political leaders stop and negotiate.
statements.venue: Papal press conference — airborne, en route from Africa
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by America Magazine and PBS NewsHour from late April/May airborne press conference.

statements.text: Enough with the idolatry of self and money! Enough with the display of force! Enough with war! True strength is manifested in serving life. Many innocent people have died. Trump's threat to annihilate Iranian civilization is truly unacceptable.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV's latest condemnation of the war, combining his earlier rejection of Trump's 'annihilate Iranian civilization' threat with calls for dialogue. He has met with Secretary Rubio at the Vatican (May 7) but continues to oppose the military campaign. He condemns Iran's crackdowns while refusing to endorse the US-Israeli war.
statements.factCheckNote: Pope Leo condemnation of Trump 'annihilate' rhetoric confirmed by NPR April 11, CBS News, NBC News.
statements.source: NPR — Pope Leo condemns Iran war, Trump annihilation threat

statements.text: The attacks on Iran constitute violations of international humanitarian law. Switzerland has a duty to speak.
statements.context: Official government statement.

statements.text: The US-Israeli strikes on Iran violate international law.
statements.context: Swiss Defense Minister condemns strikes
statements.factCheckNote: Anadolu Agency confirmed.

statements.text: The death of a French soldier in Iraq is unacceptable. France is present in Iraq solely for counterterrorism. We demand a full investigation and accountability.
statements.context: First French military fatality of the conflict. A French soldier was killed in an Iraqi militia drone attack on a French counterterrorism base. Macron called it 'unacceptable' and demanded accountability, raising concerns about NATO expansion of the conflict.
statements.factCheckNote: French soldier death confirmed by French Ministry of Armed Forces and France24. France maintains counterterrorism forces in Iraq under Operation Chammal.

statements.text: France would never take part in operations to unblock the Strait of Hormuz in the current context.
