statements.context: Macron explicitly ruled out French participation in any US-led military operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, joining other European nations in rejecting Trump's demands for allied help.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News and Al Jazeera liveblog coverage of EU foreign ministers' decisions.

statements.text: It would be unrealistic to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by military means. When you want to be serious, you don't say the opposite every day of what you said the day before.
statements.context: Macron pushed back on military options for Hormuz reopening after UK-led 40-nation summit. Criticized Trump's inconsistent messaging. Called for ceasefire and negotiations as precondition for Hormuz reopening.
statements.factCheckNote: Bloomberg and France24 confirmed Macron's statements.

statements.text: I expressed my hope that the ceasefire will be fully respected by each of the belligerents, across all areas of confrontation, including in Lebanon. This is a necessary condition for the ceasefire to be credible and lasting. I have spoken with Lebanese President Aoun and PM Salam. France expresses full solidarity in face of the indiscriminate strikes. We condemn these strikes in the strongest possible terms.
statements.context: Macron on X — first Western leader to speak with Iranian President Pezeshkian since ceasefire. Demanded Lebanon inclusion. Called Israeli strikes 'indiscriminate.'
statements.venue: Post on X + phone calls with Pezeshkian, Aoun, Salam
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, France24 confirmed. Macron's X post verified.

statements.text: This conflict will certainly come to an end in the next few weeks.
statements.context: ABC 'This Week' interview. Energy Secretary Wright predicted the war would end within weeks, suggesting the administration sees a timeline for resolution. Comments come amid sustained $100+ oil prices and growing economic pressure.
statements.factCheckNote: Prediction about conflict duration. IDF Brig Gen Defrin separately indicated plans through Passover (3+ more weeks), contradicting a quick resolution.

statements.text: Iran is frighteningly close to having a nuclear weapon. Every day without a deal is a day closer to a nuclear-armed Iran.
statements.context: Wright issued the strongest nuclear warning yet from a cabinet official, telling reporters Iran's enrichment progress has accelerated during the conflict. Iran holds ~441 kg of 60%-enriched uranium. ISW separately assessed Iran is preparing infrastructure for renewed hostilities post-ceasefire.
statements.venue: Department of Energy Press Briefing, Washington DC
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Reuters and CBS News. IAEA reports support the assessment that Iran's stockpile has grown during the conflict.

statements.text: We have plans through Passover. Over 2,000 targets have been hit. 70% of Iran's missile launchers have been destroyed. More than 6,000 IRGC fighters have been killed.
statements.context: IDF Brig Gen Effie Defrin outlined the scope and timeline of Israeli operations against Iran. The Passover timeline (April 12) implies at least 3 more weeks of operations. The 6,000+ IRGC killed claim is significantly higher than Iranian government figures.
statements.factCheckNote: Strike numbers plausible given CENTCOM data. IRGC casualty claim of 6,000+ cannot be independently verified due to Iran's internet blackout. Iran's Health Ministry reports 1,444 total killed.

statements.text: We have not yet made any decisions regarding the dispatch of naval vessels.
statements.context: Responding to Trump's call for allied nations to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz. Japan, which imports ~90% of its oil through the strait, faces immense pressure but Takaichi signaled caution amid domestic opposition to military involvement.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Japan Times and Bloomberg. Japan's constitutional constraints on military deployment make this a sensitive domestic issue.

statements.text: The United States will allow Iranian oil tankers to transit through the Strait of Hormuz. We are working to ensure global energy markets are not destabilized beyond what is necessary.
statements.context: Major policy shift. Treasury Secretary announced US would not block Iranian tankers in Hormuz. Oil dropped 4-6% immediately. WSJ reports US simultaneously building multinational naval escort coalition for commercial shipping.
statements.factCheckNote: Oil markets confirmed the impact: Brent -4.3% to $100.21, WTI -6.1% to $93.50. However, the strait remains effectively closed to most commercial traffic.

statements.text: Turkey condemns the expansion of Israeli military operations into Lebanon. This aggressive action threatens the entire region's stability.
statements.context: Turkey condemned IDF ground operations in southern Lebanon. Turkey has been allowing Iranian ships through selective Hormuz passage while maintaining NATO membership, walking a delicate line.
statements.factCheckNote: Turkish MFA issued formal condemnation. Turkey's position is complicated by NATO membership and economic ties to both sides.

statements.text: Ali Larijani, former Speaker of Iran's Parliament and senior adviser to the Supreme Leader, was eliminated in a targeted strike. This sends a clear message — no one in the regime is beyond our reach.
statements.context: Israel confirmed the targeted killing of Ali Larijani, one of the most senior Iranian political figures killed in the conflict. Larijani was reportedly en route to a meeting with Mojtaba Khamenei.
statements.venue: Official statement, Israeli Ministry of Defense
statements.factCheckNote: Larijani's death confirmed by multiple Iranian and international sources. Iran's IRNA and Tasnim news agencies confirmed the killing.

statements.text: This week, the intensity of the attacks that the IDF and the U.S. military will carry out against the Iranian terrorist regime and against the infrastructures on which it relies will increase significantly, and the campaign led by U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will continue.
statements.context: Defense Minister Katz statement came within hours of Trump's "winding down" Truth Social post, directly contradicting the US president's signals. Katz also said Israel is "determined to continue leading the attack against the Iranian terrorist regime, to behead its commanders and thwart its strategic capabilities — until all security threats to the State of Israel and U.S. interests in the region are removed."
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by US News/AP and TASS.

statements.text: If this continues, we'll be sure to hit Iran so hard it will be sent back decades. The IRGC intentionally fired at population centers. The intensity of the strikes to be carried out by the IDF and the US against the Iranian terror regime will rise significantly.
statements.context: Statement at Arad after the Day 22 Iranian IRBM direct hit that injured 74 people, including threat to intensify attacks. Also announced IDF strikes on all Litani River bridges in Lebanon.
statements.factCheckNote: Katz statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Litani bridge strikes order confirmed by IDF.

statements.text: All bridges over the Litani River used by Hezbollah have been destroyed. Any village serving as a terrorist outpost will be treated like Rafah and Beit Hanoun.
statements.context: Israeli Defense Minister announcing implementation of the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' for Lebanese villages used as Hezbollah bases. Made alongside announcement of 7 overnight Beirut airstrikes.
statements.factCheckNote: Litani bridge destruction confirmed by satellite imagery and IDF statements. Threat of Rafah model application made explicitly by Katz.

statements.text: Last night, in a precise and lethal operation, the IDF eliminated the commander of the Revolutionary Guards' navy, Tangsiri, along with senior officers of the naval command. This is the man who was directly responsible for the terrorist operation of mining and blocking the Strait of Hormuz to shipping. The IDF will hunt you down and eliminate you one by one.
statements.context: Announcement of the targeted killing of IRGC Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas.
statements.factCheckNote: Tangsiri killing confirmed by IDF/Defense Minister Katz. Iran has not officially confirmed or denied.
statements.source: Washington Post — Israel says it killed IRGC navy chief Alireza Tangsiri

statements.text: IDF strikes in Iran will intensify and expand to additional targets and domains that assist the regime in building and operating weapons against Israeli civilians. Iran will pay heavy and increasing prices for attacks on Israeli civilians.
statements.context: Statement following Day 28 strikes on Arak, Ardakan, Isfahan. Katz explicitly promised an escalation campaign, matching Netanyahu's earlier vow. Israel struck an additional Ardakan yellowcake facility for the first time.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Euronews, TIME, PBS.

statements.text: Israel will intensify and expand its strikes against Iran. Today we struck the Ardakan yellowcake facility — the only one of its kind in Iran — for the first time. We also struck the Arak heavy water reactor again. The Mobarakeh steel complex is IRGC-linked and a legitimate military target. Iran’s industrial war capacity will be dismantled.
statements.context: Defense Minister Katz public statement following Day 28 nuclear and industrial strikes
statements.factCheckNote: Ardakan strike confirmed as first-ever by IDF, IAEA. Arak second strike confirmed. Mobarakeh steel IRGC ownership claim contested by some analysts.

statements.text: Israel is ready to return Iran to the Stone Age. We are waiting for the American green light to complete the elimination of the Khamenei dynasty and destroy key energy and electricity facilities.
statements.context: Public statement declaring IDF has prepared target lists for Iran's energy grid and Khamenei inner circle.
statements.factCheckNote: Euronews, Antiwar.com, NBC, WION, Daily Sabah.

statements.text: Israel is prepared to resume the war against Iran. We are awaiting US green light to complete the elimination of the Khamenei dynasty and return Iran to the Dark Age and the Stone Age by destroying key energy and electricity facilities and dismantling its national economic infrastructure. The IDF is ready both defensively and offensively, and the targets have been marked.
statements.context: Statement widely reported April 24. Signals Israel's readiness to escalate if ceasefire collapses. Represents most aggressive rhetoric from Israeli defense establishment during ceasefire period.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Euronews, Times of Israel, Daily Sabah.

statements.text: Israel may soon need to act again to eliminate existential threats posed by Iran. Iran has been pushed years backward but the job is not done.
statements.context: Warning of potential resumption of military action. Israel received 6,500 tons of military equipment via cargo ships and aircraft.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Arabiya, Times of Israel.

statements.text: The assassination of Larijani is a war crime that will not go unanswered. The cycle of war must end, but not through the murder of our statesmen.
statements.context: Parliament Speaker Qalibaf condemned the targeted killing of Ali Larijani, calling for both retaliation and an end to the cycle of violence. His dual message reflects Iran's internal tension between escalation and negotiation.
statements.factCheckNote: Emergency session confirmed by Iranian state media. Qalibaf's statement represents the legislative branch's first formal response to the Larijani killing.

statements.text: The threat of war against Iran and the region must be eliminated. We will not accept the cycle of war, ceasefire, negotiation, and war again.
statements.context: Parliament address after Larijani killing
statements.factCheckNote: PressTV confirmed statement

statements.text: The skies of Israel are unprotected. The pre-prepared plans of the Islamic Republic must be implemented now.
statements.context: Statement from Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf after Iranian ballistic missile successfully struck Dimona, evading Israeli air defenses — the first strike in proximity to Israel's nuclear research center.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz live blog Day 22 coverage of Dimona strike.

statements.text: If the US and Israel target Iranian power plants, the energy infrastructure and desalination facilities in the region will be irreversibly destroyed. This will push oil prices higher for a long time.
statements.context: Iranian Parliament Speaker warning issued in response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum threatening to strike Iranian power plants if Hormuz is not reopened.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera and multiple outlets covering Iranian response to Trump ultimatum.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran threatens energy sites if US Israel target power plants

statements.text: No negotiations have been held with the United States. Trump's claims are an attempt to manipulate the financial and oil markets. The Islamic Republic has not entered into any negotiations.
statements.context: Iranian Parliament Speaker response to Trump's public announcement of US-Iran talks. Simultaneously, Iran International reported Ghalibaf himself was the Iranian official in backchannel contact with Witkoff/Kushner via Pakistani intermediaries.
statements.factCheckNote: Ghalibaf publicly denied negotiations, but Iran International reported he was himself the contact relayed through Pakistani intermediaries. The contradiction is notable and suggests deliberate public denial alongside backchannel engagement.

statements.text: The enemy, openly, sends messages of negotiation and dialogue, but secretly is planning a ground attack. Our men are waiting for American soldiers to enter on the ground so they can set them ablaze. As long as the Americans seek Iran's surrender, our response is clear: Far be it from us to accept humiliation.
statements.context: 30-day war anniversary statement rejecting Trump's claims of diplomatic progress and warning against any US ground invasion of Iran, issued the same day Trump claimed Iran agreed to 'most of' the 15-point US demands
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al-Arabiya, NPR, NBC News, UPI. Directly contradicts Trump's claim that Iran agreed to 'most of' 15-point demands.
statements.source: NPR — Pakistan diplomatic discussions on ending Iran war

statements.text: Three parts of Iran's 10-point ceasefire proposal have been violated: Israel's continued attacks on Lebanon, entry of a drone into Iranian airspace, and denial of Iran's right to enrich uranium. In such a situation, bilateral ceasefire or negotiations is unreasonable.
statements.context: Ghalibaf accused US of violating ceasefire agreement. He is set to lead Iran's delegation at Islamabad talks, raising questions about whether Iran will still attend.
statements.factCheckNote: CNBC, Al Jazeera, CBS News confirmed Ghalibaf accusations.

statements.text: The US has violated THREE tenets of the ceasefire: Israel continuing to strike Lebanon, drone intrusion into Iranian airspace, and denial of Iran's right to nuclear enrichment. In such a situation, a bilateral ceasefire or negotiations were unreasonable.
statements.context: Iran Parliament Speaker and lead negotiator citing 3 specific violations. Will co-lead Iranian delegation at Islamabad talks.
statements.factCheckNote: US denies all three claims. Vance called Lebanon exclusion a 'misunderstanding.' Drone claim unverified.

statements.text: We are still far from the final discussion. There has been progress, but there are many gaps and some fundamental points remain. Trump made seven claims in one hour — all seven false. No enriched material will be returned to the United States.
statements.context: Televised address on Saturday evening. Acknowledged some progress but emphasized fundamental gaps remain on 1-2 issues. Denied all 7 of Trump's claims about Iranian concessions. Specifically rejected uranium handover.
statements.factCheckNote: RFE/RL, Euronews, CNN confirmed Ghalibaf's statements. Trump's claims disputed by multiple Iranian officials.

statements.text: There has been progress with the US, but many gaps and some fundamental points remain. We are still far from the final discussion. It is not the case that we think just because we are negotiating, the armed forces are not ready.
statements.context: Televised remarks as chief negotiator. Warned US forces would be fired upon if minesweepers moved 'an inch' from position. Also said 'With the continuation of the blockade, the Strait of Hormuz will not remain open.'
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed remarks. Ghalibaf plays dual role as parliament speaker and chief negotiator.

statements.text: We do not accept negotiations under the shadow of threats, and in the past two weeks, we have prepared to reveal new cards on the battlefield.
statements.context: Ghalibaf broke with hardliners, calling opponents of a deal 'extremist actors undermining negotiations.' Despite the defiant rhetoric, he received supreme leader approval to lead Iran's delegation to Islamabad Round 2.
statements.factCheckNote: 'New cards on the battlefield' language confirmed by CNBC, NBC, Washington Examiner. Approval for talks confirmed by Axios.

statements.text: A complete ceasefire only makes sense if it is not violated by the maritime blockade and the hostage-taking of the world's economy, and if the Zionist warmongering across all fronts is halted. Reopening the Strait of Hormuz is impossible with such a flagrant breach of the ceasefire.
statements.context: Statement linking Hormuz reopening to end of blockade. Ghalibaf also accused Trump of making 'seven claims in one hour, all seven of which were false' regarding uranium transfers.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Fox News liveblog, Jerusalem Post. Uranium rejection confirmed by JPost.

statements.text: You did not achieve your goals through military aggression and you will not achieve them by bullying either. Reopening Hormuz is impossible while US blockade continues.
statements.context: Part of coordinated unity statement. Stated Hormuz reopening 'impossible' with ongoing US blockade.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Press TV, Times of Israel.

statements.text: The enemy's movements, both overt and clandestine, show that despite economic and political pressure, it has not abandoned its military objectives and is seeking to start a new war.
statements.context: Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf accused the US of preparing a new military campaign against Iran on Day 82, echoing Araghchi's simultaneous claim that the US is conducting 'overt and covert' preparations for a fresh attack. The dual accusations came as Trump simultaneously maintained his 2–3 day deal deadline.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Fox News Day 82 liveblog. US officially denies attack preparations; however Trump simultaneously said military remains 'locked and loaded.' Iranian characterization of US movements as 'seeking a new war' is their interpretation of US posture.
statements.source: Fox News Iran war Day 82 liveblog

statements.text: Iran's armed forces have rebuilt themselves during the ceasefire period. If the US foolishly restarts the war, the consequences will be more crushing and bitter. Iran will not compromise on the rights of its nation and country.
statements.context: Iran's Parliament Speaker and chief nuclear negotiator Ghalibaf delivered this warning to Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir during high-level mediation talks in Tehran on May 23. The statement is Iran's sharpest and most explicit military threat since the ceasefire began on April 8. It directly references the US intelligence report that Iran's military reconstitution had exceeded all expected timelines — Ghalibaf appears to be confirming and amplifying this assessment.
statements.venue: Meeting with Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir, Tehran, May 23, 2026
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog (May 23) and ANI News. Corroborated by multiple regional outlets. Pakistan confirmed Munir's Tehran visit and meetings with Araghchi, Ghalibaf, and Pezeshkian.
statements.source: Times of Israel — Iran's top negotiator warns of crushing, bitter consequences if US foolishly restarts war

statements.text: We need to find an off-ramp. The economic damage from this conflict — oil prices, shipping disruptions, market instability — is becoming unsustainable for the global economy.
statements.context: David Sacks, Trump's AI and crypto policy adviser, publicly broke from hawkish administration messaging to call for diplomatic off-ramp. His comments reflect growing concern among economic advisers about the war's financial toll.
statements.factCheckNote: Sacks' public call for an off-ramp was notable as the first senior White House official to publicly advocate for de-escalation during the conflict.

statements.text: Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.
statements.context: Resignation letter — became highest-profile Trump official to quit over the war. 11-time combat veteran, Gold Star spouse.
statements.factCheckNote: Kent's claim about motivation is his opinion; the imminent threat assessment is debated by intelligence community

statements.text: The President concluded that the terrorist Islamist regime in Iran posed an imminent threat and he took action. He is responsible for determining what is and is not an imminent threat.
statements.context: First public statement on the war — did not personally endorse the war, only said the president determines threat level
statements.factCheckNote: Gabbard confirmed statement; notable for NOT endorsing war given her 'No War With Iran' history

statements.text: Yes there are potential leaders in Iran today who can lead the nation in the right direction, use the resources for prosperity, for infrastructure and not to send ballistic missiles to all its neighbors and block the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Danon appeared on CNN and said there are alternative Iranian leaders who could open the door to diplomacy. Declined to name specific individuals but signaled Israel has identified potential negotiating partners.
statements.factCheckNote: C-SPAN and CNN confirmed Danon's remarks. First explicit Israeli acknowledgment of potential diplomatic partners inside Iran.

speakers.name: Kevin Hassett
speakers.title: Director of the National Economic Council

statements.text: If the war were to be extended, it wouldn't really disrupt the US economy very much at all. [Consumer pain] is the last of our concerns right now.
statements.context: CNBC interview. Hassett said consumer pain from rising gas prices was 'the last of our concerns,' sparking fierce backlash from Democrats. Rep. Ted Lieu said he wanted Hassett sent to every swing district to repeat the statement.
statements.factCheckNote: Gas has risen 88 cents/gallon in a month to $3.80, diesel up $1.39 to $5.04. Consumer pain is measurable and growing.

statements.text: This year's celebrations come under different circumstances. The country is effectively in a state of war. Supporters of the Islamic Republic must not leave the squares empty.
statements.context: Police Commander Radan warned of crackdown ahead of Chaharshanbe Suri fire festival. Government sent mass text warnings that 'unconventional behaviour' could result in imprisonment and flogging.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Iran International and IranWire reporting.

statements.text: Bombing Iran will not achieve regime change. We risk a perpetual war. NATO is a defensive alliance, not an offensive one.
statements.context: Shift from initial support to open criticism of war
statements.factCheckNote: DW News confirmed

statements.text: Iran is humiliating the United States. Iranian officials are obviously negotiating very skilfully. They appear clearly stronger than one thought. I cannot see what the American exit strategy is.
statements.context: Major public criticism of US strategy in the Iran war. Represents the deepest transatlantic rift since the conflict began, with the German Chancellor openly questioning American competence in war management.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, The Hill, Bloomberg.

speakers.name: JD Vance
speakers.title: Vice President of the United States

statements.text: We are taking care of business, we are going to be out of there soon, and gas prices will come back down. The president's going to keep at it for a little while longer to ensure that once we leave, we don't have to do this again for a very, very long time. We need to neuter them for a very, very long time and that's the purpose.
statements.context: Podcast interview on the Benny Show, stating the US has accomplished most of its military objectives in Iran and will exit the conflict soon
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz, Times of Israel liveblog, and The Tribune India.

statements.text: Overnight talks with Pakistan FM Munir and Iranian FM Araghchi on the Islamabad Accord ceasefire framework. Two-phase proposal: immediate ceasefire plus Hormuz reopening, then permanent settlement. Reports suggest deal 'could be today.'
statements.context: VP Vance conducted overnight back-channel negotiations via Pakistan as intermediary ahead of Trump's April 7 8PM ET Hormuz deadline
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Breitbart and CNBC. Exact quotes not available — back-channel talks.

statements.text: Traveled to Budapest to meet PM Orban. Will be in Hungary when Trump's Hormuz deadline expires at 8pm ET. Continued indirect talks with Iran through Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir.
statements.context: VP abroad during critical war deadline — indirect talks via Pakistan as primary back-channel
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, Axios.

statements.text: Iran faces two pathways: normalcy or more pain. This war is going to conclude very shortly. We have tools in our toolkit that we so far haven't decided to use.
statements.context: VP Vance in Budapest meeting with PM Orban. Continued indirect talks with Iran through Pakistan mediation.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Times, Middle East Monitor, Fox News.

statements.text: Expected to lead US delegation at Islamabad peace talks starting Friday April 10. Field Marshal Asim Munir in contact 'all night' with Vance and Witkoff to finalize arrangements for the conclusive peace negotiations.
statements.context: VP Vance confirmed as lead US negotiator for Pakistan-hosted peace talks following the two-week ceasefire agreement.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: The Iranians thought that the ceasefire included Lebanon and it just didn't. We never made that promise. There's a legitimate misunderstanding. The ceasefire will be focused on Iran and America's allies, both Israel and the Gulf Arab states. Israel has proposed to restrain itself in Lebanon while negotiations take place. If Iran withdraws over Lebanon, that would be dumb but that's their choice.
statements.context: Vance addressed ceasefire dispute over Lebanon's inclusion from Budapest. Acknowledged 'misunderstanding' but maintained US position that Lebanon was never part of the deal.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Reuters, CBS News.

statements.text: This is a fragile truce. The Lebanon exclusion was a reasonable misunderstanding and the US never made that promise. Some people inside Iran are lying about the deal terms. I actually wonder how good Qalibaf is at understanding English.
statements.context: Vance speaking from Hungary (campaigning for Orban). Will lead US delegation at Islamabad peace talks with Witkoff and Kushner.
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera investigated Vance's claim that Iran's 10-point plan changed. Lebanon inclusion remains contested.

statements.text: If they're going to try to play us, then they're going to find that the negotiating team is not that receptive. If the Iranians are willing to negotiate in good faith, we're certainly willing to extend the open hand.
statements.context: Vance warning before departing Washington for Islamabad to lead US delegation with Witkoff and Kushner for Saturday April 12 peace talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Euronews, AP, Military.com.

statements.text: Arrived in Islamabad Saturday morning and met separately with Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif before face-to-face talks with Iran's delegation began — the first direct US-Iran negotiations since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.
statements.context: Historic Islamabad peace talks — Vance leading US delegation with Witkoff and Kushner. Direct format (not proximity talks as initially expected).
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, Al Jazeera, CNBC, NBC News.

statements.text: The bad news is that we have not reached an agreement. And I think that's bad news for Iran much more than it's bad news for the United States of America. The simple question is: do we see a fundamental commitment of will not to develop a nuclear weapon — not just now, not just two years from now, but for the long term. We haven't seen that yet. We leave here with a very simple proposal, a method of understanding that is our final and best offer. We'll see if the Iranians accept it.
statements.context: Vance press conference at Serena Hotel after 21 hours of negotiations ended without a deal. Spoke with Trump 'a half dozen times, a dozen times' during talks. Then departed aboard Air Force Two.
statements.venue: Islamabad, Pakistan — Serena Hotel Press Conference
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by NPR, AP, Newsweek, CNBC, CBS News, Al Jazeera.

statements.text: Iran is willing to engage in economic terrorism against the entire world. Two can play at that game. The ball really is in their court. We have all the cards.
statements.context: Vance on Fox News defending US blockade of Iranian ports. Accused Iran of 'economic terrorism' for shutting Strait of Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post, ANI News.

statements.text: I feel very good about where we are.
statements.context: Speaking at Turning Point USA event in Georgia. Brief but optimistic comment on Iran negotiations. Previously stated Trump told Iran the US would 'make Iran thrive' if it committed to no nuclear weapons.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios.

statements.text: Trump doesn't want to make, like, a small deal. He wants to make the grand bargain. If you guys commit to not having a nuclear weapon, we are going to make Iran thrive.
statements.context: Turning Point USA event in Georgia. Described Trump's 'grand bargain' approach. Also said: 'A lot of mistrust between Iran and the United States of America. You are not going to solve that problem overnight.' Criticized Pope Leo: 'It would be best for the Vatican to stick to matters of morality.'
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by GPB, Washington Times, PBS.

statements.text: Whether we have further conversations, whether we ultimately get to a deal, I really think the ball is in the Iranian court, because we put a lot on the table.
statements.context: Fox News interview ahead of departing for Islamabad with Witkoff and Kushner for potential Round 2 talks. Iran has not confirmed participation. Ceasefire expires April 22.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, CNN.

statements.text: We are making progress. I'm cautiously optimistic that we can get something done here.
statements.context: Vance provided a brief update on Iran negotiations as Trump traveled to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. The optimistic framing contrasts with Trump's 'deal or decimated' ultimatum issued the same day. Ceasefire expires May 17.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN and Fox News.

statements.text: Further gains have been made since the talks in Islamabad. The fundamental question is whether we make enough progress to satisfy the President's red line — Iran will never have a nuclear weapon.
statements.context: Vance issued statement on Day 76 affirming continued diplomatic progress with Iran despite Trump having rejected Tehran's latest proposal as 'totally unacceptable' days earlier. Pakistan continues to serve as primary mediator. Lebanon-Israel ceasefire expires May 17.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz liveblog and Al Jazeera Day 76 report.

statements.text: We are locked and loaded to restart the military campaign against Iran if nuclear talks fail. We are committed to ensuring Iran never obtains a nuclear weapon. A regional arms race would result if Iran acquired one — and that nuclear weapon could eventually be used to threaten American cities.
statements.context: VP Vance's May 19 statement reinforcing Trump's Truth Social post about calling off the planned Iran strike while warning of readiness to resume military action. Vance expressed preference for diplomacy while maintaining deterrence posture.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Fox News, iHeart/WVOC May 19.
statements.source: Fox News — JD Vance says Trump locked and loaded to restart military campaign on Iran if nuclear talks fail

statements.text: We think that we've made a lot of progress. We're in a pretty good spot here, but there's an option B. Iran cannot possess a nuclear weapon — that is an absolute red line for this administration.
statements.context: Vance elaborating on his May 19 'locked and loaded' statement, now adding more optimistic framing about diplomatic progress while maintaining the military threat. Issued same morning as Araghchi's trust-breakdown statement.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 82 summary article. The 'option B' framing and nuclear weapon red line are consistent with Vance's prior May 19 statement (vance-016).
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran war Day 82 summary

statements.text: The President is locked and loaded. If Iran does not abandon its nuclear weapons program, there will be a resumption of the military campaign. They have two choices: give up the nuclear weapons program or face the consequences.
statements.context: Vance publicly warned Trump is prepared to restart the Iran military campaign if nuclear talks collapse, acknowledging Iran's nuclear weapons development is a firm US red line.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Fox News, May 21, 2026.
statements.source: Fox News — JD Vance says Trump locked and loaded to restart Iran military campaign

statements.text: I feel extremely hopeful that Tehran will agree to a deal that includes a commitment not to develop nuclear weapons.
statements.context: Day 89 — Vance expressed his most optimistic public assessment of the Iran negotiations to date, in contrast to Rubio's cautious 'a few more days' framing. The statement came as Trump claimed both sides were 'close to finalizing' an inspections agreement, while Iranian hardliner Shamkhani simultaneously dismissed the inspection demand as a 'fantasy.' Vance's optimism may reflect White House desire to signal confidence before the scheduled June 2-3 direct talks in Washington.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by ABC News liveblog (May 27).
statements.source: ABC News — Iran live updates peace deal work in progress Rubio
